        Manual Checks

        Detailed Results: High Severity (CAT I)

          V-205653 - Windows Server 2019 reversible password encryption must be disabled.

          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-205653r1051062_rule
          Test Type:Automated
          Rule Result:Pass
          Version:WN19-AC-000090
          Identities:SV-103551
          V-93465
          CCI-004062 (NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: IA-5 (1) (d))
          CCI-000196 (NIST SP 800-53: IA-5 (1) (c); NIST SP 800-53A: IA-5 (1).1 (v); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: IA-5 (1) (c))
          Description: Storing passwords using reversible encryption is essentially the same as storing clear-text versions of the passwords, which are easily compromised. For this reason, this policy must never be enabled.
          Fix Text:Configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Windows Settings >> Security Settings >> Account Policies >> Password Policy >> "Store passwords using reversible encryption" to "Disabled".
          Severity:high
          Weight:10.0
          Reference:
          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows Server 2019
          Publisher:DISA
          Type:DPMS Target
          Subject:Microsoft Windows Server 2019
          Identifier:2907
          Definitions:
          Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.windows2019:def:205653
          Definition Result:true
          Title:WN19-AC-000090 - Windows Server 2019 reversible password encryption must be disabled.
          Description:Storing passwords using reversible encryption is essentially the same as storing clear-text versions of the passwords, which are easily compromised. For this reason, this policy must never be enabled.
          Class:compliance
          Tests:
          • true (All child tests must be true. (AND))
            • true (All child tests must be true. (AND))
              • true ('Store password using reversible encryption' is set to 'Disabled')
          Tests:
          Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:25330500 (passwordpolicy_test)
          Result:true
          Title:'Store password using reversible encryption' is set to 'Disabled'
          Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
          Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).
          Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:obj:20000002 (passwordpolicy_object)
          Object Comment: 
          Object Requirements:
          • Collect any available items.
          State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:25330500 (passwordpolicy_state)
          State Comment: 
          State Requirements:
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'reversible_encryption' must be equal to '0'
          Collected Item/State Result:
          [ true ]
          • max_passwd_age equals '5184000'
          • min_passwd_age equals '86400'
          • min_passwd_len equals '14'
          • password_hist_len equals '24'
          • password_complexity equals '1'
          • reversible_encryption equals '0'
          • anonymous_name_lookup equals '0'

          V-205654 - Windows Server 2019 must be configured to prevent the storage of the LAN Manager hash of passwords.

          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-205654r1051063_rule
          Test Type:Automated
          Rule Result:Pass
          Version:WN19-SO-000300
          Identities:V-93467
          SV-103553
          CCI-004062 (NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: IA-5 (1) (d))
          CCI-000196 (NIST SP 800-53: IA-5 (1) (c); NIST SP 800-53A: IA-5 (1).1 (v); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: IA-5 (1) (c))
          Description: The LAN Manager hash uses a weak encryption algorithm and there are several tools available that use this hash to retrieve account passwords. This setting controls whether a LAN Manager hash of the password is stored in the SAM the next time the password is changed.
          Fix Text:Configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Windows Settings >> Security Settings >> Local Policies >> Security Options >> "Network security: Do not store LAN Manager hash value on next password change" to "Enabled".
          Severity:high
          Weight:10.0
          Reference:
          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows Server 2019
          Publisher:DISA
          Type:DPMS Target
          Subject:Microsoft Windows Server 2019
          Identifier:2907
          Definitions:
          Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.windows2019:def:205654
          Definition Result:true
          Title:WN19-SO-000300 - Windows Server 2019 must be configured to prevent the storage of the LAN Manager hash of passwords.
          Description:The LAN Manager hash uses a weak encryption algorithm and there are several tools available that use this hash to retrieve account passwords. This setting controls whether a LAN Manager hash of the password is stored in the SAM the next time the password is changed.
          Class:compliance
          Tests:
          • true (All child tests must be true. (AND))
            • true (All child tests must be true. (AND))
              • true ('Network security: Do not store LAN Manager hash value on next password change' is set to 'Enabled')
          Tests:
          Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:25346100 (registry_test)
          Result:true
          Title:'Network security: Do not store LAN Manager hash value on next password change' is set to 'Enabled'
          Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
          Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).
          Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:obj:25346100 (registry_object)
          Object Comment:Check the contents of HKLM\System\CurrentControlSet\Control\Lsa\NoLMHash
          Object Requirements:
          • check = 'hive' must be equal to 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE'
          • check = 'key' must be equal to 'System\CurrentControlSet\Control\Lsa'
          • check = 'name' must be equal (case insensitive) to 'NoLMHash'
          State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:20000001 (registry_state)
          State Comment: 
          State Requirements:
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'type' must be equal to 'reg_dword'
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'value' must be equal to '1'
          Collected Item/State Result:
          [ true ]
          • hive equals 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE'
          • key equals 'System\CurrentControlSet\Control\Lsa'
          • name equals 'NoLMHash'
          • last_write_time equals '134212907550000000'
          • type equals 'reg_dword'
          • value equals '1'
          • windows_view equals '64_bit'

          V-205663 - Windows Server 2019 local volumes must use a format that supports NTFS attributes.

          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-205663r1137691_rule
          Test Type:Automated
          Rule Result:Pass
          Version:WN19-00-000130
          Identities:V-92991
          SV-103079
          CCI-000213 (NIST SP 800-53: AC-3; NIST SP 800-53A: AC-3.1; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: AC-3; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: AC-3)
          Description: The ability to set access permissions and auditing is critical to maintaining the security and proper access controls of a system. To support this, volumes must be formatted using a file system that supports NTFS attributes.
          Fix Text:Format volumes to use NTFS, ReFS, or CSV.
          Severity:high
          Weight:10.0
          Reference:
          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows Server 2019
          Publisher:DISA
          Type:DPMS Target
          Subject:Microsoft Windows Server 2019
          Identifier:2907
          Definitions:
          Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.windows2019:def:205663
          Definition Result:true
          Title:WN19-00-000130 - Windows Server 2019 local volumes must use a format that supports NTFS attributes.
          Description:The ability to set access permissions and auditing is critical to maintaining the security and proper access controls of a system. To support this, volumes must be formatted using a file system that supports NTFS attributes.
          Class:compliance
          Tests:
          • true (All child tests must be true. (AND))
            • true (All child tests must be true. (AND))
              • true (local volumes are formatted using NTFS, ReFS or CSVFS)
          Tests:
          Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:25425000 (wmi57_test)
          Result:true
          Title:local volumes are formatted using NTFS, ReFS or CSVFS
          Check Existence:No collected items may exist.
          Check:Result is based on check existence only.
          Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:obj:25425000 (wmi57_object)
          Object Comment: 
          Object Requirements:
          • check = 'namespace' must be equal to 'root\cimv2'
          • check = 'wql' must be equal to 'SELECT filesystem FROM win32_logicaldisk WHERE drivetype=3 AND filesystem!='NTFS' AND filesystem!='ReFS' AND filesystem!='CSVFS''
          Collected Item/State Result:
          [ exists ]
          • namespace equals 'root\cimv2'
          • wql equals 'SELECT filesystem FROM win32_logicaldisk WHERE drivetype=3 AND filesystem!='NTFS' AND filesystem!='ReFS' AND filesystem!='CSVFS''

          V-205711 - Windows Server 2019 Windows Remote Management (WinRM) client must not use Basic authentication.

          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-205711r958510_rule
          Test Type:Automated
          Rule Result:Pass
          Version:WN19-CC-000470
          Identities:V-93503
          SV-103589
          CCI-000877 (NIST SP 800-53: MA-4 c; NIST SP 800-53A: MA-4.1 (iv); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: MA-4 c; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: MA-4 c)
          Description: Basic authentication uses plain-text passwords that could be used to compromise a system. Disabling Basic authentication will reduce this potential.
          Fix Text:Configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Administrative Templates >> Windows Components >> Windows Remote Management (WinRM) >> WinRM Client >> "Allow Basic authentication" to "Disabled".
          Severity:high
          Weight:10.0
          Reference:
          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows Server 2019
          Publisher:DISA
          Type:DPMS Target
          Subject:Microsoft Windows Server 2019
          Identifier:2907
          Definitions:
          Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.windows2019:def:205711
          Definition Result:true
          Title:WN19-CC-000470 - Windows Server 2019 Windows Remote Management (WinRM) client must not use Basic authentication.
          Description:Basic authentication uses plain-text passwords that could be used to compromise a system. Disabling Basic authentication will reduce this potential.
          Class:compliance
          Tests:
          • true (All child tests must be true. (AND))
            • true (All child tests must be true. (AND))
              • true ('Allow Basic authentication' is set to 'Disabled')
          Tests:
          Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:25341600 (registry_test)
          Result:true
          Title:'Allow Basic authentication' is set to 'Disabled'
          Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
          Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).
          Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:obj:25341600 (registry_object)
          Object Comment: 
          Object Requirements:
          • check = 'hive' must be equal to 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE'
          • check = 'key' must be equal to 'Software\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\WinRM\Client'
          • check = 'name' must be equal to 'AllowBasic'
          State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:20000000 (registry_state)
          State Comment: 
          State Requirements:
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'type' must be equal to 'reg_dword'
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'value' must be equal to '0'
          Collected Item/State Result:
          [ true ]
          • hive equals 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE'
          • key equals 'Software\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\WinRM\Client'
          • name equals 'AllowBasic'
          • last_write_time equals '134213413420000000'
          • type equals 'reg_dword'
          • value equals '0'
          • windows_view equals '64_bit'

          V-205724 - Windows Server 2019 must not allow anonymous enumeration of shares.

          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-205724r1137695_rule
          Test Type:Automated
          Rule Result:Pass
          Version:WN19-SO-000230
          Identities:V-93537
          SV-103623
          CCI-001090 (NIST SP 800-53: SC-4; NIST SP 800-53A: SC-4.1; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: SC-4; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: SC-4)
          Description: Allowing anonymous logon users (null session connections) to list all account names and enumerate all shared resources can provide a map of potential points to attack the system.
          Fix Text:Configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Windows Settings >> Security Settings >> Local Policies >> Security Options >> "Network access: Do not allow anonymous enumeration of SAM accounts and shares" to "Enabled".
          Severity:high
          Weight:10.0
          Reference:
          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows Server 2019
          Publisher:DISA
          Type:DPMS Target
          Subject:Microsoft Windows Server 2019
          Identifier:2907
          Definitions:
          Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.windows2019:def:205724
          Definition Result:true
          Title:WN19-SO-000230 - Windows Server 2019 must not allow anonymous enumeration of shares.
          Description:Allowing anonymous logon users (null session connections) to list all account names and enumerate all shared resources can provide a map of potential points to attack the system.
          Class:compliance
          Tests:
          • true (All child tests must be true. (AND))
            • true (All child tests must be true. (AND))
              • true ('Network access: Do not allow anonymous enumeration of SAM accounts and shares' is set to 'Enabled')
          Tests:
          Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:25345400 (registry_test)
          Result:true
          Title:'Network access: Do not allow anonymous enumeration of SAM accounts and shares' is set to 'Enabled'
          Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
          Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).
          Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:obj:25345400 (registry_object)
          Object Comment: 
          Object Requirements:
          • check = 'hive' must be equal to 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE'
          • check = 'key' must be equal to 'SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Control\Lsa'
          • check = 'name' must be equal to 'restrictanonymous'
          State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:20000001 (registry_state)
          State Comment: 
          State Requirements:
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'type' must be equal to 'reg_dword'
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'value' must be equal to '1'
          Collected Item/State Result:
          [ true ]
          • hive equals 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE'
          • key equals 'SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Control\Lsa'
          • name equals 'restrictanonymous'
          • last_write_time equals '134212907550000000'
          • type equals 'reg_dword'
          • value equals '1'
          • windows_view equals '64_bit'

          V-205725 - Windows Server 2019 must restrict anonymous access to Named Pipes and Shares.

          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-205725r1137695_rule
          Test Type:Automated
          Rule Result:Pass
          Version:WN19-SO-000250
          Identities:V-93539
          SV-103625
          CCI-001090 (NIST SP 800-53: SC-4; NIST SP 800-53A: SC-4.1; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: SC-4; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: SC-4)
          Description: Allowing anonymous access to named pipes or shares provides the potential for unauthorized system access. This setting restricts access to those defined in "Network access: Named Pipes that can be accessed anonymously" and "Network access: Shares that can be accessed anonymously", both of which must be blank under other requirements.
          Fix Text:Configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Windows Settings >> Security Settings >> Local Policies >> Security Options >> "Network access: Restrict anonymous access to Named Pipes and Shares" to "Enabled".
          Severity:high
          Weight:10.0
          Reference:
          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows Server 2019
          Publisher:DISA
          Type:DPMS Target
          Subject:Microsoft Windows Server 2019
          Identifier:2907
          Definitions:
          Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.windows2019:def:205725
          Definition Result:true
          Title:WN19-SO-000250 - Windows Server 2019 must restrict anonymous access to Named Pipes and Shares.
          Description:Allowing anonymous access to named pipes or shares provides the potential for unauthorized system access. This setting restricts access to those defined in "Network access: Named Pipes that can be accessed anonymously" and "Network access: Shares that can be accessed anonymously", both of which must be blank under other requirements.
          Class:compliance
          Tests:
          • true (All child tests must be true. (AND))
            • true (All child tests must be true. (AND))
              • true ('Network access: Restrict anonymous access to Named Pipes and Shares' is set to 'Enabled')
          Tests:
          Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:25345600 (registry_test)
          Result:true
          Title:'Network access: Restrict anonymous access to Named Pipes and Shares' is set to 'Enabled'
          Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
          Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).
          Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:obj:25345600 (registry_object)
          Object Comment: 
          Object Requirements:
          • check = 'hive' must be equal to 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE'
          • check = 'key' must be equal to 'SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Services\LanmanServer\Parameters'
          • check = 'name' must be equal to 'restrictnullsessaccess'
          State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:20000001 (registry_state)
          State Comment: 
          State Requirements:
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'type' must be equal to 'reg_dword'
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'value' must be equal to '1'
          Collected Item/State Result:
          [ true ]
          • hive equals 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE'
          • key equals 'SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Services\LanmanServer\Parameters'
          • name equals 'restrictnullsessaccess'
          • last_write_time equals '134212836120000000'
          • type equals 'reg_dword'
          • value equals '1'
          • windows_view equals '64_bit'

          V-205739 - Windows Server 2019 permissions on the Active Directory data files must only allow System and Administrators access.

          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-205739r958726_rule
          Test Type:Automated
          Rule Applicability:Windows_DomainController
          Rule Result:Not Applicable
          Version:WN19-DC-000070
          Identities:SV-103117
          V-93029
          CCI-002235 (NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: AC-6 (10); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: AC-6 (10))
          Description: Improper access permissions for directory data-related files could allow unauthorized users to read, modify, or delete directory data or audit trails.
          Fix Text:Maintain the permissions on NTDS database and log files as follows:

          NT AUTHORITY\SYSTEM:(I)(F)
          BUILTIN\Administrators:(I)(F)

          (I) - permission inherited from parent container
          (F) - full access
          Severity:high
          Weight:10.0
          Reference:
          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows Server 2019
          Publisher:DISA
          Type:DPMS Target
          Subject:Microsoft Windows Server 2019
          Identifier:2907

          V-205750 - Windows Server 2019 Act as part of the operating system user right must not be assigned to any groups or accounts.

          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-205750r958726_rule
          Test Type:Automated
          Rule Result:Pass
          Version:WN19-UR-000020
          Identities:SV-103139
          V-93051
          CCI-002235 (NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: AC-6 (10); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: AC-6 (10))
          Description: Inappropriate granting of user rights can provide system, administrative, and other high-level capabilities.

          Accounts with the "Act as part of the operating system" user right can assume the identity of any user and gain access to resources that the user is authorized to access. Any accounts with this right can take complete control of a system.
          Fix Text:Configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Windows Settings >> Security Settings >> Local Policies >> User Rights Assignment >> "Act as part of the operating system" to be defined but containing no entries (blank).
          Severity:high
          Weight:10.0
          Reference:
          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows Server 2019
          Publisher:DISA
          Type:DPMS Target
          Subject:Microsoft Windows Server 2019
          Identifier:2907
          Definitions:
          Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.windows2019:def:205750
          Definition Result:true
          Title:WN19-UR-000020 - Windows Server 2019 Act as part of the operating system user right must not be assigned to any groups or accounts.
          Description:Inappropriate granting of user rights can provide system, administrative, and other high-level capabilities.

          Accounts with the "Act as part of the operating system" user right can assume the identity of any user and gain access to resources that the user is authorized to access. Any accounts with this right can take complete control of a system.
          Class:compliance
          Tests:
          • true (All child tests must be true. (AND))
            • true (All child tests must be true. (AND))
              • true (No users have the privilege to act as part of the operating system.)
          Tests:
          Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:25348100 (accesstoken_test)
          Result:true
          Title:No users have the privilege to act as part of the operating system.
          Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
          Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).
          Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:obj:20000008 (accesstoken_object)
          Object Comment:All security principles
          Object Requirements:
          • check = 'security_principle' must match the pattern '.+'
          State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:25348100 (accesstoken_state)
          State Comment: 
          State Requirements:
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'setcbprivilege' must be equal to '0'
          Collected Item/State Result:
          [ true ]
          • setcbprivilege equals '1' for:
          • setcbprivilege equals '0' for Access Control Assistance Operators, Administrators, Backup Operators, Certificate Service DCOM Access, Cryptographic Operators, Device Owners, Distributed COM Users, Event Log Readers, Guests, Hyper-V Administrators, IIS_IUSRS, Network Configuration Operators, Performance Log Users, Performance Monitor Users, Power Users, Print Operators, RDS Endpoint Servers, RDS Management Servers, RDS Remote Access Servers, Remote Desktop Users, Remote Management Users, Replicator, Storage Replica Administrators, System Managed Accounts Group, Users, DefaultAccount, Visitor, WDAGUtilityAccount, X_Admin, NETWORK SERVICE, LOCAL SERVICE, SYSTEM, IUSR, TERMINAL SERVER USER, Authenticated Users, ENTERPRISE DOMAIN CONTROLLERS, PROXY, ANONYMOUS LOGON, SERVICE, INTERACTIVE, BATCH, NETWORK, DIALUP, CREATOR GROUP SERVER, CREATOR OWNER SERVER, CREATOR GROUP, CREATOR OWNER, Everyone, ELAM\Enterprise Admins, ELAM\Domain Admins, Local account, NT SERVICE\ALL SERVICES, NT SERVICE\WdiServiceHost

          V-205753 - Windows Server 2019 Create a token object user right must not be assigned to any groups or accounts.

          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-205753r958726_rule
          Test Type:Automated
          Rule Result:Pass
          Version:WN19-UR-000060
          Identities:V-93057
          SV-103145
          CCI-002235 (NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: AC-6 (10); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: AC-6 (10))
          Description: Inappropriate granting of user rights can provide system, administrative, and other high-level capabilities.

          The "Create a token object" user right allows a process to create an access token. This could be used to provide elevated rights and compromise a system.
          Fix Text:Configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Windows Settings >> Security Settings >> Local Policies >> User Rights Assignment >> "Create a token object" to be defined but containing no entries (blank).
          Severity:high
          Weight:10.0
          Reference:
          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows Server 2019
          Publisher:DISA
          Type:DPMS Target
          Subject:Microsoft Windows Server 2019
          Identifier:2907
          Definitions:
          Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.windows2019:def:205753
          Definition Result:true
          Title:WN19-UR-000060 - Windows Server 2019 Create a token object user right must not be assigned to any groups or accounts.
          Description:Inappropriate granting of user rights can provide system, administrative, and other high-level capabilities.

          The "Create a token object" user right allows a process to create an access token. This could be used to provide elevated rights and compromise a system.
          Class:compliance
          Tests:
          • true (All child tests must be true. (AND))
            • true (All child tests must be true. (AND))
              • true (Create a token object - None)
          Tests:
          Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:25348600 (accesstoken_test)
          Result:true
          Title:Create a token object - None
          Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
          Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).
          Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:obj:20000008 (accesstoken_object)
          Object Comment:All security principles
          Object Requirements:
          • check = 'security_principle' must match the pattern '.+'
          State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:25348600 (accesstoken_state)
          State Comment:Create a token object
          State Requirements:
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'secreatetokenprivilege' must be equal to '0'
          Collected Item/State Result:
          [ true ]
          • secreatetokenprivilege equals '1' for:
          • secreatetokenprivilege equals '0' for Access Control Assistance Operators, Administrators, Backup Operators, Certificate Service DCOM Access, Cryptographic Operators, Device Owners, Distributed COM Users, Event Log Readers, Guests, Hyper-V Administrators, IIS_IUSRS, Network Configuration Operators, Performance Log Users, Performance Monitor Users, Power Users, Print Operators, RDS Endpoint Servers, RDS Management Servers, RDS Remote Access Servers, Remote Desktop Users, Remote Management Users, Replicator, Storage Replica Administrators, System Managed Accounts Group, Users, DefaultAccount, Visitor, WDAGUtilityAccount, X_Admin, NETWORK SERVICE, LOCAL SERVICE, SYSTEM, IUSR, TERMINAL SERVER USER, Authenticated Users, ENTERPRISE DOMAIN CONTROLLERS, PROXY, ANONYMOUS LOGON, SERVICE, INTERACTIVE, BATCH, NETWORK, DIALUP, CREATOR GROUP SERVER, CREATOR OWNER SERVER, CREATOR GROUP, CREATOR OWNER, Everyone, ELAM\Enterprise Admins, ELAM\Domain Admins, Local account, NT SERVICE\ALL SERVICES, NT SERVICE\WdiServiceHost

          V-205757 - Windows Server 2019 Debug programs: user right must only be assigned to the Administrators group.

          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-205757r958726_rule
          Test Type:Automated
          Rule Result:Pass
          Version:WN19-UR-000100
          Identities:V-93065
          SV-103153
          CCI-002235 (NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: AC-6 (10); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: AC-6 (10))
          Description: Inappropriate granting of user rights can provide system, administrative, and other high-level capabilities.

          Accounts with the "Debug programs" user right can attach a debugger to any process or to the kernel, providing complete access to sensitive and critical operating system components. This right is given to Administrators in the default configuration.
          Fix Text:Configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Windows Settings >> Security Settings >> Local Policies >> User Rights Assignment >> "Debug programs" to include only the following accounts or groups:

          - Administrators
          Severity:high
          Weight:10.0
          Reference:
          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows Server 2019
          Publisher:DISA
          Type:DPMS Target
          Subject:Microsoft Windows Server 2019
          Identifier:2907
          Definitions:
          Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.windows2019:def:205757
          Definition Result:true
          Title:WN19-UR-000100 - Windows Server 2019 Debug programs: user right must only be assigned to the Administrators group.
          Description:Inappropriate granting of user rights can provide system, administrative, and other high-level capabilities.

          Accounts with the "Debug programs" user right can attach a debugger to any process or to the kernel, providing complete access to sensitive and critical operating system components. This right is given to Administrators in the default configuration.
          Class:compliance
          Tests:
          • true (All child tests must be true. (AND))
            • true (All child tests must be true. (AND))
              • true (Debug Programs - Administrators)
          Tests:
          Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:25349000 (accesstoken_test)
          Result:true
          Title:Debug Programs - Administrators
          Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
          Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).
          Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:obj:20000009 (accesstoken_object)
          Object Comment:Administrators
          Object Requirements:
          • Collect any available items.
          Exclude Items If:
          • security_principle equals (case insensitive) 'Administrators'
          State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:25349000 (accesstoken_state)
          State Comment:Debug programs
          State Requirements:
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'sedebugprivilege' must be equal to '0'
          Collected Item/State Result:
          [ true ]
          • sedebugprivilege equals '1' for:
          • sedebugprivilege equals '0' for Replicator, NETWORK, Performance Monitor Users, Authenticated Users, SERVICE, ANONYMOUS LOGON, ELAM\Domain Admins, Performance Log Users, NT SERVICE\WdiServiceHost, Hyper-V Administrators, ENTERPRISE DOMAIN CONTROLLERS, RDS Endpoint Servers, RDS Management Servers, RDS Remote Access Servers, BATCH, PROXY, IUSR, NETWORK SERVICE, Print Operators, Remote Management Users, Access Control Assistance Operators, TERMINAL SERVER USER, Power Users, CREATOR GROUP, WDAGUtilityAccount, X_Admin, Local account, DefaultAccount, Device Owners, IIS_IUSRS, Users, Cryptographic Operators, CREATOR OWNER SERVER, SYSTEM, NT SERVICE\ALL SERVICES, Network Configuration Operators, CREATOR GROUP SERVER, System Managed Accounts Group, Certificate Service DCOM Access, Storage Replica Administrators, Backup Operators, Remote Desktop Users, CREATOR OWNER, LOCAL SERVICE, Visitor, Distributed COM Users, ELAM\Enterprise Admins, INTERACTIVE, Everyone, Event Log Readers, DIALUP, Guests

          V-205802 - Windows Server 2019 must disable the Windows Installer Always install with elevated privileges option.

          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-205802r1051079_rule
          Test Type:Automated
          Rule Result:Pass
          Version:WN19-CC-000430
          Identities:V-93201
          SV-103289
          CCI-003980 (NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: CM-11 (2))
          CCI-001812 (NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: CM-11 (2))
          Description: Standard user accounts must not be granted elevated privileges. Enabling Windows Installer to elevate privileges when installing applications can allow malicious persons and applications to gain full control of a system.
          Fix Text:Configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Administrative Templates >> Windows Components >> Windows Installer >> "Always install with elevated privileges" to "Disabled".
          Severity:high
          Weight:10.0
          Reference:
          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows Server 2019
          Publisher:DISA
          Type:DPMS Target
          Subject:Microsoft Windows Server 2019
          Identifier:2907
          Definitions:
          Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.windows2019:def:205802
          Definition Result:true
          Title:WN19-CC-000430 - Windows Server 2019 must disable the Windows Installer Always install with elevated privileges option.
          Description:Standard user accounts must not be granted elevated privileges. Enabling Windows Installer to elevate privileges when installing applications can allow malicious persons and applications to gain full control of a system.
          Class:compliance
          Tests:
          • true (All child tests must be true. (AND))
            • true (All child tests must be true. (AND))
              • true ('Always install with elevated privileges' is set to 'Disabled')
          Tests:
          Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:25341100 (registry_test)
          Result:true
          Title:'Always install with elevated privileges' is set to 'Disabled'
          Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
          Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).
          Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:obj:25341100 (registry_object)
          Object Comment: 
          Object Requirements:
          • check = 'hive' must be equal to 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE'
          • check = 'key' must be equal to 'SOFTWARE\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\Installer'
          • check = 'name' must be equal to 'AlwaysInstallElevated'
          State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:20000000 (registry_state)
          State Comment: 
          State Requirements:
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'type' must be equal to 'reg_dword'
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'value' must be equal to '0'
          Collected Item/State Result:
          [ true ]
          • hive equals 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE'
          • key equals 'SOFTWARE\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\Installer'
          • name equals 'AlwaysInstallElevated'
          • last_write_time equals '134213413420000000'
          • type equals 'reg_dword'
          • value equals '0'
          • windows_view equals '64_bit'

          V-205804 - Windows Server 2019 Autoplay must be turned off for non-volume devices.

          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-205804r958804_rule
          Test Type:Automated
          Rule Result:Pass
          Version:WN19-CC-000210
          Identities:V-93373
          SV-103459
          CCI-001764 (NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: CM-7 (2); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: CM-7 (2))
          Description: Allowing AutoPlay to execute may introduce malicious code to a system. AutoPlay begins reading from a drive as soon as media is inserted into the drive. As a result, the setup file of programs or music on audio media may start. This setting will disable AutoPlay for non-volume devices, such as Media Transfer Protocol (MTP) devices.
          Fix Text:Configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Administrative Templates >> Windows Components >> AutoPlay Policies >> "Disallow Autoplay for non-volume devices" to "Enabled".
          Severity:high
          Weight:10.0
          Reference:
          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows Server 2019
          Publisher:DISA
          Type:DPMS Target
          Subject:Microsoft Windows Server 2019
          Identifier:2907
          Definitions:
          Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.windows2019:def:205804
          Definition Result:true
          Title:WN19-CC-000210 - Windows Server 2019 Autoplay must be turned off for non-volume devices.
          Description:Allowing AutoPlay to execute may introduce malicious code to a system. AutoPlay begins reading from a drive as soon as media is inserted into the drive. As a result, the setup file of programs or music on audio media may start. This setting will disable AutoPlay for non-volume devices, such as Media Transfer Protocol (MTP) devices.
          Class:compliance
          Tests:
          • true (All child tests must be true. (AND))
            • true (All child tests must be true. (AND))
              • true ('Disallow Autoplay for non-volume devices' is set to 'Enabled')
          Tests:
          Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:25338600 (registry_test)
          Result:true
          Title:'Disallow Autoplay for non-volume devices' is set to 'Enabled'
          Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
          Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).
          Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:obj:25338600 (registry_object)
          Object Comment: 
          Object Requirements:
          • check = 'hive' must be equal to 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE'
          • check = 'key' must be equal to 'SOFTWARE\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\Explorer'
          • check = 'name' must be equal to 'NoAutoplayfornonVolume'
          State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:20000001 (registry_state)
          State Comment: 
          State Requirements:
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'type' must be equal to 'reg_dword'
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'value' must be equal to '1'
          Collected Item/State Result:
          [ true ]
          • hive equals 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE'
          • key equals 'SOFTWARE\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\Explorer'
          • name equals 'NoAutoplayfornonVolume'
          • last_write_time equals '134213413420000000'
          • type equals 'reg_dword'
          • value equals '1'
          • windows_view equals '64_bit'

          V-205805 - Windows Server 2019 default AutoRun behavior must be configured to prevent AutoRun commands.

          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-205805r958804_rule
          Test Type:Automated
          Rule Result:Pass
          Version:WN19-CC-000220
          Identities:V-93375
          SV-103461
          CCI-001764 (NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: CM-7 (2); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: CM-7 (2))
          Description: Allowing AutoRun commands to execute may introduce malicious code to a system. Configuring this setting prevents AutoRun commands from executing.
          Fix Text:Configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Administrative Templates >> Windows Components >> AutoPlay Policies >> "Set the default behavior for AutoRun" to "Enabled" with "Do not execute any autorun commands" selected.
          Severity:high
          Weight:10.0
          Reference:
          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows Server 2019
          Publisher:DISA
          Type:DPMS Target
          Subject:Microsoft Windows Server 2019
          Identifier:2907
          Definitions:
          Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.windows2019:def:205805
          Definition Result:true
          Title:WN19-CC-000220 - Windows Server 2019 default AutoRun behavior must be configured to prevent AutoRun commands.
          Description:Allowing AutoRun commands to execute may introduce malicious code to a system. Configuring this setting prevents AutoRun commands from executing.
          Class:compliance
          Tests:
          • true (All child tests must be true. (AND))
            • true (All child tests must be true. (AND))
              • true ('Default behavior for AutoRun' is set to 'Enabled' with 'Do not execute any autorun commands' selected)
          Tests:
          Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:25338700 (registry_test)
          Result:true
          Title:'Default behavior for AutoRun' is set to 'Enabled' with 'Do not execute any autorun commands' selected
          Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
          Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).
          Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:obj:25338700 (registry_object)
          Object Comment: 
          Object Requirements:
          • check = 'hive' must be equal to 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE'
          • check = 'key' must be equal to 'Software\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Policies\Explorer'
          • check = 'name' must be equal to 'NoAutorun'
          State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:20000001 (registry_state)
          State Comment: 
          State Requirements:
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'type' must be equal to 'reg_dword'
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'value' must be equal to '1'
          Collected Item/State Result:
          [ true ]
          • hive equals 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE'
          • key equals 'Software\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Policies\Explorer'
          • name equals 'NoAutorun'
          • last_write_time equals '134213413420000000'
          • type equals 'reg_dword'
          • value equals '1'
          • windows_view equals '64_bit'

          V-205806 - Windows Server 2019 AutoPlay must be disabled for all drives.

          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-205806r958804_rule
          Test Type:Automated
          Rule Result:Pass
          Version:WN19-CC-000230
          Identities:V-93377
          SV-103463
          CCI-001764 (NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: CM-7 (2); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: CM-7 (2))
          Description: Allowing AutoPlay to execute may introduce malicious code to a system. AutoPlay begins reading from a drive as soon media is inserted into the drive. As a result, the setup file of programs or music on audio media may start. By default, AutoPlay is disabled on removable drives, such as the floppy disk drive (but not the CD-ROM drive) and on network drives. Enabling this policy disables AutoPlay on all drives.
          Fix Text:Configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Administrative Templates >> Windows Components >> AutoPlay Policies >> "Turn off AutoPlay" to "Enabled" with "All Drives" selected.
          Severity:high
          Weight:10.0
          Reference:
          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows Server 2019
          Publisher:DISA
          Type:DPMS Target
          Subject:Microsoft Windows Server 2019
          Identifier:2907
          Definitions:
          Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.windows2019:def:205806
          Definition Result:true
          Title:WN19-CC-000230 - Windows Server 2019 AutoPlay must be disabled for all drives.
          Description:Allowing AutoPlay to execute may introduce malicious code to a system. AutoPlay begins reading from a drive as soon media is inserted into the drive. As a result, the setup file of programs or music on audio media may start. By default, AutoPlay is disabled on removable drives, such as the floppy disk drive (but not the CD-ROM drive) and on network drives. Enabling this policy disables AutoPlay on all drives.
          Class:compliance
          Tests:
          • true (All child tests must be true. (AND))
            • true (All child tests must be true. (AND))
              • true ('Turn off AutoPlay' is set to 'Enabled:All Drives')
          Tests:
          Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:25338800 (registry_test)
          Result:true
          Title:'Turn off AutoPlay' is set to 'Enabled:All Drives'
          Check Existence:All collected items must exist.
          Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).
          Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:obj:25338800 (registry_object)
          Object Comment: 
          Object Requirements:
          • check = 'hive' must be equal to 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE'
          • check = 'key' must be equal to 'SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Policies\Explorer'
          • check = 'name' must be equal to 'NoDriveTypeAutoRun'
          State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:25338800 (registry_state)
          State Comment:Reg_Dword type and value equals 255
          State Requirements:
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'type' must be equal to 'reg_dword'
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'value' must be equal to '255'
          Collected Item/State Result:
          [ true ]
          • hive equals 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE'
          • key equals 'SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Policies\Explorer'
          • name equals 'NoDriveTypeAutoRun'
          • last_write_time equals '134213413420000000'
          • type equals 'reg_dword'
          • value equals '255'
          • windows_view equals '64_bit'

          V-205849 - Windows Server 2019 must be maintained at a supported servicing level.

          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-205849r991589_rule
          Test Type:Automated
          Rule Result:Pass
          Version:WN19-00-000100
          Identities:SV-103303
          V-93215
          CCI-000366 (NIST SP 800-53: CM-6 b; NIST SP 800-53A: CM-6.1 (iv); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: CM-6 b; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: CM-6 b)
          Description: Systems at unsupported servicing levels will not receive security updates for new vulnerabilities, which leave them subject to exploitation. Systems must be maintained at a servicing level supported by the vendor with new security updates.
          Fix Text:Update the system to a Version 1809 (Build 17763.xxx) or greater.
          Severity:high
          Weight:10.0
          Reference:
          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows Server 2019
          Publisher:DISA
          Type:DPMS Target
          Subject:Microsoft Windows Server 2019
          Identifier:2907
          Definitions:
          Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.windows2019:def:205849
          Definition Result:true
          Title:WN19-00-000100 - Windows Server 2019 must be maintained at a supported servicing level.
          Description:Systems at unsupported servicing levels will not receive security updates for new vulnerabilities, which leave them subject to exploitation. Systems must be maintained at a servicing level supported by the vendor with new security updates.
          Class:compliance
          Tests:
          • true (All child tests must be true. (AND))
            • true (All child tests must be true. (AND))
              • true (Windows system is v1809 or later)
              • true (The Windows Build is 17763 or higher.)
          Tests:
          Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:10001800 (registry_test)
          Result:true
          Title:Windows system is v1809 or later
          Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
          Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).
          Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:obj:10001600 (registry_object)
          Object Comment: 
          Object Requirements:
          • check = 'hive' must be equal to 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE'
          • check = 'key' must be equal to 'SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Windows NT\CurrentVersion'
          • check = 'name' must be equal to 'ReleaseId'
          State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:10001800 (registry_state)
          State Comment: 
          State Requirements:
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'type' must be equal to 'reg_sz'
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'value' must be greater than or equal to '1809'
          Collected Item/State Result:
          [ true ]
          • hive equals 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE'
          • key equals 'SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Windows NT\CurrentVersion'
          • name equals 'ReleaseId'
          • last_write_time equals '134213441940000000'
          • type equals 'reg_sz'
          • value equals '1809'
          • windows_view equals '64_bit'
          Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:20584900 (registry_test)
          Result:true
          Title:The Windows Build is 17763 or higher.
          Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
          Check:At least one collected item must match the given state(s).
          Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:obj:25326301 (registry_object)
          Object Comment: 
          Object Requirements:
          • check = 'hive' must be equal to 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE'
          • check = 'key' must be equal to 'SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Windows NT\CurrentVersion'
          • check = 'name' must be equal to 'CurrentBuildNumber'
          State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:20584900 (registry_state)
          State Comment: 
          State Requirements:
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'value' must be greater than or equal to '17763'
          Collected Item/State Result:
          [ true ]
          • hive equals 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE'
          • key equals 'SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Windows NT\CurrentVersion'
          • name equals 'CurrentBuildNumber'
          • last_write_time equals '134213441940000000'
          • type equals 'reg_sz'
          • value equals '17763'
          • windows_view equals '64_bit'

          V-205908 - Windows Server 2019 must prevent local accounts with blank passwords from being used from the network.

          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-205908r991589_rule
          Test Type:Automated
          Rule Result:Pass
          Version:WN19-SO-000020
          Identities:V-93279
          SV-103367
          CCI-000366 (NIST SP 800-53: CM-6 b; NIST SP 800-53A: CM-6.1 (iv); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: CM-6 b; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: CM-6 b)
          Description: An account without a password can allow unauthorized access to a system as only the username would be required. Password policies should prevent accounts with blank passwords from existing on a system. However, if a local account with a blank password does exist, enabling this setting will prevent network access, limiting the account to local console logon only.
          Fix Text:Configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Windows Settings >> Security Settings >> Local Policies >> Security Options >> "Accounts: Limit local account use of blank passwords to console logon only" to "Enabled".
          Severity:high
          Weight:10.0
          Reference:
          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows Server 2019
          Publisher:DISA
          Type:DPMS Target
          Subject:Microsoft Windows Server 2019
          Identifier:2907
          Definitions:
          Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.windows2019:def:205908
          Definition Result:true
          Title:WN19-SO-000020 - Windows Server 2019 must prevent local accounts with blank passwords from being used from the network.
          Description:An account without a password can allow unauthorized access to a system as only the username would be required. Password policies should prevent accounts with blank passwords from existing on a system. However, if a local account with a blank password does exist, enabling this setting will prevent network access, limiting the account to local console logon only.
          Class:compliance
          Tests:
          • true (All child tests must be true. (AND))
            • true (All child tests must be true. (AND))
              • true ('Accounts: Limit local account use of blank passwords to console logon only' is set to 'Enabled')
          Tests:
          Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:25343400 (registry_test)
          Result:true
          Title:'Accounts: Limit local account use of blank passwords to console logon only' is set to 'Enabled'
          Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
          Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).
          Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:obj:25343400 (registry_object)
          Object Comment: 
          Object Requirements:
          • check = 'hive' must be equal to 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE'
          • check = 'key' must be equal to 'SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Control\Lsa'
          • check = 'name' must be equal to 'LimitBlankPasswordUse'
          State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:20000001 (registry_state)
          State Comment: 
          State Requirements:
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'type' must be equal to 'reg_dword'
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'value' must be equal to '1'
          Collected Item/State Result:
          [ true ]
          • hive equals 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE'
          • key equals 'SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Control\Lsa'
          • name equals 'LimitBlankPasswordUse'
          • last_write_time equals '134212907550000000'
          • type equals 'reg_dword'
          • value equals '1'
          • windows_view equals '64_bit'

          V-205914 - Windows Server 2019 must not allow anonymous enumeration of Security Account Manager (SAM) accounts.

          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-205914r991589_rule
          Test Type:Automated
          Rule Result:Pass
          Version:WN19-SO-000220
          Identities:V-93291
          SV-103379
          CCI-000366 (NIST SP 800-53: CM-6 b; NIST SP 800-53A: CM-6.1 (iv); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: CM-6 b; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: CM-6 b)
          Description: Anonymous enumeration of SAM accounts allows anonymous logon users (null session connections) to list all accounts names, thus providing a list of potential points to attack the system.
          Fix Text:Configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Windows Settings >> Security Settings >> Local Policies >> Security Options >> "Network access: Do not allow anonymous enumeration of SAM accounts" to "Enabled".
          Severity:high
          Weight:10.0
          Reference:
          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows Server 2019
          Publisher:DISA
          Type:DPMS Target
          Subject:Microsoft Windows Server 2019
          Identifier:2907
          Definitions:
          Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.windows2019:def:205914
          Definition Result:true
          Title:WN19-SO-000220 - Windows Server 2019 must not allow anonymous enumeration of Security Account Manager (SAM) accounts.
          Description:Anonymous enumeration of SAM accounts allows anonymous logon users (null session connections) to list all accounts names, thus providing a list of potential points to attack the system.
          Class:compliance
          Tests:
          • true (All child tests must be true. (AND))
            • true (All child tests must be true. (AND))
              • true ('Network access: Do not allow anonymous enumeration of SAM accounts' is set to 'Enabled')
          Tests:
          Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:25345300 (registry_test)
          Result:true
          Title:'Network access: Do not allow anonymous enumeration of SAM accounts' is set to 'Enabled'
          Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
          Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).
          Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:obj:25345300 (registry_object)
          Object Comment:Check for HKLM\System\CurrentControlSet\Control\Lsa\RestrictAnonymousSAM
          Object Requirements:
          • check = 'hive' must be equal to 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE'
          • check = 'key' must be equal to 'System\CurrentControlSet\Control\Lsa'
          • check = 'name' must be equal (case insensitive) to 'RestrictAnonymousSAM'
          State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:20000001 (registry_state)
          State Comment: 
          State Requirements:
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'type' must be equal to 'reg_dword'
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'value' must be equal to '1'
          Collected Item/State Result:
          [ true ]
          • hive equals 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE'
          • key equals 'System\CurrentControlSet\Control\Lsa'
          • name equals 'RestrictAnonymousSAM'
          • last_write_time equals '134212907550000000'
          • type equals 'reg_dword'
          • value equals '1'
          • windows_view equals '64_bit'

          V-205919 - Windows Server 2019 LAN Manager authentication level must be configured to send NTLMv2 response only and to refuse LM and NTLM.

          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-205919r991589_rule
          Test Type:Automated
          Rule Result:Pass
          Version:WN19-SO-000310
          Identities:SV-103389
          V-93301
          CCI-000366 (NIST SP 800-53: CM-6 b; NIST SP 800-53A: CM-6.1 (iv); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: CM-6 b; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: CM-6 b)
          Description: The Kerberos v5 authentication protocol is the default for authentication of users who are logging on to domain accounts. NTLM, which is less secure, is retained in later Windows versions for compatibility with clients and servers that are running earlier versions of Windows or applications that still use it. It is also used to authenticate logons to standalone or nondomain-joined computers that are running later versions.
          Fix Text:Configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Windows Settings >> Security Settings >> Local Policies >> Security Options >> "Network security: LAN Manager authentication level" to "Send NTLMv2 response only. Refuse LM & NTLM".
          Severity:high
          Weight:10.0
          Reference:
          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows Server 2019
          Publisher:DISA
          Type:DPMS Target
          Subject:Microsoft Windows Server 2019
          Identifier:2907
          Definitions:
          Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.windows2019:def:205919
          Definition Result:true
          Title:WN19-SO-000310 - Windows Server 2019 LAN Manager authentication level must be configured to send NTLMv2 response only and to refuse LM and NTLM.
          Description:The Kerberos v5 authentication protocol is the default for authentication of users who are logging on to domain accounts. NTLM, which is less secure, is retained in later Windows versions for compatibility with clients and servers that are running earlier versions of Windows or applications that still use it. It is also used to authenticate logons to standalone or nondomain-joined computers that are running later versions.
          Class:compliance
          Tests:
          • true (All child tests must be true. (AND))
            • true (All child tests must be true. (AND))
              • true ('Network security: LAN Manager authentication level' is set to 'Send NTLMv2 response only. Refuse LM & NTLM' (Level 5))
          Tests:
          Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:25346200 (registry_test)
          Result:true
          Title:'Network security: LAN Manager authentication level' is set to 'Send NTLMv2 response only. Refuse LM & NTLM' (Level 5)
          Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
          Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).
          Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:obj:25346200 (registry_object)
          Object Comment: 
          Object Requirements:
          • check = 'hive' must be equal to 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE'
          • check = 'key' must be equal to 'SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Control\Lsa'
          • check = 'name' must be equal to 'LmCompatibilityLevel'
          State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:25346200 (registry_state)
          State Comment:Reg_Dword type and value equals 5
          State Requirements:
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'type' must be equal to 'reg_dword'
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'value' must be equal to '5'
          Collected Item/State Result:
          [ true ]
          • hive equals 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE'
          • key equals 'SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Control\Lsa'
          • name equals 'LmCompatibilityLevel'
          • last_write_time equals '134212907550000000'
          • type equals 'reg_dword'
          • value equals '5'
          • windows_view equals '64_bit'

        Detailed Results: Medium Severity (CAT II)

          V-205625 - Windows Server 2019 must be configured to audit Account Management - Security Group Management successes.

          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-205625r958368_rule
          Test Type:Automated
          Rule Result:Pass
          Version:WN19-AU-000100
          Identities:SV-103067
          V-92979
          CCI-000018 (NIST SP 800-53: AC-2 (4); NIST SP 800-53A: AC-2 (4).1 (i and ii); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: AC-2 (4); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: AC-2 (4))
          CCI-000172 (NIST SP 800-53: AU-12 c; NIST SP 800-53A: AU-12.1 (iv); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: AU-12 c; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: AU-12 c)
          CCI-001403 (NIST SP 800-53: AC-2 (4); NIST SP 800-53A: AC-2 (4).1 (i and ii); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: AC-2 (4); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: AC-2 (4))
          CCI-001404 (NIST SP 800-53: AC-2 (4); NIST SP 800-53A: AC-2 (4).1 (i and ii); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: AC-2 (4); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: AC-2 (4))
          CCI-001405 (NIST SP 800-53: AC-2 (4); NIST SP 800-53A: AC-2 (4).1 (i and ii); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: AC-2 (4); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: AC-2 (4))
          CCI-002130 (NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: AC-2 (4); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: AC-2 (4))
          Description: Maintaining an audit trail of system activity logs can help identify configuration errors, troubleshoot service disruptions, and analyze compromises that have occurred, as well as detect attacks. Audit logs are necessary to provide a trail of evidence in case the system or network is compromised. Collecting this data is essential for analyzing the security of information assets and detecting signs of suspicious and unexpected behavior.

          Security Group Management records events such as creating, deleting, or changing security groups, including changes in group members.

          Satisfies: SRG-OS-000004-GPOS-00004, SRG-OS-000239-GPOS-00089, SRG-OS-000240-GPOS-00090, SRG-OS-000241-GPOS-00091, SRG-OS-000303-GPOS-00120, SRG-OS-000476-GPOS-00221
          Fix Text:Configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Windows Settings >> Security Settings >> Advanced Audit Policy Configuration >> System Audit Policies >> Account Management >> "Audit Security Group Management" with "Success" selected.
          Severity:medium
          Weight:10.0
          Reference:
          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows Server 2019
          Publisher:DISA
          Type:DPMS Target
          Subject:Microsoft Windows Server 2019
          Identifier:2907
          Definitions:
          Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.windows2019:def:205625
          Definition Result:true
          Title:WN19-AU-000100 - Windows Server 2019 must be configured to audit Account Management - Security Group Management successes.
          Description:Maintaining an audit trail of system activity logs can help identify configuration errors, troubleshoot service disruptions, and analyze compromises that have occurred, as well as detect attacks. Audit logs are necessary to provide a trail of evidence in case the system or network is compromised. Collecting this data is essential for analyzing the security of information assets and detecting signs of suspicious and unexpected behavior.

          Security Group Management records events such as creating, deleting, or changing security groups, including changes in group members.

          Satisfies: SRG-OS-000004-GPOS-00004, SRG-OS-000239-GPOS-00089, SRG-OS-000240-GPOS-00090, SRG-OS-000241-GPOS-00091, SRG-OS-000303-GPOS-00120, SRG-OS-000476-GPOS-00221
          Class:compliance
          Tests:
          • true (All child tests must be true. (AND))
            • true (All child tests must be true. (AND))
              • true ('Audit Security Group Management' is set to 'Success' or 'Success and Failure')
          Tests:
          Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:25330800 (auditeventpolicysubcategories_test)
          Result:true
          Title:'Audit Security Group Management' is set to 'Success' or 'Success and Failure'
          Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
          Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).
          State Operator:One or more item-state comparisons may be true.
          Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:obj:20000000 (auditeventpolicysubcategories_object)

          • Collect any available items.

          State Comment: 
          State Requirements:
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'security_group_management' must be equal to 'AUDIT_SUCCESS'
          State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:25330801 (auditeventpolicysubcategories_state)

          Description: Maintaining an audit trail of system activity logs can help identify configuration errors, troubleshoot service disruptions, and analyze compromises that have occurred, as well as detect attacks. Audit logs are necessary to provide a trail of evidence in case the system or network is compromised. Collecting this data is essential for analyzing the security of information assets and detecting signs of suspicious and unexpected behavior.

          User Account Management records events such as creating, changing, deleting, renaming, disabling, or enabling user accounts.

          Satisfies: SRG-OS-000004-GPOS-00004, SRG-OS-000239-GPOS-00089, SRG-OS-000240-GPOS-00090, SRG-OS-000241-GPOS-00091, SRG-OS-000303-GPOS-00120, SRG-OS-000476-GPOS-00221

          Definition Result:true
          Title:WN19-AU-000120 - Windows Server 2019 must be configured to audit Account Management - User Account Management failures.
          Description:Maintaining an audit trail of system activity logs can help identify configuration errors, troubleshoot service disruptions, and analyze compromises that have occurred, as well as detect attacks. Audit logs are necessary to provide a trail of evidence in case the system or network is compromised. Collecting this data is essential for analyzing the security of information assets and detecting signs of suspicious and unexpected behavior.

          User Account Management records events such as creating, changing, deleting, renaming, disabling, or enabling user accounts.

          Satisfies: SRG-OS-000004-GPOS-00004, SRG-OS-000239-GPOS-00089, SRG-OS-000240-GPOS-00090, SRG-OS-000241-GPOS-00091, SRG-OS-000303-GPOS-00120, SRG-OS-000476-GPOS-00221
          Class:compliance
          Tests:
          • true (All child tests must be true. (AND))
            • true (One or more child tests must be true. (OR))
              • false ('Audit User Account Management' is set to 'Failure')
              • true ('Audit User Account Management' is set to 'Success' and 'Failure')
          Tests:
          Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:25330900 (auditeventpolicysubcategories_test)
          Result:false
          Title:'Audit User Account Management' is set to 'Failure'
          Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
          Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).
          Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:obj:20000000 (auditeventpolicysubcategories_object)
          Object Comment: 
          Object Requirements:
          • Collect any available items.
          State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:25330900 (auditeventpolicysubcategories_state)
          State Comment:'User Account Management' is set to 'Failure'
          State Requirements:
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'user_account_management' must be equal to 'AUDIT_FAILURE'
          Collected Item/State Result:
          [ false ]
          • credential_validation equals 'AUDIT_SUCCESS_FAILURE'
          • kerberos_authentication_service equals 'AUDIT_NONE'
          • kerberos_service_ticket_operations equals 'AUDIT_NONE'
          • kerberos_ticket_events equals 'AUDIT_NONE'
          • other_account_logon_events equals 'AUDIT_NONE'
