          V-205628 - Windows Server 2019 must be configured to audit Account Management - Computer Account Management successes.

          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-205628r958368_rule
          Test Type:Automated
          Rule Applicability:Windows_DomainController
          Rule Result:Not Applicable
          Version:WN19-DC-000230
          Identities:SV-103073
          V-92985
          CCI-000018 (NIST SP 800-53: AC-2 (4); NIST SP 800-53A: AC-2 (4).1 (i and ii); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: AC-2 (4); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: AC-2 (4))
          CCI-000172 (NIST SP 800-53: AU-12 c; NIST SP 800-53A: AU-12.1 (iv); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: AU-12 c; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: AU-12 c)
          CCI-001403 (NIST SP 800-53: AC-2 (4); NIST SP 800-53A: AC-2 (4).1 (i and ii); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: AC-2 (4); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: AC-2 (4))
          CCI-001404 (NIST SP 800-53: AC-2 (4); NIST SP 800-53A: AC-2 (4).1 (i and ii); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: AC-2 (4); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: AC-2 (4))
          CCI-001405 (NIST SP 800-53: AC-2 (4); NIST SP 800-53A: AC-2 (4).1 (i and ii); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: AC-2 (4); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: AC-2 (4))
          CCI-002130 (NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: AC-2 (4); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: AC-2 (4))
          Description: Maintaining an audit trail of system activity logs can help identify configuration errors, troubleshoot service disruptions, and analyze compromises that have occurred, as well as detect attacks. Audit logs are necessary to provide a trail of evidence in case the system or network is compromised. Collecting this data is essential for analyzing the security of information assets and detecting signs of suspicious and unexpected behavior.

          Computer Account Management records events such as creating, changing, deleting, renaming, disabling, or enabling computer accounts.

          Satisfies: SRG-OS-000004-GPOS-00004, SRG-OS-000239-GPOS-00089, SRG-OS-000240-GPOS-00090, SRG-OS-000241-GPOS-00091, SRG-OS-000303-GPOS-00120, SRG-OS-000476-GPOS-00221
          Fix Text:Configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Windows Settings >> Security Settings >> Advanced Audit Policy Configuration >> System Audit Policies >> Account Management >> "Audit Computer Account Management" with "Success" selected.
          Severity:medium
          Weight:10.0
          Reference:
          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows Server 2019
          Publisher:DISA
          Type:DPMS Target
          Subject:Microsoft Windows Server 2019
          Identifier:2907

          V-205629 - Windows Server 2019 must have the number of allowed bad logon attempts configured to three or less.

          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-205629r958388_rule
          Test Type:Automated
          Rule Result:Pass
          Version:WN19-AC-000020
          Identities:V-93141
          SV-103229
          CCI-000044 (NIST SP 800-53: AC-7 a; NIST SP 800-53A: AC-7.1 (ii); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: AC-7 a; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: AC-7 a)
          Description: The account lockout feature, when enabled, prevents brute-force password attacks on the system. The higher this value is, the less effective the account lockout feature will be in protecting the local system. The number of bad logon attempts must be reasonably small to minimize the possibility of a successful password attack while allowing for honest errors made during normal user logon.
          Fix Text:Configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Windows Settings >> Security Settings >> Account Policies >> Account Lockout Policy >> "Account lockout threshold" to "3" or fewer invalid logon attempts (excluding "0", which is unacceptable).
          Severity:medium
          Weight:10.0
          Reference:
          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows Server 2019
          Publisher:DISA
          Type:DPMS Target
          Subject:Microsoft Windows Server 2019
          Identifier:2907
          Definitions:
          Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.windows2019:def:205629
          Definition Result:true
          Title:WN19-AC-000020 - Windows Server 2019 must have the number of allowed bad logon attempts configured to three or less.
          Description:The account lockout feature, when enabled, prevents brute-force password attacks on the system. The higher this value is, the less effective the account lockout feature will be in protecting the local system. The number of bad logon attempts must be reasonably small to minimize the possibility of a successful password attack while allowing for honest errors made during normal user logon.
          Class:compliance
          Tests:
          • true (All child tests must be true. (AND))
            • true (All child tests must be true. (AND))
              • true ('Account lockout threshold' is set to '3' or less)
              • true ('Account lockout threshold' is not set to '0')
          Tests:
          Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:25329800 (lockoutpolicy_test)
          Result:true
          Title:'Account lockout threshold' is set to '3' or less
          Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
          Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).
          Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:obj:20000001 (lockoutpolicy_object)
          Object Comment: 
          Object Requirements:
          • Collect any available items.
          State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:25329800 (lockoutpolicy_state)
          State Comment:Lockout Threshold is less than or equal to 3
          State Requirements:
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'lockout_threshold' must be less than or equal to '3'
          Collected Item/State Result:
          [ true ]
          • force_logoff equals '1'
          • lockout_duration equals '900'
          • lockout_observation_window equals '900'
          • lockout_threshold equals '3'
          Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:25329801 (lockoutpolicy_test)
          Result:true
          Title:'Account lockout threshold' is not set to '0'
          Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
          Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).
          Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:obj:20000001 (lockoutpolicy_object)
          Object Comment: 
          Object Requirements:
          • Collect any available items.
          State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:25329801 (lockoutpolicy_state)
          State Comment:Lockout Threshold is greater than 0
          State Requirements:
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'lockout_threshold' must be greater than '0'
          Collected Item/State Result:
          [ true ]
          • force_logoff equals '1'
          • lockout_duration equals '900'
          • lockout_observation_window equals '900'
          • lockout_threshold equals '3'

          V-205630 - Windows Server 2019 must have the period of time before the bad logon counter is reset configured to 15 minutes or greater.

          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-205630r958388_rule
          Test Type:Automated
          Rule Result:Pass
          Version:WN19-AC-000030
          Identities:SV-103231
          V-93143
          CCI-000044 (NIST SP 800-53: AC-7 a; NIST SP 800-53A: AC-7.1 (ii); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: AC-7 a; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: AC-7 a)
          CCI-002238 (NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: AC-7 b; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: AC-7 b)
          Description: The account lockout feature, when enabled, prevents brute-force password attacks on the system. This parameter specifies the period of time that must pass after failed logon attempts before the counter is reset to "0". The smaller this value is, the less effective the account lockout feature will be in protecting the local system.

          Satisfies: SRG-OS-000021-GPOS-00005, SRG-OS-000329-GPOS-00128
          Fix Text:Configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Windows Settings >> Security Settings >> Account Policies >> Account Lockout Policy >> "Reset account lockout counter after" to at least "15" minutes.
          Severity:medium
          Weight:10.0
          Reference:
          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows Server 2019
          Publisher:DISA
          Type:DPMS Target
          Subject:Microsoft Windows Server 2019
          Identifier:2907
          Definitions:
          Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.windows2019:def:205630
          Definition Result:true
          Title:WN19-AC-000030 - Windows Server 2019 must have the period of time before the bad logon counter is reset configured to 15 minutes or greater.
          Description:The account lockout feature, when enabled, prevents brute-force password attacks on the system. This parameter specifies the period of time that must pass after failed logon attempts before the counter is reset to "0". The smaller this value is, the less effective the account lockout feature will be in protecting the local system.

          Satisfies: SRG-OS-000021-GPOS-00005, SRG-OS-000329-GPOS-00128
          Class:compliance
          Tests:
          • true (All child tests must be true. (AND))
            • true (All child tests must be true. (AND))
              • true ('Reset account lockout counter after' is set to at least the required value)
              • true ('Account lockout threshold' is not set to '0')
          Tests:
          Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:25329900 (lockoutpolicy_test)
          Result:true
          Title:'Reset account lockout counter after' is set to at least the required value
          Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
          Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).
          Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:obj:20000001 (lockoutpolicy_object)
          Object Comment: 
          Object Requirements:
          • Collect any available items.
          State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:25329900 (lockoutpolicy_state)
          State Comment:Lockout Observation Window is greater than or equal to variable
          State Requirements:
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'lockout_observation_window' must be greater than or equal to '900'
          Collected Item/State Result:
          [ true ]
          • force_logoff equals '1'
          • lockout_duration equals '900'
          • lockout_observation_window equals '900'
          • lockout_threshold equals '3'
          Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:25329801 (lockoutpolicy_test)
          Result:true
          Title:'Account lockout threshold' is not set to '0'
          Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
          Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).
          Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:obj:20000001 (lockoutpolicy_object)
          Object Comment: 
          Object Requirements:
          • Collect any available items.
          State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:25329801 (lockoutpolicy_state)
          State Comment:Lockout Threshold is greater than 0
          State Requirements:
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'lockout_threshold' must be greater than '0'
          Collected Item/State Result:
          [ true ]
          • force_logoff equals '1'
          • lockout_duration equals '900'
          • lockout_observation_window equals '900'
          • lockout_threshold equals '3'

          V-205633 - Windows Server 2019 machine inactivity limit must be set to 15 minutes or less, locking the system with the screen saver.

          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-205633r958400_rule
          Test Type:Automated
          Rule Result:Pass
          Version:WN19-SO-000120
          Identities:V-92961
          SV-103049
          CCI-000056 (NIST SP 800-53: AC-11 b; NIST SP 800-53A: AC-11.1 (iii); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: AC-11 b; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: AC-11 b)
          CCI-000057 (NIST SP 800-53: AC-11 a; NIST SP 800-53A: AC-11.1 (ii); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: AC-11 a; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: AC-11 a)
          CCI-000060 (NIST SP 800-53: AC-11 (1); NIST SP 800-53A: AC-11 (1).1; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: AC-11 (1); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: AC-11 (1))
          Description: Unattended systems are susceptible to unauthorized use and should be locked when unattended. The screen saver should be set at a maximum of 15 minutes and be password protected. This protects critical and sensitive data from exposure to unauthorized personnel with physical access to the computer.

          Satisfies: SRG-OS-000028-GPOS-00009, SRG-OS-000029-GPOS-00010, SRG-OS-000031-GPOS-00012
          Fix Text:Configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Windows Settings >> Security Settings >> Local Policies >> Security Options >> "Interactive logon: Machine inactivity limit" to "900" seconds or less, excluding "0" which is effectively disabled.
          Severity:medium
          Weight:10.0
          Reference:
          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows Server 2019
          Publisher:DISA
          Type:DPMS Target
          Subject:Microsoft Windows Server 2019
          Identifier:2907
          Definitions:
          Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.windows2019:def:205633
          Definition Result:true
          Title:WN19-SO-000120 - Windows Server 2019 machine inactivity limit must be set to 15 minutes or less, locking the system with the screen saver.
          Description:Unattended systems are susceptible to unauthorized use and should be locked when unattended. The screen saver should be set at a maximum of 15 minutes and be password protected. This protects critical and sensitive data from exposure to unauthorized personnel with physical access to the computer.

          Satisfies: SRG-OS-000028-GPOS-00009, SRG-OS-000029-GPOS-00010, SRG-OS-000031-GPOS-00012
          Class:compliance
          Tests:
          • true (All child tests must be true. (AND))
            • true (All child tests must be true. (AND))
              • true (The machine inactivity limit is set to 15 minutes or less)
          Tests:
          Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.windows:tst:25344400 (registry_test)
          Result:true
          Title:The machine inactivity limit is set to 15 minutes or less
          Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
          Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).
          State Operator:All item-state comparisons must be true.
          Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.windows:obj:25344400 (registry_object)
          Object Comment: 
          Object Requirements:
          • check = 'hive' must be equal (case insensitive) to 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE'
          • check = 'key' must be equal (case insensitive) to 'Software\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Policies\System'
          • check = 'name' must be equal (case insensitive) to 'InactivityTimeoutSecs'
          State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.windows:ste:25344400 (registry_state)
          State Comment: 
          State Requirements:
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'type' must be equal to 'reg_dword'
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'value' must be less than or equal to '900'
          State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.windows:ste:25344401 (registry_state)
          State Comment: 
          State Requirements:
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'type' must be equal to 'reg_dword'
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'value' must be greater than '0'
          Collected Item/State Result:
          [ true ]
          • hive equals 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE'
          • key equals 'Software\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Policies\System'
          • name equals 'InactivityTimeoutSecs'
          • last_write_time equals '134213413420000000'
          • type equals 'reg_dword'
          • value equals '900'
          • windows_view equals '64_bit'

          V-205634 - Windows Server 2019 must be configured to audit logon successes.

          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-205634r958406_rule
          Test Type:Automated
          Rule Result:Pass
          Version:WN19-AU-000190
          Identities:V-92967
          SV-103055
          CCI-000067 (NIST SP 800-53: AC-17 (1); NIST SP 800-53A: AC-17 (1).1; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: AC-17 (1); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: AC-17 (1))
          CCI-000172 (NIST SP 800-53: AU-12 c; NIST SP 800-53A: AU-12.1 (iv); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: AU-12 c; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: AU-12 c)
          Description: Maintaining an audit trail of system activity logs can help identify configuration errors, troubleshoot service disruptions, and analyze compromises that have occurred, as well as detect attacks. Audit logs are necessary to provide a trail of evidence in case the system or network is compromised. Collecting this data is essential for analyzing the security of information assets and detecting signs of suspicious and unexpected behavior.

          Logon records user logons. If this is an interactive logon, it is recorded on the local system. If it is to a network share, it is recorded on the system accessed.

          Satisfies: SRG-OS-000032-GPOS-00013, SRG-OS-000470-GPOS-00214, SRG-OS-000472-GPOS-00217, SRG-OS-000473-GPOS-00218, SRG-OS-000475-GPOS-00220
          Fix Text:Configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Windows Settings >> Security Settings >> Advanced Audit Policy Configuration >> System Audit Policies >> Logon/Logoff >> "Audit Logon" with "Success" selected.
          Severity:medium
          Weight:10.0
          Reference:
          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows Server 2019
          Publisher:DISA
          Type:DPMS Target
          Subject:Microsoft Windows Server 2019
          Identifier:2907
          Definitions:
          Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.windows2019:def:205634
          Definition Result:true
          Title:WN19-AU-000190 - Windows Server 2019 must be configured to audit logon successes.
          Description:Maintaining an audit trail of system activity logs can help identify configuration errors, troubleshoot service disruptions, and analyze compromises that have occurred, as well as detect attacks. Audit logs are necessary to provide a trail of evidence in case the system or network is compromised. Collecting this data is essential for analyzing the security of information assets and detecting signs of suspicious and unexpected behavior.

          Logon records user logons. If this is an interactive logon, it is recorded on the local system. If it is to a network share, it is recorded on the system accessed.

          Satisfies: SRG-OS-000032-GPOS-00013, SRG-OS-000470-GPOS-00214, SRG-OS-000472-GPOS-00217, SRG-OS-000473-GPOS-00218, SRG-OS-000475-GPOS-00220
          Class:compliance
          Tests:
          • true (All child tests must be true. (AND))
            • true (One or more child tests must be true. (OR))
              • false ('Audit Logon' is set to 'Success')
              • true ('Audit Logon' is set to 'Success' and 'Failure')
          Tests:
          Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:25331700 (auditeventpolicysubcategories_test)
          Result:false
          Title:'Audit Logon' is set to 'Success'
          Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
          Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).
          Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:obj:20000000 (auditeventpolicysubcategories_object)
          Object Comment: 
          Object Requirements:
          • Collect any available items.
          State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:25331700 (auditeventpolicysubcategories_state)
          State Comment:'Log On' is set to 'Success'
          State Requirements:
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'logon' must be equal to 'AUDIT_SUCCESS'
          Collected Item/State Result:
          [ false ]
          • credential_validation equals 'AUDIT_SUCCESS_FAILURE'
          • kerberos_authentication_service equals 'AUDIT_NONE'
          • kerberos_service_ticket_operations equals 'AUDIT_NONE'
          • kerberos_ticket_events equals 'AUDIT_NONE'

          V-205635 - Windows Server 2019 must be configured to audit logon failures.

          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-205635r958406_rule
          Test Type:Automated
          Rule Result:Pass
          Version:WN19-AU-000200
          Identities:V-92969
          SV-103057
          CCI-000067 (NIST SP 800-53: AC-17 (1); NIST SP 800-53A: AC-17 (1).1; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: AC-17 (1); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: AC-17 (1))
          CCI-000172 (NIST SP 800-53: AU-12 c; NIST SP 800-53A: AU-12.1 (iv); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: AU-12 c; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: AU-12 c)
          Description: Maintaining an audit trail of system activity logs can help identify configuration errors, troubleshoot service disruptions, and analyze compromises that have occurred, as well as detect attacks. Audit logs are necessary to provide a trail of evidence in case the system or network is compromised. Collecting this data is essential for analyzing the security of information assets and detecting signs of suspicious and unexpected behavior.

          Logon records user logons. If this is an interactive logon, it is recorded on the local system. If it is to a network share, it is recorded on the system accessed.

          Satisfies: SRG-OS-000032-GPOS-00013, SRG-OS-000470-GPOS-00214, SRG-OS-000472-GPOS-00217, SRG-OS-000473-GPOS-00218, SRG-OS-000475-GPOS-00220
          Fix Text:Configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Windows Settings >> Security Settings >> Advanced Audit Policy Configuration >> System Audit Policies >> Logon/Logoff >> "Audit Logon" with "Failure" selected.
          Severity:medium
          Weight:10.0
          Reference:
          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows Server 2019
          Publisher:DISA
          Type:DPMS Target
          Subject:Microsoft Windows Server 2019
          Identifier:2907
          Definitions:
          Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.windows2019:def:205635
          Definition Result:true
          Title:WN19-AU-000200 - Windows Server 2019 must be configured to audit logon failures.
          Description:Maintaining an audit trail of system activity logs can help identify configuration errors, troubleshoot service disruptions, and analyze compromises that have occurred, as well as detect attacks. Audit logs are necessary to provide a trail of evidence in case the system or network is compromised. Collecting this data is essential for analyzing the security of information assets and detecting signs of suspicious and unexpected behavior.

          Logon records user logons. If this is an interactive logon, it is recorded on the local system. If it is to a network share, it is recorded on the system accessed.

          Satisfies: SRG-OS-000032-GPOS-00013, SRG-OS-000470-GPOS-00214, SRG-OS-000472-GPOS-00217, SRG-OS-000473-GPOS-00218, SRG-OS-000475-GPOS-00220
          Class:compliance
          Tests:
          • true (All child tests must be true. (AND))
            • true (One or more child tests must be true. (OR))
              • false ('Audit Logon' is set to 'Failure')
              • true ('Audit Logon' is set to 'Success' and 'Failure')
          Tests:
          Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:25331600 (auditeventpolicysubcategories_test)

          V-205636 - Windows Server 2019 Remote Desktop Services must require secure Remote Procedure Call (RPC) communications.

          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-205636r958408_rule
          Test Type:Automated
          Rule Result:Pass
          Version:WN19-CC-000370
          Identities:V-92971
          SV-103059
          CCI-000068 (NIST SP 800-53: AC-17 (2); NIST SP 800-53A: AC-17 (2).1; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: AC-17 (2); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: AC-17 (2))
          CCI-001453 (NIST SP 800-53: AC-17 (2); NIST SP 800-53A: AC-17 (2).1; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: AC-17 (2); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: AC-17 (2))
          Description: Allowing unsecure RPC communication exposes the system to man-in-the-middle attacks and data disclosure attacks. A man-in-the-middle attack occurs when an intruder captures packets between a client and server and modifies them before allowing the packets to be exchanged. Usually the attacker will modify the information in the packets in an attempt to cause either the client or server to reveal sensitive information.

          Satisfies: SRG-OS-000033-GPOS-00014, SRG-OS-000250-GPOS-00093
          Fix Text:Configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Administrative Templates >> Windows Components >> Remote Desktop Services >> Remote Desktop Session Host >> Security >> "Require secure RPC communication" to "Enabled".
          Severity:medium
          Weight:10.0
          Reference:
          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows Server 2019
          Publisher:DISA
          Type:DPMS Target
          Subject:Microsoft Windows Server 2019
          Identifier:2907
          Definitions:
          Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.windows2019:def:205636
          Definition Result:true
          Title:WN19-CC-000370 - Windows Server 2019 Remote Desktop Services must require secure Remote Procedure Call (RPC) communications.
          Description:Allowing unsecure RPC communication exposes the system to man-in-the-middle attacks and data disclosure attacks. A man-in-the-middle attack occurs when an intruder captures packets between a client and server and modifies them before allowing the packets to be exchanged. Usually the attacker will modify the information in the packets in an attempt to cause either the client or server to reveal sensitive information.

          Satisfies: SRG-OS-000033-GPOS-00014, SRG-OS-000250-GPOS-00093
          Class:compliance
          Tests:
          • true (All child tests must be true. (AND))
            • true (All child tests must be true. (AND))
              • true ('Require secure RPC communication' is set to 'Enabled')
          Tests:
          Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:25340500 (registry_test)
          Result:true
          Title:'Require secure RPC communication' is set to 'Enabled'
          Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
          Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).
          Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:obj:25340500 (registry_object)
          Object Comment: 
          Object Requirements:
          • check = 'hive' must be equal to 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE'
          • check = 'key' must be equal to 'Software\Policies\Microsoft\Windows NT\Terminal Services'
          • check = 'name' must be equal to 'fEncryptRPCTraffic'
          State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:20000001 (registry_state)
          State Comment: 
          State Requirements:
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'type' must be equal to 'reg_dword'
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'value' must be equal to '1'
          Collected Item/State Result:
          [ true ]
          • hive equals 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE'
          • key equals 'Software\Policies\Microsoft\Windows NT\Terminal Services'
          • name equals 'fEncryptRPCTraffic'
          • last_write_time equals '134213413420000000'
          • type equals 'reg_dword'
          • value equals '1'
          • windows_view equals '64_bit'

          V-205637 - Windows Server 2019 Remote Desktop Services must be configured with the client connection encryption set to High Level.

          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-205637r958408_rule
          Test Type:Automated
          Rule Result:Pass
          Version:WN19-CC-000380
          Identities:V-92973
          SV-103061
          CCI-000068 (NIST SP 800-53: AC-17 (2); NIST SP 800-53A: AC-17 (2).1; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: AC-17 (2); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: AC-17 (2))
          CCI-001453 (NIST SP 800-53: AC-17 (2); NIST SP 800-53A: AC-17 (2).1; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: AC-17 (2); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: AC-17 (2))
          Description: Remote connections must be encrypted to prevent interception of data or sensitive information. Selecting "High Level" will ensure encryption of Remote Desktop Services sessions in both directions.

          Satisfies: SRG-OS-000033-GPOS-00014, SRG-OS-000250-GPOS-00093
          Fix Text:Configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Administrative Templates >> Windows Components >> Remote Desktop Services >> Remote Desktop Session Host >> Security >> "Set client connection encryption level" to "Enabled" with "High Level" selected.
          Severity:medium
          Weight:10.0
          Reference:
          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows Server 2019
          Publisher:DISA
          Type:DPMS Target
          Subject:Microsoft Windows Server 2019
          Identifier:2907
          Definitions:
          Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.windows2019:def:205637
          Definition Result:true
          Title:WN19-CC-000380 - Windows Server 2019 Remote Desktop Services must be configured with the client connection encryption set to High Level.
          Description:Remote connections must be encrypted to prevent interception of data or sensitive information. Selecting "High Level" will ensure encryption of Remote Desktop Services sessions in both directions.

          Satisfies: SRG-OS-000033-GPOS-00014, SRG-OS-000250-GPOS-00093
          Class:compliance
          Tests:
          • true (All child tests must be true. (AND))
            • true (All child tests must be true. (AND))
              • true ('Set client connection encryption level' is set to 'Enabled' and 'High Level')
          Tests:
          Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:25340600 (registry_test)
          Result:true
          Title:'Set client connection encryption level' is set to 'Enabled' and 'High Level'
          Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
          Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).
          Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:obj:25340600 (registry_object)
          Object Comment: 
          Object Requirements:
          • check = 'hive' must be equal to 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE'
          • check = 'key' must be equal to 'Software\Policies\Microsoft\Windows NT\Terminal Services'
          • check = 'name' must be equal to 'MinEncryptionLevel'
          State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:20000003 (registry_state)
          State Comment: 
          State Requirements:
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'type' must be equal to 'reg_dword'
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'value' must be equal to '3'
          Collected Item/State Result:
          [ true ]
          • hive equals 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE'
          • key equals 'Software\Policies\Microsoft\Windows NT\Terminal Services'
          • name equals 'MinEncryptionLevel'
          • last_write_time equals '134213413420000000'
          • type equals 'reg_dword'
          • value equals '3'
          • windows_view equals '64_bit'

          V-205638 - Windows Server 2019 command line data must be included in process creation events.

          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-205638r958422_rule
          Test Type:Automated
          Rule Result:Pass
          Version:WN19-CC-000090
          Identities:V-93173
          SV-103261
          CCI-000135 (NIST SP 800-53: AU-3 (1); NIST SP 800-53A: AU-3 (1).1 (ii); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: AU-3 (1); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: AU-3 (1))
          Description: Maintaining an audit trail of system activity logs can help identify configuration errors, troubleshoot service disruptions, and analyze compromises that have occurred, as well as detect attacks. Audit logs are necessary to provide a trail of evidence in case the system or network is compromised. Collecting this data is essential for analyzing the security of information assets and detecting signs of suspicious and unexpected behavior.

          Enabling "Include command line data for process creation events" will record the command line information with the process creation events in the log. This can provide additional detail when malware has run on a system.
          Fix Text:Configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Administrative Templates >> System >> Audit Process Creation >> "Include command line in process creation events" to "Enabled".
          Severity:medium
          Weight:10.0
          Reference:
          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows Server 2019
          Publisher:DISA
          Type:DPMS Target
          Subject:Microsoft Windows Server 2019
          Identifier:2907
          Definitions:
          Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.windows2019:def:205638
          Definition Result:true
          Title:WN19-CC-000090 - Windows Server 2019 command line data must be included in process creation events.
          Description:Maintaining an audit trail of system activity logs can help identify configuration errors, troubleshoot service disruptions, and analyze compromises that have occurred, as well as detect attacks. Audit logs are necessary to provide a trail of evidence in case the system or network is compromised. Collecting this data is essential for analyzing the security of information assets and detecting signs of suspicious and unexpected behavior.

          Enabling "Include command line data for process creation events" will record the command line information with the process creation events in the log. This can provide additional detail when malware has run on a system.
          Class:compliance
          Tests:
          • true (All child tests must be true. (AND))
            • true (All child tests must be true. (AND))
              • true ('Include command line in process creation events' is set to 'Enabled')
          Tests:
          Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:25336700 (registry_test)
          Result:true
          Title:'Include command line in process creation events' is set to 'Enabled'
          Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
          Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).
          Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:obj:25336700 (registry_object)
          Object Comment: 
          Object Requirements:
          • check = 'hive' must be equal to 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE'
          • check = 'key' must be equal (case insensitive) to 'SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Policies\System\Audit'
          • check = 'name' must be equal (case insensitive) to 'ProcessCreationIncludeCmdLine_Enabled'
          State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:20000001 (registry_state)
          State Comment: 
          State Requirements:
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'type' must be equal to 'reg_dword'
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'value' must be equal to '1'
          Collected Item/State Result:
          [ true ]
          • hive equals 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE'
          • key equals 'SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Policies\System\Audit'
          • name equals 'ProcessCreationIncludeCmdLine_Enabled'
          • last_write_time equals '134213413420000000'
          • type equals 'reg_dword'
          • value equals '1'
          • windows_view equals '64_bit'

          V-205639 - Windows Server 2019 PowerShell script block logging must be enabled.

          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-205639r958422_rule
          Test Type:Automated
          Rule Result:Pass
          Version:WN19-CC-000460
          Identities:SV-103263
          V-93175
          CCI-000135 (NIST SP 800-53: AU-3 (1); NIST SP 800-53A: AU-3 (1).1 (ii); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: AU-3 (1); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: AU-3 (1))
          Description: Maintaining an audit trail of system activity logs can help identify configuration errors, troubleshoot service disruptions, and analyze compromises that have occurred, as well as detect attacks. Audit logs are necessary to provide a trail of evidence in case the system or network is compromised. Collecting this data is essential for analyzing the security of information assets and detecting signs of suspicious and unexpected behavior.

          Enabling PowerShell script block logging will record detailed information from the processing of PowerShell commands and scripts. This can provide additional detail when malware has run on a system.
          Fix Text:Configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Administrative Templates >> Windows Components >> Windows PowerShell >> "Turn on PowerShell Script Block Logging" to "Enabled".
          Severity:medium
          Weight:10.0
          Reference:
          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows Server 2019
          Publisher:DISA
          Type:DPMS Target
          Subject:Microsoft Windows Server 2019
          Identifier:2907
          Definitions:
          Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.windows2019:def:205639
          Definition Result:true
          Title:WN19-CC-000460 - Windows Server 2019 PowerShell script block logging must be enabled.
          Description:Maintaining an audit trail of system activity logs can help identify configuration errors, troubleshoot service disruptions, and analyze compromises that have occurred, as well as detect attacks. Audit logs are necessary to provide a trail of evidence in case the system or network is compromised. Collecting this data is essential for analyzing the security of information assets and detecting signs of suspicious and unexpected behavior.

          Enabling PowerShell script block logging will record detailed information from the processing of PowerShell commands and scripts. This can provide additional detail when malware has run on a system.
          Class:compliance
          Tests:
          • true (All child tests must be true. (AND))
            • true (All child tests must be true. (AND))
              • true ('Turn on PowerShell Script Block Logging' is set to 'Enabled')
          Tests:
          Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:25341400 (registry_test)
          Result:true
          Title:'Turn on PowerShell Script Block Logging' is set to 'Enabled'
          Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
          Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).
          Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:obj:25341400 (registry_object)
          Object Comment: 
          Object Requirements:
          • check = 'hive' must be equal to 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE'
          • check = 'key' must be equal (case insensitive) to 'SOFTWARE\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\PowerShell\ScriptBlockLogging'
          • check = 'name' must be equal (case insensitive) to 'EnableScriptBlockLogging'
          State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:20000001 (registry_state)
          State Comment: 
          State Requirements:
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'type' must be equal to 'reg_dword'
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'value' must be equal to '1'
          Collected Item/State Result:
          [ true ]
          • hive equals 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE'
          • key equals 'SOFTWARE\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\PowerShell\ScriptBlockLogging'
          • name equals 'EnableScriptBlockLogging'
          • last_write_time equals '134213413420000000'
          • type equals 'reg_dword'
          • value equals '1'
          • windows_view equals '64_bit'

          V-205640 - Windows Server 2019 permissions for the Application event log must prevent access by non-privileged accounts.

          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-205640r958434_rule
          Test Type:Automated
          Rule Result:Pass
          Version:WN19-AU-000030
          Identities:V-93189
          SV-103277
          CCI-000162 (NIST SP 800-53: AU-9; NIST SP 800-53A: AU-9.1; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: AU-9; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: AU-9 a)
          CCI-000163 (NIST SP 800-53: AU-9; NIST SP 800-53A: AU-9.1; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: AU-9; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: AU-9 a)
          CCI-000164 (NIST SP 800-53: AU-9; NIST SP 800-53A: AU-9.1; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: AU-9; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: AU-9 a)
          Description: Maintaining an audit trail of system activity logs can help identify configuration errors, troubleshoot service disruptions, and analyze compromises that have occurred, as well as detect attacks. Audit logs are necessary to provide a trail of evidence in case the system or network is compromised. The Application event log may be susceptible to tampering if proper permissions are not applied.

          Satisfies: SRG-OS-000057-GPOS-00027, SRG-OS-000058-GPOS-00028, SRG-OS-000059-GPOS-00029
          Fix Text:Configure the permissions on the Application event log file (Application.evtx) to prevent access by non-privileged accounts. The default permissions listed below satisfy this requirement:

          Eventlog - Full Control
          SYSTEM - Full Control
          Administrators - Full Control

          The default location is the "%SystemRoot%\System32\winevt\Logs" folder.

          If the location of the logs has been changed, when adding Eventlog to the permissions, it must be entered as "NT Service\Eventlog".
          Severity:medium
          Weight:10.0
          Reference:
          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows Server 2019
          Publisher:DISA
          Type:DPMS Target
          Subject:Microsoft Windows Server 2019
          Identifier:2907
          Definitions:
          Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.windows2019:def:205640
          Definition Result:true
          Title:WN19-AU-000030 - Windows Server 2019 permissions for the Application event log must prevent access by non-privileged accounts.
          Description:Maintaining an audit trail of system activity logs can help identify configuration errors, troubleshoot service disruptions, and analyze compromises that have occurred, as well as detect attacks. Audit logs are necessary to provide a trail of evidence in case the system or network is compromised. The Application event log may be susceptible to tampering if proper permissions are not applied.

          Satisfies: SRG-OS-000057-GPOS-00027, SRG-OS-000058-GPOS-00028, SRG-OS-000059-GPOS-00029
          Class:compliance
          Tests:
          • true (All child tests must be true. (AND))
            • true (One or more child tests must be true. (OR))
              • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
                • true (The Application event log must have at least default permissions. Strict Path)
                • false (The Application event log registry specified location must have the %SystemRoot% environment variable.) (negated)
              • true (All child tests must be true. (AND))
                • true (The Application event log must have at least default permissions. Built Path)
                • true (The Application event log registry specified location must have the %SystemRoot% environment variable.)
          Tests:
          Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:25429601 (fileeffectiverights53_test)
          Result:true
          Title:The Application event log must have at least default permissions. Strict Path
          Check Existence:No collected items may exist.
          Check:Result is based on check existence only.
          Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:obj:25429601 (fileeffectiverights53_object)
          Object Comment:Permissions of Application event log, minus those allowed. Strict filepath, as listed.
          Object Requirements:
          • Collect any available items.
          Exclude Items If:
          • trustee_sid equals 'S-1-5-80-880578595-1860270145-482643319-2788375705-1540778122'
          Exclude Items If:
          • trustee_sid equals 'S-1-5-18'
          Exclude Items If:
          • trustee_sid equals 'S-1-5-32-544'
          Exclude Items If:
          • trustee_sid equals 'S-1-5-19'
          Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:25334002 (registry_test)
          Result:true
          Title:The Application event log registry specified location must have the %SystemRoot% environment variable.
          Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
          Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).
          Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:obj:25334002 (registry_object)
          Object Comment:path Application.evtx
          Object Requirements:
          • check = 'hive' must be equal to 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE'
          • check = 'key' must be equal to 'SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Services\EventLog\Application'
          • check = 'name' must be equal to 'file'
          State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:20000016 (registry_state)
          State Comment:%SystemRoot% environment variable.
          State Requirements:
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'value' must match the pattern '^(?i)%SystemRoot%.*$'
          Collected Item/State Result:
          [ true ]
          • hive equals 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE'
          • key equals 'SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Services\EventLog\Application'
          • name equals 'file'
          • last_write_time equals '134209329790000000'
          • type equals 'reg_expand_sz'
          • value equals '%SystemRoot%\system32\winevt\Logs\Application.evtx'
          • expanded_value equals 'C:\Windows\system32\winevt\Logs\Application.evtx'
          • windows_view equals '64_bit'
          Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:25429600 (fileeffectiverights53_test)
          Result:true
          Title:The Application event log must have at least default permissions. Built Path
          Check Existence:No collected items may exist.
          Check:Result is based on check existence only.
          Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:obj:25429600 (fileeffectiverights53_object)
          Object Comment:Permissions of Application event log, minus those allowed. Built filepath.
          Object Requirements:
          • Collect any available items.
          Exclude Items If:
          • trustee_sid equals 'S-1-5-80-880578595-1860270145-482643319-2788375705-1540778122'
          Exclude Items If:
          • trustee_sid equals 'S-1-5-18'
          Exclude Items If:
          • trustee_sid equals 'S-1-5-32-544'
          Exclude Items If:
          • trustee_sid equals 'S-1-5-19'

          V-205641 - Windows Server 2019 permissions for the Security event log must prevent access by non-privileged accounts.

          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-205641r958434_rule
          Test Type:Automated
          Rule Result:Pass
          Version:WN19-AU-000040
          Identities:V-93191
          SV-103279
          CCI-000162 (NIST SP 800-53: AU-9; NIST SP 800-53A: AU-9.1; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: AU-9; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: AU-9 a)
          CCI-000163 (NIST SP 800-53: AU-9; NIST SP 800-53A: AU-9.1; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: AU-9; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: AU-9 a)
          CCI-000164 (NIST SP 800-53: AU-9; NIST SP 800-53A: AU-9.1; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: AU-9; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: AU-9 a)
          Description: Maintaining an audit trail of system activity logs can help identify configuration errors, troubleshoot service disruptions, and analyze compromises that have occurred, as well as detect attacks. Audit logs are necessary to provide a trail of evidence in case the system or network is compromised. The Security event log may disclose sensitive information or be susceptible to tampering if proper permissions are not applied.

          Satisfies: SRG-OS-000057-GPOS-00027, SRG-OS-000058-GPOS-00028, SRG-OS-000059-GPOS-00029
          Fix Text:Configure the permissions on the Security event log file (Security.evtx) to prevent access by non-privileged accounts. The default permissions listed below satisfy this requirement:

          Eventlog - Full Control
          SYSTEM - Full Control
          Administrators - Full Control

          The default location is the "%SystemRoot%\System32\winevt\Logs" folder.

          If the location of the logs has been changed, when adding Eventlog to the permissions, it must be entered as "NT Service\Eventlog".
          Severity:medium
          Weight:10.0
          Reference:
          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows Server 2019
          Publisher:DISA
          Type:DPMS Target
          Subject:Microsoft Windows Server 2019
          Identifier:2907
          Definitions:
          Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.windows2019:def:205641

Audit logs are necessary to provide a trail of evidence in case the system or network is compromised. The Security event log may disclose sensitive information or be susceptible to tampering if proper permissions are not applied.

          Satisfies: SRG-OS-000057-GPOS-00027, SRG-OS-000058-GPOS-00028, SRG-OS-000059-GPOS-00029
          Class:compliance
          Tests:
          • true (All child tests must be true. (AND))
            • true (One or more child tests must be true. (OR))
              • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
                • true (The Security event log must have at least default permissions. Strict Path)
                • false (The Security event log registry specified location must have the %SystemRoot% environment variable.) (negated)
              • true (All child tests must be true. (AND))
                • true (The Security event log must have at least default permissions. Built Path)
                • true (The Security event log registry specified location must have the %SystemRoot% environment variable.)
          Tests:
          Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:25429701 (fileeffectiverights53_test)
          Result:true
          Title:The Security event log must have at least default permissions. Strict Path
          Check Existence:No collected items may exist.
          Check:Result is based on check existence only.
          Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:obj:25429701 (fileeffectiverights53_object)
          Object Comment:Permissions of Security event log, minus those allowed. Strict filepath, as listed.

          • Collect any available items.

          • trustee_sid equals 'S-1-5-80-880578595-1860270145-482643319-2788375705-1540778122'
          Exclude Items If:
          • trustee_sid equals 'S-1-5-18'
          Exclude Items If:

          Exclude Items If:
          • trustee_sid equals 'S-1-5-19'

          Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.

          [ true ]
          • hive equals 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE'
          • key equals 'SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Services\EventLog\System'
          • name equals 'file'

          • value equals '%SystemRoot%\system32\winevt\Logs\System.evtx'
          • expanded_value equals 'C:\Windows\system32\winevt\Logs\System.evtx'
          • windows_view equals '64_bit'

          Result:true
          Title:The System event log must have at least default permissions. Built Path

          Check:Result is based on check existence only.

          V-205643 - Windows Server 2019 Manage auditing and security log user right must only be assigned to the Administrators group.

          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-205643r958434_rule
          Test Type:Automated
          Rule Result:Pass
          Version:WN19-UR-000170
          Identities:V-93197
          SV-103285
          CCI-000162 (NIST SP 800-53: AU-9; NIST SP 800-53A: AU-9.1; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: AU-9; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: AU-9 a)
          CCI-000163 (NIST SP 800-53: AU-9; NIST SP 800-53A: AU-9.1; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: AU-9; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: AU-9 a)
          CCI-000164 (NIST SP 800-53: AU-9; NIST SP 800-53A: AU-9.1; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: AU-9; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: AU-9 a)
          CCI-000171 (NIST SP 800-53: AU-12 b; NIST SP 800-53A: AU-12.1 (iii); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: AU-12 b; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: AU-12 b)
          CCI-001914 (NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: AU-12 (3); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: AU-12 (3))
          Description: Inappropriate granting of user rights can provide system, administrative, and other high-level capabilities.

          Accounts with the "Manage auditing and security log" user right can manage the security log and change auditing configurations. This could be used to clear evidence of tampering.

          Satisfies: SRG-OS-000057-GPOS-00027, SRG-OS-000058-GPOS-00028, SRG-OS-000059-GPOS-00029, SRG-OS-000063-GPOS-00032, SRG-OS-000337-GPOS-00129
          Fix Text:Configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Windows Settings >> Security Settings >> Local Policies >> User Rights Assignment >> "Manage auditing and security log" to include only the following accounts or groups:

          - Administrators
          Severity:medium
          Weight:10.0
          Reference:
          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows Server 2019
          Publisher:DISA
          Type:DPMS Target
          Subject:Microsoft Windows Server 2019
          Identifier:2907
          Definitions:
          Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.windows2019:def:205643
          Definition Result:true
          Title:WN19-UR-000170 - Windows Server 2019 Manage auditing and security log user right must only be assigned to the Administrators group.
          Description:Inappropriate granting of user rights can provide system, administrative, and other high-level capabilities.

          Accounts with the "Manage auditing and security log" user right can manage the security log and change auditing configurations. This could be used to clear evidence of tampering.

          Satisfies: SRG-OS-000057-GPOS-00027, SRG-OS-000058-GPOS-00028, SRG-OS-000059-GPOS-00029, SRG-OS-000063-GPOS-00032, SRG-OS-000337-GPOS-00129
          Class:compliance
          Tests:
          • true (All child tests must be true. (AND))
            • true (All child tests must be true. (AND))
              • true (The 'Manage auditing and security log' user right is only assigned to the Administrators group.)
          Tests:
          Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:25350100 (accesstoken_test)
          Result:true
          Title:The 'Manage auditing and security log' user right is only assigned to the Administrators group.
          Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
          Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).
          Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:obj:20000009 (accesstoken_object)
          Object Comment:Administrators
          Object Requirements:
          • Collect any available items.
          Exclude Items If:
          • security_principle equals (case insensitive) 'Administrators'
          State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:25350100 (accesstoken_state)
          State Comment: 
          State Requirements:
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'sesecurityprivilege' must be equal to '0'
          Collected Item/State Result:
          [ true ]
          • sesecurityprivilege equals '1' for:
          • sesecurityprivilege equals '0' for Replicator, NETWORK, Performance Monitor Users, Authenticated Users, SERVICE, ANONYMOUS LOGON, ELAM\Domain Admins, Performance Log Users, NT SERVICE\WdiServiceHost, Hyper-V Administrators, ENTERPRISE DOMAIN CONTROLLERS, RDS Endpoint Servers, RDS Management Servers, RDS Remote Access Servers, BATCH, PROXY, IUSR, NETWORK SERVICE, Print Operators, Remote Management Users, Access Control Assistance Operators, TERMINAL SERVER USER, Power Users, CREATOR GROUP, WDAGUtilityAccount, X_Admin, Local account, DefaultAccount, Device Owners, IIS_IUSRS, Users, Cryptographic Operators, CREATOR OWNER SERVER, SYSTEM, NT SERVICE\ALL SERVICES, Network Configuration Operators, CREATOR GROUP SERVER, System Managed Accounts Group, Certificate Service DCOM Access, Storage Replica Administrators, Backup Operators, Remote Desktop Users, CREATOR OWNER, LOCAL SERVICE, Visitor, Distributed COM Users, ELAM\Enterprise Admins, INTERACTIVE, Everyone, Event Log Readers, DIALUP, Guests

          V-205644 - Windows Server 2019 must force audit policy subcategory settings to override audit policy category settings.

          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-205644r958442_rule
          Test Type:Automated
          Rule Result:Pass
          Version:WN19-SO-000050
          Identities:V-93151
          SV-103239
          CCI-000169 (NIST SP 800-53: AU-12 a; NIST SP 800-53A: AU-12.1 (ii); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: AU-12 a; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: AU-12 a)
          Description: Maintaining an audit trail of system activity logs can help identify configuration errors, troubleshoot service disruptions, and analyze compromises that have occurred, as well as detect attacks. Audit logs are necessary to provide a trail of evidence in case the system or network is compromised. Collecting this data is essential for analyzing the security of information assets and detecting signs of suspicious and unexpected behavior.
          This setting allows administrators to enable more precise auditing capabilities.
          Fix Text:Configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Windows Settings >> Security Settings >> Local Policies >> Security Options >> "Audit: Force audit policy subcategory settings (Windows Vista or later) to override audit policy category settings" to "Enabled".
          Severity:medium
          Weight:10.0
          Reference:
          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows Server 2019
          Publisher:DISA
          Type:DPMS Target
          Subject:Microsoft Windows Server 2019
          Identifier:2907
          Definitions:
          Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.windows2019:def:205644
          Definition Result:true
          Title:WN19-SO-000050 - Windows Server 2019 must force audit policy subcategory settings to override audit policy category settings.
          Description:Maintaining an audit trail of system activity logs can help identify configuration errors, troubleshoot service disruptions, and analyze compromises that have occurred, as well as detect attacks. Audit logs are necessary to provide a trail of evidence in case the system or network is compromised. Collecting this data is essential for analyzing the security of information assets and detecting signs of suspicious and unexpected behavior.
          This setting allows administrators to enable more precise auditing capabilities.
          Class:compliance
          Tests:
          • true (All child tests must be true. (AND))
            • true (All child tests must be true. (AND))
              • true (Audit policy using subcategories is enabled.)
          Tests:
          Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:25343700 (registry_test)
          Result:true
          Title:Audit policy using subcategories is enabled.
          Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
          Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).
          Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:obj:25343700 (registry_object)
          Object Comment: 
          Object Requirements:
          • check = 'hive' must be equal (case insensitive) to 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE'
          • check = 'key' must be equal (case insensitive) to 'SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Control\Lsa'
          • check = 'name' must be equal (case insensitive) to 'SCENoApplyLegacyAuditPolicy'
          State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:20000001 (registry_state)
          State Comment: 
          State Requirements:
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'type' must be equal to 'reg_dword'
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'value' must be equal to '1'
          Collected Item/State Result:
          [ true ]
          • hive equals 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE'
          • key equals 'SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Control\Lsa'
          • name equals 'SCENoApplyLegacyAuditPolicy'
          • last_write_time equals '134212907550000000'
          • type equals 'reg_dword'
          • value equals '1'
          • windows_view equals '64_bit'

          V-205648 - Windows Server 2019 must have the DoD Root Certificate Authority (CA) certificates installed in the Trusted Root Store.

          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-205648r958448_rule
          Test Type:Automated
          Rule Result:Pass
          Version:WN19-PK-000010
          Identities:SV-103573
          V-93487
          CCI-000185 (NIST SP 800-53: IA-5 (2); NIST SP 800-53A: IA-5 (2).1; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: IA-5 (2) (a); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: IA-5 (2) (b) (1))
          CCI-002470 (NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: SC-23 (5); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: SC-23 (5))
          Description: To ensure secure DoD websites and DoD-signed code are properly validated, the system must trust the DoD Root CAs. The DoD root certificates will ensure that the trust chain is established for server certificates issued from the DoD CAs.

          Satisfies: SRG-OS-000066-GPOS-00034, SRG-OS-000403-GPOS-00182
          Fix Text:Install the DoD Root CA certificates:
          DoD Root CA 3
          DoD Root CA 4
          DoD Root CA 5
          DoD Root CA 6

          The InstallRoot tool is available on Cyber Exchange at https://cyber.mil/pki-pke/tools-configuration-files. Certificate bundles published by the PKI can be found at https://crl.gds.disa.mil/.
          Severity:medium
          Weight:10.0
          Reference:
          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows Server 2019
          Publisher:DISA
          Type:DPMS Target
          Subject:Microsoft Windows Server 2019
          Identifier:2907
          Definitions:
          Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.windows2019:def:205648
          Definition Result:true
          Title:WN19-PK-000010 - Windows Server 2019 must have the DoD Root Certificate Authority (CA) certificates installed in the Trusted Root Store.
          Description:To ensure secure DoD websites and DoD-signed code are properly validated, the system must trust the DoD Root CAs. The DoD root certificates will ensure that the trust chain is established for server certificates issued from the DoD CAs.

          Satisfies: SRG-OS-000066-GPOS-00034, SRG-OS-000403-GPOS-00182
          Class:compliance
          Tests:
          • true (All child tests must be true. (AND))
            • true (All child tests must be true. (AND))
              • true (The DoD Root CA 3 Certificate expiring 12/30/2029 is installed into the Trusted Root Store.)
              • true (The DoD Root CA 4 Certificate expiring 7/25/2032 is installed into the Trusted Root Store.)
              • true (The DoD Root CA 5 Certificate expiring 6/14/2041 is installed into the Trusted Root Store.)
              • true (The DoD Root CA 6 Certificate expiring 1/24/2053 is installed into the Trusted Root Store.)
          Tests:
          Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:25342700 (registry_test)
          Result:true
          Title:The DoD Root CA 3 Certificate expiring 12/30/2029 is installed into the Trusted Root Store.
          Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
          Check:Result is based on check existence only.
          Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:obj:25342700 (registry_object)
          Object Comment: 
          Object Requirements:
          • Collect any available items.

          V-205649 - Windows Server 2019 must have the DoD Interoperability Root Certificate Authority (CA) cross-certificates installed in the Untrusted Certificates Store on unclassified systems.

          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-205649r958448_rule
          Test Type:Automated
          Rule Result:Fail
          Version:WN19-PK-000020
          Identities:V-93489
          SV-103575
          CCI-000185 (NIST SP 800-53: IA-5 (2); NIST SP 800-53A: IA-5 (2).1; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: IA-5 (2) (a); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: IA-5 (2) (b) (1))
          CCI-002470 (NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: SC-23 (5); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: SC-23 (5))
          Description: To ensure users do not experience denial of service when performing certificate-based authentication to DoD websites due to the system chaining to a root other than DoD Root CAs, the DoD Interoperability Root CA cross-certificates must be installed in the Untrusted Certificate Store. This requirement only applies to unclassified systems.

          Satisfies: SRG-OS-000066-GPOS-00034, SRG-OS-000403-GPOS-00182
          Fix Text:Install the DoD Interoperability Root CA cross-certificates on unclassified systems.

          Issued To - Issued By - Thumbprint

          DoD Root CA 3 - DoD Interoperability Root CA 2 - 49CBE933151872E17C8EAE7F0ABA97FB610F6477

          Administrators should run the Federal Bridge Certification Authority (FBCA) Cross-Certificate Removal Tool once as an administrator and once as the current user.

          The FBCA Cross-Certificate Remover Tool and User Guide are available on Cyber Exchange at https://cyber.mil/pki-pke/tools-configuration-files.
          Severity:medium
          Weight:10.0
          Reference:
          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows Server 2019
          Publisher:DISA
          Type:DPMS Target
          Subject:Microsoft Windows Server 2019
          Identifier:2907
          Definitions:
          Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.windows2019:def:205649
          Definition Result:false
          Title:WN19-PK-000020 - Windows Server 2019 must have the DoD Interoperability Root Certificate Authority (CA) cross-certificates installed in the Untrusted Certificates Store on unclassified systems.
          Description:To ensure users do not experience denial of service when performing certificate-based authentication to DoD websites due to the system chaining to a root other than DoD Root CAs, the DoD Interoperability Root CA cross-certificates must be installed in the Untrusted Certificate Store. This requirement only applies to unclassified systems.

          Satisfies: SRG-OS-000066-GPOS-00034, SRG-OS-000403-GPOS-00182
          Class:compliance
          Tests:
          • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
            • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
              • false (DoD Interoperability Root CA 2 certificate expiring 11/16/2024 is installed in the Untrusted Certificates Store)
          Tests:
          Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:25342900 (registry_test)
          Result:false
          Title:DoD Interoperability Root CA 2 certificate expiring 11/16/2024 is installed in the Untrusted Certificates Store
          Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
          Check:Result is based on check existence only.
          Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:obj:25342900 (registry_object)
          Object Comment: 
          Object Requirements:
          • Collect any available items.
          Result Analysis:Existence requirement is 'at_least_one_exists' and no items were collected, so result is false.

          V-205650 - Windows Server 2019 must have the US DoD CCEB Interoperability Root CA cross-certificates in the Untrusted Certificates Store on unclassified systems.

          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-205650r1107646_rule
          Test Type:Automated
          Rule Result:Fail
          Version:WN19-PK-000030
          Identities:V-93491
          SV-103577
          CCI-000185 (NIST SP 800-53: IA-5 (2); NIST SP 800-53A: IA-5 (2).1; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: IA-5 (2) (a); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: IA-5 (2) (b) (1))
          CCI-002470 (NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: SC-23 (5); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: SC-23 (5))
          Description: To ensure users do not experience denial of service when performing certificate-based authentication to DoD websites due to the system chaining to a root other than DoD Root CAs, the US DoD CCEB Interoperability Root CA cross-certificates must be installed in the Untrusted Certificate Store. This requirement only applies to unclassified systems.

          Satisfies: SRG-OS-000066-GPOS-00034, SRG-OS-000403-GPOS-00182
          Fix Text:Install the US DoD CCEB Interoperability Root CA cross-certificate on unclassified systems.

          Issued To - Issued By - Thumbprint

          DoD Root CA 3 - US DoD CCEB Interoperability Root CA 2 - 9B74964506C7ED9138070D08D5F8B969866560C8
          DoD Root CA 6 - US DOD CCEB Interoperability Root CA 2 -D471CA32F7A692CE6CBB6196BD3377FE4DBCD106

          Administrators should run the Federal Bridge Certification Authority (FBCA) Cross-Certificate Removal Tool once as an administrator and once as the current user.

          The FBCA Cross-Certificate Remover Tool and User Guide are available on Cyber Exchange at https://dl.cyber.mil/pki-pke/msi/InstallRoot_5.6x32.msi. Certificate bundles published by the PKI can be found at https://crl.gds.disa.mil/.
          Severity:medium
          Weight:10.0
          Reference:
          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows Server 2019
          Publisher:DISA
          Type:DPMS Target
          Subject:Microsoft Windows Server 2019
          Identifier:2907
          Definitions:

          Definition Result:false

This requirement only applies to unclassified systems.

          Satisfies: SRG-OS-000066-GPOS-00034, SRG-OS-000403-GPOS-00182
          Class:compliance

            • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
              • false (US DoD CCEB Interoperability Root CA 2 cross-certificate expiring 7/18/2025 is installed in the Untrusted Certificates Store)
              • false (US DoD CCEB Interoperability Root CA 2 cross-certificate expiring 7/18/2026 is installed in the Untrusted Certificates Store)
          Tests:
          Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:25343000 (registry_test)
          Result:false
          Title:US DoD CCEB Interoperability Root CA 2 cross-certificate expiring 7/18/2025 is installed in the Untrusted Certificates Store

          Check:Result is based on check existence only.
