          V-205651 - Windows Server 2019 users must be required to enter a password to access private keys stored on the computer.

          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-205651r958450_rule
          Test Type:Automated
          Rule Result:Pass
          Version:WN19-SO-000350
          Identities:V-93493
          SV-103579
          CCI-000186 (NIST SP 800-53: IA-5 (2); NIST SP 800-53A: IA-5 (2).1; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: IA-5 (2) (b); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: IA-5 (2) (a) (1))
          Description: If the private key is discovered, an attacker can use the key to authenticate as an authorized user and gain access to the network infrastructure.

          The cornerstone of the PKI is the private key used to encrypt or digitally sign information.

          If the private key is stolen, this will lead to the compromise of the authentication and non-repudiation gained through PKI because the attacker can use the private key to digitally sign documents and pretend to be the authorized user.

          Both the holders of a digital certificate and the issuing authority must protect the computers, storage devices, or whatever they use to keep the private keys.
          Fix Text:Configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Windows Settings >> Security Settings >> Local Policies >> Security Options >> "System cryptography: Force strong key protection for user keys stored on the computer" to "User must enter a password each time they use a key".
          Severity:medium
          Weight:10.0
          Reference:
          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows Server 2019
          Publisher:DISA
          Type:DPMS Target
          Subject:Microsoft Windows Server 2019
          Identifier:2907
          Definitions:
          Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.windows2019:def:205651
          Definition Result:true
          Title:WN19-SO-000350 - Windows Server 2019 users must be required to enter a password to access private keys stored on the computer.
          Description:If the private key is discovered, an attacker can use the key to authenticate as an authorized user and gain access to the network infrastructure.

          The cornerstone of the PKI is the private key used to encrypt or digitally sign information.

          If the private key is stolen, this will lead to the compromise of the authentication and non-repudiation gained through PKI because the attacker can use the private key to digitally sign documents and pretend to be the authorized user.

          Both the holders of a digital certificate and the issuing authority must protect the computers, storage devices, or whatever they use to keep the private keys.
          Class:compliance
          Tests:
          • true (All child tests must be true. (AND))
            • true (All child tests must be true. (AND))
              • true (Users are required to enter a password to access private keys stored on the computer.)
          Tests:
          Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:25447900 (registry_test)
          Result:true
          Title:Users are required to enter a password to access private keys stored on the computer.
          Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
          Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).
          Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:obj:25447900 (registry_object)
          Object Comment: 
          Object Requirements:
          • check = 'hive' must be equal (case insensitive) to 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE'
          • check = 'key' must be equal (case insensitive) to 'SOFTWARE\Policies\Microsoft\Cryptography'
          • check = 'name' must be equal (case insensitive) to 'ForceKeyProtection'
          State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:20000002 (registry_state)
          State Comment: 
          State Requirements:
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'type' must be equal to 'reg_dword'
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'value' must be equal to '2'
          Collected Item/State Result:
          [ true ]
          • hive equals 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE'
          • key equals 'SOFTWARE\Policies\Microsoft\Cryptography'
          • name equals 'ForceKeyProtection'
          • last_write_time equals '134212788620000000'
          • type equals 'reg_dword'
          • value equals '2'
          • windows_view equals '64_bit'

          V-205652 - Windows Server 2019 must have the built-in Windows password complexity policy enabled.

          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-205652r1051061_rule
          Test Type:Automated
          Rule Result:Pass
          Version:WN19-AC-000080
          Identities:V-93459
          SV-103545
          CCI-004066 (NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: IA-5 (1) (h))
          CCI-000192 (NIST SP 800-53: IA-5 (1) (a); NIST SP 800-53A: IA-5 (1).1 (v); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: IA-5 (1) (a))
          CCI-000193 (NIST SP 800-53: IA-5 (1) (a); NIST SP 800-53A: IA-5 (1).1 (v); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: IA-5 (1) (a))
          CCI-000194 (NIST SP 800-53: IA-5 (1) (a); NIST SP 800-53A: IA-5 (1).1 (v); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: IA-5 (1) (a))
          CCI-001619 (NIST SP 800-53: IA-5 (1) (a); NIST SP 800-53A: IA-5 (1).1 (v); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: IA-5 (1) (a))
          Description: The use of complex passwords increases their strength against attack. The built-in Windows password complexity policy requires passwords to contain at least three of the four types of characters (numbers, uppercase and lowercase letters, and special characters) and prevents the inclusion of user names or parts of user names.

          Satisfies: SRG-OS-000069-GPOS-00037, SRG-OS-000070-GPOS-00038, SRG-OS-000071-GPOS-00039, SRG-OS-000266-GPOS-00101
          Fix Text:Configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Windows Settings >> Security Settings >> Account Policies >> Password Policy >> "Password must meet complexity requirements" to "Enabled".
          Severity:medium
          Weight:10.0
          Reference:
          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows Server 2019
          Publisher:DISA
          Type:DPMS Target
          Subject:Microsoft Windows Server 2019
          Identifier:2907
          Definitions:
          Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.windows2019:def:205652
          Definition Result:true
          Title:WN19-AC-000080 - Windows Server 2019 must have the built-in Windows password complexity policy enabled.
          Description:The use of complex passwords increases their strength against attack. The built-in Windows password complexity policy requires passwords to contain at least three of the four types of characters (numbers, uppercase and lowercase letters, and special characters) and prevents the inclusion of user names or parts of user names.

          Satisfies: SRG-OS-000069-GPOS-00037, SRG-OS-000070-GPOS-00038, SRG-OS-000071-GPOS-00039, SRG-OS-000266-GPOS-00101
          Class:compliance
          Tests:
          • true (All child tests must be true. (AND))
            • true (All child tests must be true. (AND))
              • true ('Password must meet complexity requirements' is set to 'Enabled')
          Tests:
          Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:25330400 (passwordpolicy_test)
          Result:true
          Title:'Password must meet complexity requirements' is set to 'Enabled'
          Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
          Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).
          Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:obj:20000002 (passwordpolicy_object)
          Object Comment: 
          Object Requirements:
          • Collect any available items.
          State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:25330400 (passwordpolicy_state)
          State Comment: 
          State Requirements:
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'password_complexity' must be equal to '1'
          Collected Item/State Result:
          [ true ]
          • max_passwd_age equals '5184000'
          • min_passwd_age equals '86400'
          • min_passwd_len equals '14'
          • password_hist_len equals '24'
          • password_complexity equals '1'
          • reversible_encryption equals '0'
          • anonymous_name_lookup equals '0'

          V-205655 - Windows Server 2019 unencrypted passwords must not be sent to third-party Server Message Block (SMB) servers.

          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-205655r987796_rule
          Test Type:Automated
          Rule Result:Pass
          Version:WN19-SO-000180
          Identities:V-93469
          SV-103555
          CCI-000197 (NIST SP 800-53: IA-5 (1) (c); NIST SP 800-53A: IA-5 (1).1 (v); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: IA-5 (1) (c); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: IA-5 (1) (c))
          Description: Some non-Microsoft SMB servers only support unencrypted (plain-text) password authentication. Sending plain-text passwords across the network when authenticating to an SMB server reduces the overall security of the environment. Check with the vendor of the SMB server to determine if there is a way to support encrypted password authentication.
          Fix Text:Configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Windows Settings >> Security Settings >> Local Policies >> Security Options >> "Microsoft Network Client: Send unencrypted password to third-party SMB servers" to "Disabled".
          Severity:medium
          Weight:10.0
          Reference:
          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows Server 2019
          Publisher:DISA
          Type:DPMS Target
          Subject:Microsoft Windows Server 2019
          Identifier:2907
          Definitions:
          Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.windows2019:def:205655
          Definition Result:true
          Title:WN19-SO-000180 - Windows Server 2019 unencrypted passwords must not be sent to third-party Server Message Block (SMB) servers.
          Description:Some non-Microsoft SMB servers only support unencrypted (plain-text) password authentication. Sending plain-text passwords across the network when authenticating to an SMB server reduces the overall security of the environment. Check with the vendor of the SMB server to determine if there is a way to support encrypted password authentication.
          Class:compliance
          Tests:
          • true (All child tests must be true. (AND))
            • true (All child tests must be true. (AND))
              • true ('Microsoft Network Client: Send unencrypted password to connect to third-party SMB servers' is set to 'Disabled')
          Tests:
          Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:25345000 (registry_test)
          Result:true
          Title:'Microsoft Network Client: Send unencrypted password to connect to third-party SMB servers' is set to 'Disabled'
          Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
          Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).
          Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:obj:25345000 (registry_object)
          Object Comment: 
          Object Requirements:
          • check = 'hive' must be equal to 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE'
          • check = 'key' must be equal to 'System\CurrentControlSet\Services\LanmanWorkstation\Parameters'
          • check = 'name' must be equal to 'EnablePlainTextPassword'
          State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:20000000 (registry_state)
          State Comment: 
          State Requirements:
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'type' must be equal to 'reg_dword'
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'value' must be equal to '0'
          Collected Item/State Result:
          [ true ]
          • hive equals 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE'
          • key equals 'System\CurrentControlSet\Services\LanmanWorkstation\Parameters'
          • name equals 'EnablePlainTextPassword'
          • last_write_time equals '134212788620000000'
          • type equals 'reg_dword'
          • value equals '0'
          • windows_view equals '64_bit'

          V-205656 - Windows Server 2019 minimum password age must be configured to at least one day.

          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-205656r1051064_rule
          Test Type:Automated
          Rule Result:Pass
          Version:WN19-AC-000060
          Identities:SV-103557
          V-93471
          CCI-004066 (NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: IA-5 (1) (h))
          CCI-000198 (NIST SP 800-53: IA-5 (1) (d); NIST SP 800-53A: IA-5 (1).1 (v); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: IA-5 (1) (d))
          Description: Permitting passwords to be changed in immediate succession within the same day allows users to cycle passwords through their history database. This enables users to effectively negate the purpose of mandating periodic password changes.
          Fix Text:Configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Windows Settings >> Security Settings >> Account Policies >> Password Policy >> "Minimum password age" to at least "1" day.
          Severity:medium
          Weight:10.0
          Reference:
          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows Server 2019
          Publisher:DISA
          Type:DPMS Target
          Subject:Microsoft Windows Server 2019
          Identifier:2907
          Definitions:
          Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.windows2019:def:205656
          Definition Result:true
          Title:WN19-AC-000060 - Windows Server 2019 minimum password age must be configured to at least one day.
          Description:Permitting passwords to be changed in immediate succession within the same day allows users to cycle passwords through their history database. This enables users to effectively negate the purpose of mandating periodic password changes.
          Class:compliance
          Tests:
          • true (All child tests must be true. (AND))
            • true (All child tests must be true. (AND))
              • true ('Minimum Password Age' is set to at least '1' day)
          Tests:
          Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:25330200 (passwordpolicy_test)
          Result:true
          Title:'Minimum Password Age' is set to at least '1' day
          Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
          Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).
          Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:obj:20000002 (passwordpolicy_object)
          Object Comment: 
          Object Requirements:
          • Collect any available items.

          V-205659 - Windows Server 2019 maximum password age must be configured to 60 days or less.

          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-205659r1051067_rule
          Test Type:Automated
          Rule Result:Pass
          Version:WN19-AC-000050
          Identities:V-93477
          SV-103563
          CCI-004066 (NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: IA-5 (1) (h))
          CCI-000199 (NIST SP 800-53: IA-5 (1) (d); NIST SP 800-53A: IA-5 (1).1 (v); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: IA-5 (1) (d))
          Description: The longer a password is in use, the greater the opportunity for someone to gain unauthorized knowledge of the passwords. Scheduled changing of passwords hinders the ability of unauthorized system users to crack passwords and gain access to a system.
          Fix Text:Configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Windows Settings >> Security Settings >> Account Policies >> Password Policy >> "Maximum password age" to "60" days or less (excluding "0", which is unacceptable).
          Severity:medium
          Weight:10.0
          Reference:
          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows Server 2019
          Publisher:DISA
          Type:DPMS Target
          Subject:Microsoft Windows Server 2019
          Identifier:2907
          Definitions:
          Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.windows2019:def:205659
          Definition Result:true
          Title:WN19-AC-000050 - Windows Server 2019 maximum password age must be configured to 60 days or less.
          Description:The longer a password is in use, the greater the opportunity for someone to gain unauthorized knowledge of the passwords. Scheduled changing of passwords hinders the ability of unauthorized system users to crack passwords and gain access to a system.
          Class:compliance
          Tests:
          • true (All child tests must be true. (AND))
            • true (All child tests must be true. (AND))
              • true (Maximum Password Age is set to 60 days or less, but greater than 0.)
          Tests:
          Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:25330100 (passwordpolicy_test)
          Result:true
          Title:Maximum Password Age is set to 60 days or less, but greater than 0.
          Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
          Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).
          State Operator:All item-state comparisons must be true.
          Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:obj:20000002 (passwordpolicy_object)
          Object Comment: 
          Object Requirements:
          • Collect any available items.
          State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:25330100 (passwordpolicy_state)
          State Comment: 
          State Requirements:
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'max_passwd_age' must be less than or equal to '5184000'
          State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:25330101 (passwordpolicy_state)
          State Comment: 
          State Requirements:
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'max_passwd_age' must be greater than '0'
          Collected Item/State Result:
          [ true ]
          • max_passwd_age equals '5184000'
          • min_passwd_age equals '86400'
          • min_passwd_len equals '14'
          • password_hist_len equals '24'
          • password_complexity equals '1'
          • reversible_encryption equals '0'
          • anonymous_name_lookup equals '0'

          V-205660 - Windows Server 2019 password history must be configured to 24 passwords remembered.

          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-205660r1000129_rule
          Test Type:Automated
          Rule Result:Pass
          Version:WN19-AC-000040
          Identities:V-93479
          SV-103565
          CCI-004061 (NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: IA-5 (1) (b))
          Description: A system is more vulnerable to unauthorized access when system users recycle the same password several times without being required to change to a unique password on a regularly scheduled basis. This enables users to effectively negate the purpose of mandating periodic password changes. The default value is "24" for Windows domain systems. DOD has decided this is the appropriate value for all Windows systems.
          Fix Text:Configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Windows Settings >> Security Settings >> Account Policies >> Password Policy >> "Enforce password history" to "24" passwords remembered.
          Severity:medium
          Weight:10.0
          Reference:
          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows Server 2019
          Publisher:DISA
          Type:DPMS Target
          Subject:Microsoft Windows Server 2019
          Identifier:2907
          Definitions:
          Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.windows2019:def:205660
          Definition Result:true
          Title:WN19-AC-000040 - Windows Server 2019 password history must be configured to 24 passwords remembered.
          Description:A system is more vulnerable to unauthorized access when system users recycle the same password several times without being required to change to a unique password on a regularly scheduled basis. This enables users to effectively negate the purpose of mandating periodic password changes. The default value is "24" for Windows domain systems. DoD has decided this is the appropriate value for all Windows systems.
          Class:compliance
          Tests:
          • true (All child tests must be true. (AND))
            • true (All child tests must be true. (AND))
              • true ('Enforce password history' is set to '24' passwords or more)
          Tests:
          Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:25330000 (passwordpolicy_test)
          Result:true
          Title:'Enforce password history' is set to '24' passwords or more
          Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
          Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).
          Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:obj:20000002 (passwordpolicy_object)
          Object Comment: 
          Object Requirements:
          • Collect any available items.
          State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:25330000 (passwordpolicy_state)
          State Comment:Password History Length is greater than or equal to variable
          State Requirements:
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'password_hist_len' must be greater than or equal to '24'
          Collected Item/State Result:
          [ true ]
          • max_passwd_age equals '5184000'
          • min_passwd_age equals '86400'
          • min_passwd_len equals '14'
          • password_hist_len equals '24'
          • password_complexity equals '1'
          • reversible_encryption equals '0'
          • anonymous_name_lookup equals '0'

          V-205662 - Windows Server 2019 minimum password length must be configured to 14 characters.

          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-205662r1051069_rule
          Test Type:Automated
          Rule Result:Pass
          Version:WN19-AC-000070
          Identities:V-93463
          SV-103549
          CCI-004066 (NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: IA-5 (1) (h))
          CCI-000205 (NIST SP 800-53: IA-5 (1) (a); NIST SP 800-53A: IA-5 (1).1 (i); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: IA-5 (1) (a))
          Description: Information systems not protected with strong password schemes (including passwords of minimum length) provide the opportunity for anyone to crack the password, thus gaining access to the system and compromising the device, information, or the local network.
          Fix Text:Configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Windows Settings >> Security Settings >> Account Policies >> Password Policy >> "Minimum password length" to "14" characters.
          Severity:medium
          Weight:10.0
          Reference:
          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows Server 2019
          Publisher:DISA
          Type:DPMS Target
          Subject:Microsoft Windows Server 2019
          Identifier:2907

          Class:compliance
          Tests:

            • true (All child tests must be true. (AND))
              • true ('Minimum password length' is set to at least '14' characters)
          Tests:
          Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:25330300 (passwordpolicy_test)
          Result:true
          Title:'Minimum password length' is set to at least '14' characters
          Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
          Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).
          Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:obj:20000002 (passwordpolicy_object)
          Object Comment: 
          Object Requirements:
          • Collect any available items.
          State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:25330300 (passwordpolicy_state)
          State Comment:Minimum Password Length is greater than or equal to 14
          State Requirements:
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'min_passwd_len' must be greater than or equal to '14'

          V-205665 - Windows Server 2019 Access this computer from the network user right must only be assigned to the Administrators, Authenticated Users, and Enterprise Domain Controllers groups on domain controllers.

          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-205665r1137691_rule
          Test Type:Automated
          Rule Applicability:Windows_DomainController
          Rule Result:Not Applicable
          Version:WN19-DC-000340
          Identities:SV-103083
          V-92995
          CCI-000213 (NIST SP 800-53: AC-3; NIST SP 800-53A: AC-3.1; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: AC-3; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: AC-3)
          Description: Inappropriate granting of user rights can provide system, administrative, and other high-level capabilities.

          Accounts with the "Access this computer from the network" right may access resources on the system, and this right must be limited to those requiring it.
          Fix Text:Configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Windows Settings >> Security Settings >> Local Policies >> User Rights Assignment >> "Access this computer from the network" to include only the following accounts or groups:

          - Administrators
          - Authenticated Users
          - Enterprise Domain Controllers
          Severity:medium
          Weight:10.0
          Reference:
          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows Server 2019
          Publisher:DISA
          Type:DPMS Target
          Subject:Microsoft Windows Server 2019
          Identifier:2907

          V-205666 - Windows Server 2019 Allow log on through Remote Desktop Services user right must only be assigned to the Administrators group on domain controllers.

          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-205666r1137691_rule
          Test Type:Automated
          Rule Applicability:Windows_DomainController
          Rule Result:Not Applicable
          Version:WN19-DC-000360
          Identities:V-92997
          SV-103085
          CCI-000213 (NIST SP 800-53: AC-3; NIST SP 800-53A: AC-3.1; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: AC-3; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: AC-3)
          Description: Inappropriate granting of user rights can provide system, administrative, and other high-level capabilities.

          Accounts with the "Allow log on through Remote Desktop Services" user right can access a system through Remote Desktop.
          Fix Text:Configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Windows Settings >> Security Settings >> Local Policies >> User Rights Assignment >> "Allow log on through Remote Desktop Services" to include only the following accounts or groups:

          - Administrators
          Severity:medium
          Weight:10.0
          Reference:
          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows Server 2019
          Publisher:DISA
          Type:DPMS Target
          Subject:Microsoft Windows Server 2019
          Identifier:2907

          V-205667 - Windows Server 2019 Deny access to this computer from the network user right on domain controllers must be configured to prevent unauthenticated access.

          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-205667r1137691_rule
          Test Type:Automated
          Rule Applicability:Windows_DomainController
          Rule Result:Not Applicable
          Version:WN19-DC-000370
          Identities:SV-103087
          V-92999
          CCI-000213 (NIST SP 800-53: AC-3; NIST SP 800-53A: AC-3.1; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: AC-3; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: AC-3)
          Description: Inappropriate granting of user rights can provide system, administrative, and other high-level capabilities.

          The "Deny access to this computer from the network" user right defines the accounts that are prevented from logging on from the network.

          The Guests group must be assigned this right to prevent unauthenticated access.
          Fix Text:Configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Windows Settings >> Security Settings >> Local Policies >> User Rights Assignment >> "Deny access to this computer from the network" to include the following:

          - Guests Group
          Severity:medium
          Weight:10.0
          Reference:
          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows Server 2019
          Publisher:DISA
          Type:DPMS Target
          Subject:Microsoft Windows Server 2019
          Identifier:2907

          V-205668 - Windows Server 2019 Deny log on as a batch job user right on domain controllers must be configured to prevent unauthenticated access.

          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-205668r1137691_rule
          Test Type:Automated
          Rule Applicability:Windows_DomainController
          Rule Result:Not Applicable
          Version:WN19-DC-000380
          Identities:SV-103089
          V-93001
          CCI-000213 (NIST SP 800-53: AC-3; NIST SP 800-53A: AC-3.1; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: AC-3; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: AC-3)
          Description: Inappropriate granting of user rights can provide system, administrative, and other high-level capabilities.

          The "Deny log on as a batch job" user right defines accounts that are prevented from logging on to the system as a batch job, such as Task Scheduler.

          The Guests group must be assigned to prevent unauthenticated access.
          Fix Text:Configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Windows Settings >> Security Settings >> Local Policies >> User Rights Assignment >> "Deny log on as a batch job" to include the following:

          - Guests Group
          Severity:medium
          Weight:10.0
          Reference:
          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows Server 2019
          Publisher:DISA
          Type:DPMS Target
          Subject:Microsoft Windows Server 2019
          Identifier:2907

          V-205669 - Windows Server 2019 Deny log on as a service user right must be configured to include no accounts or groups (blank) on domain controllers.

          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-205669r1137691_rule
          Test Type:Automated
          Rule Applicability:Windows_DomainController
          Rule Result:Not Applicable
          Version:WN19-DC-000390
          Identities:V-93003
          SV-103091
          CCI-000213 (NIST SP 800-53: AC-3; NIST SP 800-53A: AC-3.1; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: AC-3; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: AC-3)
          Description: Inappropriate granting of user rights can provide system, administrative, and other high-level capabilities.

          The "Deny log on as a service" user right defines accounts that are denied logon as a service.

          Incorrect configurations could prevent services from starting and result in a denial of service.
          Fix Text:Configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Windows Settings >> Security Settings >> Local Policies >> User Rights Assignment >> "Deny log on as a service" to include no entries (blank).
          Severity:medium
          Weight:10.0
          Reference:
          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows Server 2019
          Publisher:DISA
          Type:DPMS Target
          Subject:Microsoft Windows Server 2019
          Identifier:2907

          V-205670 - Windows Server 2019 Deny log on locally user right on domain controllers must be configured to prevent unauthenticated access.

          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-205670r1137691_rule
          Test Type:Automated
          Rule Applicability:Windows_DomainController
          Rule Result:Not Applicable
          Version:WN19-DC-000400
          Identities:SV-103093
          V-93005
          CCI-000213 (NIST SP 800-53: AC-3; NIST SP 800-53A: AC-3.1; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: AC-3; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: AC-3)
          Description: Inappropriate granting of user rights can provide system, administrative, and other high-level capabilities.

          The "Deny log on locally" user right defines accounts that are prevented from logging on interactively.

          The Guests group must be assigned this right to prevent unauthenticated access.
          Fix Text:Configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Windows Settings >> Security Settings >> Local Policies >> User Rights Assignment >> "Deny log on locally" to include the following:

          - Guests Group
          Severity:medium
          Weight:10.0
          Reference:
          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows Server 2019
          Publisher:DISA
          Type:DPMS Target
          Subject:Microsoft Windows Server 2019
          Identifier:2907

          V-205671 - Windows Server 2019 "Access this computer from the network" user right must only be assigned to the Administrators and Authenticated Users groups on domain-joined member servers and standalone or nondomain-joined systems.

          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-205671r1137691_rule
          Test Type:Automated
          Rule Applicability:Windows_MemberServer
          Rule Applicability:Windows_StandaloneServer
          Rule Result:Pass
          Version:WN19-MS-000070
          Identities:V-93007
          SV-103095
          CCI-000213 (NIST SP 800-53: AC-3; NIST SP 800-53A: AC-3.1; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: AC-3; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: AC-3)
          Description: Inappropriate granting of user rights can provide system, administrative, and other high-level capabilities.

          Accounts with the "Access this computer from the network" user right may access resources on the system, and this right must be limited to those requiring it.
          Fix Text:Configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Windows Settings >> Security Settings >> Local Policies >> User Rights Assignment >> "Access this computer from the network" to include only the following accounts or groups:

          - Administrators
          - Authenticated Users
          Severity:medium
          Weight:10.0
          Reference:
          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows Server 2019
          Publisher:DISA
          Type:DPMS Target
          Subject:Microsoft Windows Server 2019
          Identifier:2907
          Definitions:
          Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.windows2019:def:205671
          Definition Result:true
          Title:WN19-MS-000070 - Windows Server 2019 "Access this computer from the network" user right must only be assigned to the Administrators and Authenticated Users groups on domain-joined member servers and standalone or nondomain-joined systems.
          Description:Inappropriate granting of user rights can provide system, administrative, and other high-level capabilities.

          Accounts with the "Access this computer from the network" user right may access resources on the system, and this right must be limited to those requiring it.
          Class:compliance
          Tests:
          • true (All child tests must be true. (AND))
            • true (All child tests must be true. (AND))
              • true (The 'Access this computer from the network to include only the following accounts or groups' user right is assigned only to Administrators and Authenticated Users)
          Tests:
          Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:25443400 (accesstoken_test)
          Result:true
          Title:The 'Access this computer from the network to include only the following accounts or groups' user right is assigned only to Administrators and Authenticated Users
          Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
          Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).
          Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:obj:25443400 (accesstoken_object)
          Object Comment: 
          Object Requirements:
          • Collect any available items.
          Exclude Items If:
          • security_principle equals (case insensitive) 'Administrators'
          Exclude Items If:
          • security_principle equals (case insensitive) 'Authenticated Users'
          State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:25348000 (accesstoken_state)
          State Comment: 
          State Requirements:
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'senetworklogonright' must be equal to '0'
          Collected Item/State Result:
          [ true ]
          • senetworklogonright equals '1' for:
          • senetworklogonright equals '0' for Replicator, NETWORK, Performance Monitor Users, SERVICE, ANONYMOUS LOGON, ELAM\Domain Admins, Performance Log Users, NT SERVICE\WdiServiceHost, Hyper-V Administrators, ENTERPRISE DOMAIN CONTROLLERS, RDS Endpoint Servers, RDS Management Servers, RDS Remote Access Servers, BATCH, PROXY, IUSR, NETWORK SERVICE, Print Operators, Remote Management Users, Access Control Assistance Operators, TERMINAL SERVER USER, Power Users, CREATOR GROUP, WDAGUtilityAccount, X_Admin, Local account, DefaultAccount, Device Owners, IIS_IUSRS, Users, Cryptographic Operators, CREATOR OWNER SERVER, SYSTEM, NT SERVICE\ALL SERVICES, Network Configuration Operators, CREATOR GROUP SERVER, System Managed Accounts Group, Certificate Service DCOM Access, Storage Replica Administrators, Backup Operators, Remote Desktop Users, CREATOR OWNER, LOCAL SERVICE, Visitor, Distributed COM Users, ELAM\Enterprise Admins, INTERACTIVE, Everyone, Event Log Readers, DIALUP, Guests

          V-205672 - Windows Server 2019 "Deny access to this computer from the network" user right on domain-joined member servers must be configured to prevent access from highly privileged domain accounts and local accounts and from unauthenticated access on all systems.

          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-205672r1137691_rule
          Test Type:Automated
          Rule Applicability:Windows_MemberServer
          Rule Applicability:Windows_StandaloneServer
          Rule Result:Pass
          Version:WN19-MS-000080
          Identities:V-93009
          SV-103097
          CCI-000213 (NIST SP 800-53: AC-3; NIST SP 800-53A: AC-3.1; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: AC-3; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: AC-3)
          Description: Inappropriate granting of user rights can provide system, administrative, and other high-level capabilities.

          The "Deny access to this computer from the network" user right defines the accounts that are prevented from logging on from the network.

          In an Active Directory Domain, denying logons to the Enterprise Admins and Domain Admins groups on lower-trust systems helps mitigate the risk of privilege escalation from credential theft attacks, which could lead to the compromise of an entire domain.

          Local accounts on domain-joined systems must also be assigned this right to decrease the risk of lateral movement resulting from credential theft attacks.

          The Guests group must be assigned this right to prevent unauthenticated access.
          Fix Text:Configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Windows Settings >> Security Settings >> Local Policies >> User Rights Assignment >> "Deny access to this computer from the network" to include the following:

          Domain Systems Only:
          - Enterprise Admins group
          - Domain Admins group
          - "Local account and member of Administrators group" or "Local account" (see Note below)

          All Systems:
          - Guests group

          Note: These are built-in security groups. "Local account" is more restrictive but may cause issues on servers such as systems that provide failover clustering.
          Severity:medium
          Weight:10.0
          Reference:
          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows Server 2019
          Publisher:DISA
          Type:DPMS Target
          Subject:Microsoft Windows Server 2019
          Identifier:2907
          Definitions:
          Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.windows2019:def:205672
          Definition Result:true
          Title:WN19-MS-000080 - Windows Server 2019 "Deny access to this computer from the network" user right on domain-joined member servers must be configured to prevent access from highly privileged domain accounts and local accounts and from unauthenticated access on all systems.
          Description:Inappropriate granting of user rights can provide system, administrative, and other high-level capabilities.

          The "Deny access to this computer from the network" user right defines the accounts that are prevented from logging on from the network.

          In an Active Directory Domain, denying logons to the Enterprise Admins and Domain Admins groups on lower-trust systems helps mitigate the risk of privilege escalation from credential theft attacks, which could lead to the compromise of an entire domain.

          Local accounts on domain-joined systems must also be assigned this right to decrease the risk of lateral movement resulting from credential theft attacks.

          The Guests group must be assigned this right to prevent unauthenticated access.
          Class:compliance
          Tests:
          • true (All child tests must be true. (AND))
            • true (All child tests must be true. (AND))
              • true (One or more child tests must be true. (OR))
                • true (All child tests must be true. (AND))
                  • true (One or more child tests must be true. (OR))
                    • true (Deny access to this computer from the network - Local account)
                    • false (Deny access to this computer from the network - Local account and member of Administrators group)
                  • true (Deny access to this computer from the network - Domain Admins)
                  • true (Deny access to this computer from the network - Enterprise Admins)
                  • true (All child tests must be true. (AND))
                    • true (The system is a Windows Domain Member Server)
                • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
                  • false (The system is a Windows Standalone Server)
              • true (Deny access to this computer from the network - Guests)
          Tests:
          Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:25349103 (accesstoken_test)
          Result:true
          Title:Deny access to this computer from the network - Local account
          Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
          Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).
          Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:obj:20000007 (accesstoken_object)
          Object Comment: 
          Object Requirements:
          • check = 'security_principle' must match the pattern '^(?i)(NT AUTHORITY\\)?Local account$'
          State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:25349100 (accesstoken_state)
          State Comment:Deny access to this computer from the network
          State Requirements:
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'sedenynetworklogonright' must be equal to '1'
          Collected Item/State Result:
          [ true ]
          • sedenynetworklogonright equals '1' for: Local account
          • sedenynetworklogonright equals '0' for
          Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:25443500 (accesstoken_test)
          Result:false
          Title:Deny access to this computer from the network - Local account and member of Administrators group
          Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
          Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).
          Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:obj:25443500 (accesstoken_object)
          Object Comment: 
          Object Requirements:
          • check = 'security_principle' must match the pattern '^(?i)(NT AUTHORITY\\)?Local account and member of Administrators group$'
          State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:25349100 (accesstoken_state)
          State Comment:Deny access to this computer from the network
          State Requirements:
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'sedenynetworklogonright' must be equal to '1'
          Collected Item/State Result:
          [ false ]
          • sedenynetworklogonright equals '1' for:
          • sedenynetworklogonright equals '0' for
          Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:25349101 (accesstoken_test)
          Result:true
          Title:Deny access to this computer from the network - Domain Admins
          Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
          Check:At least one collected item must match the given state(s).
          Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:obj:20000003 (accesstoken_object)
          Object Comment: 
          Object Requirements:
          • check = 'security_principle' must match the pattern '^(?i)(.+\\)?Domain Admins$'

          V-205673 - Windows Server 2019 "Deny log on as a batch job" user right on domain-joined member servers must be configured to prevent access from highly privileged domain accounts and from unauthenticated access on all systems.

          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-205673r1137691_rule
          Test Type:Automated
          Rule Applicability:Windows_MemberServer
          Rule Applicability:Windows_StandaloneServer
          Rule Result:Pass
          Version:WN19-MS-000090
          Identities:V-93011
          SV-103099
          CCI-000213 (NIST SP 800-53: AC-3; NIST SP 800-53A: AC-3.1; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: AC-3; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: AC-3)
          Description: Inappropriate granting of user rights can provide system, administrative, and other high-level capabilities.

          The "Deny log on as a batch job" user right defines accounts that are prevented from logging on to the system as a batch job, such as Task Scheduler.

          In an Active Directory Domain, denying logons to the Enterprise Admins and Domain Admins groups on lower-trust systems helps mitigate the risk of privilege escalation from credential theft attacks, which could lead to the compromise of an entire domain.

          The Guests group must be assigned to prevent unauthenticated access.
          Fix Text:Configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Windows Settings >> Security Settings >> Local Policies >> User Rights Assignment >> "Deny log on as a batch job" to include the following:

          Domain Systems Only:
          - Enterprise Admins Group
          - Domain Admins Group

          All Systems:
          - Guests Group
          Severity:medium
          Weight:10.0
          Reference:
          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows Server 2019
          Publisher:DISA
          Type:DPMS Target
          Subject:Microsoft Windows Server 2019
          Identifier:2907
          Definitions:
          Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.windows2019:def:205673
          Definition Result:true
          Title:WN19-MS-000090 - Windows Server 2019 "Deny log on as a batch job" user right on domain-joined member servers must be configured to prevent access from highly privileged domain accounts and from unauthenticated access on all systems.
          Description:Inappropriate granting of user rights can provide system, administrative, and other high-level capabilities.

          The "Deny log on as a batch job" user right defines accounts that are prevented from logging on to the system as a batch job, such as Task Scheduler.

          In an Active Directory Domain, denying logons to the Enterprise Admins and Domain Admins groups on lower-trust systems helps mitigate the risk of privilege escalation from credential theft attacks, which could lead to the compromise of an entire domain.

          The Guests group must be assigned to prevent unauthenticated access.
          Class:compliance
          Tests:
          • true (All child tests must be true. (AND))
            • true (One or more child tests must be true. (OR))
              • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
                • true (Deny log on as a batch job is assigned to Guests)
                • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
                  • false (The system is a Windows Standalone Server)
              • true (All child tests must be true. (AND))
                • true (Deny log on as a batch job is assigned to Guests)
                • true (The 'Deny log on as a batch job' user right is assigned to the Domain Admin groups.)
                • true (The 'Deny log on as a batch job' user right is assigned to the Enterprise Admin groups.)
                • true (All child tests must be true. (AND))
                  • true (The system is a Windows Domain Member Server)
          Tests:
          Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:25442200 (accesstoken_test)
          Result:true
          Title:Deny log on as a batch job is assigned to Guests
          Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
          Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).
          Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:obj:20000006 (accesstoken_object)
          Object Comment: 
          Object Requirements:
          • check = 'security_principle' must be equal (case insensitive) to 'Guests'
          State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:25349200 (accesstoken_state)
          State Comment: 
          State Requirements:
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'sedenybatchLogonright' must be equal to '1'
          Collected Item/State Result:
          [ true ]
          • sedenybatchLogonright equals '1' for: Guests
          • sedenybatchLogonright equals '0' for
          Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:10000300 (wmi57_test)
          Result:false
          Title:The system is a Windows Standalone Server
          Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
          Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).
          Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:obj:10000100 (wmi57_object)
          Object Comment: 
          Object Requirements:
          • check = 'namespace' must be equal to 'root\cimv2'
          • check = 'wql' must be equal to 'SELECT DomainRole FROM win32_computersystem'
          State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:10000300 (wmi57_state)
          State Comment:Standalone Server
          State Requirements:
          • for all 'result' the following must be true:
            • all domainrole must be equal to '2'
          Collected Item/State Result:
          [ false ]
          • namespace equals 'root\cimv2'
          • wql equals 'SELECT DomainRole FROM win32_computersystem'
          • collected 'result' result:
            • domainrole = '3'
          Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:25349200 (accesstoken_test)
          Result:true
          Title:The 'Deny log on as a batch job' user right is assigned to the Domain Admin groups.
          Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
          Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).
          Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:obj:20000003 (accesstoken_object)
          Object Comment: 
          Object Requirements:
          • check = 'security_principle' must match the pattern '^(?i)(.+\\)?Domain Admins$'
          State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:25349200 (accesstoken_state)
          State Comment: 
          State Requirements:
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'sedenybatchLogonright' must be equal to '1'
          Collected Item/State Result:
          [ true ]
          • sedenybatchLogonright equals '1' for: ELAM\Domain Admins
          • sedenybatchLogonright equals '0' for
          Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:25349201 (accesstoken_test)
          Result:true

          • sedenybatchLogonright equals '0' for

          V-205674 - Windows Server 2019 "Deny log on as a service" user right on domain-joined member servers must be configured to prevent access from highly privileged domain accounts. No other groups or accounts must be assigned this right.

          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-205674r1137691_rule
          Test Type:Automated
          Rule Applicability:Windows_MemberServer
          Rule Result:Pass
          Version:WN19-MS-000100
          Identities:SV-103101
          V-93013
          CCI-000213 (NIST SP 800-53: AC-3; NIST SP 800-53A: AC-3.1; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: AC-3; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: AC-3)
          Description: Inappropriate granting of user rights can provide system, administrative, and other high-level capabilities.

          The "Deny log on as a service" user right defines accounts that are denied logon as a service.

          In an Active Directory Domain, denying logons to the Enterprise Admins and Domain Admins groups on lower-trust systems helps mitigate the risk of privilege escalation from credential theft attacks, which could lead to the compromise of an entire domain.

          Incorrect configurations could prevent services from starting and result in a denial of service.
          Fix Text:Configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Windows Settings >> Security Settings >> Local Policies >> User Rights Assignment >> "Deny log on as a service" to include the following:

          Domain systems:
          - Enterprise Admins Group
          - Domain Admins Group
          Severity:medium
          Weight:10.0
          Reference:
          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows Server 2019
          Publisher:DISA
          Type:DPMS Target
          Subject:Microsoft Windows Server 2019
          Identifier:2907
          Definitions:
          Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.windows2019:def:205674
          Definition Result:true
          Title:WN19-MS-000100 - Windows Server 2019 "Deny log on as a service" user right on domain-joined member servers must be configured to prevent access from highly privileged domain accounts. No other groups or accounts must be assigned this right.
          Description:Inappropriate granting of user rights can provide system, administrative, and other high-level capabilities.

          The "Deny log on as a service" user right defines accounts that are denied logon as a service.

          In an Active Directory Domain, denying logons to the Enterprise Admins and Domain Admins groups on lower-trust systems helps mitigate the risk of privilege escalation from credential theft attacks, which could lead to the compromise of an entire domain.

          Incorrect configurations could prevent services from starting and result in a denial of service.
          Class:compliance
          Tests:
          • true (All child tests must be true. (AND))
            • true (All child tests must be true. (AND))
              • true (Deny log on as a service - Domain Admins)
              • true (Deny log on as a service - Enterprise Admins)
          Tests:
          Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:25349300 (accesstoken_test)
          Result:true
          Title:Deny log on as a service - Domain Admins
          Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
          Check:At least one collected item must match the given state(s).
          Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:obj:20000003 (accesstoken_object)
          Object Comment: 
          Object Requirements:
          • check = 'security_principle' must match the pattern '^(?i)(.+\\)?Domain Admins$'
          State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:25349300 (accesstoken_state)
          State Comment:Deny log on as a service
          State Requirements:
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'sedenyservicelogonright' must be equal to '1'
          Collected Item/State Result:
          [ true ]
          • sedenyservicelogonright equals '1' for: ELAM\Domain Admins
          • sedenyservicelogonright equals '0' for
          Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:25349301 (accesstoken_test)
          Result:true
          Title:Deny log on as a service - Enterprise Admins
          Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
          Check:At least one collected item must match the given state(s).
          Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:obj:20000004 (accesstoken_object)
          Object Comment: 
          Object Requirements:
          • check = 'security_principle' must match the pattern '^(?i)(.+\\)?Enterprise Admins$'
          State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:25349300 (accesstoken_state)
          State Comment:Deny log on as a service
          State Requirements:
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'sedenyservicelogonright' must be equal to '1'
          Collected Item/State Result:
          [ true ]
          • sedenyservicelogonright equals '1' for: ELAM\Enterprise Admins
          • sedenyservicelogonright equals '0' for

          V-205675 - Windows Server 2019 "Deny log on locally" user right on domain-joined member servers must be configured to prevent access from highly privileged domain accounts and from unauthenticated access on all systems.

          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-205675r1137691_rule
          Test Type:Automated
          Rule Applicability:Windows_MemberServer
          Rule Applicability:Windows_StandaloneServer
          Rule Result:Pass
          Version:WN19-MS-000110
          Identities:SV-103103
          V-93015
          CCI-000213 (NIST SP 800-53: AC-3; NIST SP 800-53A: AC-3.1; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: AC-3; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: AC-3)
          Description: Inappropriate granting of user rights can provide system, administrative, and other high-level capabilities.

          The "Deny log on locally" user right defines accounts that are prevented from logging on interactively.

          In an Active Directory Domain, denying logons to the Enterprise Admins and Domain Admins groups on lower-trust systems helps mitigate the risk of privilege escalation from credential theft attacks, which could lead to the compromise of an entire domain.

          The Guests group must be assigned this right to prevent unauthenticated access.
          Fix Text:Configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Windows Settings >> Security Settings >> Local Policies >> User Rights Assignment >> "Deny log on locally" to include the following:

          Domain Systems Only:
          - Enterprise Admins Group
          - Domain Admins Group

          All Systems:
          - Guests Group
          Severity:medium
          Weight:10.0
          Reference:
          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows Server 2019
          Publisher:DISA
          Type:DPMS Target
          Subject:Microsoft Windows Server 2019
          Identifier:2907
          Definitions:
          Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.windows2019:def:205675
          Definition Result:true
          Title:WN19-MS-000110 - Windows Server 2019 "Deny log on locally" user right on domain-joined member servers must be configured to prevent access from highly privileged domain accounts and from unauthenticated access on all systems.
          Description:Inappropriate granting of user rights can provide system, administrative, and other high-level capabilities.

          The "Deny log on locally" user right defines accounts that are prevented from logging on interactively.

          In an Active Directory Domain, denying logons to the Enterprise Admins and Domain Admins groups on lower-trust systems helps mitigate the risk of privilege escalation from credential theft attacks, which could lead to the compromise of an entire domain.

          The Guests group must be assigned this right to prevent unauthenticated access.
          Class:compliance
          Tests:
          • true (All child tests must be true. (AND))
            • true (All child tests must be true. (AND))
              • true (One or more child tests must be true. (OR))
                • true (All child tests must be true. (AND))
                  • true (Deny log on locally - Domain Admins)
                  • true (Deny log on as a locally - Enterprise Admins)
                • false (System is not a member of a domain)
              • true (Deny log on locally - Guests)
          Tests:
          Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:25349402 (accesstoken_test)
          Result:true
          Title:Deny log on locally - Domain Admins
          Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
          Check:At least one collected item must match the given state(s).
          Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:obj:20000003 (accesstoken_object)
          Object Comment: 
          Object Requirements:
          • check = 'security_principle' must match the pattern '^(?i)(.+\\)?Domain Admins$'
          State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:25349400 (accesstoken_state)
          State Comment:Deny log on locally
          State Requirements:
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'sedenyinteractivelogonright' must be equal to '1'
          Collected Item/State Result:
          [ true ]
          • sedenyinteractivelogonright equals '1' for: ELAM\Domain Admins
          • sedenyinteractivelogonright equals '0' for
          Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:25349403 (accesstoken_test)
          Result:true
          Title:Deny log on as a locally - Enterprise Admins
          Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
          Check:At least one collected item must match the given state(s).
          Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:obj:20000004 (accesstoken_object)
          Object Comment: 
          Object Requirements:
          • check = 'security_principle' must match the pattern '^(?i)(.+\\)?Enterprise Admins$'
          State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:25349400 (accesstoken_state)
          State Comment:Deny log on locally
          State Requirements:
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'sedenyinteractivelogonright' must be equal to '1'
          Collected Item/State Result:
          [ true ]
          • sedenyinteractivelogonright equals '1' for: ELAM\Enterprise Admins
          • sedenyinteractivelogonright equals '0' for
          Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:25349401 (wmi57_test)
          Result:false
          Title:System is not a member of a domain
          Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
          Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).
          State Operator:One or more item-state comparisons may be true.
          Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:obj:10000100 (wmi57_object)
          Object Comment: 
          Object Requirements:
          • check = 'namespace' must be equal to 'root\cimv2'
          • check = 'wql' must be equal to 'SELECT DomainRole FROM win32_computersystem'
          State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:10000100 (wmi57_state)
          State Comment:Standalone Workstation
          State Requirements:
          • for all 'result' the following must be true:
            • all domainrole must be equal to '0'
          State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:10000300 (wmi57_state)
          State Comment:Standalone Server
          State Requirements:
          • for all 'result' the following must be true:
            • all domainrole must be equal to '2'
          Collected Item/State Result:
          [ false ]
          • namespace equals 'root\cimv2'
          • wql equals 'SELECT DomainRole FROM win32_computersystem'

          V-205676 - Windows Server 2019 Allow log on locally user right must only be assigned to the Administrators group.

          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-205676r1137691_rule
          Test Type:Automated
          Rule Result:Pass
          Version:WN19-UR-000030
          Identities:V-93017
          SV-103105
          CCI-000213 (NIST SP 800-53: AC-3; NIST SP 800-53A: AC-3.1; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: AC-3; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: AC-3)
          Description: Inappropriate granting of user rights can provide system, administrative, and other high-level capabilities.

          Accounts with the "Allow log on locally" user right can log on interactively to a system.
          Fix Text:Configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Windows Settings >> Security Settings >> Local Policies >> User Rights Assignment >> "Allow log on locally" to include only the following accounts or groups:

          - Administrators
          Severity:medium
          Weight:10.0
          Reference:
          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows Server 2019
          Publisher:DISA
          Type:DPMS Target
          Subject:Microsoft Windows Server 2019
          Identifier:2907
          Definitions:
          Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.windows2019:def:205676
          Definition Result:true
          Title:WN19-UR-000030 - Windows Server 2019 Allow log on locally user right must only be assigned to the Administrators group.
          Description:Inappropriate granting of user rights can provide system, administrative, and other high-level capabilities.

          Accounts with the "Allow log on locally" user right can log on interactively to a system.
          Class:compliance
          Tests:
          • true (All child tests must be true. (AND))
            • true (All child tests must be true. (AND))
              • true (Allow log on locally - Administrators)
          Tests:
          Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:25449300 (accesstoken_test)
          Result:true
          Title:Allow log on locally - Administrators
          Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
          Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).
          Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:obj:20000009 (accesstoken_object)
          Object Comment:Administrators
          Object Requirements:
          • Collect any available items.
          Exclude Items If:
          • security_principle equals (case insensitive) 'Administrators'
          State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:25348200 (accesstoken_state)
          State Comment:Allow log on locally
          State Requirements:
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'seinteractivelogonright' must be equal to '0'
          Collected Item/State Result:
          [ true ]
          • seinteractivelogonright equals '1' for:
          • seinteractivelogonright equals '0' for Replicator, NETWORK, Performance Monitor Users, Authenticated Users, SERVICE, ANONYMOUS LOGON, ELAM\Domain Admins, Performance Log Users, NT SERVICE\WdiServiceHost, Hyper-V Administrators, ENTERPRISE DOMAIN CONTROLLERS, RDS Endpoint Servers, RDS Management Servers, RDS Remote Access Servers, BATCH, PROXY, IUSR, NETWORK SERVICE, Print Operators, Remote Management Users, Access Control Assistance Operators, TERMINAL SERVER USER, Power Users, CREATOR GROUP, WDAGUtilityAccount, X_Admin, Local account, DefaultAccount, Device Owners, IIS_IUSRS, Users, Cryptographic Operators, CREATOR OWNER SERVER, SYSTEM, NT SERVICE\ALL SERVICES, Network Configuration Operators, CREATOR GROUP SERVER, System Managed Accounts Group, Certificate Service DCOM Access, Storage Replica Administrators, Backup Operators, Remote Desktop Users, CREATOR OWNER, LOCAL SERVICE, Visitor, Distributed COM Users, ELAM\Enterprise Admins, INTERACTIVE, Everyone, Event Log Readers, DIALUP, Guests

          V-205678 - Windows Server 2019 must not have the Fax Server role installed.

          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-205678r958478_rule
          Test Type:Automated
          Rule Result:Pass
          Version:WN19-00-000320
          Identities:V-93383
          SV-103469
          CCI-000381 (NIST SP 800-53: CM-7; NIST SP 800-53A: CM-7.1 (ii); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: CM-7 a; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: CM-7 a)
          Description: Unnecessary services increase the attack surface of a system. Some of these services may not support required levels of authentication or encryption or may provide unauthorized access to the system.
          Fix Text:Uninstall the "Fax Server" role.

          Start "Server Manager".

          Select the server with the role.

          Scroll down to "ROLES AND FEATURES" in the right pane.

          Select "Remove Roles and Features" from the drop-down "TASKS" list.

          Select the appropriate server on the "Server Selection" page and click "Next".

          Deselect "Fax Server" on the "Roles" page.

          Click "Next" and "Remove" as prompted.
          Severity:medium
          Weight:10.0
          Reference:
          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows Server 2019
          Publisher:DISA
          Type:DPMS Target
          Subject:Microsoft Windows Server 2019
          Identifier:2907
          Definitions:
          Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.windows2019:def:205678
          Definition Result:true
          Title:WN19-00-000320 - Windows Server 2019 must not have the Fax Server role installed.
          Description:Unnecessary services increase the attack surface of a system. Some of these services may not support required levels of authentication or encryption or may provide unauthorized access to the system.
          Class:compliance
          Tests:
          • true (All child tests must be true. (AND))
            • true (All child tests must be true. (AND))
              • true (The Fax server role is not installed.)
          Tests:
          Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:25426900 (wmi57_test)
          Result:true
          Title:The Fax server role is not installed.
          Check Existence:Zero or more collected items may exist.
          Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).
          Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:obj:25426900 (wmi57_object)
          Object Comment:Fax Server
          Object Requirements:
          • check = 'namespace' must be equal to 'root\cimv2'
          • check = 'wql' must be equal to 'SELECT startmode FROM Win32_Service WHERE name='fax''
          State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:25426900 (wmi57_state)
          State Comment:StartMode is Disabled
          State Requirements:
          • for all 'result' the following must be true:
            • all startmode must be equal to 'Disabled'
          Collected Item/State Result:
          [ does not exist ]
          • namespace equals 'root\cimv2'
          • wql equals 'SELECT startmode FROM Win32_Service WHERE name='fax''

          V-205680 - Windows Server 2019 must not have Simple TCP/IP Services installed.

          Start "Server Manager".

          V-205681 - Windows Server 2019 must not have the TFTP Client installed.

          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-205681r958478_rule
          Test Type:Automated
          Rule Result:Pass
          Version:WN19-00-000370
          Identities:SV-103475
          V-93389
          CCI-000381 (NIST SP 800-53: CM-7; NIST SP 800-53A: CM-7.1 (ii); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: CM-7 a; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: CM-7 a)
          Description: Unnecessary services increase the attack surface of a system. Some of these services may not support required levels of authentication or encryption or may provide unauthorized access to the system.
          Fix Text:Uninstall the "TFTP Client" feature.

          Start "Server Manager".

          Select the server with the feature.

          Scroll down to "ROLES AND FEATURES" in the right pane.

          Select "Remove Roles and Features" from the drop-down "TASKS" list.

          Select the appropriate server on the "Server Selection" page and click "Next".

          Deselect "TFTP Client" on the "Features" page.

          Click "Next" and "Remove" as prompted.

          Weight:10.0

          Class:compliance
          Tests:
          • true (All child tests must be true. (AND))
            • true (All child tests must be true. (AND))
              • true (The TFTP Client is not installed on the system)
          Tests:
          Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:25327900 (wmi57_test)
          Result:true
          Title:The TFTP Client is not installed on the system
          Check Existence:Zero or more collected items may exist.
          Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).
          Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:obj:25327900 (wmi57_object)
          Object Comment: 
          Object Requirements:
          • check = 'namespace' must be equal to 'root\cimv2'
          • check = 'wql' must be equal to 'SELECT installstate FROM win32_optionalfeature WHERE name = 'TFTP''
          State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:25327900 (wmi57_state)
          State Comment:

          • for all 'result' the following must be true:
            • all installstate must be equal to '2'
          Collected Item/State Result:

          • wql equals 'SELECT installstate FROM win32_optionalfeature WHERE name = 'TFTP''
