          V-205682 - Windows Server 2019 must not have the Server Message Block (SMB) v1 protocol installed.

          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-205682r958478_rule
          Test Type:Automated
          Rule Result:Pass
          Version:WN19-00-000380
          Identities:V-93391
          SV-103477
          CCI-000381 (NIST SP 800-53: CM-7; NIST SP 800-53A: CM-7.1 (ii); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: CM-7 a; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: CM-7 a)
          Description: SMBv1 is a legacy protocol that uses the MD5 algorithm as part of SMB. MD5 is known to be vulnerable to a number of attacks such as collision and preimage attacks and is not FIPS compliant.
          Fix Text:Uninstall the SMBv1 protocol.

          Open "Windows PowerShell" with elevated privileges (run as administrator).

          Enter "Uninstall-WindowsFeature -Name FS-SMB1 -Restart".
          (Omit the Restart parameter if an immediate restart of the system cannot be done.)

          Alternately:

          Start "Server Manager".

          Select the server with the feature.

          Scroll down to "ROLES AND FEATURES" in the right pane.

          Select "Remove Roles and Features" from the drop-down "TASKS" list.

          Select the appropriate server on the "Server Selection" page and click "Next".

          Deselect "SMB 1.0/CIFS File Sharing Support" on the "Features" page.

          Click "Next" and "Remove" as prompted.
          Severity:medium
          Weight:10.0
          Reference:
          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows Server 2019
          Publisher:DISA
          Type:DPMS Target
          Subject:Microsoft Windows Server 2019
          Identifier:2907
          Definitions:
          Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.windows2019:def:205682
          Definition Result:true
          Title:WN19-00-000380 - Windows Server 2019 must not have the Server Message Block (SMB) v1 protocol installed.
          Description:SMBv1 is a legacy protocol that uses the MD5 algorithm as part of SMB. MD5 is known to be vulnerable to a number of attacks such as collision and preimage attacks and is not FIPS compliant.
          Class:compliance
          Tests:
          • true (All child tests must be true. (AND))
            • true (One or more child tests must be true. (OR))
              • true (All child tests must be true. (AND))
                • true (One or more child tests must be true. (OR))
                  • true ('SMB 1.0/CIFS File Sharing Support' feature is not installed)
                  • true ('Configure SMBv1 Server' is set to 'Disabled')
                • true (One or more child tests must be true. (OR))
                  • true ('SMB 1.0/CIFS File Sharing Support' feature is not installed)
                  • true ('Configure SMBv1 client driver' is set to 'Enabled' with 'Disable driver (recommended)' selected for 'Configure MrxSmb10 driver')
              • true ('SMB 1.0/CIFS File Sharing Support' feature is not installed)
          Tests:
          Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:25328600 (wmi57_test)
          Result:true
          Title:'SMB 1.0/CIFS File Sharing Support' feature is not installed
          Check Existence:All collected items must exist.
          Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).
          Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:obj:25328600 (wmi57_object)
          Object Comment: 
          Object Requirements:
          • check = 'namespace' must be equal to 'root\cimv2'
          • check = 'wql' must be equal to 'SELECT installstate FROM win32_optionalfeature WHERE name = 'SMB1Protocol''
          State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:25328600 (wmi57_state)
          State Comment:Check SMB v1 Protocol Win32_OptionalFeature object InstallState is equal to 2
          State Requirements:
          • for all 'result' the following must be true:
            • at least one installstate must be equal to '2'
          Collected Item/State Result:
          [ true ]
          • namespace equals 'root\cimv2'
          • wql equals 'SELECT installstate FROM win32_optionalfeature WHERE name = 'SMB1Protocol''
          • collected 'result' result:
            • installstate = '2'
          Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:25328700 (registry_test)
          Result:true
          Title:'Configure SMBv1 Server' is set to 'Disabled'
          Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
          Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).
          Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:obj:25328700 (registry_object)
          Object Comment: 
          Object Requirements:
          • check = 'hive' must be equal to 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE'
          • check = 'key' must be equal to 'SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Services\LanmanServer\Parameters'
          • check = 'name' must be equal to 'SMB1'
          State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:20000000 (registry_state)
          State Comment: 
          State Requirements:
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'type' must be equal to 'reg_dword'
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'value' must be equal to '0'
          Collected Item/State Result:
          [ true ]
          • hive equals 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE'
          • key equals 'SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Services\LanmanServer\Parameters'
          • name equals 'SMB1'
          • last_write_time equals '134212836120000000'
          • type equals 'reg_dword'
          • value equals '0'
          • windows_view equals '64_bit'
          Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:25328800 (registry_test)
          Result:true
          Title:'Configure SMBv1 client driver' is set to 'Enabled' with 'Disable driver (recommended)' selected for 'Configure MrxSmb10 driver'
          Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
          Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).
          Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:obj:25328800 (registry_object)
          Object Comment: 
          Object Requirements:
          • check = 'hive' must be equal to 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE'
          • check = 'key' must be equal to 'SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Services\mrxsmb10'
          • check = 'name' must be equal to 'Start'
          State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:20000004 (registry_state)
          State Comment:Reg_Dword type and value equals 4
          State Requirements:
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'type' must be equal to 'reg_dword'
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'value' must be equal to '4'
          Collected Item/State Result:
          [ true ]
          • hive equals 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE'
          • key equals 'SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Services\mrxsmb10'
          • name equals 'Start'
          • last_write_time equals '134212788620000000'
          • type equals 'reg_dword'
          • value equals '4'
          • windows_view equals '64_bit'

          V-205683 - Windows Server 2019 must have the Server Message Block (SMB) v1 protocol disabled on the SMB server.

          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-205683r958478_rule
          Test Type:Automated
          Rule Result:Pass
          Version:WN19-00-000390
          Identities:SV-103479
          V-93393
          CCI-000381 (NIST SP 800-53: CM-7; NIST SP 800-53A: CM-7.1 (ii); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: CM-7 a; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: CM-7 a)
          Description: SMBv1 is a legacy protocol that uses the MD5 algorithm as part of SMB. MD5 is known to be vulnerable to a number of attacks such as collision and preimage attacks as well as not being FIPS compliant.
          Fix Text:Configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Administrative Templates >> MS Security Guide >> "Configure SMBv1 Server" to "Disabled".

          The system must be restarted for the change to take effect.

          This policy setting requires the installation of the SecGuide custom templates included with the STIG package. "SecGuide.admx" and "SecGuide.adml" must be copied to the \Windows\PolicyDefinitions and \Windows\PolicyDefinitions\en-US directories respectively.
          Severity:medium
          Weight:10.0
          Reference:
          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows Server 2019
          Publisher:DISA
          Type:DPMS Target
          Subject:Microsoft Windows Server 2019
          Identifier:2907
          Definitions:
          Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.windows2019:def:205683
          Definition Result:true
          Title:WN19-00-000390 - Windows Server 2019 must have the Server Message Block (SMB) v1 protocol disabled on the SMB server.
          Description:SMBv1 is a legacy protocol that uses the MD5 algorithm as part of SMB. MD5 is known to be vulnerable to a number of attacks such as collision and preimage attacks as well as not being FIPS compliant.
          Class:compliance
          Tests:
          • true (All child tests must be true. (AND))
            • true (One or more child tests must be true. (OR))
              • true ('SMB 1.0/CIFS File Sharing Support' feature is not installed)
              • true ('Configure SMBv1 Server' is set to 'Disabled')
          Tests:
          Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:25328600 (wmi57_test)
          Result:true
          Title:'SMB 1.0/CIFS File Sharing Support' feature is not installed
          Check Existence:All collected items must exist.
          Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).
          Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:obj:25328600 (wmi57_object)
          Object Comment: 
          Object Requirements:
          • check = 'namespace' must be equal to 'root\cimv2'
          • check = 'wql' must be equal to 'SELECT installstate FROM win32_optionalfeature WHERE name = 'SMB1Protocol''
          State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:25328600 (wmi57_state)
          State Comment:Check SMB v1 Protocol Win32_OptionalFeature object InstallState is equal to 2
          State Requirements:
          • for all 'result' the following must be true:
            • at least one installstate must be equal to '2'
          Collected Item/State Result:
          [ true ]
          • namespace equals 'root\cimv2'
          • wql equals 'SELECT installstate FROM win32_optionalfeature WHERE name = 'SMB1Protocol''
          • collected 'result' result:
            • installstate = '2'
          Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:25328700 (registry_test)
          Result:true
          Title:'Configure SMBv1 Server' is set to 'Disabled'
          Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
          Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).
          Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:obj:25328700 (registry_object)
          Object Comment: 
          Object Requirements:
          • check = 'hive' must be equal to 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE'
          • check = 'key' must be equal to 'SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Services\LanmanServer\Parameters'
          • check = 'name' must be equal to 'SMB1'
          State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:20000000 (registry_state)
          State Comment: 
          State Requirements:
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'type' must be equal to 'reg_dword'
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'value' must be equal to '0'
          Collected Item/State Result:
          [ true ]
          • hive equals 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE'
          • key equals 'SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Services\LanmanServer\Parameters'
          • name equals 'SMB1'
          • last_write_time equals '134212836120000000'
          • type equals 'reg_dword'
          • value equals '0'
          • windows_view equals '64_bit'

          V-205684 - Windows Server 2019 must have the Server Message Block (SMB) v1 protocol disabled on the SMB client.

          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-205684r958478_rule
          Test Type:Automated
          Rule Result:Pass
          Version:WN19-00-000400
          Identities:V-93395
          SV-103481
          CCI-000381 (NIST SP 800-53: CM-7; NIST SP 800-53A: CM-7.1 (ii); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: CM-7 a; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: CM-7 a)
          Description: SMBv1 is a legacy protocol that uses the MD5 algorithm as part of SMB. MD5 is known to be vulnerable to a number of attacks such as collision and preimage attacks as well as not being FIPS compliant.
          Fix Text:Configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Administrative Templates >> MS Security Guide >> "Configure SMBv1 client driver" to "Enabled" with "Disable driver (recommended)" selected for "Configure MrxSmb10 driver".

          The system must be restarted for the changes to take effect.

          This policy setting requires the installation of the SecGuide custom templates included with the STIG package. "SecGuide.admx" and "SecGuide.adml" must be copied to the \Windows\PolicyDefinitions and \Windows\PolicyDefinitions\en-US directories respectively.
          Severity:medium
          Weight:10.0
          Reference:
          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows Server 2019
          Publisher:DISA
          Type:DPMS Target
          Subject:Microsoft Windows Server 2019
          Identifier:2907
          Definitions:
          Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.windows2019:def:205684
          Definition Result:true
          Title:WN19-00-000400 - Windows Server 2019 must have the Server Message Block (SMB) v1 protocol disabled on the SMB client.
          Description:SMBv1 is a legacy protocol that uses the MD5 algorithm as part of SMB. MD5 is known to be vulnerable to a number of attacks such as collision and preimage attacks as well as not being FIPS compliant.
          Class:compliance
          Tests:
          • true (All child tests must be true. (AND))
            • true (One or more child tests must be true. (OR))
              • true ('SMB 1.0/CIFS File Sharing Support' feature is not installed)
              • true ('Configure SMBv1 client driver' is set to 'Enabled' with 'Disable driver (recommended)' selected for 'Configure MrxSmb10 driver')
          Tests:
          Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:25328600 (wmi57_test)
          Result:true
          Title:'SMB 1.0/CIFS File Sharing Support' feature is not installed
          Check Existence:All collected items must exist.
          Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).
          Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:obj:25328600 (wmi57_object)
          Object Comment: 
          Object Requirements:
          • check = 'namespace' must be equal to 'root\cimv2'
          • check = 'wql' must be equal to 'SELECT installstate FROM win32_optionalfeature WHERE name = 'SMB1Protocol''
          State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:25328600 (wmi57_state)
          State Comment:Check SMB v1 Protocol Win32_OptionalFeature object InstallState is equal to 2
          State Requirements:
          • for all 'result' the following must be true:
            • at least one installstate must be equal to '2'
          Collected Item/State Result:
          [ true ]
          • namespace equals 'root\cimv2'
          • wql equals 'SELECT installstate FROM win32_optionalfeature WHERE name = 'SMB1Protocol''
          • collected 'result' result:
            • installstate = '2'
          Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:25328800 (registry_test)
          Result:true
          Title:'Configure SMBv1 client driver' is set to 'Enabled' with 'Disable driver (recommended)' selected for 'Configure MrxSmb10 driver'

          Object Comment:

          • check = 'hive' must be equal to 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE'
          • check = 'key' must be equal to 'SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Services\mrxsmb10'
          • check = 'name' must be equal to 'Start'

          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'value' must be equal to '4'

          V-205685 - Windows Server 2019 must not have Windows PowerShell 2.0 installed.

          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-205685r958478_rule
          Test Type:Automated
          Rule Result:Pass
          Version:WN19-00-000410
          Identities:V-93397
          SV-103483
          CCI-000381 (NIST SP 800-53: CM-7; NIST SP 800-53A: CM-7.1 (ii); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: CM-7 a; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: CM-7 a)
          Description: Windows PowerShell 5.x added advanced logging features that can provide additional detail when malware has been run on a system. Disabling the Windows PowerShell 2.0 mitigates against a downgrade attack that evades the Windows PowerShell 5.x script block logging feature.
          Fix Text:Uninstall the "Windows PowerShell 2.0 Engine".

          Start "Server Manager".

          Select the server with the feature.

          Scroll down to "ROLES AND FEATURES" in the right pane.

          Select "Remove Roles and Features" from the drop-down "TASKS" list.

          Select the appropriate server on the "Server Selection" page and click "Next".

          Deselect "Windows PowerShell 2.0 Engine" under "Windows PowerShell" on the "Features" page.

          Click "Next" and "Remove" as prompted.
          Severity:medium
          Weight:10.0
          Reference:
          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows Server 2019
          Publisher:DISA
          Type:DPMS Target
          Subject:Microsoft Windows Server 2019
          Identifier:2907
          Definitions:
          Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.windows2019:def:205685
          Definition Result:true
          Title:WN19-00-000410 - Windows Server 2019 must not have Windows PowerShell 2.0 installed.
          Description:Windows PowerShell 5.x added advanced logging features that can provide additional detail when malware has been run on a system. Disabling the Windows PowerShell 2.0 mitigates against a downgrade attack that evades the Windows PowerShell 5.x script block logging feature.
          Class:compliance
          Tests:
          • true (All child tests must be true. (AND))
            • true (All child tests must be true. (AND))
              • true (Windows PowerShell 2.0 Engine server feature is not installed)
          Tests:
          Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:20568500 (wmi57_test)
          Result:true
          Title:Windows PowerShell 2.0 Engine server feature is not installed
          Check Existence:All collected items must exist.
          Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).
          Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:obj:20568500 (wmi57_object)
          Object Comment: 
          Object Requirements:
          • check = 'namespace' must be equal to 'root\cimv2'
          • check = 'wql' must be equal to 'SELECT id FROM win32_serverfeature'
          State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:20568500 (wmi57_state)
          State Comment:ID 411 (Windows PowerShell 2.0 Engine) is NOT in the result set
          State Requirements:
          • for all 'result' the following must be true:
            • no id must be equal to '411'
          Collected Item/State Result:
          [ true ]
          • namespace equals 'root\cimv2'
          • wql equals 'SELECT id FROM win32_serverfeature'
          • collected 'result' result:
            • id = '481'
          • collected 'result' result:
            • id = '418'
          • collected 'result' result:
            • id = '466'
          • collected 'result' result:
            • id = '420'
          • collected 'result' result:
            • id = '425'
          • collected 'result' result:
            • id = '412'
          • collected 'result' result:
            • id = '351'
          • collected 'result' result:
            • id = '417'
          • collected 'result' result:
            • id = '482'
          • collected 'result' result:
            • id = '1003'
          • collected 'result' result:
            • id = '340'
          • collected 'result' result:
            • id = '338'

          V-205686 - Windows Server 2019 must prevent the display of slide shows on the lock screen.

          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-205686r958478_rule
          Test Type:Automated
          Rule Result:Pass
          Version:WN19-CC-000010
          Identities:V-93399
          SV-103485
          CCI-000381 (NIST SP 800-53: CM-7; NIST SP 800-53A: CM-7.1 (ii); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: CM-7 a; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: CM-7 a)
          Description: Slide shows that are displayed on the lock screen could display sensitive information to unauthorized personnel. Turning off this feature will limit access to the information to a logged-on user.
          Fix Text:Configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Administrative Templates >> Control Panel >> Personalization >> "Prevent enabling lock screen slide show" to "Enabled".
          Severity:medium
          Weight:10.0
          Reference:
          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows Server 2019
          Publisher:DISA
          Type:DPMS Target
          Subject:Microsoft Windows Server 2019
          Identifier:2907
          Definitions:
          Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.windows2019:def:205686
          Definition Result:true
          Title:WN19-CC-000010 - Windows Server 2019 must prevent the display of slide shows on the lock screen.
          Description:Slide shows that are displayed on the lock screen could display sensitive information to unauthorized personnel. Turning off this feature will limit access to the information to a logged-on user.
          Class:compliance
          Tests:
          • true (All child tests must be true. (AND))
            • true (All child tests must be true. (AND))
              • true (Verifies 'Prevent enabling lock screen slide show' is set to 'Enabled')
          Tests:
          Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:25335200 (registry_test)
          Result:true
          Title:Verifies 'Prevent enabling lock screen slide show' is set to 'Enabled'
          Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
          Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).
          Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:obj:25335200 (registry_object)
          Object Comment:Registry object for HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\SOFTWARE\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\Personalization\NoLockScreenSlideshow
          Object Requirements:
          • check = 'hive' must be equal to 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE'
          • check = 'key' must be equal to 'SOFTWARE\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\Personalization'
          • check = 'name' must be equal to 'NoLockScreenSlideshow'
          State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:20000001 (registry_state)
          State Comment: 
          State Requirements:
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'type' must be equal to 'reg_dword'
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'value' must be equal to '1'
          Collected Item/State Result:
          [ true ]
          • hive equals 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE'
          • key equals 'SOFTWARE\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\Personalization'
          • name equals 'NoLockScreenSlideshow'
          • last_write_time equals '134213413420000000'
          • type equals 'reg_dword'
          • value equals '1'
          • windows_view equals '64_bit'

          V-205687 - Windows Server 2019 must have WDigest Authentication disabled.

          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-205687r958478_rule
          Test Type:Automated
          Rule Result:Pass
          Version:WN19-CC-000020
          Identities:SV-103487
          V-93401
          CCI-000381 (NIST SP 800-53: CM-7; NIST SP 800-53A: CM-7.1 (ii); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: CM-7 a; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: CM-7 a)
          Description: When the WDigest Authentication protocol is enabled, plain-text passwords are stored in the Local Security Authority Subsystem Service (LSASS), exposing them to theft. WDigest is disabled by default in Windows Server 2019. This setting ensures this is enforced.
          Fix Text:Configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Administrative Templates >> MS Security Guide >> "WDigest Authentication (disabling may require KB2871997)" to "Disabled".

          This policy setting requires the installation of the SecGuide custom templates included with the STIG package. "SecGuide.admx" and " SecGuide.adml" must be copied to the \Windows\PolicyDefinitions and \Windows\PolicyDefinitions\en-US directories respectively.
          Severity:medium
          Weight:10.0
          Reference:
          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows Server 2019
          Publisher:DISA
          Type:DPMS Target
          Subject:Microsoft Windows Server 2019
          Identifier:2907
          Definitions:
          Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.windows2019:def:205687
          Definition Result:true
          Title:WN19-CC-000020 - Windows Server 2019 must have WDigest Authentication disabled.
          Description:When the WDigest Authentication protocol is enabled, plain-text passwords are stored in the Local Security Authority Subsystem Service (LSASS), exposing them to theft. WDigest is disabled by default in Windows Server 2019. This setting ensures this is enforced.
          Class:compliance
          Tests:
          • true (All child tests must be true. (AND))
            • true (All child tests must be true. (AND))
              • true (WDigest Authentication is disabled)
          Tests:
          Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:25335800 (registry_test)
          Result:true
          Title:WDigest Authentication is disabled
          Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
          Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).
          Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:obj:25335800 (registry_object)
          Object Comment: 
          Object Requirements:
          • check = 'hive' must be equal to 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE'
          • check = 'key' must be equal to 'SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Control\SecurityProviders\Wdigest'
          • check = 'name' must be equal to 'UseLogonCredential'
          State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:20000000 (registry_state)
          State Comment: 
          State Requirements:
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'type' must be equal to 'reg_dword'
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'value' must be equal to '0'
          Collected Item/State Result:
          [ true ]
          • hive equals 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE'
          • key equals 'SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Control\SecurityProviders\Wdigest'
          • name equals 'UseLogonCredential'
          • last_write_time equals '134212788620000000'
          • type equals 'reg_dword'
          • value equals '0'
          • windows_view equals '64_bit'

          V-205688 - Windows Server 2019 downloading print driver packages over HTTP must be turned off.

          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-205688r958478_rule
          Test Type:Automated
          Rule Result:Pass
          Version:WN19-CC-000150
          Identities:SV-103489
          V-93403
          CCI-000381 (NIST SP 800-53: CM-7; NIST SP 800-53A: CM-7.1 (ii); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: CM-7 a; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: CM-7 a)
          Description: Some features may communicate with the vendor, sending system information or downloading data or components for the feature. Turning off this capability will prevent potentially sensitive information from being sent outside the enterprise and will prevent uncontrolled updates to the system.

          This setting prevents the computer from downloading print driver packages over HTTP.
          Fix Text:Configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Administrative Templates >> System >> Internet Communication Management >> Internet Communication settings >> "Turn off downloading of print drivers over HTTP" to "Enabled".
          Severity:medium
          Weight:10.0
          Reference:
          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows Server 2019
          Publisher:DISA
          Type:DPMS Target
          Subject:Microsoft Windows Server 2019
          Identifier:2907
          Definitions:
          Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.windows2019:def:205688
          Definition Result:true
          Title:WN19-CC-000150 - Windows Server 2019 downloading print driver packages over HTTP must be turned off.
          Description:Some features may communicate with the vendor, sending system information or downloading data or components for the feature. Turning off this capability will prevent potentially sensitive information from being sent outside the enterprise and will prevent uncontrolled updates to the system.

          This setting prevents the computer from downloading print driver packages over HTTP.
          Class:compliance
          Tests:
          • true (All child tests must be true. (AND))
            • true (All child tests must be true. (AND))
              • true ('Turn off downloading of print drivers over HTTP' is set to 'Enabled')
          Tests:
          Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:25337400 (registry_test)
          Result:true
          Title:'Turn off downloading of print drivers over HTTP' is set to 'Enabled'
          Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
          Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).
          Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:obj:25337400 (registry_object)
          Object Comment: 
          Object Requirements:
          • check = 'hive' must be equal to 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE'
          • check = 'key' must be equal to 'SOFTWARE\Policies\Microsoft\Windows NT\Printers'
          • check = 'name' must be equal to 'DisableWebPnPDownload'
          State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:20000001 (registry_state)
          State Comment: 
          State Requirements:
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'type' must be equal to 'reg_dword'
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'value' must be equal to '1'
          Collected Item/State Result:
          [ true ]
          • hive equals 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE'
          • key equals 'SOFTWARE\Policies\Microsoft\Windows NT\Printers'
          • name equals 'DisableWebPnPDownload'
          • last_write_time equals '134213413420000000'
          • type equals 'reg_dword'
          • value equals '1'
          • windows_view equals '64_bit'

          V-205689 - Windows Server 2019 printing over HTTP must be turned off.

          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-205689r958478_rule
          Test Type:Automated
          Rule Result:Pass
          Version:WN19-CC-000160
          Identities:SV-103491
          V-93405
          CCI-000381 (NIST SP 800-53: CM-7; NIST SP 800-53A: CM-7.1 (ii); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: CM-7 a; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: CM-7 a)
          Description: Some features may communicate with the vendor, sending system information or downloading data or components for the feature. Turning off this capability will prevent potentially sensitive information from being sent outside the enterprise and will prevent uncontrolled updates to the system.

          This setting prevents the client computer from printing over HTTP, which allows the computer to print to printers on the intranet as well as the Internet.
          Fix Text:Configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Administrative Templates >> System >> Internet Communication Management >> Internet Communication settings >> "Turn off printing over HTTP" to "Enabled".
          Severity:medium
          Weight:10.0
          Reference:
          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows Server 2019
          Publisher:DISA
          Type:DPMS Target
          Subject:Microsoft Windows Server 2019
          Identifier:2907
          Definitions:
          Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.windows2019:def:205689
          Definition Result:true
          Title:WN19-CC-000160 - Windows Server 2019 printing over HTTP must be turned off.
          Description:Some features may communicate with the vendor, sending system information or downloading data or components for the feature. Turning off this capability will prevent potentially sensitive information from being sent outside the enterprise and will prevent uncontrolled updates to the system.

          This setting prevents the client computer from printing over HTTP, which allows the computer to print to printers on the intranet as well as the Internet.
          Class:compliance
          Tests:
          • true (All child tests must be true. (AND))
            • true (All child tests must be true. (AND))
              • true ('Turn off printing over HTTP' is set to 'Enabled')
          Tests:
          Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:25337600 (registry_test)
          Result:true
          Title:'Turn off printing over HTTP' is set to 'Enabled'
          Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
          Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).
          Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:obj:25337600 (registry_object)
          Object Comment: 
          Object Requirements:
          • check = 'hive' must be equal to 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE'
          • check = 'key' must be equal to 'SOFTWARE\Policies\Microsoft\Windows NT\Printers'
          • check = 'name' must be equal to 'DisableHTTPPrinting'
          State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:20000001 (registry_state)
          State Comment: 
          State Requirements:
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'type' must be equal to 'reg_dword'
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'value' must be equal to '1'
          Collected Item/State Result:
          [ true ]
          • hive equals 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE'
          • key equals 'SOFTWARE\Policies\Microsoft\Windows NT\Printers'
          • name equals 'DisableHTTPPrinting'
          • last_write_time equals '134213413420000000'
          • type equals 'reg_dword'
          • value equals '1'
          • windows_view equals '64_bit'

          V-205690 - Windows Server 2019 network selection user interface (UI) must not be displayed on the logon screen.

          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-205690r958478_rule
          Test Type:Automated
          Rule Result:Pass
          Version:WN19-CC-000170
          Identities:SV-103493
          V-93407
          CCI-000381 (NIST SP 800-53: CM-7; NIST SP 800-53A: CM-7.1 (ii); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: CM-7 a; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: CM-7 a)
          Description: Enabling interaction with the network selection UI allows users to change connections to available networks without signing in to Windows.
          Fix Text:Configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Administrative Templates >> System >> Logon >> "Do not display network selection UI" to "Enabled".
          Severity:medium
          Weight:10.0
          Reference:

          Publisher:DISA
          Type:DPMS Target

            • true (All child tests must be true. (AND))
              • true (Verifies 'Do not display network selection UI' is set to 'Enabled')

          Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:25337800 (registry_test)

          Title:Verifies 'Do not display network selection UI' is set to 'Enabled'
          Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
          Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).
          Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:obj:25337800 (registry_object)
          Object Comment:HKLM\SOFTWARE\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\System\DontDisplayNetworkSelectionUI
          Object Requirements:
          • check = 'hive' must be equal to 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE'
          • check = 'key' must be equal to 'SOFTWARE\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\System'
          • check = 'name' must be equal to 'DontDisplayNetworkSelectionUI'
          State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:20000001 (registry_state)
          State Comment:

          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'type' must be equal to 'reg_dword'
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'value' must be equal to '1'

          V-205692 - Windows Server 2019 Windows Defender SmartScreen must be enabled.

          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-205692r958478_rule
          Test Type:Automated
          Rule Result:Pass
          Version:WN19-CC-000300
          Identities:V-93411
          SV-103497
          CCI-000381 (NIST SP 800-53: CM-7; NIST SP 800-53A: CM-7.1 (ii); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: CM-7 a; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: CM-7 a)
          Description: Windows Defender SmartScreen helps protect systems from programs downloaded from the internet that may be malicious. Enabling SmartScreen can block potentially malicious programs or warn users.
          Fix Text:Configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Administrative Templates >> Windows Components >> File Explorer >> "Configure Windows Defender SmartScreen" to "Enabled" with either option "Warn" or "Warn and prevent bypass" selected.

          Windows 2019 includes duplicate policies for this setting. It can also be configured under Computer Configuration >> Administrative Templates >> Windows Components >> Windows Defender SmartScreen >> Explorer.
          Severity:medium
          Weight:10.0
          Reference:
          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows Server 2019
          Publisher:DISA
          Type:DPMS Target
          Subject:Microsoft Windows Server 2019
          Identifier:2907
          Definitions:
          Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.windows2019:def:205692
          Definition Result:true
          Title:WN19-CC-000300 - Windows Server 2019 Windows Defender SmartScreen must be enabled.
          Description:Windows Defender SmartScreen helps protect systems from programs downloaded from the internet that may be malicious. Enabling SmartScreen can block potentially malicious programs or warn users.
          Class:compliance
          Tests:
          • true (All child tests must be true. (AND))
            • true (All child tests must be true. (AND))
              • true ('Configure Windows SmartScreen' is set to 'Enabled')
          Tests:
          Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:25339500 (registry_test)
          Result:true
          Title:'Configure Windows SmartScreen' is set to 'Enabled'
          Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
          Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).
          Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:obj:25339500 (registry_object)
          Object Comment: 
          Object Requirements:
          • check = 'hive' must be equal to 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE'
          • check = 'key' must be equal to 'Software\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\System'
          • check = 'name' must be equal to 'EnableSmartScreen'
          State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:20000001 (registry_state)
          State Comment: 
          State Requirements:
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'type' must be equal to 'reg_dword'
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'value' must be equal to '1'
          Collected Item/State Result:
          [ true ]
          • hive equals 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE'
          • key equals 'Software\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\System'
          • name equals 'EnableSmartScreen'
          • last_write_time equals '134213413420000000'
          • type equals 'reg_dword'
          • value equals '1'
          • windows_view equals '64_bit'

          V-205693 - Windows Server 2019 must disable Basic authentication for RSS feeds over HTTP.

          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-205693r958478_rule
          Test Type:Automated
          Rule Result:Pass
          Version:WN19-CC-000400
          Identities:V-93413
          SV-103499
          CCI-000381 (NIST SP 800-53: CM-7; NIST SP 800-53A: CM-7.1 (ii); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: CM-7 a; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: CM-7 a)
          Description: Basic authentication uses plain-text passwords that could be used to compromise a system. Disabling Basic authentication will reduce this potential.
          Fix Text:The default behavior is for the Windows RSS platform to not use Basic authentication over HTTP connections.

          If this needs to be corrected, configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Administrative Templates >> Windows Components >> RSS Feeds >> "Turn on Basic feed authentication over HTTP" to "Not Configured" or "Disabled".
          Severity:medium
          Weight:10.0
          Reference:
          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows Server 2019
          Publisher:DISA
          Type:DPMS Target
          Subject:Microsoft Windows Server 2019
          Identifier:2907
          Definitions:
          Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.windows2019:def:205693
          Definition Result:true
          Title:WN19-CC-000400 - Windows Server 2019 must disable Basic authentication for RSS feeds over HTTP.
          Description:Basic authentication uses plain-text passwords that could be used to compromise a system. Disabling Basic authentication will reduce this potential.
          Class:compliance
          Tests:
          • true (All child tests must be true. (AND))
            • true (All child tests must be true. (AND))
              • true ('Turn on Basic feed authentication over HTTP' is set to 'Not Configured' or 'Disabled')
          Tests:
          Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:25340800 (registry_test)
          Result:true
          Title:'Turn on Basic feed authentication over HTTP' is set to 'Not Configured' or 'Disabled'
          Check Existence:Zero or more collected items may exist.
          Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).
          Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:obj:25340800 (registry_object)
          Object Comment: 
          Object Requirements:
          • check = 'hive' must be equal to 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE'
          • check = 'key' must be equal to 'Software\Policies\Microsoft\Internet Explorer\Feeds'
          • check = 'name' must be equal to 'AllowBasicAuthInClear'
          State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:20000000 (registry_state)
          State Comment: 
          State Requirements:
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'type' must be equal to 'reg_dword'
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'value' must be equal to '0'

          V-205694 - Windows Server 2019 must prevent Indexing of encrypted files.

          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-205694r958478_rule
          Test Type:Automated
          Rule Result:Pass
          Version:WN19-CC-000410
          Identities:V-93415
          SV-103501
          CCI-000381 (NIST SP 800-53: CM-7; NIST SP 800-53A: CM-7.1 (ii); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: CM-7 a; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: CM-7 a)
          Description: Indexing of encrypted files may expose sensitive data. This setting prevents encrypted files from being indexed.
          Fix Text:Configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Administrative Templates >> Windows Components >> Search >> "Allow indexing of encrypted files" to "Disabled".
          Severity:medium
          Weight:10.0
          Reference:
          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows Server 2019
          Publisher:DISA
          Type:DPMS Target
          Subject:Microsoft Windows Server 2019
          Identifier:2907
          Definitions:
          Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.windows2019:def:205694
          Definition Result:true
          Title:WN19-CC-000410 - Windows Server 2019 must prevent Indexing of encrypted files.
          Description:Indexing of encrypted files may expose sensitive data. This setting prevents encrypted files from being indexed.
          Class:compliance
          Tests:
          • true (All child tests must be true. (AND))
            • true (All child tests must be true. (AND))
              • true ('Allow indexing of encrypted files' is set to 'Disabled')
          Tests:
          Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:25340900 (registry_test)
          Result:true
          Title:'Allow indexing of encrypted files' is set to 'Disabled'
          Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
          Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).
          Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:obj:25340900 (registry_object)
          Object Comment: 
          Object Requirements:
          • check = 'hive' must be equal to 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE'
          • check = 'key' must be equal to 'SOFTWARE\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\Windows Search'
          • check = 'name' must be equal to 'AllowIndexingEncryptedStoresOrItems'
          State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:20000000 (registry_state)
          State Comment: 
          State Requirements:
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'type' must be equal to 'reg_dword'
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'value' must be equal to '0'
          Collected Item/State Result:
          [ true ]
          • hive equals 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE'
          • key equals 'SOFTWARE\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\Windows Search'
          • name equals 'AllowIndexingEncryptedStoresOrItems'
          • last_write_time equals '134213413420000000'
          • type equals 'reg_dword'
          • value equals '0'
          • windows_view equals '64_bit'

          V-205696 - Windows Server 2019 local users on domain-joined member servers must not be enumerated.

          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-205696r958478_rule
          Test Type:Automated
          Rule Applicability:Windows_MemberServer
          Rule Result:Pass
          Version:WN19-MS-000030
          Identities:SV-103505
          V-93419
          CCI-000381 (NIST SP 800-53: CM-7; NIST SP 800-53A: CM-7.1 (ii); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: CM-7 a; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: CM-7 a)
          Description: The username is one part of logon credentials that could be used to gain access to a system. Preventing the enumeration of users limits this information to authorized personnel.
          Fix Text:Configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Administrative Templates >> System >> Logon >> "Enumerate local users on domain-joined computers" to "Disabled".
          Severity:medium
          Weight:10.0
          Reference:
          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows Server 2019
          Publisher:DISA
          Type:DPMS Target
          Subject:Microsoft Windows Server 2019
          Identifier:2907
          Definitions:
          Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.windows2019:def:205696
          Definition Result:true

          Description:The username is one part of logon credentials that could be used to gain access to a system. Preventing the enumeration of users limits this information to authorized personnel.
          Class:compliance
          Tests:
          • true (All child tests must be true. (AND))
            • true (All child tests must be true. (AND))
              • true ('Enumerate local users on domain-joined computers' is set to 'Disabled')
          Tests:
          Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:25337900 (registry_test)
          Result:true
          Title:'Enumerate local users on domain-joined computers' is set to 'Disabled'
          Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
          Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).
          Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:obj:25337900 (registry_object)
          Object Comment: 
          Object Requirements:
          • check = 'hive' must be equal to 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE'
          • check = 'key' must be equal to 'Software\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\System'
          • check = 'name' must be equal to 'EnumerateLocalUsers'
          State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:20000000 (registry_state)
          State Comment: 
          State Requirements:
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'type' must be equal to 'reg_dword'
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'value' must be equal to '0'
          Collected Item/State Result:
          [ true ]
          • hive equals 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE'
          • key equals 'Software\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\System'
          • name equals 'EnumerateLocalUsers'
          • last_write_time equals '134213413420000000'
          • type equals 'reg_dword'
          • value equals '0'
          • windows_view equals '64_bit'

          V-205697 - Windows Server 2019 must not have the Microsoft FTP service installed unless required by the organization.

          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-205697r958480_rule
          Test Type:Automated
          Rule Result:Pass
          Version:WN19-00-000330
          Identities:SV-103507
          V-93421
          CCI-000382 (NIST SP 800-53: CM-7; NIST SP 800-53A: CM-7.1 (iii); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: CM-7 b; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: CM-7 b)
          Description: Unnecessary services increase the attack surface of a system. Some of these services may not support required levels of authentication or encryption.
          Fix Text:Uninstall the "FTP Server" role.

          Start "Server Manager".

          Select the server with the role.

          Scroll down to "ROLES AND FEATURES" in the right pane.

          Select "Remove Roles and Features" from the drop-down "TASKS" list.

          Select the appropriate server on the "Server Selection" page and click "Next".

          Deselect "FTP Server" under "Web Server (IIS)" on the "Roles" page.

          Click "Next" and "Remove" as prompted.
          Severity:medium
          Weight:10.0
          Reference:
          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows Server 2019
          Publisher:DISA
          Type:DPMS Target
          Subject:Microsoft Windows Server 2019
          Identifier:2907
          Definitions:
          Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.windows2019:def:205697
          Definition Result:true
          Title:WN19-00-000330 - Windows Server 2019 must not have the Microsoft FTP service installed unless required by the organization.
          Description:Unnecessary services increase the attack surface of a system. Some of these services may not support required levels of authentication or encryption.
          Class:compliance
          Tests:
          • true (All child tests must be true. (AND))
            • true (All child tests must be true. (AND))
              • true (Check to make sure the FTP server role is not present.)
          Tests:
          Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:25427000 (wmi57_test)
          Result:true
          Title:Check to make sure the FTP server role is not present.
          Check Existence:No collected items may exist.
          Check:Result is based on check existence only.
          Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:obj:25427000 (wmi57_object)
          Object Comment:FTP Server
          Object Requirements:
          • check = 'namespace' must be equal to 'root\cimv2'
          • check = 'wql' must be equal to 'SELECT startmode FROM Win32_Service WHERE name='ftpsvc''
          Collected Item/State Result:
          [ exists ]
          • namespace equals 'root\cimv2'
          • wql equals 'SELECT startmode FROM Win32_Service WHERE name='ftpsvc''

          V-205698 - Windows Server 2019 must not have the Telnet Client installed.

          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-205698r958480_rule
          Test Type:Automated
          Rule Result:Pass
          Version:WN19-00-000360
          Identities:V-93423
          SV-103509
          CCI-000382 (NIST SP 800-53: CM-7; NIST SP 800-53A: CM-7.1 (iii); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: CM-7 b; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: CM-7 b)
          Description: Unnecessary services increase the attack surface of a system. Some of these services may not support required levels of authentication or encryption or may provide unauthorized access to the system.
          Fix Text:Uninstall the "Telnet Client" feature.

          Start "Server Manager".

          Select the server with the feature.

          Scroll down to "ROLES AND FEATURES" in the right pane.

          Select "Remove Roles and Features" from the drop-down "TASKS" list.

          Select the appropriate server on the "Server Selection" page and click "Next".

          Deselect "Telnet Client" on the "Features" page.

          Click "Next" and "Remove" as prompted.
          Severity:medium
          Weight:10.0
          Reference:
          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows Server 2019
          Publisher:DISA
          Type:DPMS Target
          Subject:Microsoft Windows Server 2019
          Identifier:2907
          Definitions:
          Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.windows2019:def:205698
          Definition Result:true
          Title:WN19-00-000360 - Windows Server 2019 must not have the Telnet Client installed.
          Description:Unnecessary services increase the attack surface of a system. Some of these services may not support required levels of authentication or encryption or may provide unauthorized access to the system.
          Class:compliance
          Tests:
          • true (All child tests must be true. (AND))
            • true (All child tests must be true. (AND))
              • true (The Telnet Client is not installed on the system)
          Tests:
          Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:25327800 (wmi57_test)
          Result:true
          Title:The Telnet Client is not installed on the system
          Check Existence:All collected items must exist.
          Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).
          Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:obj:25327800 (wmi57_object)
          Object Comment: 
          Object Requirements:
          • check = 'namespace' must be equal to 'root\cimv2'
          • check = 'wql' must be equal to 'SELECT installstate FROM win32_optionalfeature WHERE name = 'TelnetClient''
          State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:25327800 (wmi57_state)
          State Comment: 
          State Requirements:
          • for all 'result' the following must be true:
            • at least one installstate must be equal to '2'
          Collected Item/State Result:
          [ true ]
          • namespace equals 'root\cimv2'
          • wql equals 'SELECT installstate FROM win32_optionalfeature WHERE name = 'TelnetClient''
          • collected 'result' result:
            • installstate = '2'

          V-205702 - Windows Server 2019 Kerberos user logon restrictions must be enforced.

          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-205702r1051071_rule
          Test Type:Automated
          Rule Applicability:Windows_DomainController
          Rule Result:Not Applicable
          Version:WN19-DC-000020
          Identities:V-93443
          SV-103529
          CCI-001942 (NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: IA-2 (9))
          Description: This policy setting determines whether the Kerberos Key Distribution Center (KDC) validates every request for a session ticket against the user rights policy of the target computer. The policy is enabled by default, which is the most secure setting for validating that access to target resources is not circumvented.

          Satisfies: SRG-OS-000112-GPOS-00057, SRG-OS-000113-GPOS-00058
          Fix Text:Configure the policy value in the Default Domain Policy for Computer Configuration >> Policies >> Windows Settings >> Security Settings >> Account Policies >> Kerberos Policy >> "Enforce user logon restrictions" to "Enabled".
          Severity:medium
          Weight:10.0
          Reference:
          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows Server 2019
          Publisher:DISA
          Type:DPMS Target
          Subject:Microsoft Windows Server 2019
          Identifier:2907

          V-205703 - Windows Server 2019 Kerberos service ticket maximum lifetime must be limited to 600 minutes or less.

          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-205703r1051072_rule
          Test Type:Automated
          Rule Applicability:Windows_DomainController
          Rule Result:Not Applicable
          Version:WN19-DC-000030
          Identities:SV-103531
          V-93445
          CCI-001941 (NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: IA-2 (8); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: IA-2 (8))
          CCI-001942 (NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: IA-2 (9))
          Description: This setting determines the maximum amount of time (in minutes) that a granted session ticket can be used to access a particular service. Session tickets are used only to authenticate new connections with servers. Ongoing operations are not interrupted if the session ticket used to authenticate the connection expires during the connection.

          Satisfies: SRG-OS-000112-GPOS-00057, SRG-OS-000113-GPOS-00058
          Fix Text:Configure the policy value in the Default Domain Policy for Computer Configuration >> Policies >> Windows Settings >> Security Settings >> Account Policies >> Kerberos Policy >> "Maximum lifetime for service ticket" to a maximum of "600" minutes, but not "0", which equates to "Ticket doesn't expire".
          Severity:medium
          Weight:10.0
          Reference:
          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows Server 2019
          Publisher:DISA
          Type:DPMS Target
          Subject:Microsoft Windows Server 2019
          Identifier:2907

          V-205704 - Windows Server 2019 Kerberos user ticket lifetime must be limited to 10 hours or less.

          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-205704r1051073_rule
          Test Type:Automated
          Rule Applicability:Windows_DomainController
          Rule Result:Not Applicable
          Version:WN19-DC-000040
          Identities:V-93447
          SV-103533
          CCI-001941 (NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: IA-2 (8); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: IA-2 (8))
          CCI-001942 (NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: IA-2 (9))
          Description: In Kerberos, there are two types of tickets: Ticket Granting Tickets (TGTs) and Service Tickets. Kerberos tickets have a limited lifetime so the time an attacker has to implement an attack is limited. This policy controls how long TGTs can be renewed. With Kerberos, the user's initial authentication to the domain controller results in a TGT, which is then used to request Service Tickets to resources. Upon startup, each computer gets a TGT before requesting a service ticket to the domain controller and any other computers it needs to access. For services that start up under a specified user account, users must always get a TGT first and then get Service Tickets to all computers and services accessed.

          Satisfies: SRG-OS-000112-GPOS-00057, SRG-OS-000113-GPOS-00058
          Fix Text:Configure the policy value in the Default Domain Policy for Computer Configuration >> Policies >> Windows Settings >> Security Settings >> Account Policies >> Kerberos Policy >> "Maximum lifetime for user ticket" to a maximum of "10" hours but not "0", which equates to "Ticket doesn't expire".
          Severity:medium
          Weight:10.0
          Reference:
          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows Server 2019
          Publisher:DISA
          Type:DPMS Target
          Subject:Microsoft Windows Server 2019
          Identifier:2907

          V-205705 - Windows Server 2019 Kerberos policy user ticket renewal maximum lifetime must be limited to seven days or less.

          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-205705r1051074_rule
          Test Type:Automated
          Rule Applicability:Windows_DomainController
          Rule Result:Not Applicable
          Version:WN19-DC-000050
          Identities:SV-103535
          V-93449
          CCI-001941 (NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: IA-2 (8); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: IA-2 (8))
          CCI-001942 (NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: IA-2 (9))
          Description: This setting determines the period of time (in days) during which a user's TGT may be renewed. This security configuration limits the amount of time an attacker has to crack the TGT and gain access.

          Satisfies: SRG-OS-000112-GPOS-00057, SRG-OS-000113-GPOS-00058
          Fix Text:Configure the policy value in the Default Domain Policy for Computer Configuration >> Policies >> Windows Settings >> Security Settings >> Account Policies >> Kerberos Policy >> "Maximum lifetime for user ticket renewal" to a maximum of "7" days or less.
          Severity:medium
          Weight:10.0
          Reference:
          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows Server 2019
          Publisher:DISA
          Type:DPMS Target
          Subject:Microsoft Windows Server 2019
          Identifier:2907

          V-205706 - Windows Server 2019 computer clock synchronization tolerance must be limited to five minutes or less.

          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-205706r1051075_rule
          Test Type:Automated
          Rule Applicability:Windows_DomainController
          Rule Result:Not Applicable
          Version:WN19-DC-000060
          Identities:V-93451
          SV-103537
          CCI-001941 (NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: IA-2 (8); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: IA-2 (8))
          CCI-001942 (NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: IA-2 (9))
          Description: This setting determines the maximum time difference (in minutes) that Kerberos will tolerate between the time on a client's clock and the time on a server's clock while still considering the two clocks synchronous. To prevent replay attacks, Kerberos uses timestamps as part of its protocol definition. For timestamps to work properly, the clocks of the client and the server need to be in sync as much as possible.

          Satisfies: SRG-OS-000112-GPOS-00057, SRG-OS-000113-GPOS-00058
          Fix Text:Configure the policy value in the Default Domain Policy for Computer Configuration >> Windows Settings >> Security Settings >> Account Policies >> Kerberos Policy >> "Maximum tolerance for computer clock synchronization" to a maximum of "5" minutes or less.
          Severity:medium
          Weight:10.0
          Reference:
          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows Server 2019
          Publisher:DISA
          Type:DPMS Target
          Subject:Microsoft Windows Server 2019
          Identifier:2907

          V-205708 - Windows Server 2019 Kerberos encryption types must be configured to prevent the use of DES and RC4 encryption suites.

          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-205708r971535_rule
          Test Type:Automated
          Rule Result:Pass
          Version:WN19-SO-000290
          Identities:V-93495
          SV-103581
          CCI-000803 (NIST SP 800-53: IA-7; NIST SP 800-53A: IA-7.1; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: IA-7; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: IA-7)
          Description: Certain encryption types are no longer considered secure. The DES and RC4 encryption suites must not be used for Kerberos encryption.

          Note: Organizations with domain controllers running earlier versions of Windows where RC4 encryption is enabled, selecting "The other domain supports Kerberos AES Encryption" on domain trusts, may be required to allow client communication across the trust relationship.
          Fix Text:Configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Windows Settings >> Security Settings >> Local Policies >> Security Options >> "Network security: Configure encryption types allowed for Kerberos" to "Enabled" with only the following selected:

          AES128_HMAC_SHA1
          AES256_HMAC_SHA1
          Future encryption types

          Note: Organizations with domain controllers running earlier versions of Windows where RC4 encryption is enabled, selecting "The other domain supports Kerberos AES Encryption" on domain trusts, may be required to allow client communication across the trust relationship.
          Severity:medium
          Weight:10.0
          Reference:
          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows Server 2019
          Publisher:DISA
          Type:DPMS Target
          Subject:Microsoft Windows Server 2019
          Identifier:2907
          Definitions:
          Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.windows2019:def:205708
          Definition Result:true
          Title:WN19-SO-000290 - Windows Server 2019 Kerberos encryption types must be configured to prevent the use of DES and RC4 encryption suites.
          Description:Certain encryption types are no longer considered secure. The DES and RC4 encryption suites must not be used for Kerberos encryption.

          Note: Organizations with domain controllers running earlier versions of Windows where RC4 encryption is enabled, selecting "The other domain supports Kerberos AES Encryption" on domain trusts, may be required to allow client communication across the trust relationship.
          Class:compliance
          Tests:
          • true (All child tests must be true. (AND))
            • true (All child tests must be true. (AND))
              • true ('Network Security: Configure encryption types allowed for Kerberos' is set to 'Enabled' with 'AES128_HMAC_SHA1', 'AES256_HMAC_SHA1', 'Future encryption types')
          Tests:
          Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:25346000 (registry_test)
          Result:true
          Title:'Network Security: Configure encryption types allowed for Kerberos' is set to 'Enabled' with 'AES128_HMAC_SHA1', 'AES256_HMAC_SHA1', 'Future encryption types'
          Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
          Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).
          Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:obj:25346000 (registry_object)
          Object Comment: 
          Object Requirements:
          • check = 'hive' must be equal to 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE'
          • check = 'key' must be equal to 'SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Policies\System\Kerberos\Parameters'
          • check = 'name' must be equal to 'SupportedEncryptionTypes'
          State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:25346000 (registry_state)
          State Comment:Reg_Dword type and value equals 2147483640 for AES128_HMAC_SHA1, AES256_HMAC_SHA1, and Future_Encryption_Types
          State Requirements:
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'type' must be equal to 'reg_dword'
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'value' must be equal to '2147483640'
          Collected Item/State Result:
          [ true ]
          • hive equals 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE'
          • key equals 'SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Policies\System\Kerberos\Parameters'
          • name equals 'SupportedEncryptionTypes'
          • last_write_time equals '134212788620000000'
          • type equals 'reg_dword'
          • value equals '2147483640'
          • windows_view equals '64_bit'

          V-205709 - Windows Server 2019 must have the built-in guest account disabled.

          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-205709r958504_rule
          Test Type:Automated
          Rule Result:Pass
          Version:WN19-SO-000010
          Identities:V-93497
          SV-103583
          CCI-000804 (NIST SP 800-53: IA-8; NIST SP 800-53A: IA-8.1; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: IA-8; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: IA-8)
          Description: A system faces an increased vulnerability threat if the built-in guest account is not disabled. This is a known account that exists on all Windows systems and cannot be deleted. This account is initialized during the installation of the operating system with no password assigned.
          Fix Text:Configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Windows Settings >> Security Settings >> Local Policies >> Security Options >> "Accounts: Guest account status" to "Disabled".
          Severity:medium
          Weight:10.0
          Reference:
          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows Server 2019
          Publisher:DISA
          Type:DPMS Target
          Subject:Microsoft Windows Server 2019
          Identifier:2907
          Definitions:
          Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.windows2019:def:205709
          Definition Result:true
          Title:WN19-SO-000010 - Windows Server 2019 must have the built-in guest account disabled.
          Description:A system faces an increased vulnerability threat if the built-in guest account is not disabled. This is a known account that exists on all Windows systems and cannot be deleted. This account is initialized during the installation of the operating system with no password assigned.
          Class:compliance
          Tests:
          • true (All child tests must be true. (AND))
            • true (All child tests must be true. (AND))
              • true (The built-in guest account is disabled.)
          Tests:
          Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:25343300 (user_sid55_test)
          Result:true
          Title:The built-in guest account is disabled.
          Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
          Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).
          Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:obj:25343300 (user_sid55_object)
          Object Comment:Built-in Guest account
          Object Requirements:
          • for user_sid, the following must be true:
            • user_sid must be equal to 'S-1-5-21-227057802-2767407349-872160095-501'
          State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:20000017 (user_sid55_state)
          State Comment: 
          State Requirements:
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'enabled' must be equal to '0'
          Collected Item/State Result:
          [ true ]
          • user_sid equals 'S-1-5-21-227057802-2767407349-872160095-501'
          • enabled equals 'false'
          • group_sid equals 'S-1-5-32-546'
          • last_logon equals '0'
          • user equals 'Visitor'
          • group equals 'Guests'
          • full_name equals ''
          • comment equals 'Built-in account for guest access to the computer/domain'
          • password_age_days equals '0'

          V-205712 - Windows Server 2019 Windows Remote Management (WinRM) client must not use Digest authentication.

          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-205712r958510_rule
          Test Type:Automated
          Rule Result:Pass
          Version:WN19-CC-000490
          Identities:SV-103591
          V-93505
          CCI-000877 (NIST SP 800-53: MA-4 c; NIST SP 800-53A: MA-4.1 (iv); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: MA-4 c; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: MA-4 c)
          Description: Digest authentication is not as strong as other options and may be subject to man-in-the-middle attacks. Disallowing Digest authentication will reduce this potential.
          Fix Text:Configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Administrative Templates >> Windows Components >> Windows Remote Management (WinRM) >> WinRM Client >> "Disallow Digest authentication" to "Enabled".
          Severity:medium
          Weight:10.0
          Reference:
          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows Server 2019
          Publisher:DISA
          Type:DPMS Target
          Subject:Microsoft Windows Server 2019
          Identifier:2907
          Definitions:
          Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.windows2019:def:205712
          Definition Result:true
          Title:WN19-CC-000490 - Windows Server 2019 Windows Remote Management (WinRM) client must not use Digest authentication.
          Description:Digest authentication is not as strong as other options and may be subject to man-in-the-middle attacks. Disallowing Digest authentication will reduce this potential.
          Class:compliance
          Tests:
          • true (All child tests must be true. (AND))
            • true (All child tests must be true. (AND))
              • true ('Disallow Digest authentication' is set to 'Enabled')
          Tests:
          Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:25342100 (registry_test)
          Result:true
          Title:'Disallow Digest authentication' is set to 'Enabled'
          Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
          Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).
          Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:obj:25342100 (registry_object)
          Object Comment: 
          Object Requirements:

          • check = 'key' must be equal to 'Software\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\WinRM\Client'
          • check = 'name' must be equal to 'AllowDigest'

          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'value' must be equal to '0'
