          V-205751 - Windows Server 2019 Back up files and directories user right must only be assigned to the Administrators group.

          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-205751r958726_rule
          Test Type:Automated
          Rule Result:Pass
          Version:WN19-UR-000040
          Identities:V-93053
          SV-103141
          CCI-002235 (NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: AC-6 (10); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: AC-6 (10))
          Description: Inappropriate granting of user rights can provide system, administrative, and other high-level capabilities.

          Accounts with the "Back up files and directories" user right can circumvent file and directory permissions and could allow access to sensitive data.
          Fix Text:Configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Windows Settings >> Security Settings >> Local Policies >> User Rights Assignment >> "Back up files and directories" to include only the following accounts or groups:

          - Administrators
          Severity:medium
          Weight:10.0
          Reference:
          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows Server 2019
          Publisher:DISA
          Type:DPMS Target
          Subject:Microsoft Windows Server 2019
          Identifier:2907
          Definitions:
          Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.windows2019:def:205751
          Definition Result:true
          Title:WN19-UR-000040 - Windows Server 2019 Back up files and directories user right must only be assigned to the Administrators group.
          Description:Inappropriate granting of user rights can provide system, administrative, and other high-level capabilities.

          Accounts with the "Back up files and directories" user right can circumvent file and directory permissions and could allow access to sensitive data.
          Class:compliance
          Tests:
          • true (All child tests must be true. (AND))
            • true (All child tests must be true. (AND))
              • true (Back up files and directories - Administrators)
          Tests:
          Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:25348300 (accesstoken_test)
          Result:true
          Title:Back up files and directories - Administrators
          Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
          Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).
          Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:obj:20000009 (accesstoken_object)
          Object Comment:Administrators
          Object Requirements:
          • Collect any available items.
          Exclude Items If:
          • security_principle equals (case insensitive) 'Administrators'
          State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:25348300 (accesstoken_state)
          State Comment:Back up files and directories
          State Requirements:
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'sebackupprivilege' must be equal to '0'
          Collected Item/State Result:
          [ true ]
          • sebackupprivilege equals '1' for:
          • sebackupprivilege equals '0' for Replicator, NETWORK, Performance Monitor Users, Authenticated Users, SERVICE, ANONYMOUS LOGON, ELAM\Domain Admins, Performance Log Users, NT SERVICE\WdiServiceHost, Hyper-V Administrators, ENTERPRISE DOMAIN CONTROLLERS, RDS Endpoint Servers, RDS Management Servers, RDS Remote Access Servers, BATCH, PROXY, IUSR, NETWORK SERVICE, Print Operators, Remote Management Users, Access Control Assistance Operators, TERMINAL SERVER USER, Power Users, CREATOR GROUP, WDAGUtilityAccount, X_Admin, Local account, DefaultAccount, Device Owners, IIS_IUSRS, Users, Cryptographic Operators, CREATOR OWNER SERVER, SYSTEM, NT SERVICE\ALL SERVICES, Network Configuration Operators, CREATOR GROUP SERVER, System Managed Accounts Group, Certificate Service DCOM Access, Storage Replica Administrators, Backup Operators, Remote Desktop Users, CREATOR OWNER, LOCAL SERVICE, Visitor, Distributed COM Users, ELAM\Enterprise Admins, INTERACTIVE, Everyone, Event Log Readers, DIALUP, Guests

          V-205752 - Windows Server 2019 Create a pagefile user right must only be assigned to the Administrators group.

          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-205752r958726_rule
          Test Type:Automated
          Rule Result:Pass
          Version:WN19-UR-000050
          Identities:SV-103143
          V-93055
          CCI-002235 (NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: AC-6 (10); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: AC-6 (10))
          Description: Inappropriate granting of user rights can provide system, administrative, and other high-level capabilities.

          Accounts with the "Create a pagefile" user right can change the size of a pagefile, which could affect system performance.
          Fix Text:Configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Windows Settings >> Security Settings >> Local Policies >> User Rights Assignment >> "Create a pagefile" to include only the following accounts or groups:

          - Administrators
          Severity:medium
          Weight:10.0
          Reference:
          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows Server 2019
          Publisher:DISA
          Type:DPMS Target
          Subject:Microsoft Windows Server 2019
          Identifier:2907
          Definitions:
          Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.windows2019:def:205752
          Definition Result:true
          Title:WN19-UR-000050 - Windows Server 2019 Create a pagefile user right must only be assigned to the Administrators group.
          Description:Inappropriate granting of user rights can provide system, administrative, and other high-level capabilities.

          Accounts with the "Create a pagefile" user right can change the size of a pagefile, which could affect system performance.
          Class:compliance
          Tests:
          • true (All child tests must be true. (AND))
            • true (All child tests must be true. (AND))
              • true (Create a pagefile - Administrators)
          Tests:
          Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:25348500 (accesstoken_test)
          Result:true
          Title:Create a pagefile - Administrators
          Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
          Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).
          Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:obj:20000009 (accesstoken_object)
          Object Comment:Administrators
          Object Requirements:
          • Collect any available items.
          Exclude Items If:
          • security_principle equals (case insensitive) 'Administrators'
          State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:25348500 (accesstoken_state)
          State Comment:Create a pagefile
          State Requirements:
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'secreatepagefileprivilege' must be equal to '0'
          Collected Item/State Result:
          [ true ]
          • secreatepagefileprivilege equals '1' for:
          • secreatepagefileprivilege equals '0' for Replicator, NETWORK, Performance Monitor Users, Authenticated Users, SERVICE, ANONYMOUS LOGON, ELAM\Domain Admins, Performance Log Users, NT SERVICE\WdiServiceHost, Hyper-V Administrators, ENTERPRISE DOMAIN CONTROLLERS, RDS Endpoint Servers, RDS Management Servers, RDS Remote Access Servers, BATCH, PROXY, IUSR, NETWORK SERVICE, Print Operators, Remote Management Users, Access Control Assistance Operators, TERMINAL SERVER USER, Power Users, CREATOR GROUP, WDAGUtilityAccount, X_Admin, Local account, DefaultAccount, Device Owners, IIS_IUSRS, Users, Cryptographic Operators, CREATOR OWNER SERVER, SYSTEM, NT SERVICE\ALL SERVICES, Network Configuration Operators, CREATOR GROUP SERVER, System Managed Accounts Group, Certificate Service DCOM Access, Storage Replica Administrators, Backup Operators, Remote Desktop Users, CREATOR OWNER, LOCAL SERVICE, Visitor, Distributed COM Users, ELAM\Enterprise Admins, INTERACTIVE, Everyone, Event Log Readers, DIALUP, Guests

          V-205754 - Windows Server 2019 Create global objects user right must only be assigned to Administrators, Service, Local Service, and Network Service.

          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-205754r958726_rule
          Test Type:Automated
          Rule Result:Pass
          Version:WN19-UR-000070
          Identities:V-93059
          SV-103147
          CCI-002235 (NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: AC-6 (10); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: AC-6 (10))
          Description: Inappropriate granting of user rights can provide system, administrative, and other high-level capabilities.

          Accounts with the "Create global objects" user right can create objects that are available to all sessions, which could affect processes in other users' sessions.
          Fix Text:Configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Windows Settings >> Security Settings >> Local Policies >> User Rights Assignment >> "Create global objects" to include only the following accounts or groups:

          - Administrators
          - Service
          - Local Service
          - Network Service
          Severity:medium
          Weight:10.0
          Reference:
          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows Server 2019
          Publisher:DISA
          Type:DPMS Target
          Subject:Microsoft Windows Server 2019
          Identifier:2907
          Definitions:
          Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.windows2019:def:205754
          Definition Result:true
          Title:WN19-UR-000070 - Windows Server 2019 Create global objects user right must only be assigned to Administrators, Service, Local Service, and Network Service.
          Description:Inappropriate granting of user rights can provide system, administrative, and other high-level capabilities.

          Accounts with the "Create global objects" user right can create objects that are available to all sessions, which could affect processes in other users' sessions.
          Class:compliance
          Tests:
          • true (All child tests must be true. (AND))
            • true (All child tests must be true. (AND))
              • true (Create global objects - Administrators, Service, Local Service, Network Service)
          Tests:
          Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:25348700 (accesstoken_test)
          Result:true
          Title:Create global objects - Administrators, Service, Local Service, Network Service
          Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
          Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).
          Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:obj:20000013 (accesstoken_object)
          Object Comment:Administrators, Local Service, Network Service, Service
          Object Requirements:
          • Collect any available items.
          Exclude Items If:
          • security_principle equals (case insensitive) 'Administrators'
          Exclude Items If:
          • security_principle equals (case insensitive) 'LOCAL SERVICE'
          Exclude Items If:
          • security_principle equals (case insensitive) 'NETWORK SERVICE'
          Exclude Items If:
          • security_principle equals (case insensitive) 'SERVICE'
          State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:25348700 (accesstoken_state)
          State Comment:Create global objects
          State Requirements:
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'secreateglobalprivilege' must be equal to '0'
          Collected Item/State Result:
          [ true ]
          • secreateglobalprivilege equals '1' for:
          • secreateglobalprivilege equals '0' for Replicator, NETWORK, Performance Monitor Users, Authenticated Users, ANONYMOUS LOGON, ELAM\Domain Admins, Performance Log Users, NT SERVICE\WdiServiceHost, Hyper-V Administrators, ENTERPRISE DOMAIN CONTROLLERS, RDS Endpoint Servers, RDS Management Servers, RDS Remote Access Servers, BATCH, PROXY, IUSR, Print Operators, Remote Management Users, Access Control Assistance Operators, TERMINAL SERVER USER, Power Users, CREATOR GROUP, WDAGUtilityAccount, X_Admin, Local account, DefaultAccount, Device Owners, IIS_IUSRS, Users, Cryptographic Operators, CREATOR OWNER SERVER, SYSTEM, NT SERVICE\ALL SERVICES, Network Configuration Operators, CREATOR GROUP SERVER, System Managed Accounts Group, Certificate Service DCOM Access, Storage Replica Administrators, Backup Operators, Remote Desktop Users, CREATOR OWNER, Visitor, Distributed COM Users, ELAM\Enterprise Admins, INTERACTIVE, Everyone, Event Log Readers, DIALUP, Guests

          V-205755 - Windows Server 2019 Create permanent shared objects user right must not be assigned to any groups or accounts.

          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-205755r958726_rule
          Test Type:Automated
          Rule Result:Pass
          Version:WN19-UR-000080
          Identities:SV-103149
          V-93061
          CCI-002235 (NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: AC-6 (10); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: AC-6 (10))
          Description: Inappropriate granting of user rights can provide system, administrative, and other high-level capabilities.

          Accounts with the "Create permanent shared objects" user right could expose sensitive data by creating shared objects.
          Fix Text:Configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Windows Settings >> Security Settings >> Local Policies >> User Rights Assignment >> "Create permanent shared objects" to be defined but containing no entries (blank).
          Severity:medium
          Weight:10.0
          Reference:
          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows Server 2019
          Publisher:DISA
          Type:DPMS Target
          Subject:Microsoft Windows Server 2019
          Identifier:2907
          Definitions:
          Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.windows2019:def:205755
          Definition Result:true
          Title:WN19-UR-000080 - Windows Server 2019 Create permanent shared objects user right must not be assigned to any groups or accounts.
          Description:Inappropriate granting of user rights can provide system, administrative, and other high-level capabilities.

          Accounts with the "Create permanent shared objects" user right could expose sensitive data by creating shared objects.
          Class:compliance
          Tests:
          • true (All child tests must be true. (AND))
            • true (All child tests must be true. (AND))
              • true (No groups or accounts are granted the 'Create permanent shared objects' user right.)
          Tests:
          Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:25348800 (accesstoken_test)
          Result:true
          Title:No groups or accounts are granted the 'Create permanent shared objects' user right.
          Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
          Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).
          Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:obj:20000008 (accesstoken_object)
          Object Comment:All security principles
          Object Requirements:
          • check = 'security_principle' must match the pattern '.+'
          State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:25348800 (accesstoken_state)
          State Comment: 
          State Requirements:
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'secreatepermanentprivilege' must be equal to '0'
          Collected Item/State Result:
          [ true ]
          • secreatepermanentprivilege equals '1' for:
          • secreatepermanentprivilege equals '0' for Access Control Assistance Operators, Administrators, Backup Operators, Certificate Service DCOM Access, Cryptographic Operators, Device Owners, Distributed COM Users, Event Log Readers, Guests, Hyper-V Administrators, IIS_IUSRS, Network Configuration Operators, Performance Log Users, Performance Monitor Users, Power Users, Print Operators, RDS Endpoint Servers, RDS Management Servers, RDS Remote Access Servers, Remote Desktop Users, Remote Management Users, Replicator, Storage Replica Administrators, System Managed Accounts Group, Users, DefaultAccount, Visitor, WDAGUtilityAccount, X_Admin, NETWORK SERVICE, LOCAL SERVICE, SYSTEM, IUSR, TERMINAL SERVER USER, Authenticated Users, ENTERPRISE DOMAIN CONTROLLERS, PROXY, ANONYMOUS LOGON, SERVICE, INTERACTIVE, BATCH, NETWORK, DIALUP, CREATOR GROUP SERVER, CREATOR OWNER SERVER, CREATOR GROUP, CREATOR OWNER, Everyone, ELAM\Enterprise Admins, ELAM\Domain Admins, Local account, NT SERVICE\ALL SERVICES, NT SERVICE\WdiServiceHost

          V-205756 - Windows Server 2019 Create symbolic links user right must only be assigned to the Administrators group.

          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-205756r958726_rule
          Test Type:Automated
          Rule Result:Pass
          Version:WN19-UR-000090
          Identities:SV-103151
          V-93063
          CCI-002235 (NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: AC-6 (10); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: AC-6 (10))
          Description: Inappropriate granting of user rights can provide system, administrative, and other high-level capabilities.

          Accounts with the "Create symbolic links" user right can create pointers to other objects, which could expose the system to attack.
          Fix Text:Configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Windows Settings >> Security Settings >> Local Policies >> User Rights Assignment >> "Create symbolic links" to include only the following accounts or groups:

          - Administrators

          Systems that have the Hyper-V role will also have "Virtual Machines" given this user right. If this needs to be added manually, enter it as "NT Virtual Machine\Virtual Machines".
          Severity:medium
          Weight:10.0
          Reference:
          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows Server 2019
          Publisher:DISA
          Type:DPMS Target
          Subject:Microsoft Windows Server 2019
          Identifier:2907
          Definitions:
          Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.windows2019:def:205756
          Definition Result:true
          Title:WN19-UR-000090 - Windows Server 2019 Create symbolic links user right must only be assigned to the Administrators group.
          Description:Inappropriate granting of user rights can provide system, administrative, and other high-level capabilities.

          Accounts with the "Create symbolic links" user right can create pointers to other objects, which could expose the system to attack.
          Class:compliance
          Tests:
          • true (All child tests must be true. (AND))
            • true (One or more child tests must be true. (OR))
              • true (All child tests must be true. (AND))
                • true (Create symbolic links - Administrators, Virtual Machines)
                • true (Hyper-V Role is installed)
              • true (Create symbolic links - Administrators)
          Tests:
          Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:25449901 (accesstoken_test)
          Result:true
          Title:Create symbolic links - Administrators, Virtual Machines
          Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
          Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).
          Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:obj:25449901 (accesstoken_object)
          Object Comment:Filter for Administrators, Virtual Machines
          Object Requirements:
          • Collect any available items.
          Exclude Items If:
          • security_principle equals (case insensitive) 'Administrators'
          Exclude Items If:
          • security_principle matches the pattern '^(?i)(NT VIRTUAL MACHINE\\)?Virtual Machines$'
          State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:25348900 (accesstoken_state)
          State Comment:Create symbolic links
          State Requirements:
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'secreatesymboliclinkprivilege' must be equal to '0'
          Collected Item/State Result:
          [ true ]
          • secreatesymboliclinkprivilege equals '1' for:
          • secreatesymboliclinkprivilege equals '0' for Replicator, NETWORK, Performance Monitor Users, Authenticated Users, SERVICE, ANONYMOUS LOGON, ELAM\Domain Admins, Performance Log Users, NT SERVICE\WdiServiceHost, Hyper-V Administrators, ENTERPRISE DOMAIN CONTROLLERS, RDS Endpoint Servers, RDS Management Servers, RDS Remote Access Servers, BATCH, PROXY, IUSR, NETWORK SERVICE, Print Operators, Remote Management Users, Access Control Assistance Operators, TERMINAL SERVER USER, Power Users, CREATOR GROUP, WDAGUtilityAccount, X_Admin, Local account, DefaultAccount, Device Owners, IIS_IUSRS, Users, Cryptographic Operators, CREATOR OWNER SERVER, SYSTEM, NT SERVICE\ALL SERVICES, Network Configuration Operators, CREATOR GROUP SERVER, System Managed Accounts Group, Certificate Service DCOM Access, Storage Replica Administrators, Backup Operators, Remote Desktop Users, CREATOR OWNER, LOCAL SERVICE, Visitor, Distributed COM Users, ELAM\Enterprise Admins, INTERACTIVE, Everyone, Event Log Readers, DIALUP, Guests
          Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:25449902 (wmi57_test)
          Result:true
          Title:Hyper-V Role is installed
          Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
          Check:Result is based on check existence only.
          Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:obj:20000014 (wmi57_object)
          Object Comment: 
          Object Requirements:
          • check = 'namespace' must be equal to 'root\cimv2'
          • check = 'wql' must be equal to 'SELECT ID FROM Win32_ServerFeature'

          [ exists ]
          • namespace equals 'root\cimv2'

          • collected 'result' result:
            • id = '425'
          • collected 'result' result:
            • id = '412'
          • collected 'result' result:
            • id = '351'
          • collected 'result' result:
            • id = '417'
          • collected 'result' result:
            • id = '482'
          • collected 'result' result:
            • id = '1003'
          • collected 'result' result:
            • id = '340'
          • collected 'result' result:
            • id = '338'
          Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:25348900 (accesstoken_test)

          V-205758 - Windows Server 2019 Force shutdown from a remote system user right must only be assigned to the Administrators group.

          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-205758r958726_rule
          Test Type:Automated
          Rule Result:Pass
          Version:WN19-UR-000110
          Identities:V-93067
          SV-103155
          CCI-002235 (NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: AC-6 (10); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: AC-6 (10))
          Description: Inappropriate granting of user rights can provide system, administrative, and other high-level capabilities.

          Accounts with the "Force shutdown from a remote system" user right can remotely shut down a system, which could result in a denial of service.
          Fix Text:Configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Windows Settings >> Security Settings >> Local Policies >> User Rights Assignment >> "Force shutdown from a remote system" to include only the following accounts or groups:

          - Administrators
          Severity:medium
          Weight:10.0
          Reference:

          Publisher:DISA
          Type:DPMS Target
          Subject:Microsoft Windows Server 2019
          Identifier:2907

          Definition Result:true
          Title:WN19-UR-000110 - Windows Server 2019 Force shutdown from a remote system user right must only be assigned to the Administrators group.
          Description:Inappropriate granting of user rights can provide system, administrative, and other high-level capabilities.

          Accounts with the "Force shutdown from a remote system" user right can remotely shut down a system, which could result in a denial of service.
          Class:compliance
          Tests:
          • true (All child tests must be true. (AND))
            • true (All child tests must be true. (AND))
              • true (Force shutdown from a remote system - Administrators)
          Tests:
          Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:25349700 (accesstoken_test)
          Result:true
          Title:Force shutdown from a remote system - Administrators
          Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
          Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).

          Object Comment:Administrators
          Object Requirements:
          • Collect any available items.
          Exclude Items If:
          • security_principle equals (case insensitive) 'Administrators'

          V-205759 - Windows Server 2019 Generate security audits user right must only be assigned to Local Service and Network Service.

          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-205759r958726_rule
          Test Type:Automated
          Rule Result:Pass
          Version:WN19-UR-000120
          Identities:V-93069
          SV-103157
          CCI-002235 (NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: AC-6 (10); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: AC-6 (10))
          Description: Inappropriate granting of user rights can provide system, administrative, and other high-level capabilities.

          The "Generate security audits" user right specifies users and processes that can generate Security Log audit records, which must only be the system service accounts defined.
          Fix Text:Configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Windows Settings >> Security Settings >> Local Policies >> User Rights Assignment >> "Generate security audits" to include only the following accounts or groups:

          - Local Service
          - Network Service
          Severity:medium
          Weight:10.0
          Reference:
          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows Server 2019
          Publisher:DISA
          Type:DPMS Target
          Subject:Microsoft Windows Server 2019
          Identifier:2907
          Definitions:
          Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.windows2019:def:205759
          Definition Result:true
          Title:WN19-UR-000120 - Windows Server 2019 Generate security audits user right must only be assigned to Local Service and Network Service.
          Description:Inappropriate granting of user rights can provide system, administrative, and other high-level capabilities.

          The "Generate security audits" user right specifies users and processes that can generate Security Log audit records, which must only be the system service accounts defined.
          Class:compliance
          Tests:
          • true (All child tests must be true. (AND))
            • true (All child tests must be true. (AND))
              • true (Generate security audits - Local Service, Network Service)
          Tests:
          Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:25450200 (accesstoken_test)
          Result:true
          Title:Generate security audits - Local Service, Network Service
          Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
          Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).
          Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:obj:25450200 (accesstoken_object)
          Object Comment:Filter for Local Service and Network Service
          Object Requirements:
          • Collect any available items.
          Exclude Items If:
          • security_principle equals (case insensitive) 'LOCAL SERVICE'
          Exclude Items If:
          • security_principle equals (case insensitive) 'NETWORK SERVICE'
          State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:25450200 (accesstoken_state)
          State Comment:Generate security audits
          State Requirements:
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'seauditprivilege' must be equal to '0'
          Collected Item/State Result:
          [ true ]
          • seauditprivilege equals '1' for:
          • seauditprivilege equals '0' for Replicator, Administrators, NETWORK, Performance Monitor Users, Authenticated Users, SERVICE, ANONYMOUS LOGON, ELAM\Domain Admins, Performance Log Users, NT SERVICE\WdiServiceHost, Hyper-V Administrators, ENTERPRISE DOMAIN CONTROLLERS, RDS Endpoint Servers, RDS Management Servers, RDS Remote Access Servers, BATCH, PROXY, IUSR, Print Operators, Remote Management Users, Access Control Assistance Operators, TERMINAL SERVER USER, Power Users, CREATOR GROUP, WDAGUtilityAccount, X_Admin, Local account, DefaultAccount, Device Owners, IIS_IUSRS, Users, Cryptographic Operators, CREATOR OWNER SERVER, SYSTEM, NT SERVICE\ALL SERVICES, Network Configuration Operators, CREATOR GROUP SERVER, System Managed Accounts Group, Certificate Service DCOM Access, Storage Replica Administrators, Backup Operators, Remote Desktop Users, CREATOR OWNER, Visitor, Distributed COM Users, ELAM\Enterprise Admins, INTERACTIVE, Everyone, Event Log Readers, DIALUP, Guests

          V-205760 - Windows Server 2019 Impersonate a client after authentication user right must only be assigned to Administrators, Service, Local Service, and Network Service.

          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-205760r958726_rule
          Test Type:Automated
          Rule Result:Pass
          Version:WN19-UR-000130
          Identities:SV-103159
          V-93071
          CCI-002235 (NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: AC-6 (10); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: AC-6 (10))
          Description: Inappropriate granting of user rights can provide system, administrative, and other high-level capabilities.

          The "Impersonate a client after authentication" user right allows a program to impersonate another user or account to run on their behalf. An attacker could use this to elevate privileges.
          Fix Text:Configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Windows Settings >> Security Settings >> Local Policies >> User Rights Assignment >> "Impersonate a client after authentication" to include only the following accounts or groups:

          - Administrators
          - Service
          - Local Service
          - Network Service
          Severity:medium
          Weight:10.0
          Reference:
          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows Server 2019
          Publisher:DISA
          Type:DPMS Target
          Subject:Microsoft Windows Server 2019
          Identifier:2907
          Definitions:
          Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.windows2019:def:205760
          Definition Result:true
          Title:WN19-UR-000130 - Windows Server 2019 Impersonate a client after authentication user right must only be assigned to Administrators, Service, Local Service, and Network Service.
          Description:Inappropriate granting of user rights can provide system, administrative, and other high-level capabilities.

          The "Impersonate a client after authentication" user right allows a program to impersonate another user or account to run on their behalf. An attacker could use this to elevate privileges.
          Class:compliance
          Tests:
          • true (All child tests must be true. (AND))
            • true (All child tests must be true. (AND))
              • true (No groups or accounts are granted the 'Impersonate a client after authentication' user right.)
          Tests:
          Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:22097500 (accesstoken_test)
          Result:true
          Title:No groups or accounts are granted the 'Impersonate a client after authentication' user right.
          Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
          Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).

          Object Comment:Administrators, Local Service, Network Service, Service
          Object Requirements:
          • Collect any available items.
          Exclude Items If:
          • security_principle equals (case insensitive) 'Administrators'
          Exclude Items If:
          • security_principle equals (case insensitive) 'LOCAL SERVICE'
          Exclude Items If:
          • security_principle equals (case insensitive) 'NETWORK SERVICE'
          Exclude Items If:
          • security_principle equals (case insensitive) 'SERVICE'
          State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:22097500 (accesstoken_state)
          State Comment:

          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'seimpersonateprivilege' must be equal to '0'

          V-205761 - Windows Server 2019 Increase scheduling priority: user right must only be assigned to the Administrators group.

          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-205761r958726_rule
          Test Type:Automated
          Rule Result:Pass
          Version:WN19-UR-000140
          Identities:SV-103161
          V-93073
          CCI-002235 (NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: AC-6 (10); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: AC-6 (10))
          Description: Inappropriate granting of user rights can provide system, administrative, and other high-level capabilities.

          Accounts with the "Increase scheduling priority" user right can change a scheduling priority, causing performance issues or a denial of service.
          Fix Text:Configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Windows Settings >> Security Settings >> Local Policies >> User Rights Assignment >> "Increase scheduling priority" to include only the following accounts or groups:

          - Administrators
          Severity:medium
          Weight:10.0

          Publisher:DISA

          Title:WN19-UR-000140 - Windows Server 2019 Increase scheduling priority: user right must only be assigned to the Administrators group.
          Description:Inappropriate granting of user rights can provide system, administrative, and other high-level capabilities.

          Accounts with the "Increase scheduling priority" user right can change a scheduling priority, causing performance issues or a denial of service.
          Class:compliance
          Tests:
          • true (All child tests must be true. (AND))
            • true (All child tests must be true. (AND))
              • true (Increase scheduling priority - Administrators)
          Tests:
          Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:25450400 (accesstoken_test)
          Result:true

          Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
          Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).

          Object Comment:Administrators
          Object Requirements:
          • Collect any available items.
          Exclude Items If:
          • security_principle equals (case insensitive) 'Administrators'

          V-205762 - Windows Server 2019 Load and unload device drivers user right must only be assigned to the Administrators group.

          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-205762r958726_rule
          Test Type:Automated
          Rule Result:Pass
          Version:WN19-UR-000150
          Identities:SV-103163
          V-93075
          CCI-002235 (NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: AC-6 (10); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: AC-6 (10))
          Description: Inappropriate granting of user rights can provide system, administrative, and other high-level capabilities.

          The "Load and unload device drivers" user right allows a user to load device drivers dynamically on a system. This could be used by an attacker to install malicious code.
          Fix Text:Configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Windows Settings >> Security Settings >> Local Policies >> User Rights Assignment >> "Load and unload device drivers" to include only the following accounts or groups:

          - Administrators
          Severity:medium
          Weight:10.0
          Reference:
          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows Server 2019
          Publisher:DISA
          Type:DPMS Target
          Subject:Microsoft Windows Server 2019
          Identifier:2907
          Definitions:
          Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.windows2019:def:205762
          Definition Result:true
          Title:WN19-UR-000150 - Windows Server 2019 Load and unload device drivers user right must only be assigned to the Administrators group.
          Description:Inappropriate granting of user rights can provide system, administrative, and other high-level capabilities.

          The "Load and unload device drivers" user right allows a user to load device drivers dynamically on a system. This could be used by an attacker to install malicious code.
          Class:compliance
          Tests:
          • true (All child tests must be true. (AND))
            • true (All child tests must be true. (AND))
              • true (Load and unload device drivers - Administrators)
          Tests:
          Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:25349900 (accesstoken_test)
          Result:true
          Title:Load and unload device drivers - Administrators
          Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
          Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).
          Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:obj:20000009 (accesstoken_object)
          Object Comment:Administrators
          Object Requirements:
          • Collect any available items.
          Exclude Items If:
          • security_principle equals (case insensitive) 'Administrators'
          State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:25349900 (accesstoken_state)
          State Comment:Load and unload device drivers
          State Requirements:
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'seloaddriverprivilege' must be equal to '0'
          Collected Item/State Result:
          [ true ]
          • seloaddriverprivilege equals '1' for:
          • seloaddriverprivilege equals '0' for Replicator, NETWORK, Performance Monitor Users, Authenticated Users, SERVICE, ANONYMOUS LOGON, ELAM\Domain Admins, Performance Log Users, NT SERVICE\WdiServiceHost, Hyper-V Administrators, ENTERPRISE DOMAIN CONTROLLERS, RDS Endpoint Servers, RDS Management Servers, RDS Remote Access Servers, BATCH, PROXY, IUSR, NETWORK SERVICE, Print Operators, Remote Management Users, Access Control Assistance Operators, TERMINAL SERVER USER, Power Users, CREATOR GROUP, WDAGUtilityAccount, X_Admin, Local account, DefaultAccount, Device Owners, IIS_IUSRS, Users, Cryptographic Operators, CREATOR OWNER SERVER, SYSTEM, NT SERVICE\ALL SERVICES, Network Configuration Operators, CREATOR GROUP SERVER, System Managed Accounts Group, Certificate Service DCOM Access, Storage Replica Administrators, Backup Operators, Remote Desktop Users, CREATOR OWNER, LOCAL SERVICE, Visitor, Distributed COM Users, ELAM\Enterprise Admins, INTERACTIVE, Everyone, Event Log Readers, DIALUP, Guests

          V-205763 - Windows Server 2019 Lock pages in memory user right must not be assigned to any groups or accounts.

          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-205763r958726_rule
          Test Type:Automated
          Rule Result:Pass
          Version:WN19-UR-000160
          Identities:V-93077
          SV-103165
          CCI-002235 (NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: AC-6 (10); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: AC-6 (10))
          Description: Inappropriate granting of user rights can provide system, administrative, and other high-level capabilities.

          The "Lock pages in memory" user right allows physical memory to be assigned to processes, which could cause performance issues or a denial of service.
          Fix Text:Configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Windows Settings >> Security Settings >> Local Policies >> User Rights Assignment >> "Lock pages in memory" to be defined but containing no entries (blank).
          Severity:medium
          Weight:10.0
          Reference:
          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows Server 2019
          Publisher:DISA
          Type:DPMS Target
          Subject:Microsoft Windows Server 2019
          Identifier:2907
          Definitions:
          Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.windows2019:def:205763
          Definition Result:true
          Title:WN19-UR-000160 - Windows Server 2019 Lock pages in memory user right must not be assigned to any groups or accounts.
          Description:Inappropriate granting of user rights can provide system, administrative, and other high-level capabilities.

          The "Lock pages in memory" user right allows physical memory to be assigned to processes, which could cause performance issues or a denial of service.
          Class:compliance
          Tests:
          • true (All child tests must be true. (AND))
            • true (All child tests must be true. (AND))
              • true (Lock pages in memory - None)
          Tests:
          Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:25350000 (accesstoken_test)
          Result:true
          Title:Lock pages in memory - None
          Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
          Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).
          Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:obj:20000008 (accesstoken_object)
          Object Comment:All security principles
          Object Requirements:
          • check = 'security_principle' must match the pattern '.+'
          State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:25350000 (accesstoken_state)
          State Comment:Lock pages in memory
          State Requirements:
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'selockmemoryprivilege' must be equal to '0'
          Collected Item/State Result:
          [ true ]
          • selockmemoryprivilege equals '1' for:
          • selockmemoryprivilege equals '0' for Access Control Assistance Operators, Administrators, Backup Operators, Certificate Service DCOM Access, Cryptographic Operators, Device Owners, Distributed COM Users, Event Log Readers, Guests, Hyper-V Administrators, IIS_IUSRS, Network Configuration Operators, Performance Log Users, Performance Monitor Users, Power Users, Print Operators, RDS Endpoint Servers, RDS Management Servers, RDS Remote Access Servers, Remote Desktop Users, Remote Management Users, Replicator, Storage Replica Administrators, System Managed Accounts Group, Users, DefaultAccount, Visitor, WDAGUtilityAccount, X_Admin, NETWORK SERVICE, LOCAL SERVICE, SYSTEM, IUSR, TERMINAL SERVER USER, Authenticated Users, ENTERPRISE DOMAIN CONTROLLERS, PROXY, ANONYMOUS LOGON, SERVICE, INTERACTIVE, BATCH, NETWORK, DIALUP, CREATOR GROUP SERVER, CREATOR OWNER SERVER, CREATOR GROUP, CREATOR OWNER, Everyone, ELAM\Enterprise Admins, ELAM\Domain Admins, Local account, NT SERVICE\ALL SERVICES, NT SERVICE\WdiServiceHost

          V-205765 - Windows Server 2019 Perform volume maintenance tasks user right must only be assigned to the Administrators group.

          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-205765r958726_rule
          Test Type:Automated
          Rule Result:Pass
          Version:WN19-UR-000190
          Identities:V-93081
          SV-103169
          CCI-002235 (NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: AC-6 (10); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: AC-6 (10))
          Description: Inappropriate granting of user rights can provide system, administrative, and other high-level capabilities.

          Accounts with the "Perform volume maintenance tasks" user right can manage volume and disk configurations. This could be used to delete volumes, resulting in data loss or a denial of service.
          Fix Text:Configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Windows Settings >> Security Settings >> Local Policies >> User Rights Assignment >> "Perform volume maintenance tasks" to include only the following accounts or groups:

          V-205767 - Windows Server 2019 Restore files and directories user right must only be assigned to the Administrators group.

          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-205767r958726_rule
          Test Type:Automated
          Rule Result:Pass

          Identities:V-93085
          SV-103173
          CCI-002235 (NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: AC-6 (10); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: AC-6 (10))
          Description: Inappropriate granting of user rights can provide system, administrative, and other high-level capabilities.

          Accounts with the "Restore files and directories" user right can circumvent file and directory permissions and could allow access to sensitive data. It could also be used to overwrite more current data.

          V-205769 - Windows Server 2019 must be configured to audit Account Management - Other Account Management Events successes.

          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-205769r958732_rule
          Test Type:Automated
          Rule Result:Pass
          Version:WN19-AU-000090
          Identities:V-93089
          SV-103177
          CCI-000172 (NIST SP 800-53: AU-12 c; NIST SP 800-53A: AU-12.1 (iv); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: AU-12 c; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: AU-12 c)
          CCI-002234 (NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: AC-6 (9); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: AC-6 (9))
          Description: Maintaining an audit trail of system activity logs can help identify configuration errors, troubleshoot service disruptions, and analyze compromises that have occurred, as well as detect attacks. Audit logs are necessary to provide a trail of evidence in case the system or network is compromised. Collecting this data is essential for analyzing the security of information assets and detecting signs of suspicious and unexpected behavior.

          Other Account Management Events records events such as the access of a password hash or the Password Policy Checking API being called.

          Satisfies: SRG-OS-000327-GPOS-00127, SRG-OS-000064-GPOS-00033, SRG-OS-000462-GPOS-00206, SRG-OS-000466-GPOS-00210
          Fix Text:Configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Windows Settings >> Security Settings >> Advanced Audit Policy Configuration >> System Audit Policies >> Account Management >> "Audit Other Account Management Events" with "Success" selected.
          Severity:medium
          Weight:10.0
          Reference:
          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows Server 2019
          Publisher:DISA
          Type:DPMS Target
          Subject:Microsoft Windows Server 2019
          Identifier:2907
          Definitions:
          Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.windows2019:def:205769
          Definition Result:true
          Title:WN19-AU-000090 - Windows Server 2019 must be configured to audit Account Management - Other Account Management Events successes.
          Description:Maintaining an audit trail of system activity logs can help identify configuration errors, troubleshoot service disruptions, and analyze compromises that have occurred, as well as detect attacks. Audit logs are necessary to provide a trail of evidence in case the system or network is compromised. Collecting this data is essential for analyzing the security of information assets and detecting signs of suspicious and unexpected behavior.

          Other Account Management Events records events such as the access of a password hash or the Password Policy Checking API being called.

          Satisfies: SRG-OS-000327-GPOS-00127, SRG-OS-000064-GPOS-00033, SRG-OS-000462-GPOS-00206, SRG-OS-000466-GPOS-00210
          Class:compliance
          Tests:
          • true (All child tests must be true. (AND))
            • true (All child tests must be true. (AND))
              • true (Audit - Other Account Management Events - 'Success only' or 'Success and Failure')
          Tests:
          Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:25430200 (auditeventpolicysubcategories_test)
          Result:true
          Title:Audit - Other Account Management Events - 'Success only' or 'Success and Failure'
          Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
          Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).
          State Operator:One or more item-state comparisons may be true.
          Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:obj:20000000 (auditeventpolicysubcategories_object)
          Object Comment: 
          Object Requirements:
          • Collect any available items.
          State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:25430200 (auditeventpolicysubcategories_state)
          State Comment: 
          State Requirements:
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'other_account_management_events' must be equal to 'AUDIT_SUCCESS'
          State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:25430201 (auditeventpolicysubcategories_state)
          State Comment: 
          State Requirements:
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'other_account_management_events' must be equal to 'AUDIT_SUCCESS_FAILURE'
          Collected Item/State Result:
          [ true ]
          • credential_validation equals 'AUDIT_SUCCESS_FAILURE'

          V-205770 - Windows Server 2019 must be configured to audit Detailed Tracking - Process Creation successes.

          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-205770r958732_rule
          Test Type:Automated
          Rule Result:Pass
          Version:WN19-AU-000140
          Identities:V-93091
          SV-103179
          CCI-000172 (NIST SP 800-53: AU-12 c; NIST SP 800-53A: AU-12.1 (iv); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: AU-12 c; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: AU-12 c)
          CCI-002234 (NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: AC-6 (9); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: AC-6 (9))
          Description: Maintaining an audit trail of system activity logs can help identify configuration errors, troubleshoot service disruptions, and analyze compromises that have occurred, as well as detect attacks. Audit logs are necessary to provide a trail of evidence in case the system or network is compromised. Collecting this data is essential for analyzing the security of information assets and detecting signs of suspicious and unexpected behavior.

          Process Creation records events related to the creation of a process and the source.

          Satisfies: SRG-OS-000327-GPOS-00127, SRG-OS-000471-GPOS-00215
          Fix Text:Configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Windows Settings >> Security Settings >> Advanced Audit Policy Configuration >> System Audit Policies >> Detailed Tracking >> "Audit Process Creation" with "Success" selected.
          Severity:medium
          Weight:10.0
          Reference:
          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows Server 2019
          Publisher:DISA
          Type:DPMS Target
          Subject:Microsoft Windows Server 2019
          Identifier:2907
          Definitions:
          Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.windows2019:def:205770
          Definition Result:true
          Title:WN19-AU-000140 - Windows Server 2019 must be configured to audit Detailed Tracking - Process Creation successes.
          Description:Maintaining an audit trail of system activity logs can help identify configuration errors, troubleshoot service disruptions, and analyze compromises that have occurred, as well as detect attacks. Audit logs are necessary to provide a trail of evidence in case the system or network is compromised. Collecting this data is essential for analyzing the security of information assets and detecting signs of suspicious and unexpected behavior.

          Process Creation records events related to the creation of a process and the source.

Audit logs are necessary to provide a trail of evidence in case the system or network is compromised. Collecting this data is essential for analyzing the security of information assets and detecting signs of suspicious and unexpected behavior.

          Audit Policy Change records events related to changes in audit policy.

          V-205772 - Windows Server 2019 must be configured to audit Policy Change - Audit Policy Change failures.

          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-205772r958732_rule
          Test Type:Automated
          Rule Result:Pass
          Version:WN19-AU-000270
          Identities:SV-103183
          V-93095
          CCI-000172 (NIST SP 800-53: AU-12 c; NIST SP 800-53A: AU-12.1 (iv); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: AU-12 c; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: AU-12 c)
          CCI-002234 (NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: AC-6 (9); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: AC-6 (9))
          Description: Maintaining an audit trail of system activity logs can help identify configuration errors, troubleshoot service disruptions, and analyze compromises that have occurred, as well as detect attacks. Audit logs are necessary to provide a trail of evidence in case the system or network is compromised. Collecting this data is essential for analyzing the security of information assets and detecting signs of suspicious and unexpected behavior.

          Audit Policy Change records events related to changes in audit policy.

          Satisfies: SRG-OS-000327-GPOS-00127, SRG-OS-000458-GPOS-00203, SRG-OS-000463-GPOS-00207, SRG-OS-000468-GPOS-00212
          Fix Text:Configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Windows Settings >> Security Settings >> Advanced Audit Policy Configuration >> System Audit Policies >> Policy Change >> "Audit Audit Policy Change" with "Failure" selected.
          Severity:medium
          Weight:10.0
          Reference:
          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows Server 2019
          Publisher:DISA
          Type:DPMS Target
          Subject:Microsoft Windows Server 2019
          Identifier:2907
          Definitions:
          Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.windows2019:def:205772
          Definition Result:true
          Title:WN19-AU-000270 - Windows Server 2019 must be configured to audit Policy Change - Audit Policy Change failures.
          Description:Maintaining an audit trail of system activity logs can help identify configuration errors, troubleshoot service disruptions, and analyze compromises that have occurred, as well as detect attacks. Audit logs are necessary to provide a trail of evidence in case the system or network is compromised. Collecting this data is essential for analyzing the security of information assets and detecting signs of suspicious and unexpected behavior.

          Audit Policy Change records events related to changes in audit policy.

          Satisfies: SRG-OS-000327-GPOS-00127, SRG-OS-000458-GPOS-00203, SRG-OS-000463-GPOS-00207, SRG-OS-000468-GPOS-00212
          Class:compliance
          Tests:
          • true (All child tests must be true. (AND))
            • true (All child tests must be true. (AND))
              • true (Audit Policy Change - 'Failure only' or 'Success and Failure')
          Tests:
          Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:25432000 (auditeventpolicysubcategories_test)
          Result:true
          Title:Audit Policy Change - 'Failure only' or 'Success and Failure'
          Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
          Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).
          State Operator:One or more item-state comparisons may be true.
          Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:obj:20000000 (auditeventpolicysubcategories_object)
          Object Comment: 
          Object Requirements:
          • Collect any available items.
          State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:25332501 (auditeventpolicysubcategories_state)
          State Comment:'Audit Policy Change' is set to 'Success' and 'Failure'
          State Requirements:
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'audit_policy_change' must be equal to 'AUDIT_SUCCESS_FAILURE'
          State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:25432000 (auditeventpolicysubcategories_state)
          State Comment: 
          State Requirements:
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'audit_policy_change' must be equal to 'AUDIT_FAILURE'
          Collected Item/State Result:
          [ true ]
          • credential_validation equals 'AUDIT_SUCCESS_FAILURE'
          • kerberos_authentication_service equals 'AUDIT_NONE'
          • kerberos_service_ticket_operations equals 'AUDIT_NONE'

          V-205773 - Windows Server 2019 must be configured to audit Policy Change - Authentication Policy Change successes.

          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-205773r958732_rule
          Test Type:Automated
          Rule Result:Pass
          Version:WN19-AU-000280
          Identities:V-93097
          SV-103185
          CCI-000172 (NIST SP 800-53: AU-12 c; NIST SP 800-53A: AU-12.1 (iv); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: AU-12 c; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: AU-12 c)
          CCI-002234 (NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: AC-6 (9); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: AC-6 (9))
          Description: Maintaining an audit trail of system activity logs can help identify configuration errors, troubleshoot service disruptions, and analyze compromises that have occurred, as well as detect attacks. Audit logs are necessary to provide a trail of evidence in case the system or network is compromised. Collecting this data is essential for analyzing the security of information assets and detecting signs of suspicious and unexpected behavior.

          Authentication Policy Change records events related to changes in authentication policy, including Kerberos policy and Trust changes.

          Satisfies: SRG-OS-000327-GPOS-00127, SRG-OS-000064-GPOS-00033, SRG-OS-000462-GPOS-00206, SRG-OS-000466-GPOS-00210
          Fix Text:Configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Windows Settings >> Security Settings >> Advanced Audit Policy Configuration >> System Audit Policies >> Policy Change >> "Audit Authentication Policy Change" with "Success" selected.
          Severity:medium
          Weight:10.0
          Reference:
          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows Server 2019
          Publisher:DISA
          Type:DPMS Target
          Subject:Microsoft Windows Server 2019
          Identifier:2907
          Definitions:
          Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.windows2019:def:205773
          Definition Result:true

          Description:Maintaining an audit trail of system activity logs can help identify configuration errors, troubleshoot service disruptions, and analyze compromises that have occurred, as well as detect attacks. Audit logs are necessary to provide a trail of evidence in case the system or network is compromised. Collecting this data is essential for analyzing the security of information assets and detecting signs of suspicious and unexpected behavior.

          Authentication Policy Change records events related to changes in authentication policy, including Kerberos policy and Trust changes.

          Satisfies: SRG-OS-000327-GPOS-00127, SRG-OS-000064-GPOS-00033, SRG-OS-000462-GPOS-00206, SRG-OS-000466-GPOS-00210

          Tests:
          • true (All child tests must be true. (AND))

          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-205774r958732_rule

          CCI-000172 (NIST SP 800-53: AU-12 c; NIST SP 800-53A: AU-12.1 (iv); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: AU-12 c; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: AU-12 c)
          CCI-002234 (NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: AC-6 (9); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: AC-6 (9))
          Description: Maintaining an audit trail of system activity logs can help identify configuration errors, troubleshoot service disruptions, and analyze compromises that have occurred, as well as detect attacks. Audit logs are necessary to provide a trail of evidence in case the system or network is compromised. Collecting this data is essential for analyzing the security of information assets and detecting signs of suspicious and unexpected behavior.

          Authorization Policy Change records events related to changes in user rights, such as "Create a token object".

          Satisfies: SRG-OS-000327-GPOS-00127, SRG-OS-000064-GPOS-00033, SRG-OS-000462-GPOS-00206, SRG-OS-000466-GPOS-00210
          Fix Text:Configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Windows Settings >> Security Settings >> Advanced Audit Policy Configuration >> System Audit Policies >> Policy Change >> "Audit Authorization Policy Change" with "Success" selected.

          Weight:10.0

          Type:DPMS Target
          Subject:Microsoft Windows Server 2019
          Identifier:2907
          Definitions:

Collecting this data is essential for analyzing the security of information assets and detecting signs of suspicious and unexpected behavior.

          Authorization Policy Change records events related to changes in user rights, such as "Create a token object".

          • security_state_change equals 'AUDIT_SUCCESS'
          • security_system_extension equals 'AUDIT_SUCCESS'
          • system_integrity equals 'AUDIT_SUCCESS_FAILURE'
          • group_membership equals 'AUDIT_SUCCESS'
          • pnp_activity equals 'AUDIT_SUCCESS'
          • user_device_claims equals 'AUDIT_NONE'
          • audit_detailedtracking_tokenrightadjusted equals 'AUDIT_NONE'

          V-205775 - Windows Server 2019 must be configured to audit Privilege Use - Sensitive Privilege Use successes.

          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-205775r958732_rule
          Test Type:Automated
          Rule Result:Pass
          Version:WN19-AU-000300
          Identities:V-93101
          SV-103189
          CCI-000172 (NIST SP 800-53: AU-12 c; NIST SP 800-53A: AU-12.1 (iv); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: AU-12 c; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: AU-12 c)
          CCI-002234 (NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: AC-6 (9); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: AC-6 (9))
          Description: Maintaining an audit trail of system activity logs can help identify configuration errors, troubleshoot service disruptions, and analyze compromises that have occurred, as well as detect attacks. Audit logs are necessary to provide a trail of evidence in case the system or network is compromised. Collecting this data is essential for analyzing the security of information assets and detecting signs of suspicious and unexpected behavior.

          Sensitive Privilege Use records events related to use of sensitive privileges, such as "Act as part of the operating system" or "Debug programs".

          Satisfies: SRG-OS-000327-GPOS-00127, SRG-OS-000064-GPOS-00033, SRG-OS-000462-GPOS-00206, SRG-OS-000466-GPOS-00210
          Fix Text:Configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Windows Settings >> Security Settings >> Advanced Audit Policy Configuration >> System Audit Policies >> Privilege Use >> "Audit Sensitive Privilege Use" with "Success" selected.
          Severity:medium
          Weight:10.0
          Reference:
          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows Server 2019
          Publisher:DISA
          Type:DPMS Target
          Subject:Microsoft Windows Server 2019
          Identifier:2907
          Definitions:
          Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.windows2019:def:205775
          Definition Result:true
          Title:WN19-AU-000300 - Windows Server 2019 must be configured to audit Privilege Use - Sensitive Privilege Use successes.
          Description:Maintaining an audit trail of system activity logs can help identify configuration errors, troubleshoot service disruptions, and analyze compromises that have occurred, as well as detect attacks. Audit logs are necessary to provide a trail of evidence in case the system or network is compromised. Collecting this data is essential for analyzing the security of information assets and detecting signs of suspicious and unexpected behavior.

          Sensitive Privilege Use records events related to use of sensitive privileges, such as "Act as part of the operating system" or "Debug programs".

          Satisfies: SRG-OS-000327-GPOS-00127, SRG-OS-000064-GPOS-00033, SRG-OS-000462-GPOS-00206, SRG-OS-000466-GPOS-00210
          Class:compliance
          Tests:
          • true (All child tests must be true. (AND))
            • true (All child tests must be true. (AND))
              • true ('Sensitive Privilege Use' is set to 'Success' or 'Success and Failure')
          Tests:
          Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:25332900 (auditeventpolicysubcategories_test)
          Result:true
          Title:'Sensitive Privilege Use' is set to 'Success' or 'Success and Failure'
          Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
          Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).
          State Operator:One or more item-state comparisons may be true.

          • Collect any available items.

          V-205777 - Windows Server 2019 must be configured to audit System - IPsec Driver successes.

          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-205777r958732_rule
          Test Type:Automated
          Rule Result:Pass
          Version:WN19-AU-000320
          Identities:V-93105
          SV-103193
          CCI-000172 (NIST SP 800-53: AU-12 c; NIST SP 800-53A: AU-12.1 (iv); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: AU-12 c; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: AU-12 c)
          CCI-002234 (NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: AC-6 (9); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: AC-6 (9))
          Description: Maintaining an audit trail of system activity logs can help identify configuration errors, troubleshoot service disruptions, and analyze compromises that have occurred, as well as detect attacks. Audit logs are necessary to provide a trail of evidence in case the system or network is compromised. Collecting this data is essential for analyzing the security of information assets and detecting signs of suspicious and unexpected behavior.

          IPsec Driver records events related to the IPsec Driver, such as dropped packets.

          Satisfies: SRG-OS-000327-GPOS-00127, SRG-OS-000458-GPOS-00203, SRG-OS-000463-GPOS-00207, SRG-OS-000468-GPOS-00212
          Fix Text:Configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Windows Settings >> Security Settings >> Advanced Audit Policy Configuration >> System Audit Policies >> System >> "Audit IPsec Driver" with "Success" selected.
          Severity:medium
          Weight:10.0
          Reference:
          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows Server 2019
          Publisher:DISA
          Type:DPMS Target
          Subject:Microsoft Windows Server 2019
          Identifier:2907
          Definitions:

          V-205779 - Windows Server 2019 must be configured to audit System - Other System Events successes.

          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-205779r958732_rule
          Test Type:Automated
          Rule Result:Pass
          Version:WN19-AU-000340
          Identities:SV-103197
          V-93109
          CCI-000172 (NIST SP 800-53: AU-12 c; NIST SP 800-53A: AU-12.1 (iv); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: AU-12 c; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: AU-12 c)
          CCI-002234 (NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: AC-6 (9); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: AC-6 (9))
          Description: Maintaining an audit trail of system activity logs can help identify configuration errors, troubleshoot service disruptions, and analyze compromises that have occurred, as well as detect attacks. Audit logs are necessary to provide a trail of evidence in case the system or network is compromised. Collecting this data is essential for analyzing the security of information assets and detecting signs of suspicious and unexpected behavior.

          Audit Other System Events records information related to cryptographic key operations and the Windows Firewall service.

          Satisfies: SRG-OS-000327-GPOS-00127, SRG-OS-000458-GPOS-00203, SRG-OS-000463-GPOS-00207, SRG-OS-000468-GPOS-00212
          Fix Text:Configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Windows Settings >> Security Settings >> Advanced Audit Policy Configuration >> System Audit Policies >> System >> "Audit Other System Events" with "Success" selected.
          Severity:medium
          Weight:10.0
          Reference:
          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows Server 2019
          Publisher:DISA
          Type:DPMS Target
          Subject:Microsoft Windows Server 2019
          Identifier:2907
          Definitions:
          Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.windows2019:def:205779
          Definition Result:true
          Title:WN19-AU-000340 - Windows Server 2019 must be configured to audit System - Other System Events successes.
          Description:Maintaining an audit trail of system activity logs can help identify configuration errors, troubleshoot service disruptions, and analyze compromises that have occurred, as well as detect attacks. Audit logs are necessary to provide a trail of evidence in case the system or network is compromised. Collecting this data is essential for analyzing the security of information assets and detecting signs of suspicious and unexpected behavior.

          Audit Other System Events records information related to cryptographic key operations and the Windows Firewall service.

          Satisfies: SRG-OS-000327-GPOS-00127, SRG-OS-000458-GPOS-00203, SRG-OS-000463-GPOS-00207, SRG-OS-000468-GPOS-00212
          Class:compliance
          Tests:
          • true (All child tests must be true. (AND))
            • true (All child tests must be true. (AND))
              • true ('Other System Events' is set to 'Success' or 'Success and Failure')
          Tests:
          Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:25333100 (auditeventpolicysubcategories_test)
          Result:true
          Title:'Other System Events' is set to 'Success' or 'Success and Failure'
          Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
          Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).
          State Operator:One or more item-state comparisons may be true.
          Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:obj:20000000 (auditeventpolicysubcategories_object)
          Object Comment: 
          Object Requirements:
          • Collect any available items.
          State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:25333100 (auditeventpolicysubcategories_state)
          State Comment: 
          State Requirements:
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'other_system_events' must be equal to 'AUDIT_SUCCESS'
          State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:25333101 (auditeventpolicysubcategories_state)
          State Comment: 
          State Requirements:
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'other_system_events' must be equal to 'AUDIT_SUCCESS_FAILURE'

          [ true ]

          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-205781r958732_rule
          Test Type:Automated

          V-93113
          CCI-000172 (NIST SP 800-53: AU-12 c; NIST SP 800-53A: AU-12.1 (iv); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: AU-12 c; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: AU-12 c)
          CCI-002234 (NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: AC-6 (9); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: AC-6 (9))
          Description: Maintaining an audit trail of system activity logs can help identify configuration errors, troubleshoot service disruptions, and analyze compromises that have occurred, as well as detect attacks. Audit logs are necessary to provide a trail of evidence in case the system or network is compromised. Collecting this data is essential for analyzing the security of information assets and detecting signs of suspicious and unexpected behavior.

          Security State Change records events related to changes in the security state, such as startup and shutdown of the system.

          Satisfies: SRG-OS-000327-GPOS-00127, SRG-OS-000458-GPOS-00203, SRG-OS-000463-GPOS-00207, SRG-OS-000468-GPOS-00212
          Fix Text:Configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Windows Settings >> Security Settings >> Advanced Audit Policy Configuration >> System Audit Policies >> System >> "Audit Security State Change" with "Success" selected.
          Severity:medium
          Weight:10.0
          Reference:
          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows Server 2019
          Publisher:DISA
          Type:DPMS Target
          Subject:Microsoft Windows Server 2019
          Identifier:2907
          Definitions:
          Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.windows2019:def:205781
          Definition Result:true

          Description:Maintaining an audit trail of system activity logs can help identify configuration errors, troubleshoot service disruptions, and analyze compromises that have occurred, as well as detect attacks. Audit logs are necessary to provide a trail of evidence in case the system or network is compromised. Collecting this data is essential for analyzing the security of information assets and detecting signs of suspicious and unexpected behavior.

          Security State Change records events related to changes in the security state, such as startup and shutdown of the system.

              • false (Audit - Security State Change - Success and Failure)

          V-205782 - Windows Server 2019 must be configured to audit System - Security System Extension successes.

          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-205782r958732_rule
          Test Type:Automated
          Rule Result:Pass
          Version:WN19-AU-000370
          Identities:V-93115
          SV-103203
          CCI-000172 (NIST SP 800-53: AU-12 c; NIST SP 800-53A: AU-12.1 (iv); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: AU-12 c; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: AU-12 c)
          CCI-002234 (NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: AC-6 (9); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: AC-6 (9))
          Description: Maintaining an audit trail of system activity logs can help identify configuration errors, troubleshoot service disruptions, and analyze compromises that have occurred, as well as detect attacks. Audit logs are necessary to provide a trail of evidence in case the system or network is compromised. Collecting this data is essential for analyzing the security of information assets and detecting signs of suspicious and unexpected behavior.

          Security System Extension records events related to extension code being loaded by the security subsystem.

          Satisfies: SRG-OS-000327-GPOS-00127, SRG-OS-000458-GPOS-00203, SRG-OS-000463-GPOS-00207, SRG-OS-000468-GPOS-00212
          Fix Text:Configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Windows Settings >> Security Settings >> Advanced Audit Policy Configuration >> System Audit Policies >> System >> "Audit Security System Extension" with "Success" selected.
          Severity:medium
          Weight:10.0
          Reference:
          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows Server 2019
          Publisher:DISA
          Type:DPMS Target
          Subject:Microsoft Windows Server 2019
          Identifier:2907
          Definitions:
          Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.windows2019:def:205782
          Definition Result:true
          Title:WN19-AU-000370 - Windows Server 2019 must be configured to audit System - Security System Extension successes.
          Description:Maintaining an audit trail of system activity logs can help identify configuration errors, troubleshoot service disruptions, and analyze compromises that have occurred, as well as detect attacks. Audit logs are necessary to provide a trail of evidence in case the system or network is compromised. Collecting this data is essential for analyzing the security of information assets and detecting signs of suspicious and unexpected behavior.

          Security System Extension records events related to extension code being loaded by the security subsystem.

          Satisfies: SRG-OS-000327-GPOS-00127, SRG-OS-000458-GPOS-00203, SRG-OS-000463-GPOS-00207, SRG-OS-000468-GPOS-00212
          Class:compliance
          Tests:
          • true (All child tests must be true. (AND))
            • true (One or more child tests must be true. (OR))
              • true ('Audit Security System Extension' is set to 'Success')
              • false ('Audit Security System Extension' is set to 'Success' and 'Failure')
          Tests:
          Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:25333400 (auditeventpolicysubcategories_test)
          Result:true
          Title:'Audit Security System Extension' is set to 'Success'
          Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
          Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).
          Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:obj:20000000 (auditeventpolicysubcategories_object)
          Object Comment: 
          Object Requirements:
          • Collect any available items.

          State Comment:'Security System Extension' is set to 'Success'
          State Requirements:
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'security_system_extension' must be equal to 'AUDIT_SUCCESS'

          V-205783 - Windows Server 2019 must be configured to audit System - System Integrity successes.

          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-205783r958732_rule
          Test Type:Automated
          Rule Result:Pass
          Version:WN19-AU-000380
          Identities:V-93117
          SV-103205
          CCI-000172 (NIST SP 800-53: AU-12 c; NIST SP 800-53A: AU-12.1 (iv); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: AU-12 c; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: AU-12 c)
          CCI-002234 (NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: AC-6 (9); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: AC-6 (9))
          Description: Maintaining an audit trail of system activity logs can help identify configuration errors, troubleshoot service disruptions, and analyze compromises that have occurred, as well as detect attacks. Audit logs are necessary to provide a trail of evidence in case the system or network is compromised. Collecting this data is essential for analyzing the security of information assets and detecting signs of suspicious and unexpected behavior.

          System Integrity records events related to violations of integrity to the security subsystem.

          Satisfies: SRG-OS-000327-GPOS-00127, SRG-OS-000471-GPOS-00215, SRG-OS-000471-GPOS-00216, SRG-OS-000477-GPOS-00222
          Fix Text:Configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Windows Settings >> Security Settings >> Advanced Audit Policy Configuration >> System Audit Policies >> System >> "Audit System Integrity" with "Success" selected.
          Severity:medium
          Weight:10.0
          Reference:
          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows Server 2019
          Publisher:DISA
          Type:DPMS Target
          Subject:Microsoft Windows Server 2019
          Identifier:2907
          Definitions:
          Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.windows2019:def:205783
          Definition Result:true
          Title:WN19-AU-000380 - Windows Server 2019 must be configured to audit System - System Integrity successes.
          Description:Maintaining an audit trail of system activity logs can help identify configuration errors, troubleshoot service disruptions, and analyze compromises that have occurred, as well as detect attacks. Audit logs are necessary to provide a trail of evidence in case the system or network is compromised. Collecting this data is essential for analyzing the security of information assets and detecting signs of suspicious and unexpected behavior.

          System Integrity records events related to violations of integrity to the security subsystem.

          Satisfies: SRG-OS-000327-GPOS-00127, SRG-OS-000471-GPOS-00215, SRG-OS-000471-GPOS-00216, SRG-OS-000477-GPOS-00222
          Class:compliance

          • true (All child tests must be true. (AND))

              • true (Audit - System Integrity - Success only)

          CCI-002234 (NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: AC-6 (9); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: AC-6 (9))
          Description: Maintaining an audit trail of system activity logs can help identify configuration errors, troubleshoot service disruptions, and analyze compromises that have occurred, as well as detect attacks. Audit logs are necessary to provide a trail of evidence in case the system or network is compromised. Collecting this data is essential for analyzing the security of information assets and detecting signs of suspicious and unexpected behavior.

          System Integrity records events related to violations of integrity to the security subsystem.

          Satisfies: SRG-OS-000327-GPOS-00127, SRG-OS-000471-GPOS-00215, SRG-OS-000471-GPOS-00216, SRG-OS-000477-GPOS-00222
