          Description:Maintaining an audit trail of system activity logs can help identify configuration errors, troubleshoot service disruptions, and analyze compromises that have occurred, as well as detect attacks. Audit logs are necessary to provide a trail of evidence in case the system or network is compromised. Collecting this data is essential for analyzing the security of information assets and detecting signs of suspicious and unexpected behavior.

          System Integrity records events related to violations of integrity to the security subsystem.

              • true (Audit - System Integrity - Success and Failure)

          V-205791 - Windows Server 2019 must be configured to audit DS Access - Directory Service Access successes.

          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-205791r958732_rule
          Test Type:Automated
          Rule Applicability:Windows_DomainController
          Rule Result:Not Applicable
          Version:WN19-DC-000240
          Identities:SV-103221
          V-93133
          CCI-000172 (NIST SP 800-53: AU-12 c; NIST SP 800-53A: AU-12.1 (iv); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: AU-12 c; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: AU-12 c)
          CCI-002234 (NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: AC-6 (9); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: AC-6 (9))
          Description: Maintaining an audit trail of system activity logs can help identify configuration errors, troubleshoot service disruptions, and analyze compromises that have occurred, as well as detect attacks. Audit logs are necessary to provide a trail of evidence in case the system or network is compromised. Collecting this data is essential for analyzing the security of information assets and detecting signs of suspicious and unexpected behavior.

          Audit Directory Service Access records events related to users accessing an Active Directory object.

          Satisfies: SRG-OS-000327-GPOS-00127, SRG-OS-000458-GPOS-00203, SRG-OS-000463-GPOS-00207, SRG-OS-000468-GPOS-00212
          Fix Text:Configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Windows Settings >> Security Settings >> Advanced Audit Policy Configuration >> System Audit Policies >> DS Access >> "Directory Service Access" with "Success" selected.
          Severity:medium
          Weight:10.0
          Reference:
          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows Server 2019
          Publisher:DISA
          Type:DPMS Target
          Subject:Microsoft Windows Server 2019
          Identifier:2907

          V-205792 - Windows Server 2019 must be configured to audit DS Access - Directory Service Access failures.

          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-205792r958732_rule
          Test Type:Automated
          Rule Applicability:Windows_DomainController
          Rule Result:Not Applicable
          Version:WN19-DC-000250
          Identities:SV-103223
          V-93135
          CCI-000172 (NIST SP 800-53: AU-12 c; NIST SP 800-53A: AU-12.1 (iv); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: AU-12 c; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: AU-12 c)
          CCI-002234 (NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: AC-6 (9); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: AC-6 (9))
          Description: Maintaining an audit trail of system activity logs can help identify configuration errors, troubleshoot service disruptions, and analyze compromises that have occurred, as well as detect attacks. Audit logs are necessary to provide a trail of evidence in case the system or network is compromised. Collecting this data is essential for analyzing the security of information assets and detecting signs of suspicious and unexpected behavior.

          Audit Directory Service Access records events related to users accessing an Active Directory object.

          Satisfies: SRG-OS-000327-GPOS-00127, SRG-OS-000458-GPOS-00203, SRG-OS-000463-GPOS-00207, SRG-OS-000468-GPOS-00212
          Fix Text:Configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Windows Settings >> Security Settings >> Advanced Audit Policy Configuration >> System Audit Policies >> DS Access >> "Directory Service Access" with "Failure" selected.
          Severity:medium
          Weight:10.0
          Reference:
          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows Server 2019
          Publisher:DISA
          Type:DPMS Target
          Subject:Microsoft Windows Server 2019
          Identifier:2907

          V-205793 - Windows Server 2019 must be configured to audit DS Access - Directory Service Changes successes.

          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-205793r958732_rule
          Test Type:Automated
          Rule Applicability:Windows_DomainController
          Rule Result:Not Applicable
          Version:WN19-DC-000260
          Identities:V-93137
          SV-103225
          CCI-000172 (NIST SP 800-53: AU-12 c; NIST SP 800-53A: AU-12.1 (iv); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: AU-12 c; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: AU-12 c)
          CCI-002234 (NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: AC-6 (9); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: AC-6 (9))
          Description: Maintaining an audit trail of system activity logs can help identify configuration errors, troubleshoot service disruptions, and analyze compromises that have occurred, as well as detect attacks. Audit logs are necessary to provide a trail of evidence in case the system or network is compromised. Collecting this data is essential for analyzing the security of information assets and detecting signs of suspicious and unexpected behavior.

          Audit Directory Service Changes records events related to changes made to objects in Active Directory Domain Services.

          Satisfies: SRG-OS-000327-GPOS-00127, SRG-OS-000458-GPOS-00203, SRG-OS-000463-GPOS-00207, SRG-OS-000468-GPOS-00212
          Fix Text:Configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Windows Settings >> Security Settings >> Advanced Audit Policy Configuration >> System Audit Policies >> DS Access >> "Directory Service Changes" with "Success" selected.
          Severity:medium
          Weight:10.0
          Reference:
          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows Server 2019
          Publisher:DISA
          Type:DPMS Target
          Subject:Microsoft Windows Server 2019
          Identifier:2907

          V-205795 - Windows Server 2019 account lockout duration must be configured to 15 minutes or greater.

          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-205795r958736_rule
          Test Type:Automated
          Rule Result:Pass
          Version:WN19-AC-000010
          Identities:V-93145
          SV-103233
          CCI-002238 (NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: AC-7 b; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: AC-7 b)
          Description: The account lockout feature, when enabled, prevents brute-force password attacks on the system. This parameter specifies the period of time that an account will remain locked after the specified number of failed logon attempts.
          Fix Text:Configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Windows Settings >> Security Settings >> Account Policies >> Account Lockout Policy >> "Account lockout duration" to "15" minutes or greater.

          A value of "0" is also acceptable, requiring an administrator to unlock the account.
          Severity:medium
          Weight:10.0
          Reference:
          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows Server 2019
          Publisher:DISA
          Type:DPMS Target
          Subject:Microsoft Windows Server 2019
          Identifier:2907
          Definitions:
          Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.windows2019:def:205795
          Definition Result:true
          Title:WN19-AC-000010 - Windows Server 2019 account lockout duration must be configured to 15 minutes or greater.
          Description:The account lockout feature, when enabled, prevents brute-force password attacks on the system. This parameter specifies the period of time that an account will remain locked after the specified number of failed logon attempts.
          Class:compliance
          Tests:
          • true (All child tests must be true. (AND))
            • true (All child tests must be true. (AND))
              • true (One or more child tests must be true. (OR))
                • false ('Account lockout duration' is set to '0')
                • true ('Account lockout duration' is set to '15 minutes' or greater)
              • true ('Account lockout threshold' is not set to '0')
          Tests:
          Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:25329700 (lockoutpolicy_test)
          Result:false
          Title:'Account lockout duration' is set to '0'
          Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
          Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).
          State Operator:One or more item-state comparisons may be true.
          Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:obj:20000001 (lockoutpolicy_object)
          Object Comment: 
          Object Requirements:
          • Collect any available items.
          State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:25329700 (lockoutpolicy_state)
          State Comment:Lockout Duration equals -1
          State Requirements:
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'lockout_duration' must be less than or equal to '0'
          State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:25329701 (lockoutpolicy_state)
          State Comment:Lockout Duration equals 4294967295
          State Requirements:
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'lockout_duration' must be equal to '4294967295'
          Collected Item/State Result:
          [ false ]
          • force_logoff equals '1'
          • lockout_duration equals '900'
          • lockout_observation_window equals '900'
          • lockout_threshold equals '3'
          Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:25329701 (lockoutpolicy_test)
          Result:true
          Title:'Account lockout duration' is set to '15 minutes' or greater
          Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
          Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).
          Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:obj:20000001 (lockoutpolicy_object)
          Object Comment: 
          Object Requirements:
          • Collect any available items.
          State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:25329702 (lockoutpolicy_state)
          State Comment:Lockout Duration greater than or equals 900
          State Requirements:
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'lockout_duration' must be greater than or equal to '900'
          Collected Item/State Result:
          [ true ]
          • force_logoff equals '1'
          • lockout_duration equals '900'
          • lockout_observation_window equals '900'
          • lockout_threshold equals '3'
          Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:25329801 (lockoutpolicy_test)
          Result:true
          Title:'Account lockout threshold' is not set to '0'
          Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
          Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).
          Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:obj:20000001 (lockoutpolicy_object)
          Object Comment: 
          Object Requirements:
          • Collect any available items.
          State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:25329801 (lockoutpolicy_state)
          State Comment:Lockout Threshold is greater than 0
          State Requirements:
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'lockout_threshold' must be greater than '0'
          Collected Item/State Result:
          [ true ]
          • force_logoff equals '1'
          • lockout_duration equals '900'
          • lockout_observation_window equals '900'
          • lockout_threshold equals '3'

          V-205796 - Windows Server 2019 Application event log size must be configured to 32768 KB or greater.

          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-205796r958752_rule
          Test Type:Automated
          Rule Result:Pass
          Version:WN19-CC-000270
          Identities:SV-103265
          V-93177
          CCI-001849 (NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: AU-4; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: AU-4)
          Description: Inadequate log size will cause the log to fill up quickly. This may prevent audit events from being recorded properly and require frequent attention by administrative personnel.
          Fix Text:Configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Administrative Templates >> Windows Components >> Event Log Service >> Application >> "Specify the maximum log file size (KB)" to "Enabled" with a "Maximum Log Size (KB)" of "32768" or greater.
          Severity:medium
          Weight:10.0
          Reference:
          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows Server 2019
          Publisher:DISA
          Type:DPMS Target
          Subject:Microsoft Windows Server 2019
          Identifier:2907
          Definitions:
          Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.windows2019:def:205796
          Definition Result:true
          Title:WN19-CC-000270 - Windows Server 2019 Application event log size must be configured to 32768 KB or greater.
          Description:Inadequate log size will cause the log to fill up quickly. This may prevent audit events from being recorded properly and require frequent attention by administrative personnel.
          Class:compliance
          Tests:
          • true (All child tests must be true. (AND))
            • true (All child tests must be true. (AND))
              • true (The Application event log size is configured to 32768 KB or greater)
          Tests:
          Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:25333700 (registry_test)
          Result:true
          Title:The Application event log size is configured to 32768 KB or greater
          Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
          Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).
          Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:obj:25333700 (registry_object)
          Object Comment: 
          Object Requirements:
          • check = 'hive' must be equal (case insensitive) to 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE'
          • check = 'key' must be equal (case insensitive) to 'SOFTWARE\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\EventLog\Application'
          • check = 'name' must be equal (case insensitive) to 'MaxSize'
          State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:25333700 (registry_state)
          State Comment: 
          State Requirements:
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'type' must be equal to 'reg_dword'
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'value' must be greater than or equal to '32768'
          Collected Item/State Result:
          [ true ]
          • hive equals 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE'
          • key equals 'SOFTWARE\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\EventLog\Application'
          • name equals 'MaxSize'
          • last_write_time equals '134213413420000000'
          • type equals 'reg_dword'
          • value equals '32768'
          • windows_view equals '64_bit'

          V-205797 - Windows Server 2019 security event log size must be configured to a value that holds at least one week's worth of audit records.

          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-205797r1186384_rule
          Test Type:Automated
          Rule Result:Pass
          Version:WN19-CC-000280
          Identities:SV-103267
          V-93179
          CCI-001849 (NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: AU-4; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: AU-4)
          Description: Inadequate log size will cause the log to fill up quickly. This may prevent audit events from being recorded properly and require frequent attention by administrative personnel. System administrators (SAs) must monitor the volume of security event audit records and increase the registry value for the log size as needed.
          Fix Text:Configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Administrative Templates >> Windows Components >> Event Log Service >> Security >> "Specify the maximum log file size (KB)" to "Enabled" with a "Maximum Log Size (KB)" of a value that will contain one week of audit records or greater.
          Severity:medium
          Weight:10.0
          Reference:
          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows Server 2019
          Publisher:DISA
          Type:DPMS Target
          Subject:Microsoft Windows Server 2019
          Identifier:2907
          Definitions:
          Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.windows2019:def:205797
          Definition Result:true
          Title:WN19-CC-000280 - Windows Server 2019 security event log size must be configured to a value that holds at least one week's worth of audit records.
          Description:Inadequate log size will cause the log to fill up quickly. This may prevent audit events from being recorded properly and require frequent attention by administrative personnel. System administrators (SAs) must monitor the volume of security event audit records and increase the registry value for the log size as needed.
          Class:compliance
          Tests:
          • true (All child tests must be true. (AND))
            • true (All child tests must be true. (AND))
              • true (The Security event log size is configured to 5120000 KB or greater)
          Tests:
          Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:25333800 (registry_test)
          Result:true
          Title:The Security event log size is configured to 5120000 KB or greater
          Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
          Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).
          Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:obj:25333800 (registry_object)
          Object Comment: 
          Object Requirements:
          • check = 'hive' must be equal (case insensitive) to 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE'
          • check = 'key' must be equal (case insensitive) to 'SOFTWARE\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\EventLog\Security'
          • check = 'name' must be equal (case insensitive) to 'MaxSize'
          State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:25333800 (registry_state)
          State Comment: 
          State Requirements:
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'type' must be equal to 'reg_dword'
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'value' must be greater than or equal to '5120000'
          Collected Item/State Result:
          [ true ]
          • hive equals 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE'
          • key equals 'SOFTWARE\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\EventLog\Security'
          • name equals 'MaxSize'
          • last_write_time equals '134213413420000000'
          • type equals 'reg_dword'
          • value equals '5120000'
          • windows_view equals '64_bit'

          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-205798r958752_rule

          Object Comment:

          • check = 'hive' must be equal (case insensitive) to 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE'
          • check = 'key' must be equal (case insensitive) to 'SOFTWARE\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\EventLog\System'
          • check = 'name' must be equal (case insensitive) to 'MaxSize'
          State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:25333700 (registry_state)

          [ true ]

          • name equals 'MaxSize'
          • last_write_time equals '134213413420000000'

          V-205801 - Windows Server 2019 must prevent users from changing installation options.

          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-205801r1051078_rule

          CCI-003980 (NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: CM-11 (2))
          CCI-001812 (NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: CM-11 (2))
          Description: Installation options for applications are typically controlled by administrators. This setting prevents users from changing installation options that may bypass security features.
          Fix Text:Configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Administrative Templates >> Windows Components >> Windows Installer >> "Allow user control over installs" to "Disabled".

          V-205808 - Windows Server 2019 must not save passwords in the Remote Desktop Client.

          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-205808r1051080_rule
          Test Type:Automated
          Rule Result:Pass
          Version:WN19-CC-000340
          Identities:V-93425
          SV-103511
          CCI-002038 (NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: IA-11; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: IA-11)
          Description: Saving passwords in the Remote Desktop Client could allow an unauthorized user to establish a remote desktop session to another system. The system must be configured to prevent users from saving passwords in the Remote Desktop Client.

          Satisfies: SRG-OS-000373-GPOS-00157, SRG-OS-000373-GPOS-00156
          Fix Text:Configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Administrative Templates >> Windows Components >> Remote Desktop Services >> Remote Desktop Connection Client >> "Do not allow passwords to be saved" to "Enabled".
          Severity:medium
          Weight:10.0
          Reference:
          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows Server 2019
          Publisher:DISA

          Identifier:2907

          Satisfies: SRG-OS-000373-GPOS-00157, SRG-OS-000373-GPOS-00156
          Class:compliance

          • true (All child tests must be true. (AND))

          Title:'Do not allow passwords to be saved' is set to 'Enabled'
          Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
          Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).

          Object Comment:

          • check = 'key' must be equal to 'SOFTWARE\Policies\Microsoft\Windows NT\Terminal Services'
          • check = 'name' must be equal to 'DisablePasswordSaving'

          V-205809 - Windows Server 2019 Remote Desktop Services must always prompt a client for passwords upon connection.

          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-205809r1051081_rule
          Test Type:Automated
          Rule Result:Pass
          Version:WN19-CC-000360
          Identities:V-93427
          SV-103513
          CCI-002038 (NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: IA-11; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: IA-11)
          Description: This setting controls the ability of users to supply passwords automatically as part of their remote desktop connection. Disabling this setting would allow anyone to use the stored credentials in a connection item to connect to the terminal server.

          Satisfies: SRG-OS-000373-GPOS-00157, SRG-OS-000373-GPOS-00156
          Fix Text:Configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Administrative Templates >> Windows Components >> Remote Desktop Services >> Remote Desktop Session Host >> Security >> "Always prompt for password upon connection" to "Enabled".
          Severity:medium
          Weight:10.0

          V-205811 - Windows Server 2019 User Account Control approval mode for the built-in Administrator must be enabled.

          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-205811r1051083_rule
          Test Type:Automated
          Rule Result:Pass
          Version:WN19-SO-000380
          Identities:SV-103517
          V-93431
          CCI-002038 (NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: IA-11; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: IA-11)
          Description: User Account Control (UAC) is a security mechanism for limiting the elevation of privileges, including administrative accounts, unless authorized. This setting configures the built-in Administrator account so that it runs in Admin Approval Mode.

          Satisfies: SRG-OS-000373-GPOS-00157, SRG-OS-000373-GPOS-00156
          Fix Text:Configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Windows Settings >> Security Settings >> Local Policies >> Security Options >> "User Account Control: Admin Approval Mode for the Built-in Administrator account" to "Enabled".
          Severity:medium
          Weight:10.0
          Reference:
          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows Server 2019
          Publisher:DISA
          Type:DPMS Target
          Subject:Microsoft Windows Server 2019
          Identifier:2907
          Definitions:
          Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.windows2019:def:205811
          Definition Result:true
          Title:WN19-SO-000380 - Windows Server 2019 User Account Control approval mode for the built-in Administrator must be enabled.
          Description:User Account Control (UAC) is a security mechanism for limiting the elevation of privileges, including administrative accounts, unless authorized. This setting configures the built-in Administrator account so that it runs in Admin Approval Mode.

          Satisfies: SRG-OS-000373-GPOS-00157, SRG-OS-000373-GPOS-00156
          Class:compliance
          Tests:
          • true (All child tests must be true. (AND))
            • true (All child tests must be true. (AND))

          Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:25346800 (registry_test)
          Result:true
          Title:Verifies 'User Account Control: Admin Approval Mode for the Built-in Administrator account' is set to 'Enabled'

          Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).
          Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:obj:25346800 (registry_object)

          • check = 'key' must be equal to 'Software\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Policies\System'
          • check = 'name' must be equal to 'FilterAdministratorToken'
          State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:20000001 (registry_state)
          State Comment:

          V-205812 - Windows Server 2019 User Account Control must automatically deny standard user requests for elevation.

          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-205812r1051084_rule
          Test Type:Automated
          Rule Result:Pass
          Version:WN19-SO-000410
          Identities:V-93433
          SV-103519
          CCI-002038 (NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: IA-11; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: IA-11)
          Description: User Account Control (UAC) is a security mechanism for limiting the elevation of privileges, including administrative accounts, unless authorized. This setting controls the behavior of elevation when requested by a standard user account.

          V-205814 - Windows Server 2019 must restrict unauthenticated Remote Procedure Call (RPC) clients from connecting to the RPC server on domain-joined member servers and standalone or nondomain-joined systems.

          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-205814r971545_rule
          Test Type:Automated
          Rule Applicability:Windows_MemberServer
          Rule Applicability:Windows_StandaloneServer
          Rule Result:Pass
          Version:WN19-MS-000040
          Identities:V-93453
          SV-103539
          CCI-001967 (NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: IA-3 (1); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: IA-3 (1))
          Description: Unauthenticated RPC clients may allow anonymous access to sensitive information. Configuring RPC to restrict unauthenticated RPC clients from connecting to the RPC server will prevent anonymous connections.
          Fix Text:Configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Administrative Templates >> System >> Remote Procedure Call >> "Restrict Unauthenticated RPC clients" to "Enabled" with "Authenticated" selected.
          Severity:medium
          Weight:10.0
          Reference:
          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows Server 2019
          Publisher:DISA

            • true (All child tests must be true. (AND))

          Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
          Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).
          Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:obj:25338300 (registry_object)
          Object Comment: 
          Object Requirements:
          • check = 'hive' must be equal to 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE'

          V-205815 - Windows Server 2019 computer account password must not be prevented from being reset.

          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-205815r971545_rule
          Test Type:Automated
          Rule Result:Pass
          Version:WN19-SO-000090
          Identities:V-93455
          SV-103541
          CCI-001967 (NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: IA-3 (1); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: IA-3 (1))
          Description: Computer account passwords are changed automatically on a regular basis. Disabling automatic password changes can make the system more vulnerable to malicious access. Frequent password changes can be a significant safeguard for the system. A new password for the computer account will be generated every 30 days.
          Fix Text:Configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Windows Settings >> Security Settings >> Local Policies >> Security Options >> "Domain member: Disable machine account password changes" to "Disabled".
          Severity:medium
          Weight:10.0
          Reference:
          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows Server 2019
          Publisher:DISA
          Type:DPMS Target
          Subject:Microsoft Windows Server 2019
          Identifier:2907
          Definitions:
          Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.windows2019:def:205815
          Definition Result:true
          Title:WN19-SO-000090 - Windows Server 2019 computer account password must not be prevented from being reset.
          Description:Computer account passwords are changed automatically on a regular basis. Disabling automatic password changes can make the system more vulnerable to malicious access. Frequent password changes can be a significant safeguard for the system. A new password for the computer account will be generated every 30 days.
          Class:compliance
          Tests:
          • true (All child tests must be true. (AND))
            • true (All child tests must be true. (AND))
              • true ('Domain member: Disable machine account password changes' is set to 'Disabled')
          Tests:
          Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:25344100 (registry_test)
          Result:true
          Title:'Domain member: Disable machine account password changes' is set to 'Disabled'
          Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
          Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).
          Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:obj:25344100 (registry_object)
          Object Comment: 
          Object Requirements:
          • check = 'hive' must be equal to 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE'
          • check = 'key' must be equal to 'SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Services\Netlogon\Parameters'
          • check = 'name' must be equal to 'DisablePasswordChange'
          State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:20000000 (registry_state)
          State Comment: 
          State Requirements:
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'type' must be equal to 'reg_dword'
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'value' must be equal to '0'
          Collected Item/State Result:
          [ true ]
          • hive equals 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE'
          • key equals 'SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Services\Netlogon\Parameters'
          • name equals 'DisablePasswordChange'
          • last_write_time equals '134212706400000000'
          • type equals 'reg_dword'
          • value equals '0'
          • windows_view equals '64_bit'

          V-205816 - Windows Server 2019 Windows Remote Management (WinRM) client must not allow unencrypted traffic.

          CCI-003123 (NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: MA-4 (6); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: MA-4 (6))
          Description: Unencrypted remote access to a system can allow sensitive information to be compromised. Windows remote management connections must be encrypted to prevent this.

          Fix Text:Configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Administrative Templates >> Windows Components >> Windows Remote Management (WinRM) >> WinRM Client >> "Allow unencrypted traffic" to "Disabled".

          V-205820 - Windows Server 2019 domain controllers must require LDAP access signing.

          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-205820r958908_rule
          Test Type:Automated
          Rule Applicability:Windows_DomainController
          Rule Result:Not Applicable
          Version:WN19-DC-000320
          Identities:V-93545
          SV-103631
          CCI-002418 (NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: SC-8; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: SC-8)
          CCI-002421 (NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: SC-8 (1); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: SC-8 (1))
          Description: Unsigned network traffic is susceptible to man-in-the-middle attacks, where an intruder captures packets between the server and the client and modifies them before forwarding them to the client. In the case of an LDAP server, this means that an attacker could cause a client to make decisions based on false records from the LDAP directory. The risk of an attacker pulling this off can be decreased by implementing strong physical security measures to protect the network infrastructure. Furthermore, implementing Internet Protocol security (IPsec) authentication header mode (AH), which performs mutual authentication and packet integrity for Internet Protocol (IP) traffic, can make all types of man-in-the-middle attacks extremely difficult.

          Satisfies: SRG-OS-000423-GPOS-00187, SRG-OS-000424-GPOS-00188
          Fix Text:Configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Windows Settings >> Security Settings >> Local Policies >> Security Options >> "Domain controller: LDAP server signing requirements" to "Require signing".
          Severity:medium
          Weight:10.0
          Reference:
          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows Server 2019
          Publisher:DISA
          Type:DPMS Target
          Subject:Microsoft Windows Server 2019
          Identifier:2907

          V-205821 - Windows Server 2019 setting Domain member: Digitally encrypt or sign secure channel data (always) must be configured to Enabled.

          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-205821r958908_rule
          Test Type:Automated
          Rule Result:Pass
          Version:WN19-SO-000060
          Identities:V-93547
          SV-103633
          CCI-002418 (NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: SC-8; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: SC-8)
          CCI-002421 (NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: SC-8 (1); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: SC-8 (1))
          Description: Requests sent on the secure channel are authenticated, and sensitive information (such as passwords) is encrypted, but not all information is encrypted. If this policy is enabled, outgoing secure channel traffic will be encrypted and signed.

          Satisfies: SRG-OS-000423-GPOS-00187, SRG-OS-000424-GPOS-00188
          Fix Text:Configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Windows Settings >> Security Settings >> Local Policies >> Security Options >> "Domain member: Digitally encrypt or sign secure channel data (always)" to "Enabled".
          Severity:medium
          Weight:10.0
          Reference:
          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows Server 2019
          Publisher:DISA
          Type:DPMS Target
          Subject:Microsoft Windows Server 2019
          Identifier:2907

          Definition Result:true

          Satisfies: SRG-OS-000423-GPOS-00187, SRG-OS-000424-GPOS-00188

          Tests:
          • true (All child tests must be true. (AND))
            • true (All child tests must be true. (AND))
              • true ('Domain member: Digitally encrypt or sign secure channel data (always)' is set to 'Enabled')
          Tests:
          Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:25343800 (registry_test)
          Result:true
          Title:'Domain member: Digitally encrypt or sign secure channel data (always)' is set to 'Enabled'
          Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
          Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).

          • check = 'key' must be equal to 'SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Services\Netlogon\Parameters'
          • check = 'name' must be equal to 'RequireSignOrSeal'

          V-205824 - Windows Server 2019 must be configured to require a strong session key.

          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-205824r958908_rule
          Test Type:Automated
          Rule Result:Pass
          Version:WN19-SO-000110
          Identities:SV-103639
          V-93553
          CCI-002418 (NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: SC-8; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: SC-8)
          CCI-002421 (NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: SC-8 (1); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: SC-8 (1))
          Description: A computer connecting to a domain controller will establish a secure channel. The secure channel connection may be subject to compromise, such as hijacking or eavesdropping, if strong session keys are not used to establish the connection. Requiring strong session keys enforces 128-bit encryption between systems.

          Satisfies: SRG-OS-000423-GPOS-00187, SRG-OS-000424-GPOS-00188
          Fix Text:Configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Windows Settings >> Security Settings >> Local Policies >> Security Options >> "Domain member: Require strong (Windows 2000 or Later) session key" to "Enabled".
          Severity:medium
          Weight:10.0
          Reference:
          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows Server 2019
          Publisher:DISA
          Type:DPMS Target
          Subject:Microsoft Windows Server 2019
          Identifier:2907
          Definitions:
          Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.windows2019:def:205824
          Definition Result:true
          Title:WN19-SO-000110 - Windows Server 2019 must be configured to require a strong session key.
          Description:A computer connecting to a domain controller will establish a secure channel. The secure channel connection may be subject to compromise, such as hijacking or eavesdropping, if strong session keys are not used to establish the connection. Requiring strong session keys enforces 128-bit encryption between systems.

          Satisfies: SRG-OS-000423-GPOS-00187, SRG-OS-000424-GPOS-00188
          Class:compliance
          Tests:
          • true (All child tests must be true. (AND))
            • true (All child tests must be true. (AND))
              • true ('Domain member: Require strong (Windows 2000 or Later) session key' is set to 'Enabled')
          Tests:
          Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:25344300 (registry_test)
          Result:true
          Title:'Domain member: Require strong (Windows 2000 or Later) session key' is set to 'Enabled'
          Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
          Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).
          Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:obj:25344300 (registry_object)
          Object Comment: 
          Object Requirements:
          • check = 'hive' must be equal to 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE'
          • check = 'key' must be equal to 'SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Services\Netlogon\Parameters'
          • check = 'name' must be equal to 'RequireStrongKey'
          State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:20000001 (registry_state)
          State Comment: 
          State Requirements:
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'type' must be equal to 'reg_dword'
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'value' must be equal to '1'
          Collected Item/State Result:
          [ true ]
          • hive equals 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE'
          • key equals 'SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Services\Netlogon\Parameters'
          • name equals 'RequireStrongKey'
          • last_write_time equals '134212706400000000'
          • type equals 'reg_dword'
          • value equals '1'
          • windows_view equals '64_bit'

          V-205825 - Windows Server 2019 setting Microsoft network client: Digitally sign communications (always) must be configured to Enabled.

          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-205825r958908_rule
          Test Type:Automated
          Rule Result:Pass
          Version:WN19-SO-000160
          Identities:V-93555
          SV-103641
          CCI-002418 (NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: SC-8; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: SC-8)
          CCI-002421 (NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: SC-8 (1); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: SC-8 (1))
          Description: The server message block (SMB) protocol provides the basis for many network operations. Digitally signed SMB packets aid in preventing man-in-the-middle attacks. If this policy is enabled, the SMB client will only communicate with an SMB server that performs SMB packet signing.

          Satisfies: SRG-OS-000423-GPOS-00187, SRG-OS-000424-GPOS-00188
          Fix Text:Configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Windows Settings >> Security Settings >> Local Policies >> Security Options >> "Microsoft network client: Digitally sign communications (always)" to "Enabled".
          Severity:medium
          Weight:10.0
          Reference:
          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows Server 2019
          Publisher:DISA
          Type:DPMS Target
          Subject:Microsoft Windows Server 2019
          Identifier:2907
          Definitions:
          Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.windows2019:def:205825
          Definition Result:true
          Title:WN19-SO-000160 - Windows Server 2019 setting Microsoft network client: Digitally sign communications (always) must be configured to Enabled.
          Description:The server message block (SMB) protocol provides the basis for many network operations. Digitally signed SMB packets aid in preventing man-in-the-middle attacks. If this policy is enabled, the SMB client will only communicate with an SMB server that performs SMB packet signing.

          Satisfies: SRG-OS-000423-GPOS-00187, SRG-OS-000424-GPOS-00188
          Class:compliance
          Tests:
          • true (All child tests must be true. (AND))
            • true (All child tests must be true. (AND))
              • true ('Microsoft Network Client: Digitally sign communications (always)' is set to 'Enabled')

          • check = 'name' must be equal to 'RequireSecuritySignature'

          • key equals 'System\CurrentControlSet\Services\LanmanWorkstation\Parameters'
          • name equals 'RequireSecuritySignature'
          • last_write_time equals '134212788620000000'
          • type equals 'reg_dword'
          • value equals '1'
          • windows_view equals '64_bit'

          V-205826 - Windows Server 2019 setting Microsoft network client: Digitally sign communications (if server agrees) must be configured to Enabled.

          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-205826r958908_rule
          Test Type:Automated
          Rule Result:Pass
          Version:WN19-SO-000170
          Identities:V-93557
          SV-103643
          CCI-002418 (NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: SC-8; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: SC-8)
          CCI-002421 (NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: SC-8 (1); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: SC-8 (1))
          Description: The server message block (SMB) protocol provides the basis for many network operations. If this policy is enabled, the SMB client will request packet signing when communicating with an SMB server that is enabled or required to perform SMB packet signing.

          Satisfies: SRG-OS-000423-GPOS-00187, SRG-OS-000424-GPOS-00188
          Fix Text:Configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Windows Settings >> Security Settings >> Local Policies >> Security Options >> "Microsoft network client: Digitally sign communications (if server agrees)" to "Enabled".
          Severity:medium
          Weight:10.0
          Reference:
          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows Server 2019
          Publisher:DISA
          Type:DPMS Target
          Subject:Microsoft Windows Server 2019
          Identifier:2907
          Definitions:
          Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.windows2019:def:205826
          Definition Result:true
          Title:WN19-SO-000170 - Windows Server 2019 setting Microsoft network client: Digitally sign communications (if server agrees) must be configured to Enabled.
          Description:The server message block (SMB) protocol provides the basis for many network operations. If this policy is enabled, the SMB client will request packet signing when communicating with an SMB server that is enabled or required to perform SMB packet signing.

          Satisfies: SRG-OS-000423-GPOS-00187, SRG-OS-000424-GPOS-00188
          Class:compliance
          Tests:
          • true (All child tests must be true. (AND))
            • true (All child tests must be true. (AND))
              • true ('Microsoft network client: Digitally sign communications (if server agrees)' is set to 'Enabled')
          Tests:
          Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:25446100 (registry_test)
          Result:true
          Title:'Microsoft network client: Digitally sign communications (if server agrees)' is set to 'Enabled'
          Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
          Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).
          Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:obj:25446100 (registry_object)
          Object Comment:Check the value stored at HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\System\CurrentControlSet\Services\LanmanWorkstation\Parameters\EnableSecuritySignature
          Object Requirements:
          • check = 'hive' must be equal to 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE'
          • check = 'key' must be equal to 'System\CurrentControlSet\Services\LanmanWorkstation\Parameters'
          • check = 'name' must be equal to 'EnableSecuritySignature'
          State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:20000001 (registry_state)
          State Comment: 
          State Requirements:
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'type' must be equal to 'reg_dword'
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'value' must be equal to '1'
          Collected Item/State Result:
          [ true ]
          • hive equals 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE'
          • key equals 'System\CurrentControlSet\Services\LanmanWorkstation\Parameters'
          • name equals 'EnableSecuritySignature'
          • last_write_time equals '134212788620000000'
          • type equals 'reg_dword'
          • value equals '1'
          • windows_view equals '64_bit'

          V-205827 - Windows Server 2019 setting Microsoft network server: Digitally sign communications (always) must be configured to Enabled.

          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-205827r958908_rule
          Test Type:Automated
          Rule Result:Pass
          Version:WN19-SO-000190
          Identities:SV-103645
          V-93559
          CCI-002418 (NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: SC-8; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: SC-8)
          CCI-002421 (NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: SC-8 (1); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: SC-8 (1))
          Description: The server message block (SMB) protocol provides the basis for many network operations. Digitally signed SMB packets aid in preventing man-in-the-middle attacks. If this policy is enabled, the SMB server will only communicate with an SMB client that performs SMB packet signing.

          Satisfies: SRG-OS-000423-GPOS-00187, SRG-OS-000424-GPOS-00188
          Fix Text:Configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Windows Settings >> Security Settings >> Local Policies >> Security Options >> "Microsoft network server: Digitally sign communications (always)" to "Enabled".
          Severity:medium
          Weight:10.0
          Reference:
          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows Server 2019
          Publisher:DISA
          Type:DPMS Target
          Subject:Microsoft Windows Server 2019
          Identifier:2907
          Definitions:
          Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.windows2019:def:205827
          Definition Result:true
          Title:WN19-SO-000190 - Windows Server 2019 setting Microsoft network server: Digitally sign communications (always) must be configured to Enabled.
          Description:The server message block (SMB) protocol provides the basis for many network operations. Digitally signed SMB packets aid in preventing man-in-the-middle attacks. If this policy is enabled, the SMB server will only communicate with an SMB client that performs SMB packet signing.

          Satisfies: SRG-OS-000423-GPOS-00187, SRG-OS-000424-GPOS-00188
          Class:compliance
          Tests:
          • true (All child tests must be true. (AND))
            • true (All child tests must be true. (AND))
              • true ('Microsoft network server: Digitally sign communications (always)' is set to 'Enabled')
          Tests:
          Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:25345100 (registry_test)
          Result:true
          Title:'Microsoft network server: Digitally sign communications (always)' is set to 'Enabled'
          Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
          Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).
          Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:obj:25345100 (registry_object)
          Object Comment: 
          Object Requirements:
          • check = 'hive' must be equal to 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE'
          • check = 'key' must be equal to 'SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Services\LanManServer\Parameters'
          • check = 'name' must be equal to 'RequireSecuritySignature'
          State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:20000001 (registry_state)
          State Comment: 
          State Requirements:
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'type' must be equal to 'reg_dword'
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'value' must be equal to '1'
          Collected Item/State Result:
          [ true ]
          • hive equals 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE'
          • key equals 'SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Services\LanManServer\Parameters'
          • name equals 'RequireSecuritySignature'
          • last_write_time equals '134212836120000000'
          • type equals 'reg_dword'
          • value equals '1'
          • windows_view equals '64_bit'

          V-205828 - Windows Server 2019 setting Microsoft network server: Digitally sign communications (if client agrees) must be configured to Enabled.

          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-205828r958908_rule
          Test Type:Automated
          Rule Result:Pass
          Version:WN19-SO-000200
          Identities:V-93561
          SV-103647
          CCI-002418 (NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: SC-8; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: SC-8)
          CCI-002421 (NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: SC-8 (1); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: SC-8 (1))
          Description: The server message block (SMB) protocol provides the basis for many network operations. Digitally signed SMB packets aid in preventing man-in-the-middle attacks. If this policy is enabled, the SMB server will negotiate SMB packet signing as requested by the client.

          Satisfies: SRG-OS-000423-GPOS-00187, SRG-OS-000424-GPOS-00188
          Fix Text:Configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Windows Settings >> Security Settings >> Local Policies >> Security Options >> "Microsoft network server: Digitally sign communications (if client agrees)" to "Enabled".
          Severity:medium
          Weight:10.0
          Reference:
          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows Server 2019
          Publisher:DISA
          Type:DPMS Target
          Subject:Microsoft Windows Server 2019
          Identifier:2907
          Definitions:
          Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.windows2019:def:205828
          Definition Result:true
          Title:WN19-SO-000200 - Windows Server 2019 setting Microsoft network server: Digitally sign communications (if client agrees) must be configured to Enabled.
          Description:The server message block (SMB) protocol provides the basis for many network operations. Digitally signed SMB packets aid in preventing man-in-the-middle attacks. If this policy is enabled, the SMB server will negotiate SMB packet signing as requested by the client.

          Satisfies: SRG-OS-000423-GPOS-00187, SRG-OS-000424-GPOS-00188
          Class:compliance
          Tests:
          • true (All child tests must be true. (AND))
            • true (All child tests must be true. (AND))
              • true ('Microsoft network server: Digitally sign communications (if client agrees)' is set to 'Enabled')
          Tests:
          Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:25446400 (registry_test)
          Result:true
          Title:'Microsoft network server: Digitally sign communications (if client agrees)' is set to 'Enabled'
          Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
          Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).
          Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:obj:25446400 (registry_object)
          Object Comment:Check the registry value at HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Services\LanManServer\Parameters\EnableSecuritySignature
          Object Requirements:
          • check = 'hive' must be equal to 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE'
          • check = 'key' must be equal to 'SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Services\LanManServer\Parameters'
          • check = 'name' must be equal to 'EnableSecuritySignature'
          State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:20000001 (registry_state)
          State Comment: 
          State Requirements:
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'type' must be equal to 'reg_dword'
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'value' must be equal to '1'
          Collected Item/State Result:

          • key equals 'SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Services\LanManServer\Parameters'

          • type equals 'reg_dword'
          • value equals '1'
          • windows_view equals '64_bit'

          V-205830 - Windows Server 2019 Explorer Data Execution Prevention must be enabled.

          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-205830r958928_rule
          Test Type:Automated
          Rule Result:Pass
          Version:WN19-CC-000310
          Identities:SV-103649
          V-93563
          CCI-002824 (NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: SI-16; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: SI-16)
          Description: Data Execution Prevention provides additional protection by performing checks on memory to help prevent malicious code from running. This setting will prevent Data Execution Prevention from being turned off for File Explorer.
          Fix Text:The default behavior is for data execution prevention to be turned on for File Explorer.

          If this needs to be corrected, configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Administrative Templates >> Windows Components >> File Explorer >> "Turn off Data Execution Prevention for Explorer" to "Not Configured" or "Disabled".
          Severity:medium
          Weight:10.0
          Reference:
          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows Server 2019
          Publisher:DISA
          Type:DPMS Target
          Subject:Microsoft Windows Server 2019
          Identifier:2907
          Definitions:
          Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.windows2019:def:205830
          Definition Result:true
          Title:WN19-CC-000310 - Windows Server 2019 Explorer Data Execution Prevention must be enabled.
          Description:Data Execution Prevention provides additional protection by performing checks on memory to help prevent malicious code from running. This setting will prevent Data Execution Prevention from being turned off for File Explorer.
          Class:compliance
          Tests:
          • true (All child tests must be true. (AND))
            • true (All child tests must be true. (AND))
              • true ('Turn off Data Execution Prevention for Explorer' is set to 'Disabled' or 'Not Configured')
          Tests:
          Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:25339600 (registry_test)
          Result:true
          Title:'Turn off Data Execution Prevention for Explorer' is set to 'Disabled' or 'Not Configured'
          Check Existence:Zero or more collected items may exist.
          Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).
          Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:obj:25339600 (registry_object)
          Object Comment: 
          Object Requirements:
          • check = 'hive' must be equal to 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE'
          • check = 'key' must be equal to 'Software\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\Explorer'
          • check = 'name' must be equal to 'NoDataExecutionPrevention'
          State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:20000000 (registry_state)
          State Comment: 
          State Requirements:
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'type' must be equal to 'reg_dword'
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'value' must be equal to '0'

          V-205832 - Windows Server 2019 must be configured to audit Account Logon - Credential Validation successes.

          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-205832r991578_rule
          Test Type:Automated
          Rule Result:Pass
          Version:WN19-AU-000070
          Identities:SV-103241
          V-93153
          CCI-000172 (NIST SP 800-53: AU-12 c; NIST SP 800-53A: AU-12.1 (iv); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: AU-12 c; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: AU-12 c)
          Description: Maintaining an audit trail of system activity logs can help identify configuration errors, troubleshoot service disruptions, and analyze compromises that have occurred, as well as detect attacks. Audit logs are necessary to provide a trail of evidence in case the system or network is compromised. Collecting this data is essential for analyzing the security of information assets and detecting signs of suspicious and unexpected behavior.

          Credential Validation records events related to validation tests on credentials for a user account logon.
          Fix Text:Configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Windows Settings >> Security Settings >> Advanced Audit Policy Configuration >> System Audit Policies >> Account Logon >> "Audit Credential Validation" with "Success" selected.
          Severity:medium
          Weight:10.0
          Reference:
          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows Server 2019
          Publisher:DISA
          Type:DPMS Target
          Subject:Microsoft Windows Server 2019
          Identifier:2907
          Definitions:
          Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.windows2019:def:205832
          Definition Result:true
          Title:WN19-AU-000070 - Windows Server 2019 must be configured to audit Account Logon - Credential Validation successes.
          Description:Maintaining an audit trail of system activity logs can help identify configuration errors, troubleshoot service disruptions, and analyze compromises that have occurred, as well as detect attacks. Audit logs are necessary to provide a trail of evidence in case the system or network is compromised. Collecting this data is essential for analyzing the security of information assets and detecting signs of suspicious and unexpected behavior.

          Credential Validation records events related to validation tests on credentials for a user account logon.
          Class:compliance
          Tests:
          • true (All child tests must be true. (AND))
            • true (One or more child tests must be true. (OR))
              • false ('Audit Credential Validation' is set to 'Success')
              • true ('Audit Credential Validation' is set to 'Success' and 'Failure')
          Tests:
          Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:25330700 (auditeventpolicysubcategories_test)
          Result:false
          Title:'Audit Credential Validation' is set to 'Success'
          Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
          Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).
          Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:obj:20000000 (auditeventpolicysubcategories_object)
          Object Comment: 
          Object Requirements:
          • Collect any available items.
          State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:25330700 (auditeventpolicysubcategories_state)
          State Comment:'Credential Validation' is set to 'Success'
          State Requirements:
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'credential_validation' must be equal to 'AUDIT_SUCCESS'
          Collected Item/State Result:
          [ false ]
          • credential_validation equals 'AUDIT_SUCCESS_FAILURE'
          • kerberos_authentication_service equals 'AUDIT_NONE'
          • kerberos_service_ticket_operations equals 'AUDIT_NONE'
          • kerberos_ticket_events equals 'AUDIT_NONE'
          • other_account_logon_events equals 'AUDIT_NONE'
          • application_group_management equals 'AUDIT_NONE'
          • computer_account_management equals 'AUDIT_NONE'
          • distribution_group_management equals 'AUDIT_NONE'
          • other_account_management_events equals 'AUDIT_SUCCESS'
          • security_group_management equals 'AUDIT_SUCCESS'
          • user_account_management equals 'AUDIT_SUCCESS_FAILURE'
          • dpapi_activity equals 'AUDIT_NONE'

          • process_termination equals 'AUDIT_NONE'

          • directory_service_replication equals 'AUDIT_NONE'
          • detailed_directory_service_replication equals 'AUDIT_NONE'
          • account_lockout equals 'AUDIT_FAILURE'
          • ipsec_extended_mode equals 'AUDIT_NONE'
          • ipsec_main_mode equals 'AUDIT_NONE'
          • ipsec_quick_mode equals 'AUDIT_NONE'
          • logoff equals 'AUDIT_SUCCESS'
          • logon equals 'AUDIT_SUCCESS_FAILURE'

          V-205833 - Windows Server 2019 must be configured to audit Account Logon - Credential Validation failures.

          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-205833r991578_rule

Audit logs are necessary to provide a trail of evidence in case the system or network is compromised. Collecting this data is essential for analyzing the security of information assets and detecting signs of suspicious and unexpected behavior.

          Credential Validation records events related to validation tests on credentials for a user account logon.
          Fix Text:Configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Windows Settings >> Security Settings >> Advanced Audit Policy Configuration >> System Audit Policies >> Account Logon >> "Audit Credential Validation" with "Failure" selected.

Audit logs are necessary to provide a trail of evidence in case the system or network is compromised. Collecting this data is essential for analyzing the security of information assets and detecting signs of suspicious and unexpected behavior.

          Credential Validation records events related to validation tests on credentials for a user account logon.

          V-205834 - Windows Server 2019 must be configured to audit Logon/Logoff - Group Membership successes.

          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-205834r991578_rule
          Test Type:Automated
          Rule Result:Pass
          Version:WN19-AU-000170
          Identities:V-93159
          SV-103247
          CCI-000172 (NIST SP 800-53: AU-12 c; NIST SP 800-53A: AU-12.1 (iv); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: AU-12 c; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: AU-12 c)
          Description: Maintaining an audit trail of system activity logs can help identify configuration errors, troubleshoot service disruptions, and analyze compromises that have occurred, as well as detect attacks. Audit logs are necessary to provide a trail of evidence in case the system or network is compromised. Collecting this data is essential for analyzing the security of information assets and detecting signs of suspicious and unexpected behavior.

          Audit Group Membership records information related to the group membership of a user's logon token.
          Fix Text:Configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Windows Settings >> Advanced Audit Policy Configuration >> System Audit Policies >> Logon/Logoff >> "Audit Group Membership" with "Success" selected.
          Severity:medium
          Weight:10.0
          Reference:
          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows Server 2019
          Publisher:DISA
          Type:DPMS Target
          Subject:Microsoft Windows Server 2019
          Identifier:2907
          Definitions:
          Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.windows2019:def:205834
          Definition Result:true
          Title:WN19-AU-000170 - Windows Server 2019 must be configured to audit Logon/Logoff - Group Membership successes.
          Description:Maintaining an audit trail of system activity logs can help identify configuration errors, troubleshoot service disruptions, and analyze compromises that have occurred, as well as detect attacks. Audit logs are necessary to provide a trail of evidence in case the system or network is compromised. Collecting this data is essential for analyzing the security of information assets and detecting signs of suspicious and unexpected behavior.

          Audit Group Membership records information related to the group membership of a user's logon token.
          Class:compliance
          Tests:
          • true (All child tests must be true. (AND))
            • true (One or more child tests must be true. (OR))
              • true (Audit - Logon/Logoff - Group Membership - Success Only)
              • false (Audit - Logon/Logoff - Group Membership - Success and Failure)
          Tests:
          Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:25331400 (auditeventpolicysubcategories_test)
          Result:true
          Title:Audit - Logon/Logoff - Group Membership - Success Only
          Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
          Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).
          Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:obj:20000000 (auditeventpolicysubcategories_object)
          Object Comment: 
          Object Requirements:
          • Collect any available items.
          State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:25331400 (auditeventpolicysubcategories_state)
          State Comment:'Audit Group Membership' is set to 'Success'
          State Requirements:
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'group_membership' must be equal to 'AUDIT_SUCCESS'

          V-205835 - Windows Server 2019 must be configured to audit Logon/Logoff - Special Logon successes.

          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-205835r991578_rule
          Test Type:Automated
          Rule Result:Pass
          Version:WN19-AU-000210
          Identities:V-93161
          SV-103249
          CCI-000172 (NIST SP 800-53: AU-12 c; NIST SP 800-53A: AU-12.1 (iv); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: AU-12 c; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: AU-12 c)
          Description: Maintaining an audit trail of system activity logs can help identify configuration errors, troubleshoot service disruptions, and analyze compromises that have occurred, as well as detect attacks. Audit logs are necessary to provide a trail of evidence in case the system or network is compromised. Collecting this data is essential for analyzing the security of information assets and detecting signs of suspicious and unexpected behavior.

          Special Logon records special logons that have administrative privileges and can be used to elevate processes.

          Satisfies: SRG-OS-000470-GPOS-00214, SRG-OS-000472-GPOS-00217, SRG-OS-000473-GPOS-00218, SRG-OS-000475-GPOS-00220
          Fix Text:Configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Windows Settings >> Security Settings >> Advanced Audit Policy Configuration >> System Audit Policies >> Logon/Logoff >> "Audit Special Logon" with "Success" selected.
          Severity:medium
          Weight:10.0
          Reference:
          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows Server 2019
          Publisher:DISA
          Type:DPMS Target
          Subject:Microsoft Windows Server 2019
          Identifier:2907
          Definitions:
          Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.windows2019:def:205835
          Definition Result:true
          Title:WN19-AU-000210 - Windows Server 2019 must be configured to audit Logon/Logoff - Special Logon successes.
          Description:Maintaining an audit trail of system activity logs can help identify configuration errors, troubleshoot service disruptions, and analyze compromises that have occurred, as well as detect attacks. Audit logs are necessary to provide a trail of evidence in case the system or network is compromised. Collecting this data is essential for analyzing the security of information assets and detecting signs of suspicious and unexpected behavior.

          Special Logon records special logons that have administrative privileges and can be used to elevate processes.

          Satisfies: SRG-OS-000470-GPOS-00214, SRG-OS-000472-GPOS-00217, SRG-OS-000473-GPOS-00218, SRG-OS-000475-GPOS-00220
          Class:compliance
          Tests:
          • true (All child tests must be true. (AND))

              • true (Audit - Special Logon - Success only)
              • false (Audit - Special Logon - Success and Failure)

          V-205836 - Windows Server 2019 must be configured to audit Object Access - Other Object Access Events successes.

          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-205836r991578_rule
          Test Type:Automated
          Rule Result:Pass
          Version:WN19-AU-000220
          Identities:SV-103251
          V-93163
          CCI-000172 (NIST SP 800-53: AU-12 c; NIST SP 800-53A: AU-12.1 (iv); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: AU-12 c; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: AU-12 c)
          Description: Maintaining an audit trail of system activity logs can help identify configuration errors, troubleshoot service disruptions, and analyze compromises that have occurred, as well as detect attacks. Audit logs are necessary to provide a trail of evidence in case the system or network is compromised. Collecting this data is essential for analyzing the security of information assets and detecting signs of suspicious and unexpected behavior.

          Auditing for other object access records events related to the management of task scheduler jobs and COM+ objects.
          Fix Text:Configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Windows Settings >> Security Settings >> Advanced Audit Policy Configuration >> System Audit Policies >> Object Access >> "Audit Other Object Access Events" with "Success" selected.
          Severity:medium
          Weight:10.0
          Reference:
          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows Server 2019
          Publisher:DISA
          Type:DPMS Target
          Subject:Microsoft Windows Server 2019
          Identifier:2907
          Definitions:
          Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.windows2019:def:205836
          Definition Result:true
          Title:WN19-AU-000220 - Windows Server 2019 must be configured to audit Object Access - Other Object Access Events successes.
          Description:Maintaining an audit trail of system activity logs can help identify configuration errors, troubleshoot service disruptions, and analyze compromises that have occurred, as well as detect attacks. Audit logs are necessary to provide a trail of evidence in case the system or network is compromised. Collecting this data is essential for analyzing the security of information assets and detecting signs of suspicious and unexpected behavior.

          Auditing for other object access records events related to the management of task scheduler jobs and COM+ objects.
          Class:compliance
          Tests:
          • true (All child tests must be true. (AND))
            • true (One or more child tests must be true. (OR))
              • false ('Audit Other Object Access Events' is set to 'Success')
              • true ('Audit Other Object Access Events' is set to 'Success' and 'Failure')
          Tests:
          Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:25332100 (auditeventpolicysubcategories_test)
          Result:false
          Title:'Audit Other Object Access Events' is set to 'Success'
          Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
          Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).
          Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:obj:20000000 (auditeventpolicysubcategories_object)
          Object Comment: 
          Object Requirements:
          • Collect any available items.
          State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:25332100 (auditeventpolicysubcategories_state)
          State Comment:'Other Object Access Events' is set to 'Success'
          State Requirements:
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'other_object_access_events' must be equal to 'AUDIT_SUCCESS'
          Collected Item/State Result:
          [ false ]
          • credential_validation equals 'AUDIT_SUCCESS_FAILURE'
          • kerberos_authentication_service equals 'AUDIT_NONE'

          V-205838 - Windows Server 2019 must be configured to audit logoff successes.

          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-205838r991581_rule
          Test Type:Automated
          Rule Result:Pass
          Version:WN19-AU-000180
          Identities:V-93171
          SV-103259
          CCI-000172 (NIST SP 800-53: AU-12 c; NIST SP 800-53A: AU-12.1 (iv); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: AU-12 c; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: AU-12 c)
          CCI-000366 (NIST SP 800-53: CM-6 b; NIST SP 800-53A: CM-6.1 (iv); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: CM-6 b; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: CM-6 b)
          Description: Maintaining an audit trail of system activity logs can help identify configuration errors, troubleshoot service disruptions, and analyze compromises that have occurred, as well as detect attacks. Audit logs are necessary to provide a trail of evidence in case the system or network is compromised. Collecting this data is essential for analyzing the security of information assets and detecting signs of suspicious and unexpected behavior.

          Logoff records user logoffs. If this is an interactive logoff, it is recorded on the local system. If it is to a network share, it is recorded on the system accessed.

          Satisfies: SRG-OS-000472-GPOS-00217, SRG-OS-000480-GPOS-00227
          Fix Text:Configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Windows Settings >> Security Settings >> Advanced Audit Policy Configuration >> System Audit Policies >> Logon/Logoff >> "Audit Logoff" with "Success" selected.
          Severity:medium
          Weight:10.0
          Reference:
          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows Server 2019
          Publisher:DISA
          Type:DPMS Target
          Subject:Microsoft Windows Server 2019
          Identifier:2907
          Definitions:
          Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.windows2019:def:205838
          Definition Result:true
          Title:WN19-AU-000180 - Windows Server 2019 must be configured to audit logoff successes.
          Description:Maintaining an audit trail of system activity logs can help identify configuration errors, troubleshoot service disruptions, and analyze compromises that have occurred, as well as detect attacks. Audit logs are necessary to provide a trail of evidence in case the system or network is compromised. Collecting this data is essential for analyzing the security of information assets and detecting signs of suspicious and unexpected behavior.

          Logoff records user logoffs. If this is an interactive logoff, it is recorded on the local system. If it is to a network share, it is recorded on the system accessed.

          Satisfies: SRG-OS-000472-GPOS-00217, SRG-OS-000480-GPOS-00227
          Class:compliance

          V-205839 - Windows Server 2019 must be configured to audit Detailed Tracking - Plug and Play Events successes.

          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-205839r991583_rule
          Test Type:Automated
          Rule Result:Pass
          Version:WN19-AU-000130
          Identities:V-93157
          SV-103245
          CCI-000172 (NIST SP 800-53: AU-12 c; NIST SP 800-53A: AU-12.1 (iv); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: AU-12 c; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: AU-12 c)
          Description: Maintaining an audit trail of system activity logs can help identify configuration errors, troubleshoot service disruptions, and analyze compromises that have occurred, as well as detect attacks. Audit logs are necessary to provide a trail of evidence in case the system or network is compromised. Collecting this data is essential for analyzing the security of information assets and detecting signs of suspicious and unexpected behavior.

          Plug and Play activity records events related to the successful connection of external devices.
          Fix Text:Configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Windows Settings >> Advanced Audit Policy Configuration >> System Audit Policies >> Detailed Tracking >> "Audit PNP Activity" with "Success" selected.
          Severity:medium
          Weight:10.0
          Reference:
          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows Server 2019
          Publisher:DISA
          Type:DPMS Target
          Subject:Microsoft Windows Server 2019
          Identifier:2907
          Definitions:
          Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.windows2019:def:205839
          Definition Result:true
          Title:WN19-AU-000130 - Windows Server 2019 must be configured to audit Detailed Tracking - Plug and Play Events successes.
          Description:Maintaining an audit trail of system activity logs can help identify configuration errors, troubleshoot service disruptions, and analyze compromises that have occurred, as well as detect attacks. Audit logs are necessary to provide a trail of evidence in case the system or network is compromised. Collecting this data is essential for analyzing the security of information assets and detecting signs of suspicious and unexpected behavior.

          Plug and Play activity records events related to the successful connection of external devices.
          Class:compliance
          Tests:
          • true (All child tests must be true. (AND))
            • true (One or more child tests must be true. (OR))
              • true (Audit - Detailed Tracking - PNP Activity - Success Only)
              • false (Audit - Detailed Tracking - PNP Activity - Success and Failure)
          Tests:
          Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:25331100 (auditeventpolicysubcategories_test)
          Result:true
          Title:Audit - Detailed Tracking - PNP Activity - Success Only
          Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
          Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).
          Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:obj:20000000 (auditeventpolicysubcategories_object)
          Object Comment: 
          Object Requirements:
          • Collect any available items.
          State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:25331100 (auditeventpolicysubcategories_state)
          State Comment:'Plug and Play Events' is set to 'Success'
          State Requirements:
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'pnp_activity' must be equal to 'AUDIT_SUCCESS'

          [ true ]
          • credential_validation equals 'AUDIT_SUCCESS_FAILURE'
