          V-205840 - Windows Server 2019 must be configured to audit Object Access - Removable Storage successes.

          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-205840r991583_rule
          Test Type:Automated
          Rule Result:Pass
          Version:WN19-AU-000240
          Identities:SV-103255
          V-93167
          CCI-000172 (NIST SP 800-53: AU-12 c; NIST SP 800-53A: AU-12.1 (iv); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: AU-12 c; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: AU-12 c)
          Description: Maintaining an audit trail of system activity logs can help identify configuration errors, troubleshoot service disruptions, and analyze compromises that have occurred, as well as detect attacks. Audit logs are necessary to provide a trail of evidence in case the system or network is compromised. Collecting this data is essential for analyzing the security of information assets and detecting signs of suspicious and unexpected behavior.

          Removable Storage auditing under Object Access records events related to access attempts on file system objects on removable storage devices.
          Fix Text:Configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Windows Settings >> Security Settings >> Advanced Audit Policy Configuration >> System Audit Policies >> Object Access >> "Audit Removable Storage" with "Success" selected.
          Severity:medium
          Weight:10.0
          Reference:
          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows Server 2019
          Publisher:DISA
          Type:DPMS Target
          Subject:Microsoft Windows Server 2019
          Identifier:2907
          Definitions:
          Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.windows2019:def:205840
          Definition Result:true
          Title:WN19-AU-000240 - Windows Server 2019 must be configured to audit Object Access - Removable Storage successes.
          Description:Maintaining an audit trail of system activity logs can help identify configuration errors, troubleshoot service disruptions, and analyze compromises that have occurred, as well as detect attacks. Audit logs are necessary to provide a trail of evidence in case the system or network is compromised. Collecting this data is essential for analyzing the security of information assets and detecting signs of suspicious and unexpected behavior.

          Removable Storage auditing under Object Access records events related to access attempts on file system objects on removable storage devices.

              • true (Audit - Object Access - Removable Storage - Success and Failure)

          V-205841 - Windows Server 2019 must be configured to audit Object Access - Removable Storage failures.

          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-205841r991583_rule
          Test Type:Automated
          Rule Result:Pass
          Version:WN19-AU-000250
          Identities:SV-103257
          V-93169
          CCI-000172 (NIST SP 800-53: AU-12 c; NIST SP 800-53A: AU-12.1 (iv); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: AU-12 c; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: AU-12 c)
          Description: Maintaining an audit trail of system activity logs can help identify configuration errors, troubleshoot service disruptions, and analyze compromises that have occurred, as well as detect attacks. Audit logs are necessary to provide a trail of evidence in case the system or network is compromised. Collecting this data is essential for analyzing the security of information assets and detecting signs of suspicious and unexpected behavior.

          Removable Storage auditing under Object Access records events related to access attempts on file system objects on removable storage devices.
          Fix Text:Configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Windows Settings >> Security Settings >> Advanced Audit Policy Configuration >> System Audit Policies >> Object Access >> "Audit Removable Storage" with "Failure" selected.
          Severity:medium
          Weight:10.0
          Reference:
          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows Server 2019
          Publisher:DISA
          Type:DPMS Target
          Subject:Microsoft Windows Server 2019
          Identifier:2907
          Definitions:
          Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.windows2019:def:205841
          Definition Result:true
          Title:WN19-AU-000250 - Windows Server 2019 must be configured to audit Object Access - Removable Storage failures.
          Description:Maintaining an audit trail of system activity logs can help identify configuration errors, troubleshoot service disruptions, and analyze compromises that have occurred, as well as detect attacks. Audit logs are necessary to provide a trail of evidence in case the system or network is compromised. Collecting this data is essential for analyzing the security of information assets and detecting signs of suspicious and unexpected behavior.

          Removable Storage auditing under Object Access records events related to access attempts on file system objects on removable storage devices.
          Class:compliance
          Tests:
          • true (All child tests must be true. (AND))
            • true (One or more child tests must be true. (OR))
              • false (Audit - Object Access - Removable Storage - Failure Only)
              • true (Audit - Object Access - Removable Storage - Success and Failure)
          Tests:
          Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:25332300 (auditeventpolicysubcategories_test)
          Result:false
          Title:Audit - Object Access - Removable Storage - Failure Only
          Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
          Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).
          Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:obj:20000000 (auditeventpolicysubcategories_object)
          Object Comment: 
          Object Requirements:
          • Collect any available items.
          State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:25332300 (auditeventpolicysubcategories_state)
          State Comment:'Audit Removable Storage' is set to 'Success'
          State Requirements:
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'removable_storage' must be equal to 'AUDIT_FAILURE'
          Collected Item/State Result:
          [ false ]
          • credential_validation equals 'AUDIT_SUCCESS_FAILURE'

          V-205842 - Windows Server 2019 must be configured to use FIPS-compliant algorithms for encryption, hashing, and signing.

          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-205842r1137699_rule
          Test Type:Automated
          Rule Result:Pass
          Version:WN19-SO-000360
          Identities:SV-103597
          V-93511
          CCI-002450 (NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: SC-13; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: SC-13 b)
          Description: This setting ensures the system uses algorithms that are FIPS-compliant for encryption, hashing, and signing. FIPS-compliant algorithms meet specific standards established by the U.S. Government and must be the algorithms used for all OS encryption functions.
          Fix Text:Configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Windows Settings >> Security Settings >> Local Policies >> Security Options >> "System cryptography: Use FIPS compliant algorithms for encryption, hashing, and signing" to "Enabled".
          Severity:medium
          Weight:10.0
          Reference:
          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows Server 2019
          Publisher:DISA
          Type:DPMS Target
          Subject:Microsoft Windows Server 2019
          Identifier:2907
          Definitions:
          Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.windows2019:def:205842
          Definition Result:true
          Title:WN19-SO-000360 - Windows Server 2019 must be configured to use FIPS-compliant algorithms for encryption, hashing, and signing.
          Description:This setting ensures the system uses algorithms that are FIPS-compliant for encryption, hashing, and signing. FIPS-compliant algorithms meet specific standards established by the U.S. Government and must be the algorithms used for all OS encryption functions.
          Class:compliance
          Tests:
          • true (All child tests must be true. (AND))
            • true (All child tests must be true. (AND))
              • true ('System cryptography: Use FIPS compliant algorithms for encryption, hashing, and signing' is set to 'Enabled')
          Tests:
          Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:25346600 (registry_test)
          Result:true
          Title:'System cryptography: Use FIPS compliant algorithms for encryption, hashing, and signing' is set to 'Enabled'
          Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
          Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).
          Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:obj:25346600 (registry_object)
          Object Comment: 
          Object Requirements:
          • check = 'hive' must be equal to 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE'
          • check = 'key' must be equal to 'SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Control\Lsa\FIPSAlgorithmPolicy'
          • check = 'name' must be equal to 'Enabled'
          State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:20000001 (registry_state)
          State Comment: 
          State Requirements:
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'type' must be equal to 'reg_dword'
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'value' must be equal to '1'
          Collected Item/State Result:
          [ true ]
          • hive equals 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE'
          • key equals 'SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Control\Lsa\FIPSAlgorithmPolicy'
          • name equals 'Enabled'
          • last_write_time equals '134212788620000000'
          • type equals 'reg_dword'
          • value equals '1'
          • windows_view equals '64_bit'

          V-205861 - Windows Server 2019 insecure logons to an SMB server must be disabled.

          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-205861r991589_rule
          Test Type:Automated
          Rule Result:Pass
          Version:WN19-CC-000070
          Identities:SV-103327
          V-93239
          CCI-000366 (NIST SP 800-53: CM-6 b; NIST SP 800-53A: CM-6.1 (iv); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: CM-6 b; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: CM-6 b)
          Description: Insecure guest logons allow unauthenticated access to shared folders. Shared resources on a system must require authentication to establish proper access.
          Fix Text:Configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Administrative Templates >> Network >> Lanman Workstation >> "Enable insecure guest logons" to "Disabled".
          Severity:medium
          Weight:10.0
          Reference:
          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows Server 2019
          Publisher:DISA
          Type:DPMS Target
          Subject:Microsoft Windows Server 2019
          Identifier:2907
          Definitions:
          Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.windows2019:def:205861
          Definition Result:true
          Title:WN19-CC-000070 - Windows Server 2019 insecure logons to an SMB server must be disabled.
          Description:Insecure guest logons allow unauthenticated access to shared folders. Shared resources on a system must require authentication to establish proper access.
          Class:compliance
          Tests:
          • true (All child tests must be true. (AND))
            • true (All child tests must be true. (AND))
              • true ('Enable insecure guest logons' is set to 'Disabled')
          Tests:
          Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:25336000 (registry_test)
          Result:true
          Title:'Enable insecure guest logons' is set to 'Disabled'
          Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
          Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).
          Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:obj:25336000 (registry_object)
          Object Comment: 
          Object Requirements:
          • check = 'hive' must be equal to 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE'
          • check = 'key' must be equal to 'SOFTWARE\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\LanmanWorkstation'
          • check = 'name' must be equal to 'AllowInsecureGuestAuth'
          State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:20000000 (registry_state)
          State Comment: 
          State Requirements:
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'type' must be equal to 'reg_dword'
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'value' must be equal to '0'
          Collected Item/State Result:
          [ true ]
          • hive equals 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE'
          • key equals 'SOFTWARE\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\LanmanWorkstation'
          • name equals 'AllowInsecureGuestAuth'
          • last_write_time equals '134213413420000000'
          • type equals 'reg_dword'
          • value equals '0'
          • windows_view equals '64_bit'

          V-205862 - Windows Server 2019 hardened Universal Naming Convention (UNC) paths must be defined to require mutual authentication and integrity for at least the \\*\SYSVOL and \\*\NETLOGON shares.

          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-205862r991589_rule
          Test Type:Automated
          Rule Applicability:Windows_DomainController
          Rule Applicability:Windows_MemberServer
          Rule Result:Pass
          Version:WN19-CC-000080
          Identities:SV-103329
          V-93241
          CCI-000366 (NIST SP 800-53: CM-6 b; NIST SP 800-53A: CM-6.1 (iv); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: CM-6 b; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: CM-6 b)
          Description: Additional security requirements are applied to UNC paths specified in hardened UNC paths before allowing access to them. This aids in preventing tampering with or spoofing of connections to these paths.
          Fix Text:Configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Administrative Templates >> Network >> Network Provider >> "Hardened UNC Paths" to "Enabled" with at least the following configured in "Hardened UNC Paths" (click the "Show" button to display):

          Value Name: \\*\SYSVOL
          Value: RequireMutualAuthentication=1, RequireIntegrity=1

          Value Name: \\*\NETLOGON
          Value: RequireMutualAuthentication=1, RequireIntegrity=1
          Severity:medium
          Weight:10.0
          Reference:
          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows Server 2019
          Publisher:DISA
          Type:DPMS Target
          Subject:Microsoft Windows Server 2019
          Identifier:2907
          Definitions:
          Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.windows2019:def:205862
          Definition Result:true
          Title:WN19-CC-000080 - Windows Server 2019 hardened Universal Naming Convention (UNC) paths must be defined to require mutual authentication and integrity for at least the \*\SYSVOL and \*\NETLOGON shares.
          Description:Additional security requirements are applied to UNC paths specified in hardened UNC paths before allowing access to them. This aids in preventing tampering with or spoofing of connections to these paths.
          Class:compliance
          Tests:
          • true (All child tests must be true. (AND))
            • true (All child tests must be true. (AND))
              • true (Mutual Authentication and Integrity for \\*\SYSVOL)
              • true (Mutual Authentication and Integrity for \\*\NETLOGON)
          Tests:
          Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:25336200 (registry_test)
          Result:true
          Title:Mutual Authentication and Integrity for \\*\SYSVOL
          Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
          Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).
          State Operator:All item-state comparisons must be true.
          Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:obj:25336200 (registry_object)
          Object Comment:Hardened Path Config for \\*\SYSVOL
          Object Requirements:
          • check = 'hive' must be equal to 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE'
          • check = 'key' must be equal to 'SOFTWARE\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\NetworkProvider\HardenedPaths'
          • check = 'name' must be equal to '\\*\SYSVOL'
          State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:25336200 (registry_state)
          State Comment:RequireMutualAuthentication=1
          State Requirements:
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'type' must be equal to 'reg_sz'
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'value' must match the pattern '(^| *, *)RequireMutualAuthentication=1( *, *|$)'
          State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:25336201 (registry_state)
          State Comment:No RequireMutualAuthentication!=1
          State Requirements:
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'type' must be equal to 'reg_sz'
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = no 'value' can match the pattern '(^|[ *, *])RequireMutualAuthentication=(?!1( *, *|$))'
          State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:25336202 (registry_state)
          State Comment:RequireIntegrity=1
          State Requirements:
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'type' must be equal to 'reg_sz'
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'value' must match the pattern '(^| *, *)RequireIntegrity=1( *, *|$)'
          State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:25336203 (registry_state)
          State Comment:No RequireIntegrity!=1
          State Requirements:
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'type' must be equal to 'reg_sz'
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = no 'value' can match the pattern '(^| *, *)RequireIntegrity=(?!1( *, *|$))'
          Collected Item/State Result:
          [ true ]
          • hive equals 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE'
          • key equals 'SOFTWARE\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\NetworkProvider\HardenedPaths'
          • name equals '\\*\SYSVOL'
          • last_write_time equals '134213413420000000'
          • type equals 'reg_sz'
          • value equals 'RequireMutualAuthentication=1,RequireIntegrity=1'
          • windows_view equals '64_bit'
          Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:25336201 (registry_test)
          Result:true
          Title:Mutual Authentication and Integrity for \\*\NETLOGON
          Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
          Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).
          State Operator:All item-state comparisons must be true.
          Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:obj:25336201 (registry_object)
          Object Comment:Hardened Path Config for \\*\NETLOGON
          Object Requirements:
          • check = 'hive' must be equal to 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE'
          • check = 'key' must be equal to 'SOFTWARE\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\NetworkProvider\HardenedPaths'
          • check = 'name' must be equal to '\\*\NETLOGON'
          State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:25336200 (registry_state)
          State Comment:RequireMutualAuthentication=1
          State Requirements:
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'type' must be equal to 'reg_sz'
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'value' must match the pattern '(^| *, *)RequireMutualAuthentication=1( *, *|$)'
          State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:25336201 (registry_state)
          State Comment:No RequireMutualAuthentication!=1
          State Requirements:
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'type' must be equal to 'reg_sz'
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = no 'value' can match the pattern '(^|[ *, *])RequireMutualAuthentication=(?!1( *, *|$))'
          State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:25336202 (registry_state)
          State Comment:RequireIntegrity=1
          State Requirements:
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'type' must be equal to 'reg_sz'
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'value' must match the pattern '(^| *, *)RequireIntegrity=1( *, *|$)'
          State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:25336203 (registry_state)
          State Comment:No RequireIntegrity!=1
          State Requirements:
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'type' must be equal to 'reg_sz'
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = no 'value' can match the pattern '(^| *, *)RequireIntegrity=(?!1( *, *|$))'
          Collected Item/State Result:
          [ true ]
          • hive equals 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE'
          • key equals 'SOFTWARE\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\NetworkProvider\HardenedPaths'
          • name equals '\\*\NETLOGON'
          • last_write_time equals '134213413420000000'
          • type equals 'reg_sz'
          • value equals 'RequireMutualAuthentication=1,RequireIntegrity=1'
          • windows_view equals '64_bit'

          V-205863 - Windows Server 2019 must be configured to enable Remote host allows delegation of non-exportable credentials.

          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-205863r991589_rule
          Test Type:Automated
          Rule Result:Pass
          Version:WN19-CC-000100
          Identities:V-93243
          SV-103331
          CCI-000366 (NIST SP 800-53: CM-6 b; NIST SP 800-53A: CM-6.1 (iv); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: CM-6 b; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: CM-6 b)
          Description: An exportable version of credentials is provided to remote hosts when using credential delegation which exposes them to theft on the remote host. Restricted Admin mode or Remote Credential Guard allow delegation of non-exportable credentials providing additional protection of the credentials. Enabling this configures the host to support Restricted Admin mode or Remote Credential Guard.
          Fix Text:Configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Administrative Templates >> System >> Credentials Delegation >> "Remote host allows delegation of non-exportable credentials" to "Enabled".
          Severity:medium
          Weight:10.0
          Reference:
          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows Server 2019
          Publisher:DISA
          Type:DPMS Target
          Subject:Microsoft Windows Server 2019
          Identifier:2907
          Definitions:
          Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.windows2019:def:205863
          Definition Result:true
          Title:WN19-CC-000100 - Windows Server 2019 must be configured to enable Remote host allows delegation of non-exportable credentials.
          Description:An exportable version of credentials is provided to remote hosts when using credential delegation which exposes them to theft on the remote host. Restricted Admin mode or Remote Credential Guard allow delegation of non-exportable credentials providing additional protection of the credentials. Enabling this configures the host to support Restricted Admin mode or Remote Credential Guard.
          Class:compliance
          Tests:
          • true (All child tests must be true. (AND))
            • true (All child tests must be true. (AND))
              • true ('Remote host allows delegation of non-exportable credentials' is set to 'Enabled')
          Tests:
          Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:25336800 (registry_test)
          Result:true
          Title:'Remote host allows delegation of non-exportable credentials' is set to 'Enabled'
          Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
          Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).
          Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:obj:25336800 (registry_object)
          Object Comment: 
          Object Requirements:
          • check = 'hive' must be equal to 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE'
          • check = 'key' must be equal to 'SOFTWARE\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\CredentialsDelegation'
          • check = 'name' must be equal to 'AllowProtectedCreds'
          State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:20000001 (registry_state)
          State Comment: 
          State Requirements:
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'type' must be equal to 'reg_dword'
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'value' must be equal to '1'
          Collected Item/State Result:
          [ true ]
          • hive equals 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE'
          • key equals 'SOFTWARE\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\CredentialsDelegation'
          • name equals 'AllowProtectedCreds'
          • last_write_time equals '134213413420000000'
          • type equals 'reg_dword'
          • value equals '1'
          • windows_view equals '64_bit'

          V-205865 - Windows Server 2019 Early Launch Antimalware, Boot-Start Driver Initialization Policy must prevent boot drivers identified as bad.

          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-205865r991589_rule
          Test Type:Automated
          Rule Result:Pass
          Version:WN19-CC-000130
          Identities:V-93249
          SV-103337
          CCI-000366 (NIST SP 800-53: CM-6 b; NIST SP 800-53A: CM-6.1 (iv); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: CM-6 b; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: CM-6 b)
          Description: Compromised boot drivers can introduce malware prior to protection mechanisms that load after initialization. The Early Launch Antimalware driver can limit allowed drivers based on classifications determined by the malware protection application. At a minimum, drivers determined to be bad must not be allowed.
          Fix Text:The default behavior is for Early Launch Antimalware - Boot-Start Driver Initialization policy to enforce "Good, unknown and bad but critical" (preventing "bad").

          If this needs to be corrected or a more secure setting is desired, configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Administrative Templates >> System >> Early Launch Antimalware >> "Boot-Start Driver Initialization Policy" to "Not Configured" or "Enabled" with any option other than "All" selected.
          Severity:medium
          Weight:10.0
          Reference:
          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows Server 2019
          Publisher:DISA
          Type:DPMS Target
          Subject:Microsoft Windows Server 2019
          Identifier:2907
          Definitions:
          Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.windows2019:def:205865
          Definition Result:true
          Title:WN19-CC-000130 - Windows Server 2019 Early Launch Antimalware, Boot-Start Driver Initialization Policy must prevent boot drivers identified as bad.
          Description:Compromised boot drivers can introduce malware prior to protection mechanisms that load after initialization. The Early Launch Antimalware driver can limit allowed drivers based on classifications determined by the malware protection application. At a minimum, drivers determined to be bad must not be allowed.
          Class:compliance
          Tests:
          • true (All child tests must be true. (AND))
            • true (All child tests must be true. (AND))
              • true ('Boot-Start Driver Initialization Policy' is not set to 'Enabled' with 'All' selected)
          Tests:
          Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:25434400 (registry_test)
          Result:true
          Title:'Boot-Start Driver Initialization Policy' is not set to 'Enabled' with 'All' selected
          Check Existence:Zero or more collected items may exist.
          Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).
          State Operator:One or more item-state comparisons may be true.
          Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:obj:25337200 (registry_object)
          Object Comment: 
          Object Requirements:
          • check = 'hive' must be equal to 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE'
          • check = 'key' must be equal to 'System\CurrentControlSet\Policies\EarlyLaunch'
          • check = 'name' must be equal to 'DriverLoadPolicy'
          State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:20000001 (registry_state)
          State Comment: 
          State Requirements:
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'type' must be equal to 'reg_dword'
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'value' must be equal to '1'
          State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:20000003 (registry_state)
          State Comment: 
          State Requirements:
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'type' must be equal to 'reg_dword'
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'value' must be equal to '3'
          State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:25337200 (registry_state)
          State Comment:Reg_Dword type and value equals 8
          State Requirements:
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'type' must be equal to 'reg_dword'
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'value' must be equal to '8'

          V-205866 - Windows Server 2019 group policy objects must be reprocessed even if they have not changed.

          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-205866r1135352_rule
          Test Type:Automated
          Rule Applicability:Windows_MemberServer
          Rule Applicability:Windows_StandaloneServer
          Rule Result:Pass
          Version:WN19-CC-000140
          Identities:V-93251
          SV-103339
          CCI-000366 (NIST SP 800-53: CM-6 b; NIST SP 800-53A: CM-6.1 (iv); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: CM-6 b; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: CM-6 b)
          Description: Registry entries for group policy settings can potentially be changed from the required configuration. This could occur as part of troubleshooting or by a malicious process on a compromised system. Enabling this setting and then selecting the "Process even if the Group Policy objects have not changed" option ensures the policies will be reprocessed even if none have been changed. This way, any unauthorized changes are forced to match the domain-based group policy settings again.
          Fix Text:Configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Administrative Templates >> System >> Group Policy >> "Configure registry policy processing" to "Enabled" with the option "Process even if the Group Policy objects have not changed" selected.
          Severity:medium
          Weight:10.0
          Reference:
          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows Server 2019
          Publisher:DISA
          Type:DPMS Target
          Subject:Microsoft Windows Server 2019
          Identifier:2907
          Definitions:
          Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.windows2019:def:205866
          Definition Result:true
          Title:WN19-CC-000140 - Windows Server 2019 group policy objects must be reprocessed even if they have not changed.
          Description:Registry entries for group policy settings can potentially be changed from the required configuration. This could occur as part of troubleshooting or by a malicious process on a compromised system. Enabling this setting and then selecting the "Process even if the Group Policy objects have not changed" option ensures the policies will be reprocessed even if none have been changed. This way, any unauthorized changes are forced to match the domain-based group policy settings again.
          Class:compliance
          Tests:
          • true (All child tests must be true. (AND))
            • true (All child tests must be true. (AND))
              • true (NoGPOistChanges is set to 0.)
          Tests:
          Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:25337300 (registry_test)
          Result:true
          Title:NoGPOistChanges is set to 0.
          Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
          Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).
          Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:obj:25337300 (registry_object)
          Object Comment: 
          Object Requirements:
          • check = 'hive' must be equal to 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE'
          • check = 'key' must be equal to 'Software\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\Group Policy\{35378EAC-683F-11D2-A89A-00C04FBBCFA2}'
          • check = 'name' must be equal to 'NoGPOListChanges'
          State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:20000000 (registry_state)
          State Comment: 
          State Requirements:
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'type' must be equal to 'reg_dword'
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'value' must be equal to '0'
          Collected Item/State Result:
          [ true ]
          • hive equals 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE'
          • key equals 'Software\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\Group Policy\{35378EAC-683F-11D2-A89A-00C04FBBCFA2}'
          • name equals 'NoGPOListChanges'
          • last_write_time equals '134213413420000000'
          • type equals 'reg_dword'
          • value equals '0'
          • windows_view equals '64_bit'

          V-205867 - Windows Server 2019 users must be prompted to authenticate when the system wakes from sleep (on battery).

          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-205867r991589_rule
          Test Type:Automated
          Rule Result:Pass
          Version:WN19-CC-000180
          Identities:V-93253
          SV-103341
          CCI-000366 (NIST SP 800-53: CM-6 b; NIST SP 800-53A: CM-6.1 (iv); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: CM-6 b; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: CM-6 b)
          Description: A system that does not require authentication when resuming from sleep may provide access to unauthorized users. Authentication must always be required when accessing a system. This setting ensures users are prompted for a password when the system wakes from sleep (on battery).
          Fix Text:Configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Administrative Templates >> System >> Power Management >> Sleep Settings >> "Require a password when a computer wakes (on battery)" to "Enabled".
          Severity:medium
          Weight:10.0
          Reference:
          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows Server 2019
          Publisher:DISA
          Type:DPMS Target
          Subject:Microsoft Windows Server 2019
          Identifier:2907
          Definitions:
          Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.windows2019:def:205867
          Definition Result:true
          Title:WN19-CC-000180 - Windows Server 2019 users must be prompted to authenticate when the system wakes from sleep (on battery).
          Description:A system that does not require authentication when resuming from sleep may provide access to unauthorized users. Authentication must always be required when accessing a system. This setting ensures users are prompted for a password when the system wakes from sleep (on battery).
          Class:compliance
          Tests:
          • true (All child tests must be true. (AND))
            • true (All child tests must be true. (AND))
              • true ('Require a password when a computer wakes (on battery)' is set to 'Enabled')
          Tests:
          Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:25338000 (registry_test)
          Result:true
          Title:'Require a password when a computer wakes (on battery)' is set to 'Enabled'
          Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
          Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).
          Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:obj:25338000 (registry_object)
          Object Comment: 
          Object Requirements:
          • check = 'hive' must be equal to 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE'
          • check = 'key' must be equal to 'SOFTWARE\Policies\Microsoft\Power\PowerSettings\0e796bdb-100d-47d6-a2d5-f7d2daa51f51'
          • check = 'name' must be equal to 'DCSettingIndex'
          State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:20000001 (registry_state)
          State Comment: 
          State Requirements:
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'type' must be equal to 'reg_dword'
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'value' must be equal to '1'
          Collected Item/State Result:
          [ true ]
          • hive equals 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE'
          • key equals 'SOFTWARE\Policies\Microsoft\Power\PowerSettings\0e796bdb-100d-47d6-a2d5-f7d2daa51f51'
          • name equals 'DCSettingIndex'
          • last_write_time equals '134213413420000000'
          • type equals 'reg_dword'
          • value equals '1'
          • windows_view equals '64_bit'

This setting ensures users are prompted for a password when the system wakes from sleep (plugged in).

          V-205869 - Windows Server 2019 Telemetry must be configured to Security or Basic.

          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-205869r991589_rule
          Test Type:Automated
          Rule Result:Pass
          Version:WN19-CC-000250
          Identities:SV-103345
          V-93257
          CCI-000366 (NIST SP 800-53: CM-6 b; NIST SP 800-53A: CM-6.1 (iv); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: CM-6 b; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: CM-6 b)
          Description: Some features may communicate with the vendor, sending system information or downloading data or components for the feature. Limiting this capability will prevent potentially sensitive information from being sent outside the enterprise. The "Security" option for Telemetry configures the lowest amount of data, effectively none outside of the Malicious Software Removal Tool (MSRT), Defender, and telemetry client settings. "Basic" sends basic diagnostic and usage data and may be required to support some Microsoft services.
          Fix Text:Configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Administrative Templates >> Windows Components >> Data Collection >> "Allow Telemetry" to "Enabled" with "0 - Security [Enterprise Only]" or "1 - Basic" selected in "Options".
          Severity:medium
          Weight:10.0
          Reference:
          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows Server 2019
          Publisher:DISA
          Type:DPMS Target
          Subject:Microsoft Windows Server 2019
          Identifier:2907
          Definitions:
          Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.windows2019:def:205869
          Definition Result:true
          Title:WN19-CC-000250 - Windows Server 2019 Telemetry must be configured to Security or Basic.
          Description:Some features may communicate with the vendor, sending system information or downloading data or components for the feature. Limiting this capability will prevent potentially sensitive information from being sent outside the enterprise. The "Security" option for Telemetry configures the lowest amount of data, effectively none outside of the Malicious Software Removal Tool (MSRT), Defender, and telemetry client settings. "Basic" sends basic diagnostic and usage data and may be required to support some Microsoft services.
          Class:compliance
          Tests:
          • true (All child tests must be true. (AND))
            • true (All child tests must be true. (AND))
              • true ('Allow Diagnostic Data' is set to 'Enabled' with 'Diagnostic Data Off (Not recommended)' or 'Send Required Diagnostic Data' selected in 'Options:')
          Tests:
          Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:25339300 (registry_test)
          Result:true
          Title:'Allow Diagnostic Data' is set to 'Enabled' with 'Diagnostic Data Off (Not recommended)' or 'Send Required Diagnostic Data' selected in 'Options:'
          Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
          Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).
          State Operator:One or more item-state comparisons may be true.
          Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:obj:25339300 (registry_object)
          Object Comment: 
          Object Requirements:
          • check = 'hive' must be equal to 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE'
          • check = 'key' must be equal to 'SOFTWARE\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\DataCollection'
          • check = 'name' must be equal to 'AllowTelemetry'
          State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:20000000 (registry_state)
          State Comment: 
          State Requirements:
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'type' must be equal to 'reg_dword'
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'value' must be equal to '0'
          State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:20000001 (registry_state)
          State Comment: 
          State Requirements:
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'type' must be equal to 'reg_dword'
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'value' must be equal to '1'
          Collected Item/State Result:
          [ true ]
          • hive equals 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE'
          • key equals 'SOFTWARE\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\DataCollection'
          • name equals 'AllowTelemetry'
          • last_write_time equals '134213413420000000'
          • type equals 'reg_dword'
          • value equals '1'
          • windows_view equals '64_bit'

          V-205872 - Windows Server 2019 File Explorer shell protocol must run in protected mode.

          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-205872r991589_rule
          Test Type:Automated
          Rule Result:Pass
          Version:WN19-CC-000330
          Identities:V-93263
          SV-103351
          CCI-000366 (NIST SP 800-53: CM-6 b; NIST SP 800-53A: CM-6.1 (iv); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: CM-6 b; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: CM-6 b)
          Description: The shell protocol will limit the set of folders that applications can open when run in protected mode. Restricting files an application can open to a limited set of folders increases the security of Windows.
          Fix Text:The default behavior is for shell protected mode to be turned on for File Explorer.

          If this needs to be corrected, configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Administrative Templates >> Windows Components >> File Explorer >> "Turn off shell protocol protected mode" to "Not Configured" or "Disabled".
          Severity:medium
          Weight:10.0
          Reference:
          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows Server 2019
          Publisher:DISA
          Type:DPMS Target
          Subject:Microsoft Windows Server 2019
          Identifier:2907
          Definitions:
          Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.windows2019:def:205872
          Definition Result:true
          Title:WN19-CC-000330 - Windows Server 2019 File Explorer shell protocol must run in protected mode.
          Description:The shell protocol will limit the set of folders that applications can open when run in protected mode. Restricting files an application can open to a limited set of folders increases the security of Windows.
          Class:compliance
          Tests:
          • true (All child tests must be true. (AND))
            • true (All child tests must be true. (AND))
              • true ('Turn off shell protocol protected mode' is set to 'Disabled')
          Tests:
          Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:25339800 (registry_test)
          Result:true
          Title:'Turn off shell protocol protected mode' is set to 'Disabled'
          Check Existence:Zero or more collected items may exist.
          Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).
          Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:obj:25339800 (registry_object)
          Object Comment: 
          Object Requirements:
          • check = 'hive' must be equal to 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE'
          • check = 'key' must be equal to 'SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Policies\Explorer'
          • check = 'name' must be equal to 'PreXPSP2ShellProtocolBehavior'
          State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:20000000 (registry_state)
          State Comment: 
          State Requirements:
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'type' must be equal to 'reg_dword'
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'value' must be equal to '0'

          V-205873 - Windows Server 2019 must prevent attachments from being downloaded from RSS feeds.

          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-205873r991589_rule
          Test Type:Automated
          Rule Result:Pass
          Version:WN19-CC-000390
          Identities:V-93265
          SV-103353
          CCI-000366 (NIST SP 800-53: CM-6 b; NIST SP 800-53A: CM-6.1 (iv); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: CM-6 b; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: CM-6 b)
          Description: Attachments from RSS feeds may not be secure. This setting will prevent attachments from being downloaded from RSS feeds.
          Fix Text:Configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Administrative Templates >> Windows Components >> RSS Feeds >> "Prevent downloading of enclosures" to "Enabled".
          Severity:medium
          Weight:10.0
          Reference:
          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows Server 2019
          Publisher:DISA
          Type:DPMS Target
          Subject:Microsoft Windows Server 2019
          Identifier:2907
          Definitions:
          Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.windows2019:def:205873
          Definition Result:true
          Title:WN19-CC-000390 - Windows Server 2019 must prevent attachments from being downloaded from RSS feeds.
          Description:Attachments from RSS feeds may not be secure. This setting will prevent attachments from being downloaded from RSS feeds.
          Class:compliance
          Tests:
          • true (All child tests must be true. (AND))
            • true (All child tests must be true. (AND))
              • true ('Prevent downloading of enclosures' is set to 'Enabled')
          Tests:
          Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:25340700 (registry_test)
          Result:true
          Title:'Prevent downloading of enclosures' is set to 'Enabled'
          Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
          Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).
          Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:obj:25340700 (registry_object)
          Object Comment: 
          Object Requirements:
          • check = 'hive' must be equal to 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE'
          • check = 'key' must be equal to 'SOFTWARE\Policies\Microsoft\Internet Explorer\Feeds'
          • check = 'name' must be equal to 'DisableEnclosureDownload'
          State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:20000001 (registry_state)
          State Comment: 
          State Requirements:
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'type' must be equal to 'reg_dword'
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'value' must be equal to '1'
          Collected Item/State Result:
          [ true ]
          • hive equals 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE'
          • key equals 'SOFTWARE\Policies\Microsoft\Internet Explorer\Feeds'
          • name equals 'DisableEnclosureDownload'
          • last_write_time equals '134213413420000000'
          • type equals 'reg_dword'
          • value equals '1'
          • windows_view equals '64_bit'

          V-205874 - Windows Server 2019 users must be notified if a web-based program attempts to install software.

          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-205874r991589_rule
          Test Type:Automated
          Rule Result:Pass
          Version:WN19-CC-000440
          Identities:SV-103355
          V-93267
          CCI-000366 (NIST SP 800-53: CM-6 b; NIST SP 800-53A: CM-6.1 (iv); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: CM-6 b; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: CM-6 b)
          Description: Web-based programs may attempt to install malicious software on a system. Ensuring users are notified if a web-based program attempts to install software allows them to refuse the installation.
          Fix Text:The default behavior is for Internet Explorer to warn users and select whether to allow or refuse installation when a web-based program attempts to install software on the system.

          If this needs to be corrected, configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Administrative Templates >> Windows Components >> Windows Installer >> "Prevent Internet Explorer security prompt for Windows Installer scripts" to "Not Configured" or "Disabled".
          Severity:medium
          Weight:10.0
          Reference:
          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows Server 2019
          Publisher:DISA
          Type:DPMS Target
          Subject:Microsoft Windows Server 2019
          Identifier:2907
          Definitions:
          Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.windows2019:def:205874
          Definition Result:true
          Title:WN19-CC-000440 - Windows Server 2019 users must be notified if a web-based program attempts to install software.
          Description:Web-based programs may attempt to install malicious software on a system. Ensuring users are notified if a web-based program attempts to install software allows them to refuse the installation.
          Class:compliance
          Tests:
          • true (All child tests must be true. (AND))
            • true (All child tests must be true. (AND))
              • true ('Prevent Internet Explorer security prompt for Windows Installer scripts' is set to 'Not Configured' or 'Disabled')
          Tests:
          Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:25341200 (registry_test)
          Result:true
          Title:'Prevent Internet Explorer security prompt for Windows Installer scripts' is set to 'Not Configured' or 'Disabled'
          Check Existence:Zero or more collected items may exist.
          Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).
          Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:obj:25341200 (registry_object)
          Object Comment: 
          Object Requirements:
          • check = 'hive' must be equal to 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE'
          • check = 'key' must be equal to 'Software\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\Installer'
          • check = 'name' must be equal to 'SafeForScripting'
          State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:20000000 (registry_state)
          State Comment: 
          State Requirements:
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'type' must be equal to 'reg_dword'
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'value' must be equal to '0'

          V-205876 - Windows Server 2019 domain controllers must be configured to allow reset of machine account passwords.

          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-205876r991589_rule
          Test Type:Automated
          Rule Applicability:Windows_DomainController
          Rule Result:Not Applicable
          Version:WN19-DC-000330
          Identities:V-93273
          SV-103361
          CCI-000366 (NIST SP 800-53: CM-6 b; NIST SP 800-53A: CM-6.1 (iv); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: CM-6 b; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: CM-6 b)
          Description: Enabling this setting on all domain controllers in a domain prevents domain members from changing their computer account passwords. If these passwords are weak or compromised, the inability to change them may leave these computers vulnerable.
          Fix Text:Configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Windows Settings >> Security Settings >> Local Policies >> Security Options >> "Domain controller: Refuse machine account password changes" to "Disabled".
          Severity:medium
          Weight:10.0
          Reference:
          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows Server 2019
          Publisher:DISA
          Type:DPMS Target
          Subject:Microsoft Windows Server 2019
          Identifier:2907

          V-205906 - Windows Server 2019 must limit the caching of logon credentials to four or less on domain-joined member servers.

          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-205906r991589_rule
          Test Type:Automated
          Rule Applicability:Windows_MemberServer
          Rule Result:Pass
          Version:WN19-MS-000050
          Identities:SV-103363
          V-93275
          CCI-000366 (NIST SP 800-53: CM-6 b; NIST SP 800-53A: CM-6.1 (iv); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: CM-6 b; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: CM-6 b)
          Description: The default Windows configuration caches the last logon credentials for users who log on interactively to a system. This feature is provided for system availability reasons, such as the user's machine being disconnected from the network or domain controllers being unavailable. Even though the credential cache is well protected, if a system is attacked, an unauthorized individual may isolate the password to a domain user account using a password-cracking program and gain access to the domain.
          Fix Text:Configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Windows Settings >> Security Settings >> Local Policies >> Security Options >> "Interactive Logon: Number of previous logons to cache (in case Domain Controller is not available)" to "4" logons or less.
          Severity:medium
          Weight:10.0
          Reference:
          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows Server 2019
          Publisher:DISA
          Type:DPMS Target
          Subject:Microsoft Windows Server 2019
          Identifier:2907
          Definitions:
          Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.windows2019:def:205906
          Definition Result:true
          Title:WN19-MS-000050 - Windows Server 2019 must limit the caching of logon credentials to four or less on domain-joined member servers.
          Description:The default Windows configuration caches the last logon credentials for users who log on interactively to a system. This feature is provided for system availability reasons, such as the user's machine being disconnected from the network or domain controllers being unavailable. Even though the credential cache is well protected, if a system is attacked, an unauthorized individual may isolate the password to a domain user account using a password-cracking program and gain access to the domain.
          Class:compliance
          Tests:
          • true (All child tests must be true. (AND))
            • true (All child tests must be true. (AND))
              • true ('Interactive logon: Number of previous logons to cache' is set to 4 or less)
          Tests:
          Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:25443200 (registry_test)
          Result:true
          Title:'Interactive logon: Number of previous logons to cache' is set to 4 or less
          Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
          Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).
          Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:obj:25344700 (registry_object)
          Object Comment: 
          Object Requirements:
          • check = 'hive' must be equal to 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE'
          • check = 'key' must be equal to 'SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Windows NT\CurrentVersion\Winlogon'
          • check = 'name' must be equal to 'CachedLogonsCount'
          State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:25443200 (registry_state)
          State Comment:Reg_sz value of 4 or less
          State Requirements:
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'type' must be equal to 'reg_sz'
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'value' must be less than or equal to '4'
          Collected Item/State Result:
          [ true ]
          • hive equals 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE'
          • key equals 'SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Windows NT\CurrentVersion\Winlogon'
          • name equals 'CachedLogonsCount'
          • last_write_time equals '134212836130000000'
          • type equals 'reg_sz'
          • value equals '4'
          • windows_view equals '64_bit'

          V-205909 - Windows Server 2019 built-in administrator account must be renamed.

          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-205909r991589_rule
          Test Type:Automated
          Rule Result:Pass
          Version:WN19-SO-000030
          Identities:V-93281
          SV-103369
          CCI-000366 (NIST SP 800-53: CM-6 b; NIST SP 800-53A: CM-6.1 (iv); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: CM-6 b; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: CM-6 b)
          Description: The built-in administrator account is a well-known account subject to attack. Renaming this account to an unidentified name improves the protection of this account and the system.
          Fix Text:Configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Windows Settings >> Security Settings >> Local Policies >> Security Options >> "Accounts: Rename administrator account" to a name other than "Administrator".
          Severity:medium
          Weight:10.0
          Reference:
          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows Server 2019
          Publisher:DISA
          Type:DPMS Target
          Subject:Microsoft Windows Server 2019
          Identifier:2907
          Definitions:
          Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.windows2019:def:205909
          Definition Result:true
          Title:WN19-SO-000030 - Windows Server 2019 built-in administrator account must be renamed.
          Description:The built-in administrator account is a well-known account subject to attack. Renaming this account to an unidentified name improves the protection of this account and the system.
          Class:compliance
          Tests:
          • true (All child tests must be true. (AND))
            • true (All child tests must be true. (AND))
              • true ('Accounts: Rename administrator account' is not set to 'Administrator' or 'localdomain\Administrator')
          Tests:
          Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:25343500 (sid_sid_test)
          Result:true
          Title:'Accounts: Rename administrator account' is not set to 'Administrator' or 'localdomain\Administrator'
          Check Existence:Zero or more collected items may exist.
          Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).
          State Operator:All item-state comparisons must be true.
          Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:obj:25343500 (sid_sid_object)
          Object Comment:Built-in Administrator SID
          Object Requirements:
          • check = 'trustee_sid' must match the pattern '^S-1-5-[0-9-]+-500$'
          State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:25343500 (sid_sid_state)
          State Comment:Not Administrator
          State Requirements:
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'trustee_name' must not be equal (case insensitive) to 'Administrator'
          State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:25343501 (sid_sid_state)
          State Comment:Not localdomain\Administrator
          State Requirements:
          • for alltrustee_name, the following must be true:
            • trustee_name must not be equal (case insensitive) to 'win2019-app-01\Administrator'
          Collected Item/State Result:
          [ true ]
          • trustee_sid equals 'S-1-5-21-227057802-2767407349-872160095-500'
          • trustee_name equals 'X_Admin'
          • trustee_domain equals 'win2019-app-01'

          V-205910 - Windows Server 2019 built-in guest account must be renamed.

          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-205910r991589_rule
          Test Type:Automated
          Rule Result:Pass
          Version:WN19-SO-000040
          Identities:SV-103371
          V-93283
          CCI-000366 (NIST SP 800-53: CM-6 b; NIST SP 800-53A: CM-6.1 (iv); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: CM-6 b; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: CM-6 b)
          Description: The built-in guest account is a well-known user account on all Windows systems and, as initially installed, does not require a password. This can allow access to system resources by unauthorized users. Renaming this account to an unidentified name improves the protection of this account and the system.
          Fix Text:Configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Windows Settings >> Security Settings >> Local Policies >> Security Options >> "Accounts: Rename guest account" to a name other than "Guest".
          Severity:medium
          Weight:10.0
          Reference:
          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows Server 2019
          Publisher:DISA
          Type:DPMS Target
          Subject:Microsoft Windows Server 2019
          Identifier:2907
          Definitions:
          Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.windows2019:def:205910
          Definition Result:true
          Title:WN19-SO-000040 - Windows Server 2019 built-in guest account must be renamed.
          Description:The built-in guest account is a well-known user account on all Windows systems and, as initially installed, does not require a password. This can allow access to system resources by unauthorized users. Renaming this account to an unidentified name improves the protection of this account and the system.
          Class:compliance
          Tests:
          • true (All child tests must be true. (AND))
            • true (All child tests must be true. (AND))
              • true ('Accounts: Rename Guest account' is not set to 'Guest' or 'localdomain\Guest')
          Tests:
          Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:25343600 (sid_sid_test)
          Result:true
          Title:'Accounts: Rename Guest account' is not set to 'Guest' or 'localdomain\Guest'
          Check Existence:Zero or more collected items may exist.
          Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).
          State Operator:All item-state comparisons must be true.
          Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:obj:25343600 (sid_sid_object)
          Object Comment:Built-in Guest SID
          Object Requirements:
          • check = 'trustee_sid' must match the pattern '^S-1-5-[0-9-]+-501$'
          State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:25343600 (sid_sid_state)
          State Comment:Not Guest
          State Requirements:
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'trustee_name' must not be equal (case insensitive) to 'Guest'
          State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:25343601 (sid_sid_state)
          State Comment:Not localdomain\Guest
          State Requirements:
          • for alltrustee_name, the following must be true:
            • trustee_name must not be equal (case insensitive) to 'win2019-app-01\Guest'
          Collected Item/State Result:
          [ true ]
          • trustee_sid equals 'S-1-5-21-227057802-2767407349-872160095-501'
          • trustee_name equals 'Visitor'
          • trustee_domain equals 'win2019-app-01'

          V-205911 - Windows Server 2019 maximum age for machine account passwords must be configured to 30 days or less.

          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-205911r991589_rule
          Test Type:Automated
          Rule Result:Pass
          Version:WN19-SO-000100
          Identities:SV-103373
          V-93285
          CCI-000366 (NIST SP 800-53: CM-6 b; NIST SP 800-53A: CM-6.1 (iv); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: CM-6 b; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: CM-6 b)
          Description: Computer account passwords are changed automatically on a regular basis. This setting controls the maximum password age that a machine account may have. This must be set to no more than 30 days, ensuring the machine changes its password monthly.
          Fix Text:This is the default configuration for this setting (30 days).

          Configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Windows Settings >> Security Settings >> Local Policies >> Security Options >> "Domain member: Maximum machine account password age" to "30" or less (excluding "0", which is unacceptable).
          Severity:medium
          Weight:10.0
          Reference:
          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows Server 2019
          Publisher:DISA
          Type:DPMS Target
          Subject:Microsoft Windows Server 2019
          Identifier:2907
          Definitions:
          Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.windows2019:def:205911
          Definition Result:true
          Title:WN19-SO-000100 - Windows Server 2019 maximum age for machine account passwords must be configured to 30 days or less.
          Description:Computer account passwords are changed automatically on a regular basis. This setting controls the maximum password age that a machine account may have. This must be set to no more than 30 days, ensuring the machine changes its password monthly.
          Class:compliance
          Tests:
          • true (All child tests must be true. (AND))
            • true (All child tests must be true. (AND))
              • true ('Domain member: Maximum machine account password age' is set to 30 or less (but not 0).)
          Tests:
          Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:25344200 (registry_test)
          Result:true
          Title:'Domain member: Maximum machine account password age' is set to 30 or less (but not 0).
          Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
          Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).
          State Operator:All item-state comparisons must be true.
          Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:obj:25344200 (registry_object)
          Object Comment: 
          Object Requirements:
          • check = 'hive' must be equal to 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE'
          • check = 'key' must be equal to 'SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Services\Netlogon\Parameters'
          • check = 'name' must be equal to 'MaximumPasswordAge'
          State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:25344200 (registry_state)
          State Comment: 
          State Requirements:
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'type' must be equal to 'reg_dword'
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'value' must be greater than '0'
          State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:25344201 (registry_state)
          State Comment: 
          State Requirements:
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'type' must be equal to 'reg_dword'
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'value' must be less than or equal to '30'
          Collected Item/State Result:
          [ true ]
          • hive equals 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE'
          • key equals 'SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Services\Netlogon\Parameters'
          • name equals 'MaximumPasswordAge'
          • last_write_time equals '134212706400000000'
          • type equals 'reg_dword'
          • value equals '30'
          • windows_view equals '64_bit'

          V-205912 - Windows Server 2019 Smart Card removal option must be configured to Force Logoff or Lock Workstation.

          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-205912r991589_rule
          Test Type:Automated
          Rule Result:Pass
          Version:WN19-SO-000150
          Identities:V-93287
          SV-103375
          CCI-000366 (NIST SP 800-53: CM-6 b; NIST SP 800-53A: CM-6.1 (iv); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: CM-6 b; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: CM-6 b)
          Description: Unattended systems are susceptible to unauthorized use and must be locked. Configuring a system to lock when a smart card is removed will ensure the system is inaccessible when unattended.
          Fix Text:Configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Windows Settings >> Security Settings >> Local Policies >> Security Options >> "Interactive logon: Smart card removal behavior" to "Lock Workstation" or "Force Logoff".
          Severity:medium
          Weight:10.0
          Reference:
          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows Server 2019
          Publisher:DISA
          Type:DPMS Target
          Subject:Microsoft Windows Server 2019
          Identifier:2907
          Definitions:
          Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.windows2019:def:205912
          Definition Result:true
          Title:WN19-SO-000150 - Windows Server 2019 Smart Card removal option must be configured to Force Logoff or Lock Workstation.
          Description:Unattended systems are susceptible to unauthorized use and must be locked. Configuring a system to lock when a smart card is removed will ensure the system is inaccessible when unattended.
          Class:compliance
          Tests:
          • true (All child tests must be true. (AND))
            • true (All child tests must be true. (AND))
              • true ('Interactive logon: Smart card removal behavior' is set to 'Lock Workstation' or 'Force Logoff')
          Tests:
          Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:25344800 (registry_test)
          Result:true
          Title:'Interactive logon: Smart card removal behavior' is set to 'Lock Workstation' or 'Force Logoff'
          Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
          Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).
          State Operator:One or more item-state comparisons may be true.
          Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:obj:25344800 (registry_object)
          Object Comment: 
          Object Requirements:
          • check = 'hive' must be equal to 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE'
          • check = 'key' must be equal to 'SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Windows NT\CurrentVersion\Winlogon'
          • check = 'name' must be equal to 'SCRemoveOption'
          State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:25445900 (registry_state)
          State Comment: 
          State Requirements:
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'type' must be equal to 'reg_sz'
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'value' must be equal to '1'
          State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:25445901 (registry_state)
          State Comment: 
          State Requirements:
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'type' must be equal to 'reg_sz'
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'value' must be equal to '2'
          Collected Item/State Result:
          [ true ]
          • hive equals 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE'
          • key equals 'SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Windows NT\CurrentVersion\Winlogon'
          • name equals 'SCRemoveOption'
          • last_write_time equals '134212836130000000'
          • type equals 'reg_sz'
          • value equals '1'
          • windows_view equals '64_bit'

          V-205915 - Windows Server 2019 must be configured to prevent anonymous users from having the same permissions as the Everyone group.

          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-205915r991589_rule
          Test Type:Automated
          Rule Result:Pass
          Version:WN19-SO-000240
          Identities:V-93293
          SV-103381
          CCI-000366 (NIST SP 800-53: CM-6 b; NIST SP 800-53A: CM-6.1 (iv); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: CM-6 b; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: CM-6 b)
          Description: Access by anonymous users must be restricted. If this setting is enabled, anonymous users have the same rights and permissions as the built-in Everyone group. Anonymous users must not have these permissions or rights.
          Fix Text:Configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Windows Settings >> Security Settings >> Local Policies >> Security Options >> "Network access: Let Everyone permissions apply to anonymous users" to "Disabled".
          Severity:medium
          Weight:10.0
          Reference:
          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows Server 2019
          Publisher:DISA
          Type:DPMS Target
          Subject:Microsoft Windows Server 2019
          Identifier:2907
          Definitions:
          Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.windows2019:def:205915
          Definition Result:true
          Title:WN19-SO-000240 - Windows Server 2019 must be configured to prevent anonymous users from having the same permissions as the Everyone group.
          Description:Access by anonymous users must be restricted. If this setting is enabled, anonymous users have the same rights and permissions as the built-in Everyone group. Anonymous users must not have these permissions or rights.
          Class:compliance
          Tests:
          • true (All child tests must be true. (AND))
            • true (All child tests must be true. (AND))
              • true ('Network access: Let Everyone permissions apply to anonymous users' is set to 'Disabled')
          Tests:
          Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:25345500 (registry_test)
          Result:true
          Title:'Network access: Let Everyone permissions apply to anonymous users' is set to 'Disabled'
          Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
          Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).
          Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:obj:25345500 (registry_object)
          Object Comment: 
          Object Requirements:
          • check = 'hive' must be equal to 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE'
          • check = 'key' must be equal to 'SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Control\Lsa'
          • check = 'name' must be equal (case insensitive) to 'EveryoneIncludesAnonymous'
          State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:20000000 (registry_state)
          State Comment: 
          State Requirements:
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'type' must be equal to 'reg_dword'
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'value' must be equal to '0'
          Collected Item/State Result:
          [ true ]
          • hive equals 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE'
          • key equals 'SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Control\Lsa'
          • name equals 'EveryoneIncludesAnonymous'
          • last_write_time equals '134212907550000000'
          • type equals 'reg_dword'
          • value equals '0'
          • windows_view equals '64_bit'

          V-205916 - Windows Server 2019 services using Local System that use Negotiate when reverting to NTLM authentication must use the computer identity instead of authenticating anonymously.

          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-205916r991589_rule
          Test Type:Automated
          Rule Result:Pass
          Version:WN19-SO-000260
          Identities:SV-103383
          V-93295
          CCI-000366 (NIST SP 800-53: CM-6 b; NIST SP 800-53A: CM-6.1 (iv); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: CM-6 b; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: CM-6 b)
          Description: Services using Local System that use Negotiate when reverting to NTLM authentication may gain unauthorized access if allowed to authenticate anonymously versus using the computer identity.
          Fix Text:Configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Windows Settings >> Security Settings >> Local Policies >> Security Options >> "Network security: Allow Local System to use computer identity for NTLM" to "Enabled".
          Severity:medium
          Weight:10.0
          Reference:
          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows Server 2019
          Publisher:DISA
          Type:DPMS Target
          Subject:Microsoft Windows Server 2019
          Identifier:2907
          Definitions:
          Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.windows2019:def:205916
          Definition Result:true
          Title:WN19-SO-000260 - Windows Server 2019 services using Local System that use Negotiate when reverting to NTLM authentication must use the computer identity instead of authenticating anonymously.
          Description:Services using Local System that use Negotiate when reverting to NTLM authentication may gain unauthorized access if allowed to authenticate anonymously versus using the computer identity.
          Class:compliance
          Tests:
          • true (All child tests must be true. (AND))
            • true (All child tests must be true. (AND))
              • true ('Network security: Allow Local System to use computer identity for NTLM' is set to 'Enabled'.)
          Tests:
          Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:25447000 (registry_test)

          Object Comment:

          • check = 'hive' must be equal (case insensitive) to 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE'
          • check = 'key' must be equal (case insensitive) to 'SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Control\LSA'
          • check = 'name' must be equal (case insensitive) to 'UseMachineId'

          • windows_view equals '64_bit'

          V-205917 - Windows Server 2019 must prevent NTLM from falling back to a Null session.

          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-205917r991589_rule
          Test Type:Automated
          Rule Result:Pass
          Version:WN19-SO-000270
          Identities:V-93297
          SV-103385
          CCI-000366 (NIST SP 800-53: CM-6 b; NIST SP 800-53A: CM-6.1 (iv); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: CM-6 b; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: CM-6 b)
          Description: NTLM sessions that are allowed to fall back to Null (unauthenticated) sessions may gain unauthorized access.
          Fix Text:Configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Windows Settings >> Security Settings >> Local Policies >> Security Options >> "Network security: Allow LocalSystem NULL session fallback" to "Disabled".
          Severity:medium
          Weight:10.0
          Reference:
          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows Server 2019
          Publisher:DISA
          Type:DPMS Target
          Subject:Microsoft Windows Server 2019

          Class:compliance
          Tests:
          • true (All child tests must be true. (AND))
            • true (All child tests must be true. (AND))
              • true ('Network security: Allow LocalSystem NULL session fallback' is set to 'Disabled')

          Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).

          Object Comment:

          • check = 'key' must be equal to 'SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Control\LSA\MSV1_0'
