          V-205918 - Windows Server 2019 must prevent PKU2U authentication using online identities.

          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-205918r991589_rule
          Test Type:Automated
          Rule Result:Pass
          Version:WN19-SO-000280
          Identities:SV-103387
          V-93299
          CCI-000366 (NIST SP 800-53: CM-6 b; NIST SP 800-53A: CM-6.1 (iv); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: CM-6 b; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: CM-6 b)
          Description: PKU2U is a peer-to-peer authentication protocol. This setting prevents online identities from authenticating to domain-joined systems. Authentication will be centrally managed with Windows user accounts.
          Fix Text:Configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Windows Settings >> Security Settings >> Local Policies >> Security Options >> "Network security: Allow PKU2U authentication requests to this computer to use online identities" to "Disabled".
          Severity:medium
          Weight:10.0
          Reference:
          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows Server 2019
          Publisher:DISA
          Type:DPMS Target
          Subject:Microsoft Windows Server 2019
          Identifier:2907
          Definitions:
          Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.windows2019:def:205918
          Definition Result:true
          Title:WN19-SO-000280 - Windows Server 2019 must prevent PKU2U authentication using online identities.
          Description:PKU2U is a peer-to-peer authentication protocol. This setting prevents online identities from authenticating to domain-joined systems. Authentication will be centrally managed with Windows user accounts.
          Class:compliance
          Tests:
          • true (All child tests must be true. (AND))
            • true (All child tests must be true. (AND))
              • true ('Network Security: Allow PKU2U authentication requests to this computer to use online identities' is set to 'Disabled')
          Tests:
          Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:25345900 (registry_test)
          Result:true
          Title:'Network Security: Allow PKU2U authentication requests to this computer to use online identities' is set to 'Disabled'
          Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
          Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).
          Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:obj:25345900 (registry_object)
          Object Comment:Check the registry value at HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Control\LSA\pku2u\AllowOnlineID
          Object Requirements:
          • check = 'hive' must be equal to 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE'
          • check = 'key' must be equal to 'SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Control\LSA\pku2u'
          • check = 'name' must be equal to 'AllowOnlineID'
          State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:20000000 (registry_state)
          State Comment: 
          State Requirements:
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'type' must be equal to 'reg_dword'
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'value' must be equal to '0'

          V-205920 - Windows Server 2019 must be configured to at least negotiate signing for LDAP client signing.

          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-205920r991589_rule
          Test Type:Automated
          Rule Result:Pass
          Version:WN19-SO-000320
          Identities:SV-103391
          V-93303
          CCI-000366 (NIST SP 800-53: CM-6 b; NIST SP 800-53A: CM-6.1 (iv); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: CM-6 b; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: CM-6 b)
          Description: This setting controls the signing requirements for LDAP clients. This must be set to "Negotiate signing" or "Require signing", depending on the environment and type of LDAP server in use.
          Fix Text:Configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Windows Settings >> Security Settings >> Local Policies >> Security Options >> "Network security: LDAP client signing requirements" to "Negotiate signing" at a minimum.
          Severity:medium
          Weight:10.0
          Reference:
          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows Server 2019
          Publisher:DISA
          Type:DPMS Target
          Subject:Microsoft Windows Server 2019
          Identifier:2907
          Definitions:
          Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.windows2019:def:205920
          Definition Result:true
          Title:WN19-SO-000320 - Windows Server 2019 must be configured to at least negotiate signing for LDAP client signing.
          Description:This setting controls the signing requirements for LDAP clients. This must be set to "Negotiate signing" or "Require signing", depending on the environment and type of LDAP server in use.
          Class:compliance
          Tests:
          • true (All child tests must be true. (AND))
            • true (All child tests must be true. (AND))
              • true ('Network security: LDAP client signing requirements' is set to 'Negotiate signing' or 'Require signing')
          Tests:
          Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:25346300 (registry_test)
          Result:true
          Title:'Network security: LDAP client signing requirements' is set to 'Negotiate signing' or 'Require signing'
          Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
          Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).
          State Operator:One or more item-state comparisons may be true.
          Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:obj:25346300 (registry_object)
          Object Comment: 
          Object Requirements:
          • check = 'hive' must be equal to 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE'
          • check = 'key' must be equal to 'SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Services\LDAP'
          • check = 'name' must be equal to 'LDAPClientIntegrity'
          State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:20000001 (registry_state)
          State Comment: 
          State Requirements:
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'type' must be equal to 'reg_dword'
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'value' must be equal to '1'
          State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:20000002 (registry_state)
          State Comment: 
          State Requirements:
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'type' must be equal to 'reg_dword'
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'value' must be equal to '2'
          Collected Item/State Result:
          [ true ]
          • hive equals 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE'
          • key equals 'SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Services\LDAP'
          • name equals 'LDAPClientIntegrity'
          • last_write_time equals '134208719680000000'
          • type equals 'reg_dword'
          • value equals '1'
          • windows_view equals '64_bit'

          V-205921 - Windows Server 2019 session security for NTLM SSP-based clients must be configured to require NTLMv2 session security and 128-bit encryption.

          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-205921r991589_rule
          Test Type:Automated
          Rule Result:Pass
          Version:WN19-SO-000330
          Identities:SV-103393
          V-93305
          CCI-000366 (NIST SP 800-53: CM-6 b; NIST SP 800-53A: CM-6.1 (iv); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: CM-6 b; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: CM-6 b)
          Description: Microsoft has implemented a variety of security support providers for use with Remote Procedure Call (RPC) sessions. All of the options must be enabled to ensure the maximum security level.
          Fix Text:Configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Windows Settings >> Security Settings >> Local Policies >> Security Options >> "Network security: Minimum session security for NTLM SSP based (including secure RPC) clients" to "Require NTLMv2 session security" and "Require 128-bit encryption" (all options selected).
          Severity:medium
          Weight:10.0
          Reference:
          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows Server 2019
          Publisher:DISA
          Type:DPMS Target
          Subject:Microsoft Windows Server 2019
          Identifier:2907
          Definitions:
          Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.windows2019:def:205921
          Definition Result:true
          Title:WN19-SO-000330 - Windows Server 2019 session security for NTLM SSP-based clients must be configured to require NTLMv2 session security and 128-bit encryption.
          Description:Microsoft has implemented a variety of security support providers for use with Remote Procedure Call (RPC) sessions. All of the options must be enabled to ensure the maximum security level.
          Class:compliance
          Tests:
          • true (All child tests must be true. (AND))
            • true (All child tests must be true. (AND))
              • true (The system is configured to meet the minimum session security requirement for NTLM SSP based clients.)
          Tests:
          Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:25346400 (registry_test)
          Result:true
          Title:The system is configured to meet the minimum session security requirement for NTLM SSP based clients.
          Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
          Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).
          Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:obj:25346400 (registry_object)
          Object Comment: 
          Object Requirements:
          • check = 'hive' must be equal to 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE'
          • check = 'key' must be equal to 'SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Control\Lsa\MSV1_0'
          • check = 'name' must be equal to 'NTLMMinClientSec'
          State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:25346500 (registry_state)
          State Comment:Value bitwise and 537395200
          State Requirements:
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'type' must be equal to 'reg_dword'
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'value' must be equal to '537395200'
          Collected Item/State Result:
          [ true ]
          • hive equals 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE'
          • key equals 'SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Control\Lsa\MSV1_0'
          • name equals 'NTLMMinClientSec'
          • last_write_time equals '134212788620000000'
          • type equals 'reg_dword'
          • value equals '537395200'
          • windows_view equals '64_bit'

          V-205924 - Windows Server 2019 must preserve zone information when saving attachments.

          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-205924r991589_rule
          Test Type:Automated
          Rule Result:Pass
          Version:WN19-UC-000010
          Identities:SV-103399
          V-93311
          CCI-000366 (NIST SP 800-53: CM-6 b; NIST SP 800-53A: CM-6.1 (iv); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: CM-6 b; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: CM-6 b)
          Description: Attachments from outside sources may contain malicious code. Preserving zone of origin (Internet, intranet, local, restricted) information on file attachments allows Windows to determine risk.
          Fix Text:The default behavior is for Windows to mark file attachments with their zone information.

          If this needs to be corrected, configure the policy value for User Configuration >> Administrative Templates >> Windows Components >> Attachment Manager >> "Do not preserve zone information in file attachments" to "Not Configured" or "Disabled".
          Severity:medium
          Weight:10.0
          Reference:
          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows Server 2019
          Publisher:DISA
          Type:DPMS Target
          Subject:Microsoft Windows Server 2019
          Identifier:2907
          Definitions:
          Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.windows2019:def:205924
          Definition Result:true
          Title:WN19-UC-000010 - Windows Server 2019 must preserve zone information when saving attachments.
          Description:Attachments from outside sources may contain malicious code. Preserving zone of origin (Internet, intranet, local, restricted) information on file attachments allows Windows to determine risk.
          Class:compliance
          Tests:
          • true (All child tests must be true. (AND))
            • true (All child tests must be true. (AND))
              • true ('Do not preserve zone information in file attachments' is set to 'Not Configured' or 'Disabled')
              • true (For the current user, 'Do not preserve zone information in file attachments' is set to 'Not Configured' or 'Disabled')
          Tests:
          Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:25347800 (ntuser_test)
          Result:true
          Title:'Do not preserve zone information in file attachments' is set to 'Not Configured' or 'Disabled'
          Check Existence:Zero or more collected items may exist.
          Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).
          Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:obj:25347800 (ntuser_object)
          Object Comment: 
          Object Requirements:
          • check = 'key' must be equal to 'Software\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Policies\Attachments'
          • check = 'name' must be equal to 'SaveZoneInformation'
          Exclude Items If:
          • enabled equals 'false'
          Exclude Items If:
          • days_since_modified is greater than '35'
          State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:20000023 (ntuser_state)
          State Comment: 
          State Requirements:
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'type' must be equal to 'reg_dword'
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'value' must be equal to '2'
          Collected Item/State Result:
          [ does not exist ]
          • key does not exist
          • sid equals 'S-1-5-21-227057802-2767407349-872160095-500'
          • username equals 'win2019-app-01\X_Admin'
          • account_type equals 'local'
          • logged_on equals 'false'
          • days_since_last_logon equals '1'
          • enabled equals 'true'
          • date_modified equals '134212710200000000'
          • days_since_modified equals '1'
          • filepath equals 'C:\Users\Administrator\ntuser.dat'
          Collected Item/State Result:
          [ does not exist ]
          • key does not exist
          • sid equals 'S-1-5-21-2716079480-2586227850-91879696-1103'

          • account_type equals 'domain'

          • days_since_last_logon equals '1'
          • date_modified equals '134212783120000000'
          • days_since_modified equals '1'
          • filepath equals 'C:\Users\SALam\ntuser.dat'
          Collected Item/State Result:
          [ does not exist ]
          • key does not exist
          • sid equals 'S-1-5-21-2716079480-2586227850-91879696-1106'

          • check = 'name' must be equal to 'SaveZoneInformation'

          V-205925 - Windows Server 2019 must disable automatically signing in the last interactive user after a system-initiated restart.

          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-205925r991591_rule
          Test Type:Automated
          Rule Result:Pass
          Version:WN19-CC-000450
          Identities:SV-103357
          V-93269
          CCI-000366 (NIST SP 800-53: CM-6 b; NIST SP 800-53A: CM-6.1 (iv); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: CM-6 b; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: CM-6 b)
          Description: Windows can be configured to automatically sign the user back in after a Windows Update restart. Some protections are in place to help ensure this is done in a secure fashion; however, disabling this will prevent the caching of credentials for this purpose and also ensure the user is aware of the restart.
          Fix Text:Configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Administrative Templates >> Windows Components >> Windows Logon Options >> "Sign-in last interactive user automatically after a system-initiated restart" to "Disabled".
          Severity:medium
          Weight:10.0
          Reference:
          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows Server 2019
          Publisher:DISA
          Type:DPMS Target
          Subject:Microsoft Windows Server 2019
          Identifier:2907
          Definitions:
          Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.windows2019:def:205925
          Definition Result:true
          Title:WN19-CC-000450 - Windows Server 2019 must disable automatically signing in the last interactive user after a system-initiated restart.
          Description:Windows can be configured to automatically sign the user back in after a Windows Update restart. Some protections are in place to help ensure this is done in a secure fashion; however, disabling this will prevent the caching of credentials for this purpose and also ensure the user is aware of the restart.
          Class:compliance
          Tests:
          • true (All child tests must be true. (AND))
            • true (All child tests must be true. (AND))
              • true (Verifies 'Sign-in last interactive user automatically after a system-initiated restart' is set to 'Disabled')
          Tests:
          Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:25341300 (registry_test)
          Result:true
          Title:Verifies 'Sign-in last interactive user automatically after a system-initiated restart' is set to 'Disabled'
          Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
          Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).
          Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:obj:25341300 (registry_object)
          Object Comment: 
          Object Requirements:
          • check = 'hive' must be equal to 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE'
          • check = 'key' must be equal to 'SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Policies\System'
          • check = 'name' must be equal to 'DisableAutomaticRestartSignOn'
          State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:20000001 (registry_state)
          State Comment: 
          State Requirements:
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'type' must be equal to 'reg_dword'
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'value' must be equal to '1'
          Collected Item/State Result:
          [ true ]
          • hive equals 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE'
          • key equals 'SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Policies\System'
          • name equals 'DisableAutomaticRestartSignOn'
          • last_write_time equals '134213413420000000'
          • type equals 'reg_dword'
          • value equals '1'
          • windows_view equals '64_bit'

          V-257503 - Windows Server 2019 must have PowerShell Transcription enabled.

          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-257503r958420_rule
          Test Type:Automated
          Rule Result:Pass
          Version:WN19-CC-000530
          Identities:CCI-000134 (NIST SP 800-53: AU-3; NIST SP 800-53A: AU-3.1; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: AU-3; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: AU-3 e)
          Description: Maintaining an audit trail of system activity logs can help identify configuration errors, troubleshoot service disruptions, and analyze compromises that have occurred, as well as detect attacks. Audit logs are necessary to provide a trail of evidence in case the system or network is compromised. Collecting this data is essential for analyzing the security of information assets and detecting signs of suspicious and unexpected behavior.

          Enabling PowerShell Transcription will record detailed information from the processing of PowerShell commands and scripts. This can provide additional detail when malware has run on a system.
          Fix Text:Configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Administrative Templates >> Windows Components >> Windows PowerShell >> "Turn on PowerShell Transcription" to "Enabled".

          Specify the Transcript output directory to point to a Central Log Server or another secure location to prevent user access.
          Severity:medium
          Weight:10.0
          Reference:
          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows Server 2019
          Publisher:DISA
          Type:DPMS Target
          Subject:Microsoft Windows Server 2019
          Identifier:2907
          Definitions:
          Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.windows2019:def:257503
          Definition Result:true
          Title:WN19-CC-000530 - Windows Server 2019 must have PowerShell Transcription enabled.
          Description:Maintaining an audit trail of system activity logs can help identify configuration errors, troubleshoot service disruptions, and analyze compromises that have occurred, as well as detect attacks. Audit logs are necessary to provide a trail of evidence in case the system or network is compromised. Collecting this data is essential for analyzing the security of information assets and detecting signs of suspicious and unexpected behavior.

          Enabling PowerShell Transcription will record detailed information from the processing of PowerShell commands and scripts. This can provide additional detail when malware has run on a system.
          Class:compliance
          Tests:
          • true (All child tests must be true. (AND))
            • true (All child tests must be true. (AND))
              • true (PowerShell Transcription is enabled on Windows 11)
          Tests:
          Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:25341500 (registry_test)
          Result:true
          Title:PowerShell Transcription is enabled on Windows 11
          Check Existence:All collected items must exist.
          Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).
          Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:obj:25341500 (registry_object)
          Object Comment: 
          Object Requirements:
          • check = 'hive' must be equal to 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE'
          • check = 'key' must be equal to 'SOFTWARE\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\PowerShell\Transcription'
          • check = 'name' must be equal to 'EnableTranscripting'
          State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:20000001 (registry_state)
          State Comment: 
          State Requirements:
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'type' must be equal to 'reg_dword'
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'value' must be equal to '1'
          Collected Item/State Result:
          [ true ]
          • hive equals 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE'
          • key equals 'SOFTWARE\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\PowerShell\Transcription'
          • name equals 'EnableTranscripting'
          • last_write_time equals '134213413420000000'
          • type equals 'reg_dword'
          • value equals '1'
          • windows_view equals '64_bit'

          V-278934 - Windows Server 2019 must be configured to audit file system failures.

          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-278934r1135330_rule
          Test Type:Automated
          Rule Result:Pass
          Version:WN19-AU-000581
          Identities:CCI-000172 (NIST SP 800-53: AU-12 c; NIST SP 800-53A: AU-12.1 (iv); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: AU-12 c; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: AU-12 c)
          Description: Maintaining an audit trail of system activity logs can help identify configuration errors, troubleshoot service disruptions, and analyze compromises that have occurred, as well as detect attacks. Audit logs are necessary to provide a trail of evidence in case the system or network is compromised. Collecting this data is essential for analyzing the security of information assets and detecting signs of suspicious and unexpected behavior. Credential validation records events related to validation tests on credentials for a user account logon.
          Fix Text:Configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Windows Settings >> Security Settings >> Advanced Audit Policy Configuration >> System Audit Policies >> Object Access >> "Audit File System" with "Failure" selected.
          Severity:medium
          Weight:10.0
          Reference:
          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows Server 2019
          Publisher:DISA
          Type:DPMS Target
          Subject:Microsoft Windows Server 2019
          Identifier:2907
          Definitions:
          Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.windows2019:def:278934
          Definition Result:true
          Title:WN19-AU-000581 - Windows Server 2019 must be configured to audit file system failures.
          Description:Maintaining an audit trail of system activity logs can help identify configuration errors, troubleshoot service disruptions, and analyze compromises that have occurred, as well as detect attacks. Audit logs are necessary to provide a trail of evidence in case the system or network is compromised. Collecting this data is essential for analyzing the security of information assets and detecting signs of suspicious and unexpected behavior. Credential validation records events related to validation tests on credentials for a user account logon.
          Class:compliance
          Tests:
          • true (All child tests must be true. (AND))
            • true (One or more child tests must be true. (OR))
              • false ('Audit File System' is set to 'Failure')
              • true ('Audit File System' is set to 'Success' and 'Failure')
          Tests:
          Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:27894200 (auditeventpolicysubcategories_test)
          Result:false
          Title:'Audit File System' is set to 'Failure'
          Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
          Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).
          Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:obj:20000000 (auditeventpolicysubcategories_object)
          Object Comment: 
          Object Requirements:
          • Collect any available items.
          State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:27894200 (auditeventpolicysubcategories_state)
          State Comment:'File System' is set to 'Failure'
          State Requirements:
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'file_system' must be equal to 'AUDIT_FAILURE'
          Collected Item/State Result:
          [ false ]
          • credential_validation equals 'AUDIT_SUCCESS_FAILURE'
          • kerberos_authentication_service equals 'AUDIT_NONE'
          • kerberos_service_ticket_operations equals 'AUDIT_NONE'
          • kerberos_ticket_events equals 'AUDIT_NONE'

          V-278937 - Windows Server 2019 must be configured to audit handle manipulation successes.

          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-278937r1135339_rule
          Test Type:Automated
          Rule Result:Pass
          Version:WN19-AU-000584
          Identities:CCI-000172 (NIST SP 800-53: AU-12 c; NIST SP 800-53A: AU-12.1 (iv); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: AU-12 c; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: AU-12 c)
          Description: Maintaining an audit trail of system activity logs can help identify configuration errors, troubleshoot service disruptions, and analyze compromises that have occurred, as well as detect attacks. Audit logs are necessary to provide a trail of evidence in case the system or network is compromised. Collecting this data is essential for analyzing the security of information assets and detecting signs of suspicious and unexpected behavior. Credential validation records events related to validation tests on credentials for a user account logon.
          Fix Text:Configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Windows Settings >> Security Settings >> Advanced Audit Policy Configuration >> System Audit Policies >> Object Access >> "Audit Handle Manipulation" with "Success" selected.
          Severity:medium
          Weight:10.0
          Reference:
          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows Server 2019
          Publisher:DISA
          Type:DPMS Target
          Subject:Microsoft Windows Server 2019
          Identifier:2907
          Definitions:
          Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.windows2019:def:278937
          Definition Result:true
          Title:WN19-AU-000584 - Windows Server 2019 must be configured to audit handle manipulation successes.
          Description:Maintaining an audit trail of system activity logs can help identify configuration errors, troubleshoot service disruptions, and analyze compromises that have occurred, as well as detect attacks. Audit logs are necessary to provide a trail of evidence in case the system or network is compromised. Collecting this data is essential for analyzing the security of information assets and detecting signs of suspicious and unexpected behavior. Credential validation records events related to validation tests on credentials for a user account logon.
          Class:compliance
          Tests:
          • true (All child tests must be true. (AND))
            • true (One or more child tests must be true. (OR))
              • false ('Audit Handle Manipulation' is set to 'Success')
              • true ('Audit Handle Manipulation' is set to 'Success' and 'Failure')
          Tests:
          Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:27894500 (auditeventpolicysubcategories_test)
          Result:false
          Title:'Audit Handle Manipulation' is set to 'Success'
          Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
          Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).
          Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:obj:20000000 (auditeventpolicysubcategories_object)
          Object Comment: 
          Object Requirements:
          • Collect any available items.
          State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:27894500 (auditeventpolicysubcategories_state)
          State Comment:'Handle Manipulation' is set to 'Success'
          State Requirements:
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'handle_manipulation' must be equal to 'AUDIT_SUCCESS'

          V-278938 - Windows Server 2019 must be configured to audit registry failures.

          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-278938r1135342_rule
          Test Type:Automated
          Rule Result:Pass
          Version:WN19-AU-000585
          Identities:CCI-000172 (NIST SP 800-53: AU-12 c; NIST SP 800-53A: AU-12.1 (iv); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: AU-12 c; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: AU-12 c)
          Description: Maintaining an audit trail of system activity logs can help identify configuration errors, troubleshoot service disruptions, and analyze compromises that have occurred, as well as detect attacks. Audit logs are necessary to provide a trail of evidence in case the system or network is compromised. Collecting this data is essential for analyzing the security of information assets and detecting signs of suspicious and unexpected behavior. Credential validation records events related to validation tests on credentials for a user account logon.
          Fix Text:Configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Windows Settings >> Security Settings >> Advanced Audit Policy Configuration >> System Audit Policies >> Object Access >> "Audit Registry" with "Failure" selected.
          Severity:medium
          Weight:10.0
          Reference:
          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows Server 2019
          Publisher:DISA
          Type:DPMS Target
          Subject:Microsoft Windows Server 2019
          Identifier:2907
          Definitions:

          Definition Result:true

          Description:Maintaining an audit trail of system activity logs can help identify configuration errors, troubleshoot service disruptions, and analyze compromises that have occurred, as well as detect attacks. Audit logs are necessary to provide a trail of evidence in case the system or network is compromised. Collecting this data is essential for analyzing the security of information assets and detecting signs of suspicious and unexpected behavior. Credential validation records events related to validation tests on credentials for a user account logon.
          Class:compliance

          V-278939 - Windows Server 2019 must be configured to audit registry successes.

          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-278939r1135345_rule
          Test Type:Automated
          Rule Result:Pass
          Version:WN19-AU-000586
          Identities:CCI-000172 (NIST SP 800-53: AU-12 c; NIST SP 800-53A: AU-12.1 (iv); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: AU-12 c; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: AU-12 c)
          Description: Maintaining an audit trail of system activity logs can help identify configuration errors, troubleshoot service disruptions, and analyze compromises that have occurred, as well as detect attacks. Audit logs are necessary to provide a trail of evidence in case the system or network is compromised. Collecting this data is essential for analyzing the security of information assets and detecting signs of suspicious and unexpected behavior. Credential validation records events related to validation tests on credentials for a user account logon.
          Fix Text:Configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Windows Settings >> Security Settings >> Advanced Audit Policy Configuration >> System Audit Policies >> Object Access >> "Audit Registry" with "Success" selected.
          Severity:medium
          Weight:10.0
          Reference:
          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows Server 2019
          Publisher:DISA
          Type:DPMS Target
          Subject:Microsoft Windows Server 2019
          Identifier:2907
          Definitions:
          Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.windows2019:def:278939
          Definition Result:true
          Title:WN19-AU-000586 - Windows Server 2019 must be configured to audit registry successes.
          Description:Maintaining an audit trail of system activity logs can help identify configuration errors, troubleshoot service disruptions, and analyze compromises that have occurred, as well as detect attacks. Audit logs are necessary to provide a trail of evidence in case the system or network is compromised. Collecting this data is essential for analyzing the security of information assets and detecting signs of suspicious and unexpected behavior. Credential validation records events related to validation tests on credentials for a user account logon.
          Class:compliance
          Tests:
          • true (All child tests must be true. (AND))
            • true (One or more child tests must be true. (OR))
              • false ('Audit Registry' is set to 'Success')
              • true ('Audit Registry' is set to 'Success' and 'Failure')
          Tests:
          Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:27894700 (auditeventpolicysubcategories_test)
          Result:false
          Title:'Audit Registry' is set to 'Success'
          Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
          Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).
          Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:obj:20000000 (auditeventpolicysubcategories_object)
          Object Comment: 
          Object Requirements:
          • Collect any available items.
          State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:27894700 (auditeventpolicysubcategories_state)
          State Comment:'Registry' is set to 'Success'
          State Requirements:
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'registry' must be equal to 'AUDIT_SUCCESS'
          Collected Item/State Result:
          [ false ]

          • kerberos_authentication_service equals 'AUDIT_NONE'

        Detailed Results: Low Severity (CAT III)

          V-205691 - Windows Server 2019 Application Compatibility Program Inventory must be prevented from collecting data and sending the information to Microsoft.

          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-205691r958478_rule
          Test Type:Automated
          Rule Result:Pass
          Version:WN19-CC-000200
          Identities:V-93409
          SV-103495
          CCI-000381 (NIST SP 800-53: CM-7; NIST SP 800-53A: CM-7.1 (ii); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: CM-7 a; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: CM-7 a)
          Description: Some features may communicate with the vendor, sending system information or downloading data or components for the feature. Turning off this capability will prevent potentially sensitive information from being sent outside the enterprise and will prevent uncontrolled updates to the system.

          This setting will prevent the Program Inventory from collecting data about a system and sending the information to Microsoft.
          Fix Text:Configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Administrative Templates >> Windows Components >> Application Compatibility >> "Turn off Inventory Collector" to "Enabled".
          Severity:low
          Weight:10.0
          Reference:
          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows Server 2019
          Publisher:DISA
          Type:DPMS Target
          Subject:Microsoft Windows Server 2019
          Identifier:2907
          Definitions:
          Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.windows2019:def:205691
          Definition Result:true
          Title:WN19-CC-000200 - Windows Server 2019 Application Compatibility Program Inventory must be prevented from collecting data and sending the information to Microsoft.
          Description:Some features may communicate with the vendor, sending system information or downloading data or components for the feature. Turning off this capability will prevent potentially sensitive information from being sent outside the enterprise and will prevent uncontrolled updates to the system.

          This setting will prevent the Program Inventory from collecting data about a system and sending the information to Microsoft.
          Class:compliance
          Tests:
          • true (All child tests must be true. (AND))
            • true (All child tests must be true. (AND))
              • true ('Turn off Inventory Collector' is set to 'Enabled')
          Tests:
          Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:25338500 (registry_test)
          Result:true
          Title:'Turn off Inventory Collector' is set to 'Enabled'
          Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
          Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).
          Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:obj:25338500 (registry_object)
          Object Comment: 
          Object Requirements:
          • check = 'hive' must be equal to 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE'
          • check = 'key' must be equal to 'SOFTWARE\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\AppCompat'
          • check = 'name' must be equal to 'DisableInventory'
          State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:20000001 (registry_state)
          State Comment: 
          State Requirements:
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'type' must be equal to 'reg_dword'
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'value' must be equal to '1'
          Collected Item/State Result:
          [ true ]
          • hive equals 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE'
          • key equals 'SOFTWARE\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\AppCompat'
          • name equals 'DisableInventory'
          • last_write_time equals '134213413420000000'
          • type equals 'reg_dword'
          • value equals '1'
          • windows_view equals '64_bit'

          V-205819 - Windows Server 2019 must be configured to ignore NetBIOS name release requests except from WINS servers.

          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-205819r958902_rule
          Test Type:Automated
          Rule Result:Pass
          Version:WN19-CC-000060
          Identities:SV-103627
          V-93541
          CCI-002385 (NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: SC-5; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: SC-5 a)
          Description: Configuring the system to ignore name release requests, except from WINS servers, prevents a denial of service (DoS) attack. The DoS consists of sending a NetBIOS name release request to the server for each entry in the server's cache, causing a response delay in the normal operation of the server's WINS resolution capability.
          Fix Text:Configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Administrative Templates >> MSS (Legacy) >> "MSS: (NoNameReleaseOnDemand) Allow the computer to ignore NetBIOS name release requests except from WINS servers" to "Enabled".

          This policy setting requires the installation of the MSS-Legacy custom templates included with the STIG package. "MSS-Legacy.admx" and "MSS-Legacy.adml" must be copied to the \Windows\PolicyDefinitions and \Windows\PolicyDefinitions\en-US directories respectively.
          Severity:low
          Weight:10.0
          Reference:
          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows Server 2019
          Publisher:DISA
          Type:DPMS Target
          Subject:Microsoft Windows Server 2019
          Identifier:2907
          Definitions:
          Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.windows2019:def:205819
          Definition Result:true
          Title:WN19-CC-000060 - Windows Server 2019 must be configured to ignore NetBIOS name release requests except from WINS servers.
          Description:Configuring the system to ignore name release requests, except from WINS servers, prevents a denial of service (DoS) attack. The DoS consists of sending a NetBIOS name release request to the server for each entry in the server's cache, causing a response delay in the normal operation of the server's WINS resolution capability.
          Class:compliance
          Tests:
          • true (All child tests must be true. (AND))
            • true (All child tests must be true. (AND))
              • true ('MSS: (NoNameReleaseOnDemand) Allow computer to ignore NetBIOS name release requests except from WINS servers' is set to 'Enabled')
          Tests:
          Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:25335600 (registry_test)
          Result:true
          Title:'MSS: (NoNameReleaseOnDemand) Allow computer to ignore NetBIOS name release requests except from WINS servers' is set to 'Enabled'
          Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
          Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).
          Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:obj:25335600 (registry_object)
          Object Comment: 
          Object Requirements:
          • check = 'hive' must be equal to 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE'
          • check = 'key' must be equal to 'System\CurrentControlSet\Services\Netbt\Parameters'
          • check = 'name' must be equal to 'NoNameReleaseOnDemand'
          State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:20000001 (registry_state)
          State Comment: 
          State Requirements:
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'type' must be equal to 'reg_dword'
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'value' must be equal to '1'
          Collected Item/State Result:
          [ true ]
          • hive equals 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE'
          • key equals 'System\CurrentControlSet\Services\Netbt\Parameters'
          • name equals 'NoNameReleaseOnDemand'
          • last_write_time equals '134212788620000000'
          • type equals 'reg_dword'
          • value equals '1'
          • windows_view equals '64_bit'

          V-205858 - Windows Server 2019 Internet Protocol version 6 (IPv6) source routing must be configured to the highest protection level to prevent IP source routing.

          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-205858r991589_rule
          Test Type:Automated
          Rule Result:Pass
          Version:WN19-CC-000030
          Identities:SV-103321
          V-93233
          CCI-000366 (NIST SP 800-53: CM-6 b; NIST SP 800-53A: CM-6.1 (iv); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: CM-6 b; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: CM-6 b)
          Description: Configuring the system to disable IPv6 source routing protects against spoofing.
          Fix Text:Configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Administrative Templates >> MSS (Legacy) >> "MSS: (DisableIPSourceRouting IPv6) IP source routing protection level (protects against packet spoofing)" to "Enabled" with "Highest protection, source routing is completely disabled" selected.

          This policy setting requires the installation of the MSS-Legacy custom templates included with the STIG package. "MSS-Legacy.admx" and "MSS-Legacy.adml" must be copied to the \Windows\PolicyDefinitions and \Windows\PolicyDefinitions\en-US directories respectively.
          Severity:low
          Weight:10.0
          Reference:
          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows Server 2019
          Publisher:DISA
          Type:DPMS Target
          Subject:Microsoft Windows Server 2019
          Identifier:2907
          Definitions:
          Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.windows2019:def:205858
          Definition Result:true
          Title:WN19-CC-000030 - Windows Server 2019 Internet Protocol version 6 (IPv6) source routing must be configured to the highest protection level to prevent IP source routing.
          Description:Configuring the system to disable IPv6 source routing protects against spoofing.
          Class:compliance
          Tests:
          • true (All child tests must be true. (AND))
            • true (All child tests must be true. (AND))
              • true ('MSS: (DisableIPSourceRouting IPv6) IP source routing protection level (protects against packet spoofing)' is set to 'Highest protection, source routing is completely disabled')
          Tests:
          Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:25335300 (registry_test)
          Result:true
          Title:'MSS: (DisableIPSourceRouting IPv6) IP source routing protection level (protects against packet spoofing)' is set to 'Highest protection, source routing is completely disabled'
          Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
          Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).
          Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:obj:25335300 (registry_object)
          Object Comment: 
          Object Requirements:
          • check = 'hive' must be equal to 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE'
          • check = 'key' must be equal to 'System\CurrentControlSet\Services\Tcpip6\Parameters'
          • check = 'name' must be equal to 'DisableIPSourceRouting'
          State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:20000002 (registry_state)
          State Comment: 
          State Requirements:
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'type' must be equal to 'reg_dword'
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'value' must be equal to '2'
          Collected Item/State Result:
          [ true ]
          • hive equals 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE'
          • key equals 'System\CurrentControlSet\Services\Tcpip6\Parameters'
          • name equals 'DisableIPSourceRouting'
          • last_write_time equals '134212788620000000'
          • type equals 'reg_dword'
          • value equals '2'
          • windows_view equals '64_bit'

          V-205859 - Windows Server 2019 source routing must be configured to the highest protection level to prevent Internet Protocol (IP) source routing.

          Description: Configuring the system to disable IP source routing protects against spoofing.
          Fix Text:Configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Administrative Templates >> MSS (Legacy) >> "MSS: (DisableIPSourceRouting) IP source routing protection level (protects against packet spoofing)" to "Enabled" with "Highest protection, source routing is completely disabled" selected.

          This policy setting requires the installation of the MSS-Legacy custom templates included with the STIG package. "MSS-Legacy.admx" and "MSS-Legacy.adml" must be copied to the \Windows\PolicyDefinitions and \Windows\PolicyDefinitions\en-US directories respectively.

          V-205860 - Windows Server 2019 must be configured to prevent Internet Control Message Protocol (ICMP) redirects from overriding Open Shortest Path First (OSPF)-generated routes.

          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-205860r991589_rule
          Test Type:Automated
          Rule Result:Pass
          Version:WN19-CC-000050
          Identities:SV-103325
          V-93237
          CCI-000366 (NIST SP 800-53: CM-6 b; NIST SP 800-53A: CM-6.1 (iv); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: CM-6 b; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: CM-6 b)
          Description: Allowing ICMP redirect of routes can lead to traffic not being routed properly. When disabled, this forces ICMP to be routed via the shortest path first.
          Fix Text:Configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Administrative Templates >> MSS (Legacy) >> "MSS: (EnableICMPRedirect) Allow ICMP redirects to override OSPF generated routes" to "Disabled".

          This policy setting requires the installation of the MSS-Legacy custom templates included with the STIG package. "MSS-Legacy.admx" and "MSS-Legacy.adml" must be copied to the \Windows\PolicyDefinitions and \Windows\PolicyDefinitions\en-US directories respectively.
          Severity:low
          Weight:10.0
          Reference:
          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows Server 2019
          Publisher:DISA
          Type:DPMS Target
          Subject:Microsoft Windows Server 2019
          Identifier:2907
          Definitions:
          Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.windows2019:def:205860
          Definition Result:true
          Title:WN19-CC-000050 - Windows Server 2019 must be configured to prevent Internet Control Message Protocol (ICMP) redirects from overriding Open Shortest Path First (OSPF)-generated routes.
          Description:Allowing ICMP redirect of routes can lead to traffic not being routed properly. When disabled, this forces ICMP to be routed via the shortest path first.
          Class:compliance
          Tests:
          • true (All child tests must be true. (AND))
            • true (All child tests must be true. (AND))
              • true ('MSS: (EnableICMPRedirect) Allow ICMP redirects to override OSPF generated routes' is set to 'Disabled')
          Tests:
          Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:25335500 (registry_test)
          Result:true
          Title:'MSS: (EnableICMPRedirect) Allow ICMP redirects to override OSPF generated routes' is set to 'Disabled'
          Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
          Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).
          Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:obj:25335500 (registry_object)
          Object Comment: 
          Object Requirements:
          • check = 'hive' must be equal to 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE'
          • check = 'key' must be equal to 'SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Services\Tcpip\Parameters'
          • check = 'name' must be equal to 'EnableICMPRedirect'
          State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:20000000 (registry_state)
          State Comment: 
          State Requirements:
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'type' must be equal to 'reg_dword'
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'value' must be equal to '0'
          Collected Item/State Result:
          [ true ]
          • hive equals 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE'
          • key equals 'SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Services\Tcpip\Parameters'
          • name equals 'EnableICMPRedirect'
          • last_write_time equals '134212788620000000'
          • type equals 'reg_dword'
          • value equals '0'
          • windows_view equals '64_bit'

          V-205870 - Windows Server 2019 Windows Update must not obtain updates from other PCs on the Internet.

          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-205870r991589_rule
          Test Type:Automated
          Rule Result:Pass
          Version:WN19-CC-000260
          Identities:SV-103347
          V-93259
          CCI-000366 (NIST SP 800-53: CM-6 b; NIST SP 800-53A: CM-6.1 (iv); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: CM-6 b; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: CM-6 b)
          Description: Windows Update can obtain updates from additional sources instead of Microsoft. In addition to Microsoft, updates can be obtained from and sent to PCs on the local network as well as on the Internet. This is part of the Windows Update trusted process, however to minimize outside exposure, obtaining updates from or sending to systems on the Internet must be prevented.
          Fix Text:Configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Administrative Templates >> Windows Components >> Delivery Optimization >> "Download Mode" to "Enabled" with any option except "Internet" selected.

          Acceptable selections include:

          Bypass (100)
          Group (2)
          HTTP only (0)
          LAN (1)
          Simple (99)
          Severity:low
          Weight:10.0
          Reference:
          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows Server 2019
          Publisher:DISA
          Type:DPMS Target
          Subject:Microsoft Windows Server 2019
          Identifier:2907
          Definitions:
          Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.windows2019:def:205870
          Definition Result:true
          Title:WN19-CC-000260 - Windows Server 2019 Windows Update must not obtain updates from other PCs on the Internet.
          Description:Windows Update can obtain updates from additional sources instead of Microsoft. In addition to Microsoft, updates can be obtained from and sent to PCs on the local network as well as on the Internet. This is part of the Windows Update trusted process, however to minimize outside exposure, obtaining updates from or sending to systems on the Internet must be prevented.
          Class:compliance
          Tests:
          • true (All child tests must be true. (AND))
            • true (All child tests must be true. (AND))
              • true ('DownloadMode' is set to 'Enabled' with 'HTTP only, LAN, Group, Simple or Bypass' selected.)
          Tests:
          Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:25339401 (registry_test)
          Result:true

          V-205871 - Windows Server 2019 Turning off File Explorer heap termination on corruption must be disabled.

          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-205871r991589_rule
          Test Type:Automated
          Rule Result:Pass
          Version:WN19-CC-000320
          Identities:V-93261
          SV-103349
          CCI-000366 (NIST SP 800-53: CM-6 b; NIST SP 800-53A: CM-6.1 (iv); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: CM-6 b; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: CM-6 b)
          Description: Legacy plug-in applications may continue to function when a File Explorer session has become corrupt. Disabling this feature will prevent this.
          Fix Text:The default behavior is for File Explorer heap termination on corruption to be disabled.

          If this needs to be corrected, configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Administrative Templates >> Windows Components >> File Explorer >> "Turn off heap termination on corruption" to "Not Configured" or "Disabled".
          Severity:low
          Weight:10.0
          Reference:
          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows Server 2019
          Publisher:DISA
          Type:DPMS Target
          Subject:Microsoft Windows Server 2019
          Identifier:2907
          Definitions:
          Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.windows2019:def:205871
          Definition Result:true
          Title:WN19-CC-000320 - Windows Server 2019 Turning off File Explorer heap termination on corruption must be disabled.
          Description:Legacy plug-in applications may continue to function when a File Explorer session has become corrupt. Disabling this feature will prevent this.
          Class:compliance
          Tests:
          • true (All child tests must be true. (AND))
            • true (All child tests must be true. (AND))
              • true ('Turn off heap termination on corruption' is set to 'Not Configured' or 'Disabled')
          Tests:
          Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:25339700 (registry_test)
          Result:true
          Title:'Turn off heap termination on corruption' is set to 'Not Configured' or 'Disabled'
          Check Existence:Zero or more collected items may exist.
          Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).
          Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:obj:25339700 (registry_object)
          Object Comment: 
          Object Requirements:
          • check = 'hive' must be equal to 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE'
          • check = 'key' must be equal to 'SOFTWARE\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\Explorer'
          • check = 'name' must be equal to 'NoHeapTerminationOnCorruption'
          State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:20000000 (registry_state)
          State Comment: 
          State Requirements:
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'type' must be equal to 'reg_dword'
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'value' must be equal to '0'

          V-205923 - Windows Server 2019 default permissions of global system objects must be strengthened.

          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-205923r991589_rule
          Test Type:Automated
          Rule Result:Pass
          Version:WN19-SO-000370
          Identities:V-93309
          SV-103397
          CCI-000366 (NIST SP 800-53: CM-6 b; NIST SP 800-53A: CM-6.1 (iv); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: CM-6 b; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: CM-6 b)
          Description: Windows systems maintain a global list of shared system resources such as DOS device names, mutexes, and semaphores. Each type of object is created with a default Discretionary Access Control List (DACL) that specifies who can access the objects with what permissions. When this policy is enabled, the default DACL is stronger, allowing non-administrative users to read shared objects but not to modify shared objects they did not create.
          Fix Text:Configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Windows Settings >> Security Settings >> Local Policies >> Security Options >> "System objects: Strengthen default permissions of internal system objects (e.g., Symbolic Links)" to "Enabled".
          Severity:low
          Weight:10.0
          Reference:
          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows Server 2019
          Publisher:DISA
          Type:DPMS Target
          Subject:Microsoft Windows Server 2019
          Identifier:2907
          Definitions:
          Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.windows2019:def:205923
          Definition Result:true
          Title:WN19-SO-000370 - Windows Server 2019 default permissions of global system objects must be strengthened.
          Description:Windows systems maintain a global list of shared system resources such as DOS device names, mutexes, and semaphores. Each type of object is created with a default Discretionary Access Control List (DACL) that specifies who can access the objects with what permissions. When this policy is enabled, the default DACL is stronger, allowing non-administrative users to read shared objects but not to modify shared objects they did not create.
          Class:compliance
          Tests:
          • true (All child tests must be true. (AND))
            • true (All child tests must be true. (AND))
              • true ('System objects: Strengthen default permissions of internal system objects (e.g. Symbolic links)' is set to 'Enabled'.)
          Tests:
          Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:25346700 (registry_test)
          Result:true
          Title:'System objects: Strengthen default permissions of internal system objects (e.g. Symbolic links)' is set to 'Enabled'.
          Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
          Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).
          Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:obj:25346700 (registry_object)
          Object Comment: 
          Object Requirements:
          • check = 'hive' must be equal to 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE'
          • check = 'key' must be equal to 'SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Control\Session Manager'
          • check = 'name' must be equal to 'ProtectionMode'
          State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:20000001 (registry_state)
          State Comment: 
          State Requirements:
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'type' must be equal to 'reg_dword'
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'value' must be equal to '1'
          Collected Item/State Result:
          [ true ]
          • hive equals 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE'
          • key equals 'SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Control\Session Manager'
          • name equals 'ProtectionMode'
          • last_write_time equals '134212836080000000'
          • type equals 'reg_dword'
          • value equals '1'
          • windows_view equals '64_bit'
